Comment: Ben Thanh Market is ideally located in Central Saigon. It is a must visit in any trip to Saigon. It is large and has merchandise of all kinds. It attracts crowds of people daily. The prices hate can always be battered down.
Excellent shopping experience. Although the entrances post fixed prices not true. Start at 20% and settle for a 1/3 and never higher than 1/2 if no time to bargain. Lots to see.
The biggest and busiest market I have been to. They love to bata here. Best place I like to go for smoothies.  Across the back side of the market is the best place for money exchange.

Great place to visit with the family. Even my 12 year old got into the groove of bartering.  Came away with some really cool knock off merch. I don’t know why you’d come HCMC and not visit here. Be brave and don’t be afraid to walk away!

actual visit was August 2024, but only just got round to reviewing it  This was the first place we actually visited on our trip to Ho Chi Minh, it was absolutely pouring down outside with rain bouncing off the street so it was great to be dry, and WHAT an experience it was, a bit of haggling here and there, some interesting purchases, but more importantly was the feel of the place, it had its own atmosphere, I can imagine a shy couple might be overwhelmed but we a family of four adults loved the bustle of the place, we ate on plastic stools with bowls / plates of pho resting on upturned plastic crates and the food was great. We actually went back again twice more, once to see other areas of the market which is huge we never covered the first time and once to buy umbrellas in the no haggle zone. It felt very safe and quite friendly.

It's perhaps the biggest bargain shopping market. This was exactly 7 mins. walk from our hotel - Fusion Original Saigon Centre.  Here you mainly get fabric for ladies to stitch clothes (you can just buy the fabric as well), First copy of a lot of items from clothes, shoes and watches. You get a variety of tea and coffee produced locally. And of course, loads of dry food stuff which you can carry home. And souvenirs too!  The key to shop here is not to fall for the merchandise in the very first shop. One must move around, check out the merchandise and then stop to bargain and buy. A word of caution on the bargain front. While we should bargain hard, but if we think the shop owners will listen to any ridiculous offer we make, think twice. I had an experience wherein one shop owner asked the person to get lost as he was asking her for a ridiculously high discount. In another case when I gave a price for T shirts I had to buy, the shop owner said, "Thank you" and started taking back her merchandise. That's a cue that you have asked for a bit too much. Only if you feel your monies worth for the thing you are buying, go for it else jump to the next shop.
A souvenir-filled market where bargaining is a must—some vendors ask sky-high prices! You’ll also find spots to grab a bite.

Visited a few times and market is a maze of stores, but for western women who are not a size 8-10 The choices are limited for jeans and a lot of clothing. Pricing for some items were cheaper than Hanoi markets but it pays to barter and be prepared to walk away. In general paid aprox 50% of asking price and some times less.
Very nice place to visit has variety of goods, very clean and people are so friendly and welcoming, the price are so affordable

This was a great market which was one of Saigons surviving structures. If it's made, it's probably here. Be sure to barter for your purchases. Fish, meat, wattches, jewellery and lots of clothese can be found here. There is also a Lacquer Factory near by where you can watch the work of artisans as they create beautiful items which you can buy.

Bến Thành Market is also a fantastic destination to indulge in Ho Chi Minh city’s distinctive cuisine. Notable and popular dishes can easily be found in the market to savour. We dedicate a separate article for the list of must-try food in Ben Thanh market area for you to explore.
Obrovská tržnice, ve které se můžete i ztratit, poskytuje veškeré zboží, jež byste si přáli. Můžete se zde kvalitně a levně najíst či si koupit oblečení od největších módních domů.

The market itself is a humid environment with occasional aggressive vendors pulling you or blocking your way, trying to coerce you into buying something. I went twice, once in the morning and another in the late afternoon. It seems they get more aggressive as the hour pass. If you think the price here is too expensive as compared to Saigon Square, you need to work on your bargaining. There is no way an A/C environment can sell lower than this market. Also beware of the the Shoe shine/repair scam taking place across the streets. Seen so many people falling for it. There's another gang of them over at Saigon Square.  This guy (attached photo) is probably the most honest guy within the market. I went around comparing prices also with Saigon Square. He wasn’t upset when I came back to him and bargain further. Some others would ignore you if you walk away and return later.  When I reached my hotel, I noticed a slight defect and went back asking for a change. He took the trouble to walk to another place, took out all the colours and size for me to choose again. That is even after I have removed the tag on the defective piece.  The 5 stars is solely for this man making an honest living with dignity.

Amazing old style market, vibrant and colorful. From nuts, coffee, souvenirs to clothing, mobile and electronics you can find everything here. Be ready to haggle on the prices as they quote you at least 5 times the actual price…We bot some fridge magnets, chop sticks etc just small souvenirs nothing expensive…
A fascinating market where you can discover plenty of items at fantastic prices—perfect for scoring a great deal. 😁😁😁
Här kan du pruta ner från 1,900,000 till ca 400,00-500,000. Mycket att se och bra kvalitet på väskor och liknande. Trevlig marknad.

It's a bustling maze packed with everything you can imagine, from souvenirs to local food. You definitely need to be ready to bargain with a smile! We enjoyed the energetic atmosphere and left with a few good finds. A classic Ho Chi Minh City experience.
Nice place and to much food you can see and buy anything here, but if you want to buy gift here maybe no.Because to expensive ,but I love this place.

Night views of hoi chi minh city were amazing . My guide sun was very attentive and very knowledgeable about Vietnamese history. I particularly liked the views around the river. And trying Vietnamese dishes .which were ok but that's my personal taste. I would recommend tour . If you get sun as your driver you will be happy.
I don't rate the price high when paying here when shopping but the space and the typical clock symbol of the market impressed me.

Wow You have so many choices, there is something for everyone, bags, shoes, socks, undies, t-shirt’s, souvenirs ++ great quality, yes you can barter, we had great food and juices there, happy shopping 🛍️

Busy, beehive of activity where anything and everything is available for a price. Yes, it is dirty and congested but its colourful too, with endearing sights, lingering smells and buzzing sounds. Haggle away – you’re expected to. But, please be aware that as you go further into the market, prices, products and selection changes, sometimes in your favour, other times not.

Lots of shops to shop souvenir, tee, bags, food court. Hard bargain like 50-70% less than asking price. Shop no 938 and 964 shops were good, did lot of shopping. Emy the young owner is cool n gives good bargains on items in shop.

Ben Thanh Market in Saigon is a vibrant and bustling hub of local culture, offering a wide variety of goods, from fresh produce and spices to clothing and souvenirs. The market's lively atmosphere and colorful displays make it a must-visit for tourists. While it can be crowded and prices may be higher for tourists, bargaining is part of the experience. Its central location makes it convenient to explore, and the surrounding area offers plenty of street food options to enhance your visit. Overall, Ben Thanh Market provides an authentic taste of Saigon's vibrant street culture.
It's a good place for shopping, the prices though are not Vietnam prices but for tourists it's ok . The food there is cheap and authentic.

Chợ Bến Thành là điểm đến không thể bỏ qua ở Sài Gòn! Nơi đây có đa dạng hàng hóa từ quà lưu niệm, quần áo đến ẩm thực đặc trưng. Giá cả có thể hơi cao, nên nhớ trả giá khi mua sắm. Không khí sôi động, đậm chất văn hóa Việt Nam.
Great place to buy some souvenirs, like keychain, cloth, magnet, coffee etc, but you need to bargain. Large enough, you need to check every alley. Prefer bring cash.
We adored the bustling frenzy! SO MUCH SHOPPING! SAW LOCAL CRAFTS and delicious feasts A tad too much information but so so good. Haggle hard! Fun for us!!!

Great market for food clothes bags etc. Bartering with locals is great fun they love it as much as I did. All the goods are really good copies and I am still wearing clothes from last time I was there in 2019. The food is also tasty and cheap.
Cena Vietnamitq eccellente piatti delicati e gustosi personale gentile ed educato prezzo economico ci torneremo presto

Visited in December 2024 for the 100th time (not bad for a 'Norwegian', right?). This mega market has everything under one roof, all proudly stamped with "Made in Vietnam," including local delicacies.  Pro Tips: Haggling Skills Required: Prices start high, like double what you should pay. So, channel your inner negotiator!  Why You Should Visit: It's HUGE! You could get lost (in a good way). Feel the vibrant Saigon vibe. Perfect for souvenirs and a taste of local food.  Why You Might Want to Skip: It's crowded. Like, really crowded. Bargaining with aggressive sellers can be intense. Fake goods galore. Beware!

Ignore the snobby a$$ reviews below. If you are ready for a haggle, to play the game of hunting for that perfect knock off or just want to immerse yourself in some crazy then this is the place to go. It's south east Asia. It's a market place. Wtf else is it going to be like other than hot, crowded and really loud. We love this place and have been here so many times. Come prepared to giggle at the ridiculous prices they start off with and walk away with some epic bargains. Side note... There is a shoe vendor on the far right wall (when entering from the clock tower) and their shoes are legit!! Paid $35 Australian for Nike's and New Balance and the QR codes all link. Even took a pair of Nike's in to be repaired when back in Aus and they didn't question it. They will go above and beyond for you.  Have fun bartering 😊

Don’t be put off by fixed price, everything is negotiable. If you push mildly, you’ll get it for a third of their opening offer. All the top brands on offer. Bags and sports apparel the most available.
Chợ nằm ở Thành phố Hồ Chí Minh, rất rộng và bán rất nhiều đồ, đã đi một lần nhưng vẫn chưa đi hết chợ, sẽ còn ghé lại lần sau....

This market is a tourist magnet, for those who love to snag deals for your loves at home. The dry goods were quite a snag, save for those who like dresses and suits. The dried fruits, nuts and snacks were perfect for those who want to sample local nibbles. The food isle was busiest during lunch rush so better be quick to grab a seat to sample good food.

Les odeurs des épices et la variété des plats locaux nous ont enchantés. Les vendeurs sont un peu insistants, mais c'est le charme des marchés vietnamiens. Les prix sont négociables, et nous avons trouvé de superbes souvenirs.

Entre les étals colorés et l'art du marchandage, nous nous sommes bien amusés. Les odeurs d'épices et l'ambiance animée nous ont conquis. Astuce : venez l'estomac vide, le food court est une vraie tentation !

The atmosphere was electric with vendors selling everything from local handicrafts to Vietnamese coffee. Bargaining is a must, and we had fun negotiating prices. The food court offered authentic local dishes at reasonable prices. A bit crowded but that's part of the authentic experience!

We turned shopping at Ben Thanh into our favorite sport! Bargaining became our love language as we navigated through aromatic spices and colorful textiles. The food court saved our marriage during hangry moments.  Don't forget to practice your negotiation face in the mirror! :))))

Be prepared to be constantly asked what you are looking for. I have no doubt that the stall holders will either have what you are looking for or they will get it for you. I had forgotten just how busy it is. Well worth a visit - all sorts of jewellery, footwear, shorts, shirts, hats belts wallets caps cards food sunglasses etc etc Allow plenty of time and remember they are just trying to help you

Ben Thanh Market is the best market for tourists, as they sell various kinds of souvenirs and gifts. You will get almost everything, from food to clothes, footwear, bags, ceramics, and souvenirs in the market. However, hone your bargaining skill before you go. Opt for hotels near this market, so that you can walk to and from it. Also visit the China Market for products at wholesale rates.

The market's energy was infectious, with countless stalls offering everything from local crafts to aromatic spices. We navigated the lively aisles, enjoying the thrill of bargaining and discovering unique souvenirs. It's a must-visit for families wanting to experience the dynamic spirit of Saigon's marketplace.
Good fun. Enjoyed visiting Viets Market assisted by the owner family young man TRUONG TRAN. Good knowledge of coffees.

Enjoy the experience of visiting a market place where haggling and negotiating lower prices is a must for everything you buy.  Many items to purchase such as t-shirts, bags, display items and wallets etc.  Go there and just experience the place, many of the stalls will sell the same items.
인기 만점의 이 시장에 들어서자마자 우리의 지갑이 공포에 떨기 시작했죠. 왜냐고요? 너무나 많은 유혹이 있었거든요! 향신료 냄새에 정신이 혼미해지고, 값싼 '명품' 가방들에 눈이 휘둥그레졌어요. 흥정하다 보니 어느새 베트남어 전문가가 된 것 같았답니다. 우리 신용카드가 울면서 도망가려고 했지만, 우리는 용감하게(?) 쇼핑을 계속했어요.

이 역사적인 시장은 호치민의 심장부에서 활기차게 뛰고 있어요. 우리는 이국적인 향신료, 화려한 직물, 신선한 과일의 향연 속에 푹 빠졌답니다. 상인들과 흥정하는 재미도 있었고, 현지 음식을 맛보는 것도 잊지 못할 경험이었어요. 시장 곳곳에서 베트남의 역사와 문화를 느낄 수 있었죠. 관광객과 현지인들로 북적이는 이곳은 호치민의 진정한 모습을 보여주는 곳이에요.
This market has everything but you must be able to negotiate. Offering vendors 40-50% of the asking price is a good place to start.

Excellent shopping experience, I recommend that you have a look around the stalls where they are marked as fixed price to get an idea of what you ought to be paying for clothing first. I bought several outfits there, and the prices really are fixed. After that, I gained in confidence and entered into the market itself where, one can negotiate. My objective was to try and get a similar outfit for a cheaper price and I achieved that happily and easily. The trick really is to not mind if you end up having to walk away. We enjoyed it so much that we went back the following day and I bought a couple of of the same outfits in different colours, as an example I got some trousers and a wraparound top for, the equivalent of about £6 or €7. I visited a fabric store and chose some Vietnamese silk. the lady took my details and my measurements and photograph of the outfit I wanted replicating. It was a complicated ladies top and the price for having it made in 70% silk and then delivered to my hotel room. Some five hours later was approximately €28. The quantity is incredible I am delighted.
Big market, selling all kinds of items. Prices are reasonable for many items but need to bargain a lot to buy things. :))))))
Leuke markt. Waar je echt van alles kan kopen, kleding, koffie noodle soup voor redelijke prijs en de mensen dringen zich niet zo aan je op.

Prepare for a full-on sensory experience! This bustling market is a must-see in Ho Chi Minh City. We were amazed by the sheer diversity of goods - from souvenirs and clothing to fresh produce and street food. It's crowded and busy, but a fascinating glimpse into local life. Come with a plan and be ready to haggle!

This place is really a shoppers paradise. It has all the a graded bags hats wallet jewellery clothes . Coffee . Tea. Accessories. All you want u can get there. One thing to take note. Do not accept the first price you are quoted for. Have to go down by more than 50% . You can start with 30% of the offer price . Then slowly up . This us a perfect place to shop. Most of things can nego except jewellery at shop outside. which is authentic so no room to negotiable.

I really enjoyed walking through this market. It’s definitely more of the same with the vendors trying their best to entice you to buy anything. The market is also quite well organized with the walkways quite wide and very easy to navigate. The fresh produce section on the one side is definitely not for the squeamish. Avoid if you are so inclined. The market is under roof but no AC only a few fans doing their best.

always a must ti see in Saigon. And soem great people...expensive by Vietnamese standards but you can bargain. Also some small, snazzy cheap street food stalls that are excellent value. Most people speak English so ok. Smiles everywhere

The market has the same products and several of the stores work with each other. If one store doesn’t have the size or color or style you want they’ll get it from another vendor. The aisles are small and it’s extremely hot. No a/c but a few fans. The sellers offer you a seat The end of the market has food. If squeamish don’t go there. The fake goods are good quality especially the soccer outfits Dresses / shirts can be made in a day. Bargain less than 50% of the quoted price.

Big and old market in HCM. Nowadays it becomes a tourist attraction and popular to almost checkers. There’re lot of goods such as clothes, slippers, shoes, fruit jams, vegetables…all needed items for locals’s daily lives. The price is reasonable not too expensive but some of those are fake or copied ones other brands
Ngồi chợ lịch sử của Sài Gòn. Rất đông du khách, hiện tại vô mùa tết nên mọi người lại chụp ảnh áo dài rất nhiều, làm không khí ở đây náo nhiệt nha.

Wow! Fantastic and chaotic all in one! The market can be a bit overwhelming on your own with people frantically trying to attract your attention, but the prices are great and the quality was good. The stall holders were very friendly and whilst haggling is expected some prices were so reasonable I didn't feel the need! So far everything I bought has lasted well and I would happily return.

ベンタイン市場は、ホーチミンらしい活気に満ちた場所でした。お土産探しにぴったりで、ローカルフードを試食するのも楽しかったです。ただし、暑さ対策は必須です！ 市場の中には、コーヒーをはじめ、様々な種類のコーヒー、コピ・ルアク（ジャコウネココーヒー）、ココナッツコーヒーなど、さまざまな商売が行われています。ここを通り過ぎると、香ばしい香りが立ちこめてきます。お土産になる地元製品、帽子、バッグ、小物、人形、マグネット、スポーツウェア、スポーツシューズ、セーターなどがあり、値段は交渉が必要です。

Bước vào Chợ Bến Thành, du khách như lạc vào thế giới sôi động, đầy màu sắc và âm thanh. Nơi đây không chỉ là thiên đường mua sắm với vô số mặt hàng đa dạng từ quần áo, phụ kiện đến đồ lưu niệm, mà còn là nơi để bạn khám phá văn hóa địa phương độc đáo. Thưởng thức những món ăn đường phố thơm ngon, trò chuyện với những người bán hàng thân thiện và hòa mình vào nhịp sống hối hả của Sài Gòn là những trải nghiệm không thể bỏ lỡ khi đến với Chợ Bến Thành.

So Busy! Know what you want to pay before you set foot in the place, know and name your price, start with half of the first offer and be prepared to walk, everyone loves a bargain right. Just remember your talking a fee bucks on most items so don't be a d.. and you'll get the respect and a fair haggle.
我和我的伙伴一起游览了平正市场，领会到了它是西贡购物的热门胜地。市场里人头攒动，叫卖声此起彼伏，充满一种让人兴奋的热闹喧嚣。平正市场商品琳琅满目，从纪念品、手工艺品、到衣服、配饰，应有尽有。我们在这里挑选了不少特色的小礼物带回去。记得来这里逛的时候要注意保管好随身财物，并且享受砍价的乐趣。如果你来胡志明市旅行，想要买点东西，不妨来平正市场逛逛，感受一下最原汁原味的西贡风情。

Our favourite market in Vietnam, it’s got such a buzz about it. It was terrific fun browsing the stalls and chatting with the stall owners. It’s got the usual range of goods for sale; I couldn’t resist buying some trousers, a couple of tops, some souvenirs and a few gifts for friends – you could easily get carried away and buy loads of stuff! The food stalls are terrific, if you visit this market you should definitely take some time for lunch or a snack. We enjoyed banh mi pork sandwiches which were the best we had during our two weeks in Vietnam. The market is a great place to soak up the atmosphere – but don’t forget to haggle. Highly recommended.
Built by the French in 1870, Ben Thanh market is a must-see attraction of Ho Chi Minh City. It is a bustling market in the day and a social center after sunset.

Ben Thanh Market is a shopper's paradise! From souvenirs to spices, you'll find everything under the sun. Get into the spirit, haggle with the vendors, and embrace the chaotic, vibrant atmosphere. It's the perfect place to soak up some local energy and maybe snag a few deals.

This was the first attraction I visited after checking in at my hotel that is located accross from it.  Shopping: There are many things to buy here : Shoes, Clothings, Jewelries, Fruits/Vegetables, Spices, Juices, souvenirs, etc.  Eating: There are several small restaurants offering cheap, delicious local meals. Just walk around the market and see which dishes meet your fancy.  The market is clean and very organized. Be ready to negotiate for the prices of items on display.
Es un sitio muy interesante, puedes ver como trabajan artesanos de diferentes clases, incluso hay clases de cocina. Aparte de comprar cosas muy interesantes.
This place never dissapoints It is hot busy bussly and fun Can get cool drinks any time Bargains every where. Prepare to bargain. Pearls are so good.

Very bustling and vibrant market. Great food options. The vendors did approach you to sell their products but once sat down in a street food stand/ cafe they were very friendly. Would recommend the egg coffee!
Wow… impresionante, venden de todo, ropa, souvenirs, alimentación… Es un poco de kos, tiendas muy juntas, pero muy buena experiencia, divertido y la genre muy amable.
Excellent choice of goods available and beautiful people Great value and not hassled. Able to shop at ease, so much to see

I loved the experience of the markets. Yes be prepared to haggle. the lady at shop 164 is very pleasant and helpful. Gave me very good price for my tommy t shirts checked other shops and she was the cheapest. will defiantly go back when i am there in February.
Nice shops selling selling souvenirs, Groceries and clothes. Had a good experience shopping here. Just be ready to haggle a lot.

A must-visit for anyone looking for an authentic shopping experience. With a rich history dating back to the early 1900s, the market offers a diverse range of goods, from fresh produce to souvenirs. The atmosphere is vibrant and the prices are very competitive. Whether you're looking for a unique souvenir or just a place to explore, Ben Thanh Market is the perfect place to visit.
Great food and cafe around that area There are many shops where you can get gifts and snacks. Do remember to bargain at least 40% less if you love the items.

Just want to recommend Binh at shop no. 616. Good quality and reasonably priced. They accidently left out one of the tee shirts and when I questioned it the next, they gave another shirt, no questions. You xant ask for more than that, good people, so support them

A huge market that caters to all needs, yes it is touristy and there is always somewhere cheaper and better, however, we love the vibe of the place the stall holders can get a bit exuberant, however, still being friendly, during our one month stay we ate (lunch) or drank (smoothies) during our daily walk of this wonderfully chaotic city, in our opinion the food is amazing, so much to choose from.

Know what you're getting yourself into. Even some of the YouTubers that claim they know what they're doing are getting played. Maybe I am too.... The vendors here are sharks, but I speak more than enough Vietnamese to be dangerous! Take a lap around the outer ring. These shops are all fixed price. This will give you an idea of pricing, maximum pricing, before going into the heart of the market. Bargaining is all a game. Don't be worried to walk away you will find whatever it is at another stall. $8-$15 (150K-350K) for fake NorthFace bags $3-$5 (80K-120K) for ceramic coffee phin ~$10 (80K-250K) for Patagonia cross-body and fanny-pack. Kipling cross-body/backpack $4 (80K-100K) t-shirts/ polos Once I had a couple things (Northface bag) bought for what I thought was a good price, I asked about that bag again at a new stall. The new stall would always start high, only question is do they start 2x too high or 10x too high. The food/drink inside are all good for a descent price. Grab a quick bite and a drink to go. Two gold shops across the street from Cua Tay (West Gate) for money exchange with excellent rates. Bag and luggage shops across the street from Cua Dong (East Gate) with good prices Banh Mi cart across the street Cua Bac (North Gate) is one that all the vendors/locals love

We loved the experience of seeing so many products of all sources and kinds for different budgets as well. Prices are reasonable although I have to clarify this market is more visited by tourists than the local people. There is a lot of competition going for best prices. Before buying anything as usual “ compare the pair “ then decide wether to bargain a bit more. Having a calculator is handy. I personally went back a few times to the same lady who I felt comfortable with.

Don’t go here if you don’t like crowds and find haggling confronting. This was much better than the markets in Hanoi who didn’t even try to sell cos it was pack up time in 5 mins. They wouldn’t budge on price or even look for a size I requested. This market couldn’t be more helpful even ran a block away to get the size I needed. Even the quality is great and much better than my kids ever expected.

If you can’t handle the marketers trying to sell their wears then shop somewhere less intimidating. We had the best time here haggling and laughing with the locals, they are only trying to make a living . Some awesome finds including a quirky little antique shop on the outside of the market. Offer half or less the asking price and go from there, some bargains to be had. ( advice I got from reading other reviews )
We find the food in the market food store to be very good and very reasonably priced. If you need to have your currency changed, two gold shops across the market have excellent rates.

Went to this market a few times very large for clothing and bags and assorted stuff Very affordable but don’t take first price and don’t buy straight away suggest you walk around to a few different stalls before you buy and offer half of the price they Asked for and be prepared to walk away because they will accept your offer

This is an excellent and huge indoor marketplace selling everything from food, desserts, packages foods & coffee/tea to souvenirs, luggage, backpacks, clothes, household items, etc. The vendors will bargain so you need to be prepared as the first price is way overpriced.  Our experience has been If you start at half price, can go from there. But if you have an idea of what you want to pay you should offer a little below that and start there. They don’t get offended as their job is to sell, your job is to buy. If you are too low they won’t sell and you walk or you continue negotiating.  If not comfortable with bargaining there is a fixed price section at the front area where the prices are non negotiable.  Our family got so much items here and were super happy - souvenirs, shoes, backpacks, rolling luggage, dried fruit, belts, hat, etc. There are hundreds of stalls here, it’s fun to just wander and enjoy the vibrant atmosphere. It’s a place you must visit if you are in HCMC.
massive market, where you can buy anything from rolex to meat !! still relatively clean space for such massive and popular venue ...

I spent my afternoon at Ben Thanh Market in Ho Chi Minh City, and it was such a fun experience! The market is definitely one of those must-see places if you’re in the city. It’s got that perfect mix of vibrant energy and local charm that makes it feel like you’re truly in the heart of Ho Chi Minh.

The first thing that hits you is the sheer variety of things you can find there. From fresh fruits, local spices, and snacks, to souvenirs, clothing, and handmade crafts, it’s like a maze of different goods packed into one bustling space. I couldn’t help but browse through the stalls, checking out everything from cool handmade bags to unique jewelry. The prices can be a bit higher than other spots in the city, but the quality is good, and the experience is definitely worth it.

One of the highlights for me was the food. I grabbed some delicious Vietnamese snacks like banh xeo (crispy pancakes) and fresh spring rolls, and they were AMAZING. The flavors were so fresh and tasty—definitely a must-try if you’re into street food.

Even though the market can get crowded, especially in the afternoon, it’s got such a lively atmosphere that it makes the hustle and bustle part of the fun. The vendors are friendly, and some even have a little playful banter, which makes shopping here feel more personal.

Overall, Ben Thanh Market is a great spot to get a taste of local culture and pick up some souvenirs or fresh food. It’s one of those places that feels like you’re getting a real slice of the city, and I definitely enjoyed my afternoon there!

I was on a tightly scheduled business trip and didnt have time to explore elsewhere for souvenirs. I stumbled upon this market unintentionally during my morning run. Since I was in a rush, I just went inside, picked one shop, and bought everything I needed there like a few fridge magnets and some bags. Actually I was lucky they accepted cards, as my colleague had warned me that most shops here only take cash, and I had none. I know the prices are a bit high, but with my limited time, I didnt have much choice. What I found funny was that a few other shopkeepers, whose stalls I didnt buy from, seemed irritated or frustrated when I tried to bargain, even slightly. The initial prices are often three to four times higher than in other places. If you have the time, its wise to do your due diligence and research fair prices for goods so you can negotiate effectively here or at other tourist markets like Saigon Square etc. Nevertheless, for the experience, its fine to go there once for a quick walkthrough to see the bustling action. Have fun, embrace the experience, and dont take offense if sellers get angry or curse in their native tongue. Just let it roll off and keep walking. Its a wonderful market, I can say. Its like a maze. I almost forgot which gate I came in from and ended up exiting through a different one, which was quite exciting

Ben Thanh Market is one of the most vibrant and iconic spots in Ho Chi Minh City — a place where you can truly feel the heartbeat of Vietnam. From the moment you walk in, you’re surrounded by colorful stalls selling everything from local handicrafts and clothes to aromatic spices and street food. It’s not just a market; it’s an experience. Bargaining is part of the fun here, so don’t be shy to negotiate with a smile. I especially recommend trying the freshly made bánh mì and Vietnamese iced coffee — they’re simple but unforgettable. The atmosphere is busy yet full of charm, with friendly vendors and the sound of life all around you. Even if you don’t plan to shop, walking through the aisles gives you a genuine glimpse into local culture. Located right in the city center, Ben Thanh Market is a must-visit for travelers who want to taste, see, and feel the authentic spirit of Saigon in one place.

Chợ Bến Thành is the iconic, pulsing heart of Ho Chi Minh City, and it completely earns its legendary status. More than just a market, it's a vibrant sensory institution where the energy of old and new Saigon collide under one massive, historic clock-towered roof.

Step inside to a captivating maze of commerce. The organized chaos is part of its charm: aisles overflow with dazzling silk áo dài, lacquerware, handicrafts, and every souvenir imaginable. The sheer variety is astounding, and while bargaining is part of the dance, the lively interactions with vendors—many with generations of history here—are a cultural experience in themselves.

The surrounding outdoor food stalls are a culinary paradise. The aroma of sizzling bánh xèo (sizzling pancakes), steaming phở, and robust Vietnamese coffee is irresistible. Sitting on a tiny plastic stool for a fresh, delicious meal is an essential Saigon moment.

I really enjoyed the experience. My fiancé, our friend, and I did some research about the market beforehand. We were unsure about going because online you often read that the sellers can be aggressive or pushy, and that tourists usually get higher prices. But we decided to go anyway — and we actually ended up laughing a lot with the vendors.

Of course, some sellers are pushy and will try to make you pay the highest price. But if you approach them in a respectful, polite, and even slightly humorous way, the experience becomes so much better. You can still negotiate the price in a friendly and respectful way without acting entitled — and many vendors will appreciate that and respond positively.

You do need to be able to handle the vendors calling you to their stalls, but remember that these people work extremely hard: from 6 AM to 6 PM inside the market, and from 6 AM to 10 PM outside the market. Show them respect, and they will treat you kindly as well.

Fun lively, colorful, and full of great deals.  I can see how some visitors get intimidated, annoyed, or at worse offended with this market, but it’s your fault for not doing your research. This is Ben Thanh market, don’t you know?  And if you don’t know now you know.  I love the friendly, sincere, respectful hustle of the vendors. The polite persistence, the requests for a sale, the acknowledgment of your passing their shop. It’s all here for all to enjoy and appreciate.  Getting past all that, you can get practically anything from its closest source.  Who do you think makes these Gucci bags, Chanel wallet, LV luggage, etc.  You know what’s offensive? These brands exploit the cheap skilled labor and maximize profits by playing on your insecurities about not being enough or not having enough.  The mark up is obscene. Their branding and advertising strategy is hyper successful at it.  Quench your materialistic thirst and fill your self worth by buying something.  Help a vendor out, madame. Negotiate your best price. You’ll be happy that you saved so much money for it and if you’re good at picking out quality, no one will notice.  Practice your negotiating techniques and enjoy the great banter.
On another note, the shear variety of shopping should fill all your gift and souvenir needs.  Local handmade items to name brand items.  There are food stalls to experience and on the outskirts you can enjoy a slower less aggressive sales approach.  Ben Thanh is a must experience must visit in HCMC.
If you discover it’s still not for you, I admire you for giving it a go.

Ben Thanh Market is full of energy and atmosphere, and the vendors are very friendly, making it a fun place to walk around.

However, bargaining is essential here — 30–40% discounts are completely normal, so don’t forget to negotiate.

The same items are sold at many different stalls, and you can also find them in other areas of the city, so there’s no need to rush or settle.

It’s best to visit Ben Thanh Market with the mindset of enjoying the bargaining experience rather than focusing only on shopping.

Ben Thanh Market is a lively, must-visit spot with a huge variety of local goods, souvenirs, clothes, and food all in one place. The atmosphere is energetic and very much part of the local experience. Bargaining is part of the fun here — whatever price is quoted, start by offering about 50% off, as prices are usually marked up first. From there, negotiate calmly and you’ll typically settle around 30–40% off the initial asking price. Vendors are friendly, and if you take it with a smile, it can be an enjoyable experience. What to find there
Souvenirs: Look for handicrafts, conical hats, lacquerware, and art.
Clothing and fashion: You can find traditional Vietnamese Ao Dai, fabrics, handbags, shoes, and accessories, including replica goods.
Food and spices: Sample local dishes and buy spices, coffee, tea, and fresh produce.
Jewelry: Find handmade jewelry made from silver, gold, pearls, and other gemstones.
Shopping tips
Bargain: Haggling is a key part of the experience. It is recommended to offer a lower price, sometimes starting at 40-50% of the asking price.
Check prices: Prices inside the market are often higher than outside, so compare prices if you have the chance.
Explore: The market has a central area and also extends to the surrounding streets, so be sure to explore to find hidden gems.
Accessibility
Wheelchair access: The market is equipped with a wheelchair-accessible entrance and restroom.
Hours
Daytime market: Approximately 6 AM to 6 PM.
Night market: Starts around 6 PM in the areas surrounding the market.

Bến Thành Market sits right in the heart of District 1 and is considered one of Saigon’s most iconic landmarks. Inside, you can find just about everything – from dried foods, coffee, spices, and local specialties to souvenirs, clothing, handicrafts, and even delicious street food.

The atmosphere here is lively and bustling, especially in the mornings and late afternoons. Visitors come not only to shop but also to soak in the vibrant pulse of the city.

However, prices at Bến Thành Market are often not fixed. Many stalls tend to quote higher prices, especially to foreign tourists or those unfamiliar with bargaining. To get a fair deal, it’s best to check prices at several stalls, compare, and negotiate comfortably – after all, haggling is part of the authentic Vietnamese market experience!

Overall, Bến Thành Market is well worth a visit if you want to catch a glimpse of old Saigon, pick up some local specialties, or find unique souvenirs. Just remember: don’t rush to buy from the first stall you see.

Ben Thanh Market, located in the heart of Ho Chi Minh City's District 1, is a bustling hub of activity and a cultural landmark. Known for its iconic yellow façade and clock tower, the market is a vibrant destination for both locals and tourists. It offers a wide variety of goods, including clothing, textiles, handicrafts, fresh produce, and street food.

The market is divided into sections, each specializing in different items. Visitors can find traditional Vietnamese clothing like Ao Dai, silk garments, and embroidered textiles. Souvenirs such as lacquerware, bamboo products, and handmade jewelry are popular choices. Food enthusiasts will enjoy the selection of Vietnamese coffee beans, dried fruits, spices, and local snacks.

Ben Thanh Market is also a great place to experience Vietnamese street food. From comforting bowls of pho to fresh fruit smoothies, the culinary options are diverse and delicious. The market operates from early morning until late at night, making it convenient for visitors to explore at their own pace.

While shopping, bargaining is a common practice, and visitors can often secure better deals by negotiating prices. The market's lively atmosphere, combined with its rich history and variety of offerings, makes it a must-visit destination in Ho Chi Minh City. Whether you're looking for unique souvenirs or simply want to immerse yourself in the local culture, Ben Thanh Market has something for everyone.

Ben Thanh Market is one of the most vibrant and lively shopping destinations in Vietnam, offering a truly local and memorable experience. Visiting this iconic market is not just about shopping—it is about enjoying the culture, colors, conversations, and the unique humor of Vietnamese street commerce.

Shopping Experience:
The market is a paradise for shoppers, with hundreds of stalls selling everything from souvenirs, handicrafts, clothes, bags, watches, footwear, spices, coffee, dry fruits, and local snacks to traditional Vietnamese items. Bargaining is an essential part of shopping here, and it adds excitement to every purchase. With patience and a smile, visitors can get very good deals, making shopping both fun and rewarding.

Shopkeeper Behaviour:
Shopkeepers at Ben Thanh Market are energetic, friendly, and highly experienced in dealing with tourists from all over the world. They actively invite customers, explain products, and negotiate prices with enthusiasm. While they may initially quote higher prices, their approach is generally polite and good-natured. Once bargaining begins, many shopkeepers show warmth, friendliness, and even playful behavior, making the interaction enjoyable rather than stressful.

Humorous Marketing Experience:
One of the most delightful aspects of Ben Thanh Market is the humorous and creative way shopkeepers attract customers. Many use witty English phrases, jokes, light-hearted teasing, and cheerful comments to grab attention. Their spontaneous humor and friendly banter often make shoppers laugh and feel comfortable, turning a simple purchase into an entertaining experience.

Affordability:
Ben Thanh Market is considered one of the cheapest markets in Vietnam for tourists, especially for souvenirs and local products. With effective bargaining, visitors can buy quality items at very reasonable prices compared to shopping malls or branded stores. It is an ideal place for budget-friendly shopping without compromising on variety.

Overall Impression:
Ben Thanh Market perfectly blends shopping, culture, humor, and affordability. The lively atmosphere, friendly shopkeepers, humorous marketing style, and wide range of low-priced goods make it a must-visit destination in Vietnam. For anyone who wants to experience the true spirit of Vietnamese street shopping, Ben Thanh Market is an unforgettable experience.

Walking into Bến Thành Market is like stepping into a living, breathing organism of trade, where the air is thick with the scent of grilled meats, strong Robusta coffee, dried seafood, and freshly cut tropical fruit. It’s a Costco meets IKEA fever dream, but with a distinct Southeast Asian twist…negotiation/cash is king.

Everything is here Vietnamese coffee, chopsticks, ceramic cups, phin, shot glasses, dried goods, counterfeit “Gucci” wallets, and everything in between. But remember never take the first price. The vendors start high—ridiculously high, so slash it down by 70-80% and work your way up. Negotiation isn’t just a skill here; it’s an art form. And if one shop won’t budge, keep walking another one has the same goods a few stalls down.

The food court is a safe haven from the bargaining madness, with fair prices and some truly excellent local dishes. But still, double check before ordering no harm in asking.

The secret to Bến Thành? Come with a plan know what you want, understand wholesale prices, and prepare to haggle with a smile. The game is part of the experience, and if you embrace it, it’s pure adrenaline-fueled fun.

Would I come back? Absolutely. It’s a must-do, a rite of passage, and a little bit of beautiful chaos all rolled into one.

I snagged some sweet finds, like a new bag, some cool tees, and even a pair of those comfy shoes. You definitely gotta haggle hard though, I got most of my stuff for like half price. Morning's are the quietest part of the day and the coolest. Get an idea of prices in the Fixed Price section before haggling in the market proper. Offer at least half or 40% of starting price. Food is excellent and not that much more than outside. Lots of fun and a must visit.
Having breakfast and shopping here. I love this soup noodles very much.
As a Chinese I love to share my love for this place to others. Didn’t buy anything or bargain for anything but enjoyed walking around watching people bargain :)
Great place to go shopping on local delicacies and clothes. You just need to have enough patience inside.
Its a must visited place if you stay in Ho Chi Minh. Sell shirt, bags, even souvenir are completely here.
A bit crowded when I visited. Overall it's a good place to find stuff like bags,hats,clothing, gift/art items etc. also you can get food items or grab a smoothie, like I did, and explore the  market.
Really cool place. Has a lot of stuff. Souvenirs clothes shoes watches etc. The quality is good. You can bargain. Most sellers are nice and kind. If one isn't just go to the next.

A very nice market place which was walking distance from our hotel near Walking street.

You need to do a lot of bargaining here to get the best rates out of them or you will be guilty of spending more for a lesser value product.

The market is clean and is centrally located. They sell here replicas and look alike of most brands across all products which also include watches, leather bags, lady bags and purses, t shirts, artefacts, souvenirs, fruits, dry fruits  etc

There are also a few garment shops with fixed pricing. My advice is to shops which have fixed prices so that you get the best and not taken for a ride and you end up paying the right prices as the currency there still confuses me. I had a few US dollars and would pay by US dollars for simpler understanding and all shops accept USD here in 🇻🇳

Over all a good looking building that houses the market, reasonable prices but bargaining needed, fixed prices shops advised.

Overall a lovely place for everything and anything !

Please visit !

Stay safe, be careful while riding bikes and crossing roads to have a memorable trip !

Keep 🇻🇳 clean and plastic free.

Go in for some real shopping experience where a lot of bargaining is not possible. The prices here are bit on the higher side than the regular shops but you get a lot of variety throughout the shops. The market closes by 9pm at max. There are a lot of small food joints like juice shops and some quick snacks to savour.

Great market and foreigner-friendly.

This is the most famous market in Ho Chi Minh and has English speaking vendors.

There is a section for fixed prices and a section where bargaining is expected.

The fixed prices section has relatively fair prices and even lower prices than I anticipated.

I‘d say this is a must-visit if you‘re in town.
Biggest tourist attraction in district 1. So much to see and it takes all day to walk the whole market. Don’t forget to haggle a little bit with the vendors.  Truly and overwhelmingly experience

So many things inside here. Of course you have to haggle for good price! I have my own favourite shop. Some of the seller still remember me coz I used to live in Saigon for 4 years and always buy from them.
Excellent for gifts clothes food great prices. Bargain!

Great market to shop for whatever you need. I spent several hours here. Be sure to bargain and stick to your budget. They will always complain about how they don't make money. There are over 1300 different shops here so there can be lots of competition. After spending 5+ hours here. I found the places that join with the outside can give you the best deal. My budget for t-shirt is 80k vnd and only a couple places will go that low. But there are places, so don't give in to the prices they start at. They start at 450k VND but quickly come down when you start losing interest and walking away.

Its a good place for window shopping 😂😂😂 but of course for buying the food, clothes and souvenirs. It just you need to bargain with the seller abit. They have so many good stuffs that you cannot find in other place, those handmade things. Food court is created with so many choices. Enjoy your trip in HoChiMinh City ❤️ …

Ben Thanh Market is one of the oldest and most exemplary traditional markets in Ho Chi Minh City. Inside the market, you'll find a wide variety of products, especially traditional Vietnamese street food. Around the market, there are numerous currency exchange shops with locals and tourists lining up to exchange money. There's also the Egg Yolk coffee shop nearby, which is definitely worth trying.

So many bargains if you barter  and there's a large fixed price area. Shop 732 had the highest quality Birkenstocks for the lowest price out of a dozen or more shops we went to - nice lady with awesome English. Have already been back🫡🫡😊 …

I was first here 2015 and came back 10 years later in Sept 2025. I’ve seen on TT mostly tourist complaining bout the price where you can go to a diff market to get cheaper fabric, a tourist trap, vendors are too pushy.
I can’t speak for them but I think this place is great! Definitely better choices than Tan Dinh Market. Yes the vendors are pushy but you as a tourist just take that as an experience. If you just wanna browse, just browse do not ask for price. If u think about it, it’s cheaper than wherever you’re from.
Be nice, when they kept asking what i want i just smile i say i want all because so beautiful, i cannot choose ahhhh. They laugh and all is good. I said thank you and they smile.

Ben Thant market is one of the popular spot in HCMC. There are many local stuff that you can find inside the market . Tourist and local likes to visit this market during weekend.

There are many food stall inside the market, if you wanted to have the local food you can get them all from here.

Many kind of local delicacies and also coffee can find inside the market , they will sell you the been and blend into the coffee powder for you.

Highly recommended to visit here if you are looking for some local food or local stuff and souvenir .

Great place to shop for all kinds of souvenirs, clothings, shoes to leather products. Don’t forget to bargain!!! Different types of nuts and dried fruits are also available. They pack as per the weight required.
For Grab taxi, make sure to be at the Main Entrance or they will drive off as other places are not pick up spots.
Bonus: the money exchange place is right opposite the Main Entrance.

Essential HCM local market to visit! Get your coffees grounded or beans, freeze dried fruits and enjoy authentic flavors from any of the stores here! Friendly stall owners readily served up 3 different coffee flavors for testing so that we can find our favorite ones. Ended up buying 4 packs of coffees ❤️
Nice souvenirs for good price. You must bargain "very" hard as in offer 1/3 of their asking price!

I really enjoyed this market
It has sooooooo many things
You can find local food, souvenirs, clothing, shoes, fabric, etc.
It’s really hot, no AC
Pro tip - Make sure you haggle!!!! That price is not the ACTUAL price
Take cash, most of them don’t have card machines
There are a ton of copies from famous brands that actually look legit
You can spend a good couple of hours here

Most sought after location for bargain products. Mostly fake branded goods but decent quality and can be bought for a fraction of the price. From produce to coffee from handbags to tee shirts it's all here

I would say this is the best market in HCMC. There's no air con but it has the largest selection of goods (fake designer goods, clothing, shoes, accessories, food, coffee, tea, fabric, etc.) and cheaper prices. You can negotiate the price and remember to ask for samples for any food items to taste before purchasing. I bought dried mango. I was offered samples and the package was sealed in front of me. Also, there's a HSBC ATM near the front entrance which was convenient.

Good prices if you know how to bargain.  Tip, start 75% off of what they tell you.  Don't budge on your price.  If they disagree, walk to the next booth.  Many booths have the same things.  The next booth, try 65% off.  Keep going and trying again and again, eventually you will find their bottom price and get the best possible deal.

A good bargaining market if you have some skill.
Clothes,  accessories, foods and lot of other stuff. Other than branded stores this is a good place to buy not expensive things.

This place is a 'must do' in the city. It's absolutely bonkers inside with constant cries of 'what you looking for lady', 'I have many colours', 'what size you want?'.
It can be very intimidating initially.  We went twice, once late afternoon, and once first thing. The traders were more willing to go lower on prices later in the day.
We were told to offer no more than 40% of what they initially asked for something and go from there.  You need to work out how much you're willing to pay and then be prepared to walk away if they say no. Sometimes they come after you to accept and sometimes not so then you know what is the right price! The same things are sold on lots of stalls, so you can buy the same thing at another stall with a better idea of price.
Be careful of pickpockets. It's narrow and mad, so be wary, but there are plenty of bargains on fake named products to be had, and haggling is all part of the fun!

Usual offerings albeit an easy paced (but still busy) market compared to others visited. This market contains a good variety of jewellery watches, clothing, haberdashery, and food and beverage options. The fixed price stalls located along the outer edges offer an even more relaxing experience from the usual haggling expectations.
Amazing food and shake stalls, you can buy clothes, ao dai and customize dresses and suits. It's just very hot so bring a fan

Good market for buying clothes, shoes,bags souvenirs and counterfeit products.

Avoid buying things from the front line shops when u enter.
Most of them are super rude and not polite and arrogant and super expensive, its like they are giving products for free.

Instead look for shops on the inner side.
Most of them are polite..friendly and very good.
They even offer good prices.

I did many purchase from shop no 642.
Very good people and they offer good price, its just you have to bargain a little.

This is a very busy place to visit and never stops, if you aren’t afraid to haggle you can get a good bargain otherwise you could end up paying more, so please be careful. Heaps of food options, places to shop. Something for everyone.
No better way to tap the pulse of a place - and the food stalls are a paradise.
This is full of local shops for cloths, souvenirs, shoes , bags and much more at cheap prices. However you should be able to bargain the prices to get a good offer.

We can find everything there, the vendors try to sell their products, they call up to you, but leave you if you say no, thank you. The food section is fantastic, if you want to taste local dishes just go there. Delicious and cheap. We had lunch and dinner there every day. It is open until 20.
Fabulous open air market, lots to see.

The Vietnam market experience! Awesome place to get a good deal. Don't buy from the 1st shop, walk around and get prices from other shops first.
Product quality is pretty consistent between each shop.
Buying more items means bigger % discounts.

Historic central market with a wide range of clothing, souvenirs, bags, coffee and tea, and various foods. Until early evening, there is a wide variety of Vietnamese cuisine in a wide variety of snack bars. If you want to shop a little cheaper, you can go to the surrounding streets away from the hustle and bustle.

One of the famous ones in the country. Filled with all the "prestige brands"
From clothes, shoes, hats, hand bags, backpacks through watches and much more. Prices are not set so plenty of bargaining is needed to get it all at the right price.

I kinda liked it against all odds.

Good options to shop for. Bargain is a must. Do not forget to drop the prices by atleast 60% of what they quote.
You can get good Vietnamese street food too.

Morning hours are best to bargain and find things at ease.
Nice place to buy good stuff and eat.
Huge variety of clothing, bags, sunglasses, watches, fabrics!!!! A little overwhelming! Had a great time mooching about. Great food court good selection of foods all super cheap.
Impressive market. Some areas have fixed price shops. We're not supposed to bargain in these sections.
Must visit indoor market for good bargains. If it is clothes, shoes, bags, suitcases, souvenirs, leather goods, cosmetics, watches, or food, you'll find it here!
Pretty intense however most vendors were very lovely, and welcoming! Got lots of great deals! Definitely need your bargaining skills
Not too crowded on a weekday. Can find lotus seed, coffee, durian cake, and cashew nuts and alot OEM product from big brands. Must know how to ask for discount and more discount

Do not miss this place and try do not buy something or getting lost!
Amazing shopping options

You can buy literally anything from this market. I would suggest that you come during the day though as they close early. Maybe before 6pm. The prices are doubled or tripled for tourists so make sure to haggle. Souvenir shirts that are 300000 dong are actually half the price. We bought everything half the price they initially quoted from various stores.

The durian is very good and you can eat it there. They will provide plastic gloves and tissue. You can get a better price if you buy a  items together. We bought freshly ground coffee and they gave us the cup for free.
There’s a wide variety of things, the food is inexpensive and tasty, and don’t forget to bargain.

Ben Thanh Market is a must to visit, if you are into bargaining.

They are many small shops selling all kinds of stuff ranging from food, snacks, clothings, shoes, bags, watches everything.

Marché Bến Thành (Chợ Bến Thành) : immense, vivant, et clairement incontournable si tu veux sentir l’ambiance de Hô Chi Minh-Ville. On y trouve pas mal de street food et énormément de stands de vêtements, sacs, souvenirs, etc.
Soyons honnêtes : il y a aussi beaucoup de contrefaçons. Je sais que ce n’est pas “idéal”, mais j’ai été franchement surprise par la qualité de certaines choses, et je me suis fait plaisir.
Petit conseil : les prix sont souvent annoncés très haut, donc n’hésitez pas à négocier, calmement et avec respect, ça fait partie du jeu.
Et si cet avis t’a été utile, un petit like me ferait vraiment plaisir (promis, je ne fais pas pleurer dans les chaumières 😄).

濱城市場是胡志明市最具代表性的城市象徵之一，當我從圓環穿越車潮走進市場正門時，眼前那座帶有鐘樓的建築，立刻讓人感受到西貢時期留下的歷史氣息。這裡不只是觀光客必訪的購物地，更像是一座濃縮城市日常的生活劇場。市場內部光線略帶昏黃，空氣中混合著香料、咖啡與熟食的氣味，人聲鼎沸卻又井然有序，每一條走道都充滿活力。

攤位販售的商品極具越南特色，從印有西貢字樣的T-shirt、手工編織包、漆器工藝，到各式咖啡豆與腰果乾貨，色彩與質感交織出濃厚的南洋風情。與攤商互動的過程特別有趣，他們熟練地用多國語言招呼客人，報價與議價之間形成一種旅行特有的節奏。這種帶點觀光化的商業模式，雖然價格不若在地市場親民，但卻讓人更容易接觸並理解越南如何在傳統與觀光經濟之間取得平衡。

最讓我難忘的是市場內的美食區。一碗熱騰騰的河粉、一杯滴漏咖啡配上煉乳，在小小的座位區裡，看著來自世界各地的旅人與在地人並肩用餐，那種沒有距離的氛圍，正是旅行最迷人的地方。與高級餐廳相比，這裡多了一份真實與生活感，也讓人更貼近胡志明市的日常節奏。

走出市場回頭望向鐘樓時，我感覺自己不只是逛了一個觀光景點，而是短暫參與了這座城市的呼吸。濱城市場就像胡志明市的縮影——歷史與現代交錯、在地與國際融合、喧鬧卻充滿人情味。對旅人而言，這裡買到的或許是紀念品，但真正帶走的，是一段充滿聲音、氣味與文化層次的深刻記憶。

Chợ Bến Thành là một trong những địa điểm sôi động và mang tính biểu tượng nhất ở Thành phố Hồ Chí Minh – nơi bạn có thể thực sự cảm nhận được nhịp đập của Việt Nam. Ngay từ khi bước vào, bạn sẽ được bao quanh bởi những gian hàng đầy màu sắc bán đủ mọi thứ, từ đồ thủ công mỹ nghệ địa phương và quần áo đến các loại gia vị thơm ngon và đồ ăn đường phố. Đây không chỉ là một khu chợ; mà còn là một trải nghiệm. Mặc cả là một phần thú vị ở đây, vì vậy đừng ngại ngần thương lượng với một nụ cười. Tôi đặc biệt khuyên bạn nên thử bánh mì tươi và cà phê đá Việt Nam – chúng đơn giản nhưng khó quên. Không khí nhộn nhịp nhưng đầy quyến rũ, với những người bán hàng thân thiện và âm thanh của cuộc sống xung quanh bạn. Ngay cả khi bạn không có ý định mua sắm, việc đi dạo qua các gian hàng cũng cho bạn cái nhìn chân thực về văn hóa địa phương. Nằm ngay trung tâm thành phố, chợ Bến Thành là điểm đến không thể bỏ qua đối với những du khách muốn nếm thử, nhìn thấy và cảm nhận tinh thần đích thực của Sài Gòn tại một nơi duy nhất.

Zentraler Markt der auf jeden Fall einen Besuch wert ist!
Essen, Kleidung,Kaffee und vieles mehr kann auf diesem Markt kaufen.
In manchen Bereichen kann man Handeln in andern geht das ehre weniger.
Es vieles zu entdecken und wer T-Shirts kaufen möchte wird hier auf jeden Fall fündig.

Chợ Bến Thành, không chỉ là chợ theo kiểu truyền thống mà đó còn là biểu tượng đặc trung của thành phố mang tên Bác.

Ngắm nhìn diện mạo mới, phía trước chợ Bến Thành (vòng xoay Quách Thị Trang xưa) nay đã được tô vẽ thêm những đường nét lượn sóng như biển cả, tạo cảm giác tươi mát, trẻ trung.

Chúc mừng cho đất nước đổi mới và phát triển thành công. Chúc cho bà con luôn mạnh khoẻ, làm ăn thuận lợi, nhà nhà no ấm dư giả, đất nước hưng thịnh vững mạnh!

I love Viet Nam 💖💖

Chợ Bến Thành luôn giữ gìn một không gian vô cùng sạch sẽ, tạo cảm giác dễ chịu cho mọi du khách ghé thăm. Với nhiều lối ra vào thuận tiện, việc di chuyển trong chợ trở nên vô cùng dễ dàng và không hề cảm thấy chật chội. Thật đáng quý khi nhiều tiểu thương tại đây có thể giao tiếp bằng ngoại ngữ, giúp du khách quốc tế dễ dàng tìm hiểu và mua sắm. Thái độ phục vụ niềm nở và lịch sự của họ cũng để lại ấn tượng tốt đẹp. Chợ Bến Thành thực sự là một điểm đến tuyệt vời để trải nghiệm văn hóa địa phương. 👌
Ben Thanh Market always keeps a very clean space, creating a pleasant feeling for all visitors to visit. With many convenient entrances, moving in the market becomes extremely easy and does not feel cramped. It is valuable that many small merchants here can communicate in foreign languages, making it easy for international visitors to learn and shop. Their welcoming and polite service attitude also leaves a good impression. Ben Thanh Market is truly a great destination to experience the local culture. ❤️

베트남 여행중에 축구를 좋아하는 남편과 아이와 함께 방문했어요.
벤탄시장은 기념품도 많지만, 축구 유니폼을 사기에도 괜찮은 곳이더라고요 😊

정품은 아니지만, 편하게 입을 유니폼을 찾는다면 충분히 만족할 만한 퀄리티예요.
다만, 가게마다 가격과 재질 차이가 꽤 크기 때문에 여러 군데 둘러보고 비교해보는 걸 추천합니다.

저희는 최종적으로 132번 가게에서 구매했어요.
✔️ 아스널 써드킷 유니폼 재질이 정품과 꽤 비슷했고
✔️ 사장님이 친절하고 유쾌해서 기분 좋게 쇼핑할 수 있었어요.

아스널 써드킷 포함해서 축구 유니폼 구매하실 분들은
벤탄시장 132번 가게 한 번 들러보셔도 좋을 것 같아요 👍

내돈내산 후기입니다.

観光客で活気がある市場。歩いていると日本人と察知して、日本語で話しかけてきます。たまに間違えられますが、大した正解率です。
シャツ、コーヒー、雑貨など所狭しと店があります。ふっかけた値段を言ってきますが、交渉すればかなり安くなります。
飲食コーナーのマンゴースムージーは25Kでとてもおいしいです。

Không vào chợ mua đồ nhưng ghé khu ẩm thực thấy mức giá cũng chấp nhận được, không quá cao vô lý. Khẩu phần ăn cũng khá nhiều, vị dễ ăn, các cô mời nhiệt tình. Chợ bán đa dạng các món, ghé ăn ngẫu nhiên chứ không biết hàng nào ngon nổi tiếng. Nếu chơi trung tâm chưa biết nên ăn gì thì vào khu ăn uống chợ Bến Thành cũng được vì có nhiều sự lựa chọn. Thấy bán nhiều nhất là các loại chè, bánh bèo, bún bò, các món nước. Đi từ bên cửa Phan Chu Trinh vào đường rộng hơn. Đông khách du lịch nên thỉnh thoảng phải ngồi né né, ăn nhanh.

Username: Terry OtsuSang (丘ダイバーチャネル)
Comment:
ホーチミン市の中心部にあり、歴史的で象徴的なランドマークで、活気があり、アジアのエネルギッシュな混沌を感じられます。

地元の人々（主に早朝の生鮮食品エリア）だけでなく、外国人観光客が非常に多いです。
観光客向けであるため、提示価格は高いので、
頑張って値切って下さい。ディスカウントの腕を磨きに行くには最高の市場です

我々は飯だけ食いましたが、飲食は、売ってる商品ほどではなく、ちょっと高いかな？
レベルなので、市場で食べたという観光気分で充分楽しめます。
我々顧客を取り合いするのでそれも楽しめる人には最高です。

とりあえず、なんでも売ってます。

chợ bến Thành ngày 20 tết khá đông người qua lại tới đây chụp ảnh trước chợ được doanh nghiệp khang Điền cho Sơn 1 dải sơn hình khá lạ mắt :)) tuy nhiên vẫn không làm mất đi vẻ đẹp của chợ bến Thành chỉ là hơi lạ so với mọi năm lượng người qua đây chụp ảnh trước tết khá đông mặc dù trời rất nắng mình cũng tới đây chụp ảnh mà thấy đông và nắng quá chụp mấy kiểu thôi đi về chúc mọi người có 1 trải nghiệm tốt khi tới đây xuân 2026🥰

Eine riesige Halle mit Souvenirs, T-shirts, Schmuck, Kleidung, Koffern, Restaurants und vielem mehr. Die Preise der Händler sind nicht selten 400-600% zu hoch angesetzt. Wer sofort kauft hat einfach zu viel Geld. Hier gehört handeln zum guten Ton. Nicht enttäuscht gehen, einfach den Wunschpreis nennen und abwarten.
Das Essen ist authentisch und sehr, sehr gut. Der Preis ist vergleichbar mit den der Strassenhändler. Das beste Essen hatten wir bei Kim Luong. Ganz nette Damen. Haben einige ausprobiert aber sind Immer wieder zu Dí Kim zurūck.

Non si trova nel mercato, ma giusto davanti all hotel sun an hotel 1, vicino ce anche un 7 Eleven. Il miglior pesce grigliato e io abbia mangiato in Vietnam. Non fatevi intimorire, la qualità e la freschezza è incredibile! Il signore che griglia sa bene cosa fa!

Ben Thanh Market is a historic landmark located in the center of Ho Chi Minh City. Its origins date back to the 17th century when vendors gathered near the Saigon River to trade, and the current building was constructed by the French colonial government in 1912 and officially opened in 1914. The market has witnessed the transformation of the city from colonial Saigon through the period of the Vietnam War to modern Ho Chi Minh City. Recognizable by its iconic southern clock tower, the market contains more than 1,500 stalls selling fresh produce, seafood, spices, handicrafts, textiles, souvenirs, traditional Vietnamese clothing, and a variety of local street food. Busy during the day and lively at night with surrounding night markets, it reflects local daily life, vibrant bargaining culture, and serves as a cultural and culinary hub for both residents and visitors.

濱城市場是位於胡志明市市中心的重要歷史地標，歷史可追溯至17世紀，當時商販聚集於西貢河畔進行交易；現今建築由法國殖民政府於1912年興建，並於1914年正式啟用，見證了城市從法屬西貢、經歷越戰到現代胡志明市的變遷。市場以南門鐘樓為象徵，內有超過1,500個攤位，販售新鮮蔬果、海鮮、香料、手工藝品、紡織品、紀念品、越南傳統服飾與各式在地小吃；白天熱鬧繁忙，夜晚周邊形成夜市，展現當地居民的生活樣貌與熱情的議價文化，是結合購物、美食與人文體驗的重要場所。

Bến Thành Market è uno dei simboli di Ho Chi Minh City e una tappa quasi obbligatoria per chi visita la città. Il mercato è grande, vivace e ricco di bancarelle che vendono di tutto: souvenir, abbigliamento, spezie, caffè vietnamita e street food locale. È un posto interessante per respirare l’atmosfera tipica dei mercati asiatici e fare qualche acquisto, ma bisogna essere pronti a contrattare perché i prezzi iniziali sono spesso più alti per i turisti. Può essere affollato e caldo, soprattutto nelle ore centrali della giornata. Nel complesso una visita consigliata più per l’esperienza e l’ambiente che per lo shopping in sé.

Một biểu tượng thương mại và văn hóa lâu đời của Sài Gòn. Ồn ào sau đợt trùng từ 2025 nhưng đến Tết Bính Ngọ ghé tham quan chụp hình cũng đẹp.

Chợ Bến Thành là một trong những biểu tượng lâu đời của Thành phố Hồ Chí Minh, thu hút đông đảo du khách trong và ngoài nước. Nằm ở vị trí trung tâm, khu chợ gây ấn tượng bởi kiến trúc cổ kính và không khí buôn bán nhộn nhịp suốt cả ngày. Tại đây, du khách có thể tìm thấy đa dạng mặt hàng, từ quần áo, vải vóc, đồ lưu niệm cho đến ẩm thực đường phố đặc trưng của Sài Gòn.
Ưu điểm lớn của Chợ Bến Thành là sự phong phú về món ăn, như bún riêu, phở, bánh xèo hay chè truyền thống, phù hợp để trải nghiệm văn hóa ẩm thực. Tuy nhiên, giá cả ở chợ thường cao hơn so với nơi khác, và người mua cần biết trả giá để có mức hợp lý. Ngoài ra, chợ khá đông đúc, đặc biệt vào giờ cao điểm, có thể gây bất tiện cho việc tham quan.
Nhìn chung, Chợ Bến Thành không chỉ là nơi mua sắm mà còn là điểm đến văn hóa tiêu biểu, phản ánh rõ nét nhịp sống và bản sắc của thành phố.

là khu chợ nổi tiếng và lâu đời nhất ở saigon.  ban đầu là chợ bến sông gần thành gia định (khu vực chợ lớn – rạch bến nghé xưa), lập vào TK17, từ cột gỗ, vách tre, mái lá, phục vụ giao thương đường thuỷ, buôn bán hàng nông thuỷ sản.
vào giữa TK19 chợ dời về gần thành gia định, xây bán kiên cố bằng cột gỗ, mái ngói âm dương, tường gạch thấp. phong cách kiến trúc việt - hoa.
vào 1860-1911 chợ dời đến khu quai de charner (đại lộ nguyễn huệ) xây theo phong cách kiến trúc pháp, bằng khung gạch đá, mái ngói lớn, sàn lát đá.
tuy nhiên xây trên nền đất yếu, không đáp ứng được quy mô mở rộng, nên đây là lý do chợ được di dời sang vị trí hiện nay.
chợ bến thành hiện nay xây (1912-1914) rộng 13.000 m² theo kiến trúc pháp nhiệt đới hoá, bố cục châu âu & giải pháp khí hậu miền nam. với điểm nhấn là tháp đồng hồ 4 mặt ở phía nam, 4 cổng chính ứng 4 trục đường.
công trình chợ bến thành không phô trương như nhà thờ hay dinh thự nhưng đặt trật tự, thời gian, quy hoạch vào đời sống thường nhật. kiến trúc “dạy” người dân lúc bấy giờ: đi chợ đúng giờ, sinh hoạt theo nhịp đô thị.
sau này chợ được cải tạo, bảo tồn, nâng cấp, gia cố kết cấu, thay mái ngói, hệ thống thoát nước, bổ sung hệ thống chiếu sáng, thông gió, phòng cháy chữa cháy. và không thay đổi kết cấu chính, giữ nguyên dáng dấp pháp.
chợ bến thành là:
trung tâm giao thương truyền thống của sài gòn hơn 100 năm;
gắn liền với lịch sử phát triển đô thị;
là “bảo tàng sống” về văn hóa chợ;
là điểm tham quan thú vị.

Chợ Bến Thành không chỉ là một khu chợ, mà là một lát cắt sống động của Sài Gòn – nơi thời gian dường như chậm lại để nhường chỗ cho ký ức, cảm xúc và nhịp sống rất riêng của thành phố này. Giữa trung tâm Quận 1 ồn ào, chợ Bến Thành vẫn đứng đó, trầm mặc và quen thuộc, như một người chứng nhân lặng lẽ của bao đổi thay.

Từ xa, tháp đồng hồ với bốn mặt quay về bốn hướng đã trở thành hình ảnh in sâu trong tâm trí nhiều thế hệ. Nó không phô trương, không hào nhoáng, nhưng đủ để bất cứ ai đi ngang cũng phải ngoái nhìn. Bước chân vào khu chợ, cảm giác đầu tiên không phải là mua bán, mà là một sự va chạm rất thật với đời sống: tiếng mời chào xen lẫn tiếng cười nói, mùi hương thức ăn hòa quyện cùng mùi vải mới, mùi trái cây chín và cả mùi thời gian cũ kỹ.

Chợ Bến Thành giống như một cuốn sách mở, mỗi gian hàng là một trang viết. Ở đó có những cô chú tiểu thương gắn bó với quầy hàng cả chục năm, thuộc tên từng món, nhớ mặt từng khách quen. Ánh mắt họ không chỉ bán hàng, mà còn kể chuyện – chuyện về Sài Gòn xưa, về những ngày buôn bán vất vả, về sự đổi thay của phố xá và con người. Dù khách là người địa phương hay du khách phương xa, ai bước vào đây cũng cảm nhận được một thứ năng lượng rất thật, rất đời.

Khu ẩm thực của chợ là nơi đánh thức mọi giác quan. Một tô bún bò nóng hổi, một dĩa bánh xèo vàng ruộm, hay ly chè mát lạnh đều mang theo hương vị thân quen của miền Nam. Có thể giá không rẻ như quán vỉa hè, nhưng bù lại là cảm giác được ngồi giữa lòng chợ, nhìn dòng người qua lại, để món ăn trở thành một phần của trải nghiệm chứ không chỉ là chuyện no bụng.

Chợ Bến Thành cũng có những điều khiến người ta vừa yêu vừa… phân vân. Sự đông đúc, cái nóng oi bức vào buổi trưa, hay việc phải trả giá khi mua sắm đôi lúc khiến trải nghiệm không trọn vẹn. Nhưng chính những điều ấy lại làm nên tính cách của chợ – thô ráp, chân thật và không hề được “đánh bóng”.

Rời chợ, người ta mang theo không chỉ là túi quà lưu niệm hay vài món ăn vặt, mà là một cảm giác rất Sài Gòn. Chợ Bến Thành không hoàn hảo, nhưng chính sự không hoàn hảo ấy lại khiến nơi này trở nên đáng nhớ. Đó là nơi để quay về, để hiểu hơn về thành phố này – một Sài Gòn vừa cũ vừa mới, vừa ồn ào mà cũng rất đỗi thân thương.

Ben Thanh Market, Ho Chi Minh City’nin en canlı ve enerjik noktalarından biri. İçeride hem yerel lezzetleri tadabilir hem de hediyelik eşyalardan tekstile kadar pek çok şeyi bulabilirsiniz. Pazarlık kültürü burada oldukça yaygın, o yüzden fiyatlar konusunda konuşmak gerekiyor. Yerel yaşamı hissetmek ve şehir atmosferine karışmak için mutlaka uğranması gereken bir yer olduğunu düşünüyorum.

Terletak di tengah-tengah Ho Chi Minh City (Saigon), Ben Thanh Market ialah mercu tanda paling ikonik yang wajib dikunjungi. Pasar ini sudah berusia lebih 100 tahun dan menjadi simbol kehidupan harian warga tempatan — penuh warna, bunyi dan aroma yang menggoda. Dari pakaian, kraftangan, cenderamata hingga rempah ratus dan kopi Vietnam, semuanya ada di bawah satu bumbung! ☕🧺

Salah satu pengalaman terbaik di sini ialah tawar-menawar dengan peniaga tempatan — satu budaya yang menyeronokkan! Di luar pasar pula, suasana menjadi lebih meriah menjelang petang apabila Ben Thanh Night Market dibuka, menawarkan makanan jalanan, pakaian dan aksesori dengan harga berpatutan. 🌃🍜

Jangan lepaskan peluang menikmati kopi ais Vietnam sambil menyaksikan hiruk-pikuk kota Saigon dari kafe berhampiran. Ben Thanh bukan sekadar tempat membeli-belah — ia adalah denyut nadi Saigon yang memperkenalkan anda kepada jiwa sebenar Vietnam. ❤️🇻🇳

Chợ Bến Thành không chỉ là một ngôi chợ đơn thuần mà còn là "linh hồn" của TP.HCM. Ngay khi bước chân vào cổng chính với tháp đồng hồ biểu tượng, bạn sẽ cảm nhận được ngay không khí náo nhiệt, đa sắc màu đặc trưng của một thương cảng lâu đời.
Điểm cộng lớn nhất chính là khu ẩm thực. Tại đây, bạn có thể tìm thấy mọi tinh túy của món ăn Việt từ bún riêu, gỏi cuốn đến các loại chè Nam Bộ ngọt mát. Hương vị rất đậm đà, chuẩn vị và phục vụ cực kỳ nhanh chóng. Bên cạnh đó, các gian hàng thủ công mỹ nghệ, đồ mây tre đan và lụa tơ tằm ở đây rất đẹp, phù hợp để mua làm quà phương xa.
Một vài lưu ý nhỏ: Vì là địa điểm du lịch trọng điểm nên giá cả các mặt hàng lưu niệm thường được đẩy lên khá cao. Kinh nghiệm cho bạn là hãy cứ mạnh dạn trả giá từ 1/2 đến 1/3 mức giá ban đầu. Ngoài ra, chợ khá đông đúc và hơi nóng vào giờ trưa, nên hãy chú ý bảo quản tư trang cá nhân thật cẩn thận.
Dù có một chút ồn ào và thách thức về giá cả, nhưng nếu muốn cảm nhận nhịp sống sôi động và nét văn hóa giao thương của Sài Thành, Bến Thành vẫn là điểm đến số một!

Chợ Bến Thành là một trong những biểu tượng nổi tiếng nhất của TP. Hồ Chí Minh và luôn nằm trong danh sách những điểm đến không thể bỏ qua đối với du khách. Ngay từ khi đặt chân đến, bạn sẽ ấn tượng với kiến trúc cổ điển của ngôi chợ hơn trăm năm tuổi cùng không khí nhộn nhịp, sôi động suốt cả ngày.

Bên trong chợ, không gian được chia thành nhiều khu vực rõ ràng: thực phẩm khô, quần áo, đồ lưu niệm, đặc sản và khu ăn uống. Sự đa dạng hàng hóa là điểm cộng lớn, từ những món quà lưu niệm nhỏ xinh như móc khóa, nón lá, cho đến các sản phẩm thủ công mỹ nghệ tinh xảo, tất cả đều mang đậm nét văn hóa Việt. Khu ẩm thực của chợ cũng rất hấp dẫn với các món ăn đặc trưng như bún thịt nướng, bánh xèo, chè, và nhất là món nước mía siêu mát – rất được du khách yêu thích.

Tuy nhiên, giá cả tại chợ thường khá cao so với mặt bằng chung, đặc biệt là với du khách nước ngoài. Vì vậy, bạn nên hỏi giá trước và mạnh dạn trả giá để có được mức hợp lý. Dù vậy, sự nhiệt tình và thân thiện của nhiều tiểu thương vẫn khiến trải nghiệm mua sắm trở nên dễ chịu và đáng nhớ.

Nhìn chung, chợ Bến Thành không chỉ là nơi mua sắm mà còn là điểm giao thoa văn hóa, nơi bạn có thể cảm nhận trọn vẹn nhịp sống đặc trưng của Sài Gòn. Đây chắc chắn là địa điểm rất đáng để ghé thăm.

Với lịch sử hơn 100 năm (xây dựng chính thức từ năm 1912–1914), ngôi chợ nằm ngay trung tâm Quận 1, được biết đến qua hình ảnh tháp đồng hồ đặc trưng ở cửa nam – biểu tượng không chính thức của Sài Gòn.
Nơi đây thu hút hàng triệu du khách trong và ngoài nước nhờ không khí mua bán sôi động, nhộn nhịp, đa dạng hàng hóa từ quần áo, thủ công mỹ nghệ, quà lưu niệm đến ẩm thực đường phố hấp dẫn. Chợ Bến Thành không chỉ là thiên đường mua sắm mà còn là nơi thể hiện rõ nét văn hóa giao thương đặc trưng của người Sài Gòn, trở thành điểm đến không thể bỏ qua khi khám phá thành phố. Mình góp 1 bức ảnh không có gì đặc sắc nhưng làm cho đánh giá trên Google maps có thêm màu sắc.❤️
雑貨店などの市場ですが、飲食店もたくさん入っておりグルメスポットとしても楽しめます。手作り感溢れる超ローカルフードは種類も豊富でここでなんでも揃うでしょう

Mình đi cùng hai người bạn – một người nước ngoài và một người Việt mới vào Nam, cả ba đều hào hứng khám phá. Bên trong chợ có rất nhiều gian hàng với đủ loại đặc sản, quà lưu niệm và đồ ăn hấp dẫn. Nếu ai lần đầu đến Sài Gòn, mình nghĩ chợ Bến Thành là điểm đến không thể bỏ qua!

Địa chỉ: đường Lê Lợi, phường Bến Thành, Quận 1, Hồ Chí Minh. (Cổng chính đối diện công trường Quách Thị Trang, hiện nay là nhà ga tàu ngầm Bến Thành).
Chợ Bến Thành tọa lạc ngay trung tâm Quận 1, nổi tiếng với bề dày lịch sử hơn 100 năm. Chợ cũng là một trong những biểu tượng lâu đời bậc nhất của Sài Thành. Đây không chỉ là nơi giao thương sầm uất mà còn lưu giữ tất nhiều nét văn hóa Sài Gòn xưa.

“Chợ Bến Thành là một trong những biểu tượng văn hoá – lịch sử lâu đời của TP. Hồ Chí Minh, nằm ngay trung tâm quận 1, rất thuận tiện để tham quan. Kiến trúc chợ mang nét cổ kính đặc trưng với tháp đồng hồ nổi bật, đã trở thành điểm nhận diện quen thuộc của Sài Gòn. Bên trong chợ có hàng trăm gian hàng buôn bán đa dạng: từ quần áo, giày dép, vải vóc, thủ công mỹ nghệ cho đến đồ lưu niệm, đặc sản và ẩm thực truyền thống.

Điểm thu hút nhất với du khách là khu ẩm thực, nơi có nhiều món ăn đặc trưng Nam Bộ như bún, phở, chè, bánh xèo… ngon miệng và mang đậm hương vị địa phương. Đây cũng là nơi lý tưởng để tìm mua quà lưu niệm mang dấu ấn Việt Nam.

Không gian chợ khá đông đúc, đặc biệt vào giờ cao điểm, nên bạn cần lưu ý giữ gìn tư trang cá nhân. Giá cả có thể cao hơn so với bên ngoài, nhưng nếu khéo mặc cả sẽ mua được nhiều món ưng ý. Chợ Bến Thành vừa là điểm mua sắm vừa là nơi trải nghiệm văn hoá Sài Gòn, chắc chắn là điểm dừng chân thú vị cho cả du khách trong và ngoài nước.”

Đánh giá: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (5 sao)
Chợ Bến Thành là một địa điểm không thể bỏ qua khi đến Sài Gòn. Với không gian truyền thống đặc trưng, nơi đây không chỉ là một khu chợ nổi tiếng mà còn là biểu tượng văn hóa của thành phố.
Vị trí: Chợ nằm ngay trung tâm quận 1, rất dễ dàng tìm thấy. Với vị trí thuận tiện, bạn có thể ghé thăm sau khi khám phá các điểm tham quan gần đó như Dinh Độc Lập, Nhà thờ Đức Bà hay Bảo tàng Lịch sử Việt Nam.
Không khí: Không gian trong chợ luôn sôi động với sự giao thương nhộn nhịp. Bạn có thể cảm nhận rõ nét nhịp sống của người dân địa phương qua những tiếng rao hàng, những món đồ thủ công tinh xảo và các món ăn đặc trưng của Sài Gòn.
Mặt hàng: Chợ Bến Thành là nơi bày bán rất nhiều mặt hàng từ đồ thủ công mỹ nghệ, quần áo, đồ lưu niệm cho đến các loại gia vị đặc sản. Những sản phẩm ở đây rất đa dạng, phù hợp với mọi đối tượng khách du lịch.
Ẩm thực: Một điểm cộng lớn là các quầy hàng ẩm thực trong chợ. Bạn có thể thưởng thức các món ăn đặc trưng như phở, bánh mì, gỏi cuốn, hủ tiếu và nhiều món ăn khác ngay tại đây.
Dịch vụ: Nhân viên bán hàng tại Chợ Bến Thành thân thiện và nhiệt tình. Mặc dù có thể hơi ồn ào nhưng chợ luôn có những đặc điểm riêng khiến bạn không thể nào quên.
Lời khuyên: Nếu bạn đi vào giờ cao điểm, chợ có thể hơi đông đúc, nhưng đừng bỏ lỡ cơ hội để tận hưởng không khí sôi động và mua những món quà lưu niệm độc đáo. Hãy mặc cả để có thể sở hữu những món hàng ưng ý với giá tốt nhất!

濱城市場是胡志明市最具代表性的地標之一，幾乎是每位旅客必訪的景點。市場內部規模龐大，攤位分區明顯，從越南傳統美食、咖啡、乾貨，到紀念品、手工藝品、服飾與家用品一應俱全，逛起來非常有趣。這裡能體驗到濃厚的在地氛圍與越南人的生活日常。值得一提的是，市場內的越南小吃攤非常道地，尤其是春捲、河粉和法國麵包都相當受歡迎。不過，因為遊客眾多，價格普遍偏高，建議多比較並嘗試議價，通常能獲得更合理的價格。整體而言，濱城市場不僅適合購物，也是一個能感受當地文化與熱鬧氛圍的好地方。

本物と言われますがたぶん刺繍も手縫いでなく機械ですし絹も嘘ですし基本本物じゃないと承知していれば問題なし。骨董品の偽物は日本には持ち出せないのでご注意。
また最初は値段結構ふっかけられますので、あまり興味津々剥き出しではなくフラッと見るのがポイント。去ろうとすれば安くしてくれます。ある程度許容範囲まで下げたら無理に底値まで頑張らず買っていいと思います。ツアーなどで連れて行かれるような民芸品やお土産店、パッと寄れるような高速SAと比べると多少値段を下げられていれば圧倒的に安く買えます。現地っぽいお土産ならここで買って損はないです。スーパーで買えるようなものはスーパーが安心だし値切りしなくて済むのでスーパーで売ってなさそうなお土産系とか民芸品系は買っちゃってOKです。

Chợ Bến Thành

1 địa danh hay còn gọi cách khác là một địa điểm mang dấu ấn theo dòng lịch sử.

Hồi xưa có 1 cái vòng xoay rất lớn mang nhiều kĩ niệm tại đây, nhưng năm thámg đổi mới đã nhường lại cho công trình Metro.

Nếu dừng tại trạm Chợ Bến Thành, cả nhà có thể ghé thăm ngôi chợ lâu đời này.

Khá nhiều món ăn, đồ dùng và vật phẩm lưu niệm mà bạn có thể ghé thăm.

Bên cạnh đó hàng quán ăn uống và cà phê khá nhiều là 1 lựa chọn thú vị cho người dân bản địa và khách nước ngoài.
