Chợ Bến Thành tọa lạc ở vị trí đắc địa, ngay khu vực trung tâm của TP. Hồ Chí Minh. Đây là một trong những khu chợ nổi tiếng Sài Gòn, nằm giữa các đường Phan Chu Trinh, Phan Bội Châu, Lê Thánh Tôn và Công trường Quách Thị Trang. Phía trước chợ là ga Bến Thành của tuyến Metro 1.

Trải qua nhiều thăng trầm lịch sử, chợ từng hai lần di dời, thay đổi địa điểm cũng như nhiều lần xây cất, sửa chữa do sập, cháy. Cho đến thời điểm hiện tại, chợ được xây dựng khang trang, rộng lớn ngay tại trung tâm Sài Gòn và là biểu tượng, địa điểm du lịch hút khách của thành phố mang tên Bác.

Chẳng phải ngẫu nhiên mà chợ Bến Thành lại được mệnh danh là thiên đường mua sắm của người dân Sài Gòn. Đến đây bạn có thể tìm thấy đủ mọi mặt hàng từ bình dân cho đến cao cấp. Trong đó, có nhiều gian hàng thu hút nhiều du khách quốc tế ghé đến để mua về làm quà lưu niệm như nón lá truyền thống, đồ gốm sứ, giỏ đan, tranh tre, đồ đồng.

Là khu chợ bán lẻ có quy mô lớn nhất của thành phố mang tên Bác, đến đây bạn sẽ bị choáng ngợp bởi sự đa dạng, phong phú của các mặt hàng. Từ đồ thời trang, mỹ nghệ, thủ công cho đến gia dụng, điện tử. Ghé chợ du khách còn có thể tìm thấy các loại vải và phụ liệu may mặc để tự tay thiết kế trang phục cho mình.

Ngoài ra, chợ Bến Thành còn là thiên đường đồ khô với đủ các loại mứt, bánh, hải sản khô. Cùng với đó là hàng trăm loại trái cây, nông sản ở miền Tây, miền Nam và một số vùng miền khác. Chẳng cần đi hết Việt Nam, đến khu chợ này bạn vẫn có thể được thưởng thức đặc sản từ khắp mọi nơi trên cả nước. Nếu bạn còn chưa biết mua đặc sản Sài Gòn làm quà ở đâu thì chợ Bến Thành là sự lựa chọn không thể lý tưởng hơn.

Đến chợ Bến Thành mà không trải nghiệm không khí sôi động, náo nhiệt của chợ về đêm thì quả là thiếu sót lớn. Chợ đêm bắt đầu hoạt động từ lúc 19 giờ và kéo dài đến 22 giờ để đáp ứng nhu cầu ăn uống, mua sắm của khách hàng.

Ngoài những quầy hàng thủ đông, thời trang, chợ đêm Bến Thành còn nhộn nhịp, “rôm rả” hơn bởi những quán đồ nướng, hải sản, quán bia, ăn vặt. Cứ mỗi tối khi thành phố lên đèn, người dân địa phương và khách du lịch lại quy tụ về đây để thưởng thức những món ăn hấp dẫn và mua sắm cho mình những mặt hàng yêu thích.
充滿這各國遊客的市場集散地，吃的用的穿的以及各種紀念品都可以在這裡買到。
Chợ rất nhiều mặt hàng từ quần áo, ăn uống tới đồ lưu niệm. Gửi xe 20k bên trái chợ.

Very big market, worthy for a visited 👍
摘錄ChatGPT:
濱城市場（Bến Thành Market）是胡志明市第一郡市中心的地標性室內市場，最初世代交易於濱金河（Bến Nghé River）畔，並於 1912–1914 年時由法國人重建，從此成為地標性建築，以顯眼的鐘樓聞名  ￼。

📍 基本資料
•   地點：第一郡 Bến Thành 區，坐落於 Lê Lợi、Phan Bội Châu、Phan Châu Trinh、Lê Thánh Tôn 四街交界  ￼。
•   營業時間：
•   白天市集：06:00–18:00
•   夜市：18:00–22:00  ￼。
•   規模與規格：
•   攤位約 1,500 個，小商家數約 6,000 家  ￼。
•   日均遊客逾 10,000 人  ￼。
•   建築面積約 13,056 平方公尺  ￼。

特色介紹

1. 古蹟建築＋四大入口

市場以中法合璧的建築風格為特色，明顯的鐘樓是拍照打卡熱門背景 。
東西南北四大門分別對應不同商品區域：南門主入口，東門化妝品，西門鞋品與手工藝品，北門果蔬攤  ￼。

2. 購物天堂與議價樂趣

販售商品包括越南傳統服飾（如奧黛）、刺繡、漆器、絲綢、飾品、電子產品、乾貨、日用品與紀念品  ￼。
市場盛行議價文化，觀光客可透過討價還價獲得較好價格 。

3. 美食集中地

白天晚上都有小吃攤，提供越南經典美食：米粉、春捲、螺肉河粉、燒烤海鮮、炒麵等  ￼。
周邊也有多家受推薦的店家，例如 Michelin 推薦的 Phở Việt Nam，專賣越式豬腰粿條、火腿糯米飯等  ￼。

4. 交通便利樞紐

市場外是公交總站，現代化地鐵樞紐「濱成站」（Bến Thành Station）已於 2024 年12 月啟用，串連一號線、二號線、四號線  ￼。

📝 旅遊建議

時段 推薦活動
早上6–9點 可以避開人潮，捕捉晨間市場活力，品嚐米線、河粉
下午 適合購物與討價還價，血拼奧黛、絲綢、紀念品
傍晚至夜市 品嚐夜晚特色小吃與海鮮，感受熱鬧氛圍

🎯 小貼士：
•   建議攜帶現金（越南盾），部份攤位不找零；
•   防範扒手，在人潮區注意包包；
•   避免貪小便宜購電子產品，貨品質量可能參差。

結語

濱城市場不僅是購物與美食的集中地，更是一處融合百年歷史、當地文化、建築藝術與都市交通交織的城中熱點。不論是來探索越南風情，還是挖掘美食與手作紀念品，都值得列入胡志明市旅遊行程。

Tại đây vào mỗi buổi tối rất nhộn nhịp đông vui và bạn sẽ thấy một TP Hồ Chí Minh hiện đại với người xe, cùng những tòa nhà cao tầng lung lính sáng trưng. Hòa nhịp trong không khí hân hoan đó rất nhiều bạn trẻ tận dụng những góc đường đẹp để chụp ảnh, quay video clip, tấp nập bán buôn 02 bên cổng chợ cũng tấp nập vui tươi không kém. 🏛️ Giới thiệu chung về Chợ Bến Thành

Chợ Bến Thành không chỉ là một khu chợ truyền thống mà còn là biểu tượng kiến trúc và văn hóa hơn 100 năm tuổi của Sài Gòn . Chợ tọa lạc tại trung tâm Quận 1, nơi giao nhau của bốn con phố sầm uất: Lê Lợi, Lê Thánh Tôn, Phan Bội Châu và Phan Châu Trinh . Kiến trúc Pháp thuộc địa với tháp đồng hồ đặc trưng là điểm nhấn không thể lẫn vào đâu được .

· Lịch sử: Chợ có lịch sử hình thành từ đầu thế kỷ 17, và tòa nhà chính như ngày nay được xây dựng từ năm 1912 .
· Giờ hoạt động:
· Chợ ban ngày: Mở cửa từ khoảng 6:00 sáng đến 6:00 tối .
· Chợ đêm: Bắt đầu từ khoảng 7:00 tối đến 10:00 tối hoặc nửa đêm, tập trung ở các con phố Phan Bội Châu và Phan Châu Trinh .

🌟 Trải nghiệm không thể bỏ qua

🍜 Ẩm thực đa dạng

Khu ẩm thực trong chợ và chợ đêm là thiên đường cho các tín đồ ăn uống. Bạn có thể thưởng thức vô số món ngon từ khắp mọi miền .

Món Ăn Đặc Sắc Ghi Chú
Bún riêu cua Nước dùng ngọt thanh từ cua, ăn kèm rau sống
Bún mắm Hương vị đậm đà đặc trưng miền Tây
Cơm tấm Hạt gạo tấm vỡ, kèm sườn nướng hoặc các mặn khác
Hải sản nướng Đa dạng loại, được nướng tươi ngay tại chợ đêm
Chè & Sinh tố Giải khát với nhiều loại chè Việt và sinh tố trái cây tươi

🛍️ Mua sắm quà lưu niệm

Chợ có gần 1,500 sạp hàng với hơn 6,000 tiểu thương, bạn có thể tìm thấy đủ mọi mặt hàng . Các khu vực bán hàng được phân bổ khá rõ ràng theo các cổng:

· Cổng Nam (Lê Lợi): Quần áo, vải vóc, giày dép và mỹ phẩm .
· Cổng Bắc (Lê Thánh Tôn): Trái cây tươi và hoa .
· Cổng Đông (Phan Bội Châu): Bánh kẹo, mỹ phẩm, đặc sản sấy khô .
· Cổng Tây (Phan Châu Trinh): Đồ thủ công mỹ nghệ, quà lưu niệm, đồ da .

Mẹo mua sắm: Đừng ngại mặc cả! Đây là một nét văn hóa tại các khu chợ truyền thống Việt Nam. Bạn nên so sánh giá ở vài sạp và giữ thái độ vui vẻ, lịch sự khi thương lượng .

📸 Sống ảo tại các góc check-in "sống ảo"

· Cổng chính với tháp đồng hồ: Đây là góc chụp kinh điển, biểu tượng của chợ mà bất kỳ ai cũng muốn có trong bộ sưu tập .
· Khu chợ đêm: Vào buổi tối, khu vực chợ đêm ngập tràn ánh đền sáng rực, tạo nên một khung cảnh nhộn nhịp, hoàn hảo cho những bức ảnh đường phố sống động .
· Các sạp hàng đầy màu sắc bên trong: Từ những giỏ trái cây tươi ngon ở cổng Bắc đến các sạp bánh kẹo, đồ thủ công rực rỡ, mỗi góc nhỏ đều có thể trở thành phông nền tuyệt vời.

💡 Lời khuyên từ kinh nghiệm

· Thời gian lý tưởng: Buổi sáng sớm nếu bạn muốn trải nghiệm không khí mua bán nhộn nhịp của chợ truyền thống. Buổi tối là thời điểm hoàn hảo để thưởng thức ẩm thực đường phố tại chợ đêm .
· Di chuyển: Khu vực quanh chợ rất đông đúc, khó đỗ xe. Bạn nên sử dụng taxi, xe ôm công nghệ (Grab) hoặc xe buýt để thuận tiện nhất .
· An toàn: Do rất đông khách du lịch, hãy luôn để ý tư trang, ví và điện thoại của mình để tránh trường hợp mất cắp .

Trong tương lai gần, quảng trường phía trước chợ sẽ được quy hoạch lại theo phong cách hiện đại, lấy cảm hứng từ Quảng trường Thời Đại (Times Square) ở New York, hứa hẹn mang đến một diện mạo mới và nhiều trải nghiệm thú vị hơn nữa cho du khách .

Chợ Bến Thành thực sự là một điểm đến lý tưởng, nơi bạn có thể cảm nhận trọn vẹn nhịp đập và hương vị của Sài Gòn. Hy vọng những thông tin trên sẽ giúp bạn có một chuyến tham quan và "check-in" thật vui vẻ và đáng nhớ.

very crowded market, most of the products are overpriced, but i can understand since it's located in tourist area

The Ben Thanh Market is one of the most iconic and lively spots in the city. You can find a wide variety of local food, souvenirs, clothes, and handicrafts all in one place. It’s a great place to experience the vibrant atmosphere of Ho Chi Minh City and local daily life

Fint marked med alt hvad man kan ønske sig. Her er meget af alt og som et marked skal være. Her er små listige steder og vi mødte tre søde vietnamesiske damer fra Esbjerg og vi fik lov til at spise sammen med dem. Vi fik dejlig mad- vores første måltid i Vietnam. En kæmpe oplevelse.

As most people travelling to Ho Chi Minh should be aware of this famous and popular market. It is not the cheapest of markets, but does offer a huge variety of goods, from t-shirts, trainers, jackets, ornaments as well as a great food selection, not forgetting the delicious Vietnamese coffee. However, you must have your haggling skills ready, and not to be afraid of walking away if the price is not to your liking. I would recommend offering half of the suggested asking price as a starting point. After some time, the constant asking of "do you want to buy something " can get a little irritating, especially in the heat, and remember there is no air conditioning! Overall, I would say it's an interesting experience, but not one I would like to experience again in a hurry. There are plenty of markets close by, offering the same goods at a cheaper asking price. However, when visiting Ho Chi Minh, you can tick it off your Vietnam list.

A great place to browse for souvenirs, snacks, and street food all in one stop. It can feel chaotic and some stalls open with high prices, so bargaining helps. I had a tasty quick meal and found a few unique gifts after comparing a couple of vendors. Go with patience and do not be afraid to walk away.
It's fine.  Mixture of t shirts, bags and food stalls. Mostly fakes  No prices but I'm told you can't haggle for buying items in the market
C’est une institution historique à HCMC. Un marché typique comme on en trouve d’autres au Vietnam ou au Cambodge. On peut y acheter de tout et pas bien cher.
Vorsicht! Hier wird meist ein zu hoher Preis verlangt. Unbedingt hart und geschickt verhandeln! Die anderen Märkte sind billiger.
Ein unglaublich wildes Gewusel von Menschen und Waren. Die Preise sind nicht unbedingt attraktiv. Wir haben nichts gekauft, aber die Atmosphäre genossen.

Certainement pas le marché le plus intéressant que l'on est pu voir en Asie , mais il faut quand même y faire un tour ne serait ce que pour se restaurer sur un des nombreux petit resto qui vous proposent tous à peu près des plats semblables , mais en ce qui nous concerne c'était bon et à un prix très abordable pour des Européens .

Best to note the entrance number you ente, so you can get back out this large maze of crammed booths. Want an item? You are likely to find it. Be careful of pickpockets and overpaying. Some areas are fixed price, but for most you bargain. Don’t even think about paying the asking price or more than you think. Be polite and stick to your price.

The market is a lovely and wonderful experience as long as you’re okay with crowded and narrow spaces. There’s so many options so don’t just grab the first thing you see. And there are different sections where one side is more clothes and bags and the other side is coffee, alcohol, and dried fruits. I spent more than an hour there because my dad and I started with window shopping and eventually I ended back at a shop right near front entrance of the market to grab a couple of Ao Dai. (See picture for reference shop 80). I circled back to them 3x because the black Ao Dai caught my eye and I couldn’t stop thinking about it. The ladies were very quick to assist and zipped me up so I could ensure they fit me. They’re attentive because they want your business so don’t feel intimidated when they ask you to look at more options and always remember to negotiate the price. They’re usually a lot more expensive than they should be because it is still a tourist spot. I also stopped to pick up some snacks & coffee. Shop 759-761 is also located near one of their many entrances. Everything was a reasonable price and I was able to split it amongst my friends as souvenirs. Worth checking out!!!

This indoor market was just a short walk away from our Hotel. It's open until around 6pm and offers lots of products.  Most of it is about clothes, bags, accessories and stuff like that. Cheap stuff or fake brands In the middle of the market it's all about haggle for a good price. It's fun to browse, but most products didn't catch our attention. In the outer "ring" of the market there are more - also traditional - clothes to be found. With fixed prices, so you won't have to haggle aorund. We actually liked that and found some nice clothes and caps here. Good quality, fair prices and friendly staff.  It's not mainly a food market, but there is a good number of food stalls, quite aggressively fighting for customers. If you can ignore that, you will find some enjoyable food here. We did. Quality wasn't the very best on this trip, but cheap and cheerful.  At night, when the main market is closed, lots of stalls will line up just outside the market, offering all kinds of products. A nice time to enjoy the evening, with all the bustling life, the lights...it's a nice atmosphere.  We enjoyed our visit. Not something to do daily, but surely a must if you've never been to.

Main dans la main au cœur du marché, nous avons dégusté des fruits exotiques, admiré des lanternes colorées et ri en marchandant des souvenirs. Animé, authentique et plein de vie — un incontournable pour les amoureux curieux !

Am Abend bummelten wir durch das lebendige Gewirr des Ben-Thanh-Markts – probierten exotische Früchte, feilschten um Souvenirs und teilten uns eine dampfende Schüssel Phở. Laut, chaotisch, aber voller Energie – perfekt für ein unvergessliches Paar-Abenteuer!

This is what is to be expected from this market. A very busy sometimes overwhelming place to have fun. Try your hand at bargaining, it really works. Buy some inexpensive “ merchandise “ grab some authentic local food and it will not break the bank. The usual look out for thieves caveat but that goes for many places these days.

Stop 8 on the red hop on hop off bus route . Certainly an intense shopping experience in central Ho Chi Minh City that would be comparable to that in a Turkish bazaar. Not for the faint hearted and good for seasoned bargain hunters only. Some areas have fixed prices ( Still reasonable) but most stall holders are keen to sell and may seem “Pushy” to some western buyers. I don’t think it’s an attraction I would bring children to . Otherwise an enjoyable morning provided you know what you are looking for.
Lots of bargains to be had but lots of very similar products. Bags, luggage Tshirts, shoes etc. Go early morning or later at night as it's very hot and steamy inside.

Quel chaos délicieux ! Nous avons ri, marché main dans la main entre les étals de soie, de fruits exotiques et de souvenirs. Les odeurs de phở nous ont menés à un petit stand caché – meilleur repas de notre voyage. Un peu trop de vendeurs insistants, mais tellement vivant !

Plongée vibrante et amusante dans l'énergie de Saïgon ! Nous avons adoré négocier main dans la main pour des souvenirs et déguster des spécialités aux food stalls. C'est chaotique, coloré et plein de vie.

You have to be good with negotiation, vendors quote very high price and unlike da nang market vendors are not that enthusiastic to bargain, so purchase only if you have idea about value of item, otherwise you will be ripped off.

Stort marked midt i byen med alt i souvenirs, tøj, sko, tasker, mad og drikke. Jeg var lidt overrasket over hvor frit kopivarer (stadig) sælges. Der er en lille del, hvor der er fixed prices (gangene er skiltet med dette i loftet) og ellers skal du bruge dine skills til at prutte om priserne. Vi købte souvenirs og fik lidt at drikke på de famøse plast-taburetter og vil klart anbefale dette marked et besøg for alle turister for at opleve stemningen. Kan anbefales både dag og aften
這裡可以買很多東西，旁邊又有最出名的找換店。唯一不好是比較炎熱，如果早上來，買了很多東西，又要回酒店一趟放下東西。不過胡志明市要買便宜東西就是這裡，而且要拍照留念。
市場の活気に圧倒されました！たくさんの人や物で溢れていて、歩いているだけでワクワクします。値段交渉も旅の醍醐味。美味しいローカルフードも堪能できて、二人で大満足の時間を過ごせました。とても楽しかったです！
What a whirlwind! We dove headfirst into the chaotic energy of Ben Thanh Market and loved every minute. We had a blast haggling for souvenirs and tasting all the incredible street food.

Very busy with pushy sellers, but to be expected of a market of this size. If you go in there looking for nothing in particular, prepare to come away with something as there is a lot of different items on sale and you can haggle prices down too. Definitely felt targeted towards tourists but locals there too.

Fast so etwas wie das Zentrum der Stadt. Hier laufen viele Fäden zusammen und auch der Straßenverkehr ist überwältigend. Man sollte sich wirklich beim Erreichen dieser Örtlichkeit zunächst einmal ein Plätzchen suchen, um das ganze Ensemble zu erfassen. Nach dem Betreten des Bauwerks wird es dann noch unübersichtlicher. Die Orientierung wird schwierig und wir hatten den Eindruck, man sollte sich einfach einmal treiben lassen. Nicht aber unvorsichtig werden, denn die Örtlichkeit ist nicht ganz unproblematisch. Zahlreiche Hinweisschilder weisen auch darauf hin, dass es hier Taschendiebe unterwegs sind. Tatsächlich wird es mitunter etwas eng, aber das größere Ärgernis sind die Nepper, Schlepper, Bauernfänger. Sie stellen sich in den Weg und wollen so den Besucher in bestimmte Verkaufsstände – natürlich teure - locken. Hier offenbart sich dieser Markt als eine Touristenfalle, wie sie in diversen mediterranen Regionen alltäglich sind. Nur am Rande: wir haben sonst im gesamten Land nirgendwo in Geschäften solche Anmache- erlebt. Wenn man tiefer in das Gebäude eindringt und so auch den Bereich der des Touristen-Schrotts hinter sich lässt, wird es besser. Man erreicht dann nämlich die Alltagswaren, in deren Umgebung es erheblich entspannter wird zugeht. Dies gilt auch für die Preise !! Tipp: innerhalb des Markets und auch in seiner Umgebung gibt es zahlreiche Gaststätten in denen es gute Speisen aus der Landesküche und Letztere auch zu günstigen Preisen gibt.

Good place to purchase cheap souvenirs and clothes. So many vendors peddling their good. Make sure that you negotiate the price. Always start at least half the price quoted by the vendor. Be tough in our negotiations and bring cash!
Can get hot inside but the stuff available is amazing in variety and brands ( nothing original) . It's an experience worth a visit. Home your bargaining skills before you visit!
This is a very busy place. If you are no good a haggling it can also be expensive. The food is cheap and very good. Well worth a few hours.
Sehenswerter, trubeliger Markt für Einheimische und Touristen. Sehenswert, insbesondere der Lebensmittelbereich.

Most large Asian cities have markets with knock off luxury goods, t-shirts and gifts oriented to tourists and they are fun to see. Bangkok, SIngapore and Hong Kong's knock offs are good quality, Vietnam's very, very poor. Our local tour guide advised paying half the initial price offered- fair to you and the vendor. Vendors asked for $140-$160 US dollars as a starting price for knock off purses and after negotiating fellow guests on our Viking tour were paying $60-$80 US dollars for a purse depending on the size. They are not leather and were very cheap qulity. We bought some Vietnam Disney-like t-shirts with quirky text for $20.00 a shirt. "SIlk" or "cashmere" scarves at $30.00 and $60.00 were popular purchases for our group. The market has a huge selection of gorgeous fabrics particularly Indian and a fellow guest who knows fabric quality was pleased with the wide range of fabrics she got. Oddly no silk or batik pillow covers. Fake jade bracelets with a certificate of authenticity are not jade (of course) but still selling for over $100.00. The most enjoyable part of the market for us was the spice and fruit areas that were filled with heavenly scent so made the trip a good outing

The historical charm of the market is undeniable, with its iconic clock tower and aged architecture. The bustling atmosphere was both exciting and chaotic—perfect for couples looking to dive into local culture. While the endless stalls of souvenirs, clothes, and street food were intriguing, the constant haggling felt a bit exhausting. It’s worth a visit for the experience, but don’t expect a quiet, romantic stroll. Definitely a must-see, but brace yourself for the crowds!

The historical charm of this iconic landmark was palpable, blending seamlessly with its bustling energy. We wandered through aisles brimming with colorful souvenirs, fragrant spices, and local delicacies, feeling the pulse of Vietnamese culture. Haggling with friendly vendors added a playful touch to our adventure.

Chợ Bến Thành là biểu tượng của quận 1. Khu chợ này khá cổ điển và bày bán nhiều quà lưu niệm cho du khách. Mình đã tới đây ăn bánh bèo của một tiểu thương trong chợ và hương vị ngon như lời đồn. Tuy nhiên, các cửa hàng trong chợ khá nhỏ, nếu muốn ăn thì hãy ráng kiên nhẫn ngồi nha!

We laughed our way through bustling stalls filled with vibrant goods and aromatic street food. Bargaining together brought us closer, while sampling local snacks ignited our taste buds. This lively market is chaotic but utterly charming—a must-visit for travelers craving authentic Vietnamese culture!
One of many markets throughout Asia,you have to give them a go. Always good fun to pick up some tacky souvenirs. It is all part of the travel experience.
Большой рынок - обязательное место для посещения. Я ходил в первую очередь ради того, чтобы поменять деньги, заодно прошелся по торговым рядам. Обязательно надо торговаться!!!
在胡志明市，我们一群朋友一起探索了本田市场。这里真是又大又热闹，地道的越南风情扑面而来。街边小吃和手工艺品琳琅满目，虽然人潮拥挤，但我们乐在其中。真是一个体验当地文化的好去处。 !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
我们这对情侣在胡志明市的边青市场游玩。这里人潮汹涌，热闹非凡，五光十色的商品让我们目不暇接。虽然有点拥挤，但体验到的多样文化和美食让我们觉得值得。逛完后，我们带着满满的购物袋和笑容离开了这个热情的市场。

It gets very busy around sunset and is quite crowded but can be good fun for haggling with the traders. Most stalls have similar stuff to someone else in the market so you can compare prices and make and offer to start the deal - never pay the first given price. Food area was quite off putting however, wouldn’t recommend here when there are many other better places

Прогулялся как по музею, только продавцы мешали созерцать. )) Хотя некоторые вещи можно было и посмотреть. Цены не узнавал. Но на тех ценниках, что выставлены, стоимость небольшая. Торгуются все : и немцы и шведы и русские. Это только те, что я по языку узнал. Вообще, именно здесь я увидел самое большое скопление иностранцев В Хошимине.

Particolare mercato ricco di verdure e pesce . Merita una visita per comprendere le usanze locali. Se volete ( e non fate troppo caso all'igiene) ptrete assaggiare cibo locale cucinato al momento. Particolare.

The market is fun. It is a good place for smoothies and souvenirs. Most stalls have about the same, but if you walk further, you can find a few with some uniqueness. They expect you to bargain and combine items and prices, so take advantage of the system. People are nice and laid back. The place has security in place, dogs, and cats. We went there twice!

We had researched Ben Thanh Market prior to going to Vietnam and were well aware of Haggling Process . To the uninitiated , Do not accept the first offer . Haggle Hard , be prepared to Walk away . The prices are way too high , so set a price in your mind what you feel is reasonable and if they can't agree , Walk away . This can sometimes work . For example , my Wife wanted a rechargeable Battery operated Fan . The opening offer was (Morning Price) 600000 Dong( Aud $36-00 ). I just laughed . 2nd Offer 400000 Dong .( Aud $24-00) I countered with 50000 dong (Aud $3-00) The next offer - Last Price 250000 dong (Aud $15-00) I offered 80000 dong (Aud $4-80) . They refused to go lower so we walked away . We got about 5 paces and she ran after us saying OK 80000 dong . They are still making a profit and won't sell at a loss . Always smile , be polite but firm , and you will get some bargains . The Market can be a bit overwhelming as the stall holders are competing with one another aggressively . A Smile and a no thank you and keep walking . Good Luck .

Ben Thanh Market is one the famous place for tourists whenever you have a plan visiting HoChiMinh City. They have every thing for shopping ( I mean the good copy and good price Items ). More over, here is also has local foods...where you don't need to go Restaurant. The cost is quite cheap with 2.5 USD you will have a full meal for sure. The things I don't like is here is very crowded and without AC...hundred people all the time. It opens from 7am until before 9pm. When you get here to shop and take a cab / taxi back...must be careful with taxi infront of Ben Thanh Market. Some taxi driver will take a cost higher the traditional company taxi such as Vinasun or Mailinh. These Taxis accept payment by Credit Card. If you can, walking back to your hotel is the best idea for you if the distance within 1km. Must see Ben Thanh Market. Don't miss it if you visit Saigon indeed.

Ce qui est surtout interessant, ce sont les boutiques de l Etat, à prix fixe sur tout le pourtour interieur du marché. Les prix sont tres bas et cela donne une idée pour marchander ailleurs, il y a de tout dans le non alimentaire : tee shirts pour touristes a 82 000 dg, sacs, souvenirs, contrefacons, mais aussi bcp de vêtements pour les vietnamiens. Le reste du marché est privé: contrefacons a gogo et halles alimentaires. Harcelement continu penible ici ...
Ben Thanh market is very busy and crowded. I like that you can get just about anything, however, you must negotiate prices with the venders.
The bustling atmosphere was alive with vendors shouting and shoppers weaving through the crowded aisles. We tried bargaining, but ended up in fits of laughter instead.

This market is insane!! It’s massive, crowded and loads of stalls all crammed in tight together! Of course you can’t just browse and look as they try to get you into every stall asking if you want this or that. It is quite an experience but can be a stressful one if you don’t like crammed spaces.

The maze of stalls selling everything from local spices to silk scarves was overwhelming but exciting. Remember to bargain hard - we got some great deals on coffee and traditional crafts. The food court was a highlight, serving authentic Vietnamese dishes. A must-visit for experiencing real Saigon culture!

Die Vielfalt an Gewürzen, Souvenirs und lokalen Spezialitäten war überwältigend. Das Feilschen macht zwar Spaß, aber man muss aufpassen, nicht über's Ohr gehauen zu werden. Die frischen Früchte waren köstlich! Ein faszinierender Einblick in das lokale Leben Saigons.

Great place to get plenty of goods and you will need to bargain here .. things from clothes, shoes, artificial jewellery , watches, and on and on ...all are concentrated in small communities in this market and make it a must visit if you are planning on doing some shopping in HCM city.

The aroma of Vietnamese coffee and street food was irresistible! Practiced our bargaining skills (not great, but fun trying). The food court became our favorite spot - sharing local dishes and watching the vibrant market life unfold. Overwhelming but absolutely memorable!

This market is the most prominent market in the city where you can do the local and souvenirs shopping. The prices are very much inflated and you have to bargain to get good rates. A quick tip would be to quote 0.4-0.5 times the price which vendor tells you.
Classico mercato vietnamita con passaggi stretti e con mille persone che ti chiedono di comprare. Ottimo per souvenir di tutti i tipi e anche cibo

Ben Thanh market is somewhere you will either love or hate. It is an indoor market that is packed with stalls selling food, tourist souvenirs, clothing, jewellery, tailors that make up suits, etc, etc. The stalls are close together so it can be difficult to work your way through the narrow isles. The prices are perhaps higher than elsewhere but you can bargain them down by 50% or more. The market traders can get quite pushy, not letting you browse in peace and sometimes touching you. However, there is a good choice of merchandise, some of which is good quality, and a discerning shopper can get a good bargain.

You need to be ready to haggle, and you’ll have a sensory overload of scents and bustling crowds (bonus; hot and humid!), but it’s worth a visit to experience it firsthand (I regret not buying a souvenir I saw there, because I thought I’d see it again somewhere else). The food was surprisingly good and reasonably priced (good for vegans too), but you may not want to sit too close to the stalls where they sell durian.

A fun chaotic shopping experience. This place is buzzing. A large amount of small vendors selling bags, tshirts, wallets, fabric, dresses. You name it you can get it. The bartering experience is fun if you keep it light hearted and remind your self how much their currency actually is worth. We picked up some huge bargains and to be honest the quality is good. With a visit but the constant sales pitches catches up with you after an hour or so.

This market had a lot to offer for tourists. If you are looking to buy souvenirs it is definitely the best market to come to. Do not be afraid to negotiate we were told early on that you should be able to get around 40% lower than the asking price and I was able to get a 40-50% lower price on almost everything I bought. Prices start at a lot higher than what the shop owner will sell it for and are will almost always make the sale if you want the product.

it was so much fun. it was really really hot and confusing. we got lost in the lanes but the stuff is amazing. they even have a currency exchange so we could shop even more. the ventilation is bad and most of the stores prefer cash unless you are buying a bigger amount then they will accommodate credit cards. however, I don't like bargaining so I did not shop a lot but my mom can bargain brilliantly so she bought a lot of stuff for so cheap. the quality is good and there is a lot of variety. However, since I wanted to buy knickknacks and small quantities a lot of sellers refused to sell just one magnet or one keychain. Many were disappointed if I only wanted to buy one item. We took a cab from the airport to the market and everything else was within walking distance so it was a great start of our day trip.

Mấy hôm gần đây, tôi có đi tham quan chợ Bến Thành cùng với các người bạn của tôi. Về tổng quan, chợ thực sự rất đẹp, độc lạ với những thiết kế bên trong và bên ngoài. Về các mặt hàng và giá cả, theo tôi thì tôi thấy các hàng hóa đa số là áo quần và đồ lưu niệm bên cạnh đó cũng có khu vực ăn uống tuy nhiên về giá cả thì tôi thấy không phù hợp với những thường dân như tôi, giá cả khá mắc, phù hợp cho các người nước ngoài

Great market where you can get just about anything you want/need. Very busy and be prepared to haggle on prices, even so we found it to be rather expensive and got the same items much cheaper in some of the smaller streets surrounding the market square. Definitely worth the visit for the experience.

De Ben Thanmarkt is de topper van de dag! Misschien toch voor sommigen. Rechthoekig gebouw met gestruktureerd gangenplan waardoor je de weg niet kwijt raakt. Gebouwd door de Fransen in 1914 die het ‘Les Halles Centrales’ oftewel centrale markthallen noemden. Duizenden stalletjes en kraampjes met alle denkbare produkten, met daartussen smalle gangen. Veel souvenirs en kleding. Maar ook juwelen, leder en kleding. Je kan hier je kleding op maat laten maken op één nacht. Onthoudt één ding. Hier kun je alle namaak vinden; van Nike tot Rolex.

Great markets but super hot, no where to stop but found great deals in shops around the market and more enjoyable experience. Scams, don’t buy a coconut on the street. Don’t let anyone touch your shoes. Say no keep walking. Coconut vendors can be aggressive and hit you with a stick they carry behind them. Grab ( like Uber) is best way to get around and you can pay them cash.

Fantastic market which provides a great feel for daily life in Vietnam, the market is visited by people from around the globe and offers everything from fresh seafood to trinkets and gifts. It’s worth a visit but does get very busy.

1)This is one of the MUST Visit places in HCMC. 2)The location is GOOD right in city centre, Accessible by most means of Transport (Taxis. GRABS. Bus.. ) 3)The market has four (4) gates and sectionised selling different items in each section (Clothing. Handicrafts. Electronics. Food. Flowers....)Wet market is attached. Most transport prefer "South Gate" as pick up point. However, transport charges HIGHER. Walk few meters away and call transport away from market, Likely 20-30% cheaper !! 5)BARGIN is the order of the say. Most venders up the prices, some as much as 100% more than elsewhere !! 6)The Night market starts around 1800 hour, when centre market closed. Currently, Very few stalls as compared to previous time !! 7)Do visit and Experience local Culture !! PETER (313Globetrotter)

Huge market, very central location, very touristy though (dare I say Tourist Trap?) so compare the prices well, although in the end, it's a convenient and easy place to pick up some souvenirs coz most vendors speak English:-)

the market was located right across our hotel and after our morning sightseeing, we decided to check what they have to offer. there are stalls for souvenirs, local products and food but didnt expect that their were a lot of stores that sells knock offs of high end items such as bags, shoes, watches, clothings & jewelry. Some are high quality products that can pass for a real one ( but you have to know what a real one looks and feels like. The best or challenging part of shopping in ben thanh is you need to know how to haggle. For the vendors, haggling is like a poker game, so don’t agree on their first offer no matter how much the fake ones looks real. i was told that always cut the price in half and walk away if they don’t take your offer because there are plenty of stores that sell the same item. if you start to walk away, the vendor might grab you for you to stay and they will take your offer. when shopping at the market, highly recommend that you have to be patient with plenty of time to spare to get a good deal and eat before you shop so you dont get hangry while dealing with the vendors. Also, bring cash and go shopping early or late afternoon because it’s oven hot in the middle of the day.
Fabulous and large market selling all sorts of stuff from clothes to souvenirs. Be prepared to bargain with the vendors. There is no air-conditioning here so come early

Quente, bagunçado e com comerciantes que fazem da barganha algo essencial. Se esse é seu tipo de passeio, vai adorar. Muitas comidas e produtos típicos. É muito próximo a tudo, portanto não custa nada dar um pulinho por lá.
Exciting variety of goods. Bargain your heart out. Cut the price almost in half and every vendor I tried it with went along. Many take credit cards and it appears secure. Fun time

You get absolutely anything here, it’s super busy and the stall holders do encourage you to buy. Best time is not to engage unless you want something in particular. It’s work a good look just to see the range of copy stuff. You have to be firm and haggle or bargain with them, if you’re not happy with the price just walk away, there’s going to be staff around the corner selling the same stuff and start your haggling over again.
This market has many things (clothes, souvenirs) and the sellers are quite willing to negotiate (more so than I found at other places).

You really need to bargain hard and if you’re not used to this you might find it a bit intimidating. After walking around we found a vendor that wasn’t pushy and we ended up buying all our souvenirs from them - they gave us a good price too!  One criticism - it’s really hot in there.

3 things to know  1. prices for souvenirs are quoted 5-7x what you should pay. walk away at first price and see how quickly it drops  2. the two gold shops (in)famous for currency exchange are located here: ha tam jewelry store and mai van jewelry store 10.772174569856064, 106.6974940728263  3. if you are only in vietnam for a few days, get your tourist sim card (5GB/day, for 20 days) from malaysia street. it comes preactivated and resold at 100000 vnd for a 50000 vnd face value card. how reliable it is over the long term is unknown, but it was the cheapest i encounter so far. the rest of the prices i see in hcm are just crazy, upwards to 300000 vnd
Huge market selling just about everything. No doubt has bargains but needs time to find and haggle and find acceptable quality.

The market traders here are unrelenting. If you so much show any interest in their products they will chase you until you buy. They also will put the price around 3 times higher in the hope you buy it for double the price and think you got a bargain.  My advice for shopping - find the product you want. Get prices from 3 different traders and pick the one who gave you the most fair price and then ask for slightly lower price.  I refused to give anyone my money who asked the 3 times the price even if their final price was the lowest. Its called karma.  and don't stress about an extra dollar. it means more to them than you. But at the same time, don't pay 3 times the price!!!!!

Ben Thanh market is definitely worth a visit but don’t expect to find anything different or unique. You will however find copies of almost every well known luxury brand. Prices are ‘flexible’ starting from almost double of what they would be willing to accept and it is up to the buyer to negotiate (bargain). If you walk away and they don’t call you back, you know that your offer is too low. They obviously know that women are bigger buyers than men because the cry to attract attention is always “madame’. The market is a great experience.

This is not nearly as bad as some reviewers would have you believe.  True, there is no air conditioning. And the sellers in each stall will see what you are wearing and show you that they have similar products. But we did not find them to be overly aggressive.  Compared to the Saigon Square market, this one has more t-shirts, polos, and coffee beans.  The quality seems to be a bit lower here, but still worth a visit.

Et marked som har så og si alt. Vesker, klær, suvernirer, solbriller, og en masse boder med mat. Selgerne er virkelig på jobb, de roper etter deg og tar i deg for å få solgt varene, noe som kan være meget slitsomt. Man må også være flink å prute, de er villig til å gå ned over 60% av første pris. Vi var der på kvelden, og det var veldig mange besøkende, kan bli stående i kø ettersom gangene er trange og vanskelig å gå forbi hverandre. Men verdt et besøk for opplevelsen sin del.
Nice Busy Markets heaps of stalls very crowded but at least it is air conditioned which was nice otherwise not good 😊 Heaps of copy bags, clothing and Jewelry. Worth a visit but bargain hard.

This market is everything you would expect from a market in South East Asia. It is cramped, hot and full of ‘designer’ gear at knock down prices. It is repetitive in it’s merchandise, the stall holders are persistent & relentless but it is always great fun, especially if you love a barter & a haggle. During our visit to HCMC we bought trainers, football shirts, T-shirts, ‘Crocs’, sliders, shorts, sunglasses, handbag and even a winter coat for my daughter for school when she gets home. Needless to say, we even purchased a new suitcase to carry all our new items home. :) The stall holders are always in good spirits and like a laugh and banter and are happy for you to walk away. There are also a food section and lots of stalls with Vietnamese gifts and souvenirs. You may love it or hate it but it’s definitely worth a visit when in HCMC.
Shopping area for all things Vietnam. Souvenirs, artwork, clothing, knock offs, food, drinks, etc. Great place to bargain for items.
Store owners reply with bad words not good for tourists please take again on them spoiling vietnam name thanks 🙏 i will not come again to Vietnam Thanks u 🙏 Pradeep Nawar

An asian market is a fast paced, eclectic mix of sights, smells and experiences. If you like to 'bag a bargain' and you are prepared to put in the work then this place is for you. Know what you want before you go and research what you want to pay for it. The best outcome is everyone leaves the interaction happy.  Although it may not be for everyone, if you like this type of hustle and bustle and want to try the tasty spring rolls and pho soup, we would recommend it. On a side note, be sure you use authentic taxi services in an around the markets, other private cars (look like taxi) often wait outside and do not charge on the meter.

If this is your first Asian market it’s worth a look, walking the aisles full of colour and activity is a bit of fun. The food stands offering meals, smoothies and cold drinks are a cross between local and tourist. On the outside edge the wet market, although not large is worth a look.

I don't think there is anything that you will not find in this market. T-shirts, Bags, Watches, Belts, Different types of locally packaged food and so much more. However, you need to know the art of bargaining. A quick tip would be to quote around 40 percent of the price. The shop keepers are very sweet and funny. You will also find a lot of Indian Tourists at this place. This market starts at 7 am and starts closing around 7 pm in the evening.
I stayed in Sai Gon for 3 days and I went to Ben thanh market to get some memory thing for my friends. It is a special culture market but not many special things but it was worth the visit .

If you live to shop till you drop dead this is the place to go to. A organised market with a contrast of shops. First who are fixed price and then others who quote more than four times the actual rate. So bargain hard to save your money. You can buy bags. Clothes, lacquerware, wooden carvings, purses, souvenirs, tableware, decorative and top brand fakes from Louis Vuitton to Prada to YSL. You name it and get it there. Well it is said the quality is bad which I cannot comment till we use the stuff we bought but I guess good shopping experience. Remember they can quote a two million Dong and give it to you for 400000. Not always as other places you can get barked at by rabid sellers. Watch the fun and have fun

The market is indoors and hot. There are fans scattered around and stall holders always offer you a chair but it is hard work. We were also warned in advance that the whole area is risky for tourists so were on high alert. Bargaining is essential and I wish I had known in advance that the US dollar is the preferred currency. Be prepared to bargain and bargain hard. The quoted price can start off higher than you’d pay in London. My son was brutal but got every price reduced by at least 60% which I would never have managed. There is everything under the sun and also great street food. An exhilerating day out and some brilliant purchaes.

Amazing selection of clothing, bags, watches, jewellery and souvenires . Be prepared to negotiate a better price. Everyone one polite and well mannered. I purchased numerous quality products and only wished I had more time
Many great products! Shop owners do accost you and want you to buy goods. Negotiate hard! Prices start really high! Generally friendly and fairly good natured
Well, it is an experience. At least the toilets have gotten better over time. There are many vendors selling similar stuffs, with a very large range of different prices. Bargain a lot!
Ghé qua chơi và thăm thú cho biết. Nhiều đồ để mua sắm, chủ yếu là phục vụ khách du lịch. Giá cả cũng bình thường, không đắt lắm.

Ben Thanh Market is one of the most well-known and popular markets in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam. The market boasts an array of stalls selling a wide range of goods, including clothing, souvenirs, local delicacies, and fresh produce. The market is quite hectic and crowded, but it's definitely worth a visit for any tourists looking to experience the local culture and pick up some souvenirs. Bargaining is a must, as vendors tend to inflate their prices for tourists.

Big market selling endless fake bags, scarfs, jewellery, shirts....you name it, this place probably has it. Prices are a bit higher than what you'll find in other parts of Vietnam (Hanoi for example) but there is definitely the best range. The place is busy and may be confronting for those that have never been to such a market. Most vendors are reasonable with their haggle prices.

Market is paradise for shopping lovers..but u should have bargain skills..good Market for sovinouers, art n craft of the country..shopkeepers are friendly.one can get local food n drinks ..some r fixed price shop there..

Un lugar para comprar a buen precio, aún que no todos están dispuestos a regatear(casi siempre los puestos que están en las vías principales) los puestos internos si están dispuestos a venderte a un buen precio. Encontraras ropa "normal" estampada con logos de marcas famosas, también encontraras buenas réplicas. En cuanto a backpacks, hay muchos!, Ellos simplemente pone logos famosos ( underarmour, Nike, fila, noth face) en cualquier tipo de backpack, cangurera o bolsas, algunas puedes conseguirlas entre $5 a $20 dependiendo de la calidad.
海鮮や果物ばかりじゃなく、Tシャツや偽物？やコピー商品までもう無いものはないくらいのお店がひしめいています。見るのも楽しいけど値切って買うのも面白い。おばさんに捕まってポロシャツを3枚も買わされました(笑)

So many goods on sale here from sportswear to handbags, jewellery to underwear and not forgetting vietnamese crafts and souvenirs! No air con so it does get very warm if doing a lot of shopping. Overall, the quality of the products on sale was very good. Expect to haggle, can get anything from 50-75% less than the original price quoted if you are firm. Give yourself plenty time to shop around.

One of the oldest surviving structures in the city. Plenty of shops for clothing, souvenirs, gifts. Lots of food spots as well. Closes surprisingly early though, at 9pm the majority of shops were closed.

Just like any other public market in the world, BTC, is a place where you can find most anything, from real things to fake things. If you’re not use to such a place, you may say that the vendors are pushy, aggressive and won’t take no for an answer. They will start by over-pricing an item to see if you take the bait. I say it’s all about bargaining. Ignore them and they will lower the price. Travelers should keep in mind that these vendors behave this way because they want to sell their stuff. It’s part of the culture. I went to this market to see the cute handicrafts they have. I bought several cloth pencil case. My niece bought several cute coin purse to bring back home as souvenirs. I also bought tea. Oh! So good. So much to see! At the end, it made me dizzy to see all the stuff😂 It’s still worth visiting.

If you are looking for souvenirs from vietnam..this is the place. You get great collection of shoes, bags some local stuff, accessories, etc.  Also if u r looking for good coffee..you can get beans as well as powder..before buying u can also taste the same.. we bought beans and the shopkeeper grinded it in front of us and also packed in customized packets (100gms each).  You need to have great bargaining skills..  Tip - always ask for 30-40% of the proposed price.  They accept vnd, USD or even cards. But if u want to pay by card they will charge u 3% extra.

Local market in heart of city, one can buy dry everything like souvenirs, decorative toys, local watches, garments, inner wear, dry fruits, dry sea foods, nuts, show piece, decorative items, bags, purses, Vietnamese hat, cosmetics etc. but you need to bargain a lot with the tag price.

Waren zweimal auf diesem Markt. Es gibt Souvenirs, Klamotten, Schuhe und einen Food Court. Jeder will verkaufen. Handeln ist angesagt. Die Ware hat teilweise gute Qualität für wenig Geld. Haben einiges eingekauft!

We stopped in as we wanted to get some table mats for friends. They had a good range and I got 4 - managed to drop price 50% so you need to bargain hard. It is quite overwhelming with all the touting. We didn’t stay long as we only wanted these items. Lots of fake bags.

Typical local market which is common to find in Asian countries. But they know how to treat tourist well. Well mannered and easy to talk people around. Bargaining is a key aspect, though. Could be extremely hot and humid for people not used to tropical weather, even in winters. Still a must visit for an experience of Saigon.

È un mercato coperto dove trovi un pò di tutto Dall'alimentazione alle scarpe, orologi, maglie, borse ecc ecc. tutto regolarmente griffato "copiato" più o meno bene. Dai rolex ai piumini, dalle maglie alle scarpe. Comunque è uno scorcio di vita quotidiana da vedere. Mi raccomando, se comprate, Conviene sempre trattare..
It’s a “normal” market in Asia. high voices, many people, warm, barging etc. I went once in the morning, around 9am, and that will I recommend! It’s not so many people, and you have time to think…

Very busy little market. Definitely a must go! You can’t visit Vietnam without making your way through a market. There is a lot of the same thing but there is so much to choose from! Cheap as well - make sure you do a bit of haggling. It can be a bit overwhelming as vendors constantly get you to buy. The trick is to move through slowly, scanning and say no thank you.

Definitely an experience and interesting to see. Just be prepared to keep saying "no, thank you" and keep walking. I've been to similar markets all over and this is the first time I've ever had vendors physically grab my arm to pull me toward their stall. If constant calls to buy stuff and the occasional grab will cause anxiety then stay away. But if you can turn off all emotion it's a fascinating place to walk through, snap some photos and buy things you may or may not need.  One last word of advice is to walk around the shops at the perimeter that have set prices. That will give you an idea of what things should cost. It wasn't uncommon for other vendors to start bargaining at 3-4x those prices.

Great shopping!!!!! Good rates & Decent Quality for the Bargain, ben thanh also gives you the best exchange rate , so try & get your currency exchanged inside the market. Go for 1st Copy Bags, Wallets, belts, Shoes, Coffee, Food items & great export quality garments too.
You can find everything in this market but it is quite expensive than others. Food is excellent such as street food, desert and coffee.
タイトルのとおりです。 観光客相手の強引な商売や店の雰囲気で偽物とわかる品、 値段交渉を楽しめるような人じゃないと馬鹿を見るだけです。 でも、そういう雰囲気を楽しめる人にとってはカオスさがたまらないです。 わざわざベトナムを訪問している理由が正規のブランド品購入なんて人は 絶対に近づいてはいけません。 近所にある高島屋にでも行きましょう。

Ideal for your souvenir shopping. But look around, decide what you want, pick a stall and start bargaining. It's full on and you will get haselled. Great food and possibly the best pho of our trip for just a few dollars.

The Good: lots of knockoff wears and goods for relatively cheap price (100k VND = $6 CND) for Amarni / Polo / Lacoste tshirts. Collared polo shirts / linen polos are approx 200k-250k VND.  Recommend you walk on the outer layer of Ben Thanh, as those are fixed prices. That let's you know the max ceiling you should be paying. For example, Vietnam star tshirt / iPho tshirts = $62k VND fixed price. So similar should be paying approx $50k inside Ben Thanh when negotiating. I did this on multiple occasions, so they knew I knew the price :-)  The Bad: I went here several times with my kids, as they wanted gifts to take home (eg. 3D puzzle animals, knockoff tshirts for friends) so I got to know some of the vendors. The Ugly: COVID wiped out about 25% of the vendors. You can see the many closed shops outside of Ben Thanh too. There has been no gov support for these folks , and Ben Thanh Market only reopened around June -ish. I believe Vietnam opened up to tourists on April 1, 2022. Yes, there are desperate which drives them to be aggressive marketers. But again, after going several times, they knew me as "Canada" and tried to engage me in conversation about life aboard - in their broken English ;-)  The Great: got some really awesome deals. Yes, I'm sure I could have gotten some goods for $10k-20k VND less but I was happy, and they seemed content with a fair price. It truly felt like a "Karma -tic" relationship after several days. No more aggressiveness; and I wasn't trying to hose them down their prices either.  I will always return to Ben Thanh Market - if nothing else, my kids need to learn how to negotiate prices! ha ha

it is such a market where you get everything you wish to buy for the use of your daily life. clothes shoes, purses,belts,combs,dresses,souvenirs etc... being an Indian i enjoyed my expertise at bargaining with the locals. thump rule for shopping is whatever price they quote, try to get between 35-40%. first they will say no but as you start walking they will give it to you at your price. the market is shoppers paradise and gives you thailand market feeling. all the branded stuff you will get here as copy.

A lot of the reviews comment about how there's a lot of heckling from the shop keepers. It didn't bother us and sometimes we'd joke back. We knew what we were getting ourselves into by visiting this souvenir market. They cater to tourists and so the experience will certainly be different.  Come expecting to negotiate the price down as much as 50%.  We had a great with with Kim Dung Jewelry Shop and the jewelry section in general. The shop keepers there were confident in the quality of their goods and didn't pressure or heckle when we visited. I had a necklace that I brought with me that needed a pearl restrung and they were incredibly kind and helpful.

Not the greatest fan of markets, as you spend money and lots of it, that is a fact, but this one was pretty good.  Not many visitors on the Saturday we went, which was surprising, which meant as soon as we entered we were accosted for all sorts of products and wares.... The stalls are set out well and one can buy clothes, watches, shoes, jewelry, etc etc. and it is fun to walk around but one can get lost amongst all the side stalls.  As for buying something......I bought a pair of runners, a small bum bag, and two football shirts. I am quite good at haggling and got most of the sellers down to minimum 50% of what they first asked.....although I think I still over paid on the runners, as the quality of them were not as good as first expected......... and having said that the prices are still a little over priced even with a 50% drop, as the quality isn't the best.........the football shirts are just bad knock offs for example........... .. Still though, as markets go, this was very interesting...
Rất nhiều loại hình được bán tại chợ, mình rất thích thăm quan các cửa hàng bày bán sản phẩm thủ công, nón lá và Áo Dài Việt Nam. Cân nhắc về giá trước khi mua nhé
Thi thoảng cũng vào chợ chơi, có lúc mua đồ, có lúc không, có những đồ mua đc nhưng cũng có đồ thì không bao giờ mua vì mình biết là đồ đó chất lượng kém hoặc đắt :D

This market offers traditional Vietnamese experiences in the heart of Saigon, however lately it caters more towards foreign tourists (which can be good or bad, depending on your preferences). Ben Tanh is located on the heart of Saigon that is easily accessible from most parts of the city.

Bei meiner Geschäftsreise nach Vietnam Anfang Januar 2020 war ich ein paar Tage in Ho-Chi-Minh-Stadt und habe unter anderem natürlich auch diesen großen, historischen Markt mitten in Saigon besucht, der sich in einer großen Markthalle befindet. Und innen drin ist ein unglaubliches Gefusel und Lärmpegel, also so richtig wie man sich einen asiatischen Markt vorstellt. Viele Eindrücke und Gerüche und aufgrund einer Empfehlung haben wir auch mitten im Markt im Foodbereich dann eine original Pho Soup gegessen, mitten im Geschehen, das war lecker und hat Spaß gemacht. Ich habe auch einige Bilder von dem Besuch gemacht und lade die gleich hier mit hoch. Ich finde dieser Markt ist defintiv einen Besuch wert.

Have been to many many markets across Asia. They all vary in there own way, but one thing in common, is that they are all busy, and a burst of colour. Ben Thanh Market has the lot and more. Do shop for souvenirs, and do barter. Also grab a bite to eat.

Every time I go to Ho Chi Minh City I never forget to pass by in the Ben than market This time is a bit different. No crowds and very lest tourist. A bit sad to see this! But I will always visit this market every time I am in the city
Can pick up a lot of bargains here - make sure you barter! You will be able to pick up any souvenirs you want from here.
What a place! So much to see. So much to buy! So much to eat! It was worth the visit just to walk through and see everything available. The people watching is great too.
Muitas frutas, especiarias, grãos, e animais vivos. Não vá achando que encontrará um ambiente higienizado, pq não encontrará. Mas é uma experiência imersiva fascinante

Husband and I stopped past here for some brand name knock offs. It was very hot and humid and hard to breathe at times. The bargains were good and I wish we bought more. No change rooms to try clothes on. Haggling is like nothing we had ever experienced. Watch your money and know your conversion rates. It for the feint hearted.

In the central area of Ho Chi Minh within walking distance of all the tourist offerings, just a short walk from the reunification palace is this very busy big indoor market. Passing people going about there everyday life, an old man gave me a free coconut drink which was a nice gesture. Went into the market and had a good walk around, people selling fruit, vegetables and general goods including for us noodle and rice dishes which we had at a very cheap price eating it at the side of the food stall with locals.
Lots of choices of local goods to purchase which included food, clothes and tourist gifts. Worth haggling but sometimes it's hard work. Some stalls had prices up which is preferable for browsing.

Nous y sommes allés pour une 2e fois et je suis toujours surprise de l'agressivité de certains vendeurs lorsqu'ils voient que nous sommes étrangers. En général par contre, tout va bien et nous sommes capables de faire des achats sereinement. Nous nous sommes fait un peu arnaquer par des tireurs de pousse-pousse au marché qui annoncent un prix et qui en chemin demandent un autre prix. Lorsqu'on dit non, ils nous larguent au milieu de nulle part. On est touriste et comme partout ailleurs, ils cherchent à nous tirer le plus possible, déplorable

The market was not too crowded probably because of Covid-19. So it was great to browse and look at all the things on sale. Sellers are a little pushy and souvenirs seem more expensive than other shops. But worth experiencing at Ben Thanh is the food! There were so many different types of food and drinks that it was hard to decide on what to eat! Like Hoi An market, this is a really good place to try the local dishes!
Sehr viele Touristen.Vor ort gibt es neben Speisen eine Vielzahl von Souvenirs zu kaufen.typisch für Asien wird man an jedem Stand von den Verkäufern angesprochen.
Genuine Fake is to be found here. Really busy and very cramped but sellers aren't pushy like in some other countries. Good place to find a "bargain" or little souvenir.

What I like most about this place, is the viatnamese culture, they sell almost everything here.  Friendly seller,just be extra careful before you purchase item,might be bit tricky when comes to price.  Make sure you browse around then later decide to purchase at the store you feel like one. Don't confused yourself. Bargaining is ok but once the seller offer right price just grab & go.  Nice spot touristic venue obviously, there are not that bad anyways. Highly recommended and place not to be miss out to visit.  Overall I'm happy to be here, you will experience the seller nagging you but yet interesting full of joy and colourful viatnamese culture.

Een overvolle markt met souvenirs, voeding (ook souvenirs). Wil je namaak T-shirts, sweaters, handtassen etc kopen heb je heel wat keuze. Als je stilstaat aan een krampje lopen ze als hazen om maar een maat of kleur te vinden. Ze houden je “vast”. We wilden iets kopen, hadden we het maar niet gedaan! maar voor € 15 hadden we 3 T-shirts namaak van een merk :-( Het is een mooie omgeving en mooi gebouw.
A fascinating walk through Saigon's market culture and merits a visit. Not too much we wanted to buy but the experience was worth the effort.

We visited Ben Thanh Market which is a large covered area where you can buy almost anything at a knocked down price. Probably worth bartering for any purchase, I dont think the rolex watches are swiss made!!!! Worth spending some time there just to take in the atmosphere and the range of goods for sale. Being typical Brits we didn't try the street food for sale, but there were plenty stalls to choose from and a lot of people enjoying what was on offer. we spend less than an hour there which i think was more than enough unless you are actually looking for something to buy.
great food market. just walk round. pick what you want. go the the seats at the front and wait for your food. stall selling draught beer also.

Do not buy anything without bargaining. The first price is 3-4 timr higher, so if they ask for 250K you can go down up to 50-70K. There is a shop with FIX prices - it is not possible to go down with prices there

Marché local et attrape touriste, il y a tout de même des articles vestimentaires de très bonne qualité ( quasi identique à l’original) mais il ne faut pas se laisser impressionner par l’agressivité des vendeurs ( y’a tout des mêmes des vendeurs sympa) il faut se laisser prendre au jeu et rentrer dans la négociation et aller au bout du prix que nous souhaitons, en faisant semblant de partir et dire que c’est trop cher la combine marche ... Bon shopping
Duże targowisko w starym budynku. Jest tu wszystko można także skosztować kuchni wietnamskiej. Przy zakupach jak wszędzie należy siętargować.

There were sections that posted they did not bargain. We found that we could bargain to about 50%. There was one shop who quoted really high and we didn’t bother even though she “loved Canada”, whispered her “special price” in my ear and gave us a special “first sale of the day” price.

Nice for a short walk about nothing particularly unusual or unique lots of copied merchandise souvenirs etc,main market closes at 7pm the two surrounding streets open up until midnight prepare to barter all part of the fun

Huge market. Be prepared to be pestered a lot. The guide books say the prices are fixed but they’re not, be prepared to barter and barter again, particularly in the clothing section. Never seen so many clothes. We came back the following day to buy more.
Marché très touristique donc les prix vont avec.... Très bon endroit pour faire les cadeaux.... On peut aussi y manger.

Denna marknad är hektisk och alla drar och slitet i en. Priserna startar väldigt högt men går oftast att få ner till halva priset. Gillar du inte att pruta är nog denna marknad inget för dig. Besöker du en sidogata från marknaden kan du få samma vara för 1/4 av priset
In tegenstelling van wat er op Google stond is deze markt tot 18.00 open ipv. Tot 01.00u. Lekker drukken opdringerig maar wel de moeite waard, zeker voor de minder ervaren reizigers.

The first market we visited while in Vietnam. Such a great place! The people inside are wonderful, the hustle bustle. Everyone is friendly and ready to bargain (except for vendors that state that they have fixed prices). The clothes, the shoes, the souvenirs, the food! It's all here. It's not just a market for tourists, locals come here as well to buy materials and food as well. It's a really great way to submerge yourself into the atmosphere and culture of Vietnam. Great place to visit when in Ho Chi Minh.

Bel mercato coperto dove si vende ogni genere di merce, dagli alimenti, al vestiario, dalle calzature alle borse e valige, dagli utensili da cucina ai cosmetici e prodotti da bagno, dai cappelli alle pentole. Ci sono molti banchi di Street food se si vul mangiare, un paio di farmacie e un ambulatorio medico. Naturalmente carne, pesce, uova, frutta e verdure fresche a volontà. Una delizia per gli occhi l'insieme di forme e colori. Non mancate di assaporare il profumo dei vari caffè e the vietnamiti.

Forget about the fakes and the hassle from the stall owners (who leave you alone quickly enough) Ben Thanh Market is the most famous among all markets in Vietnam, and one of the earliest surviving structures of the city, this architectural masterpiece is where the sight-seeing experience beings and is a must for a first timers in Saigon. A large rectangular structure that boasts 13,000 square meters also serves as a historical site, a landmark, a rendezvous point and a background for beautiful photo-ops. The food - all of it - is delicious
A very nice place with a Lot of food! Thought it would be bigger. In evening its livemusic and a Lot of People. Good place to sit and they will serve you at The table. Very nice food market.

Food market is nearby to the actual market. Great food options, not too big, not too small. The actual market was quite quiet which I think encouraged the market sellers to keep harassing people to buy their stuff which was annoying and made us leave sooner. If the food market was closer we would have visited it more probably.
Hated bro g grabbed and dragged towards every stall. Not impressed with prices & how much traders reduce to keep a sale. Much preferred the set price section of the market.

A must visit icon in Saigon, famous for it's food and crafts...however....the vendors can be pushy and I do not like that. Take extra precaution with your belongings especially if your travelling with kids.
Expansive, bustling indoor market with numerous vendor stalls, we purchased clothing and leather goods after negotiating the price.

Came to this market after reading about it online and taking all the advice from everyone that it’s a must visit. It is a must visit and I would definitely recommend having a look around. The aisles are tight and the traders are pushy but it’s good fun
유명해서 가보긴 했지만 비싸게 불렀다...지나다니는 행상들한테 7천원에 산 카드지갑을 벤탄내에서는 일단 백만동...5만원을 불렀다...
חובה ביקור ביום ובלילה. שוק ססגוני, מלא דוכנים, תיקים, בגדים, תבלינים, מזכרות וכל טוב. חוויה לא רגילה. מומלץ בחום

Si le marché est agréable et on peut y trouver de tout, il faut pouvoir fermement négocier car le prix de départ est très fortement surestimé. L’ambiance est cependant sympathique et les négociations se font ds la bonne humeur.

Many of the vendors on the outside ring of this market and fixed price, we found it helpful to check some of the prices in these shops before venturing into the interior of the market where you barter for pricing. Also found shop workers were easier to deal with early in the day, they got more forceful as the day went on. Usually look to pay at least 1/3rd of original asking price. With the right frame of mind this busy market can actually be a fun and interesting experience.

For rainy day and in general it’s highly recommend! You can either buy the regular souvenirs or enjoy the local food but you must look for the sports shoes area !!! They are in good quality, cheap and a very good variety! Adults shoes should cost between 500,000-700,000 Dong ( 20$-30$)

Perfect place to get some souvenirs and gifts! Around the outside bit of the market were fixed prices but in the main bit of the market in the middle you can get the prices down as the prices we found were higher in that part. There’s everything there from food to shoes to clothes to handmade gifts and cutlery etc. We didn’t eat there as we saw a lot of rats running around where the food was being cooked but for something to do in the day and a bit of shopping it’s great.
A market in the true sense, lots to see and buy. Some bargains to be had. BUT some they will try and short change you or even give you a cheaper product than the one you’ve paid for
ドンコイから歩いて15分弱。入口がいろいろあって、北側は食品系、南側は衣料、鞄、お土産。日本語での引き留めが凄いので、いちいち聞いてると時間がかかります。興味のないものはさっさとスルーしてください。ブランドのコピー商品が気になりますが、持ち帰りはバレると没収ですよ。

Variety of souvenir shop and food shop, but we need to be very careful when buy some souvenirs bcz they triple up the prices and we need to bargain as low as we can. Food shop is non halal, we only had some drinks only bcz its seems like not clean! So we dont prefer to have food there!

Un marché plutôt vide a cause du coronavirus, c’est une sensation étrange. Mais le marché est néanmoins toujours très vivant. Mais si vous voulez acheter des choses il va falloir aller dans l’autre marché moins touristique avec des prix 3 fois moins cher.

This is a great place to shop for those take home souvenirs or just browse around the varied products for sale. There is a wet end to the Market where you can find all sorts of freshly butchered meats too and other stalls selling local fruit and local coffee and snacks too. Expect to haggle for the best prices as the starting price is always way above what you can really buy what's caught your eye for. At night the street beside the Market becomes another market with food, clothing and jewelry galore! Exploring here is always a highlight and it is close to a lovely park and plenty of Hotels.

This place is a must as a visit but don’t shop alone and watch the contents of your bag. An utter rabbit warren of stalls selling every thing under the sun, thin corridors which can be quite disorientating when trying to find a stall again manned by hard selling women and more reasonable men.  There are a few things to remember when shopping here the most important being buyer beware and barter hard and be especially careful dealing with the woman shall holders as they are pretty ruthless.  We’ve shopped for clothes and bags etc in various markets throughout Vietnam and think this is a great market shopping experience but be absolutely clear on what you are looking for, don’t go to just browse and know how much you want to pay before you go shopping.  Stalls all operate in $ and dong and can quote you prices in any equivalent currency but whatever it is you shop for the first price you hear is over 50% to 60% over what you should end up at. Always be prepared to walk away, never be pressured into buying anything.  The women stall holders are the worst! They will go as far as trying to block you onto their stall to get you to buy as well as holding onto your wrist. If this happens firmly tell them to “take your hands off me” and walk away as whatever it is they are selling isn’t worth it and never loose your temper.  We found dealing with male stall holders to be far less aggressive and they gave a fairer price to start off with.  If unsure have a dry run to establish price range for whatever it is and always shop in pairs with one playing very hard to get. Never try and shop for two people at the same time you will find they give a price for all items where they price all items the same for clothes and you find they are adding lower quality items to improve the average price for the stall holder. Watch the price that appears on the calculator produced and passed to you and watch out for the phrase “how much do you want to pay” thinking very carefully before you answer.  Watch your bags and purses and always walk away ignoring the screams of madam sir madam sir madam sir as they pursue you through the market to get a sale if you are unhappy with the price or the product and enjoy the experience.
This is a place for shopping local products but prices are extremely expensive since mainly tourist visit. Need remember to bargain
A large variety of dishes available at reasonable prices, with good seating provided and music to provide a good ambience.
As the title suggests, this place has a great selection of everything. Definitely one of the more pushy/grabby markets around SE Asia but nothing you can’t get used to.

If you are looking for a market to buy souvenirs, cheap clothes, spices and all the special Vietnam food this is the place for you. It's full of small local stands , you can bargain on the price , we succeed to reduced the price by more than 50% , if you continue to walk around there is a turn to a flower market which is nice as well.
They have everything in here. But the price might be a little bit high. All in all, i love this place.
Super miejsce market, czuc klimat warto zobaczyc dobrze spedzony czas . Street food, owoce, pamiatki.

Mercato nel cuore di Saigon, sì possono trovare merci e prodotti di ogni genere. Molto affollato e caotico, i venditori sono troppo assillanti. Ogni tanto Si possono comunque fare dei buoni affari, anche se tendenzialmente è un po' caro rispetto ad altri posti. Consigliata la parte del mercato dove si pranza: piatti tipici e molto buoni.

As long as you bargain in a fun-spirited manner, you'll have a great time. Although some sellers can be rude and wrongfully touch you, the majority are willing to cater to whatever you want. I recommend staying far away from both the food and wet market however, we saw 3 rats and it smells terrible.

Helaas gingen we op het Vietnamese Nieuw Jaar naar deze markt en hoewel er veel stalletjes open waren, werden we toch door beveiligingsbeambten letterlijk weggejaagd. Was ons niet geheel duidelijk waarom. Maar de marktkooplieden, die open waren, boden de mannelijke bezoekers spontaan krukjes aan om hun wederhelft ongestoord rond te kunnen laten kijken.

Overall great place for shopping and eating local street food. Most people come here for the shopping since all vendors have a huge variety of items to bring home as souvenirs, or gifts for friends. Here you can barter with sellers and work out a price for an item so definitely it’s fun and something different rather than paying a firm price.

We love to walk through this market a few times when we are here. It’s very busy and some petty theft like phone grabbers but if you keep you stuff in your front pocket or bag in front you’ll have no problem. Lots of knock off goods and NEVER pay the asking price. The food side is interesting although we’ve never eaten there. It’s a lot of the same stuff but a cute walk though is always fun
Tipical viatnamese Market. Crowded but it has a nice athmosphere. Lot of goods, people. We have just walked through, but it worth couple of minutes.
建物内に、肉屋や野菜や果物を売る店。お土産ものを売る店、Tシャツ屋さん、靴屋さんといったいろんな店がひしめき合う市場です。中を散策していると、現地の方に、カバンは前に！と注意されました。スリに合わないようにとのこと。雑多な感じが面白いです。

I found the inside to be enjoyable and bustling and many vendors desperately trying to get you to buy their wares. There is a wide variety of everything to choose from: anything from T-shirt’s and belts to coffee and food. Prices would start high and drop slowly to around 30% of originally quoted prices after haggling.  The food areas are a bit smelly but overall the market is pretty good. If you like haggling and discussing, it’s great. If you prefer less haggling, then the area outside Nd opposition the market is a better option. Prices start generally lower and traders lower prices easily and I even got prices at around 20% of quoted prices. Saying this it was the last day before TET festival started, so they may have been in a hurry to get home. Overall, quite an interesting experience and I recommend it for those who love haggling. I even came away looking local...

This is a good, large and clean market in the heart of District 1. Everything that you need for gifts or souvenirs can be found here, and if you are like me, then negotiating is all part of the experience as everything is overpriced, so ask to purchase at one-third of the advertised price.  Although I made two visits here, both were relatively short, and I would have appreciated more time here. At the end of the trip, you are going to be reminded of the interaction you had with the seller every time you look at your souvenir, so make it a positive experience for both sides.
A faire pour le cote fruits et legumes, pour les coloris et l’atmosphère. Beaucoup trop de marchands d’articles contrefaits. A perdu de son charme en quelques annees

Tanta confusione , la gente del posto che ti tira di qua e di là per vendere la propria merce. Prodotti locali e contraffatti.. contrattare su tutto.. La sanno lunga i commercianti. Meravigliose le bancarelle con la frutta e la verdura. Un tripudio di colori ed odori. Tappa obbligata ..

Centrally located so you can't miss it. Pop in buy some authentic sportswear...then follow the delicious smell and eat all the street you can till your heart's content. Full of 'street food' stalls that actively complete for your business. A must if you find yourself in Ho Chi Minh City.
호치민을 대표하는 시장이다. 다른 시장과 크게 다를 것 없는 시장으로 볼게 많다고 할 수 는 없지만 그래도 호치민에 오면 한 번 즈음은 구경할만한 시장이다.
ベンタイン市場はほぼ日本語が片言で通じます。 結構強引な押し売りをされますが、強引な押し売りを市場？政府がセーブさせるように働いてるみたいです。 お土産、食事、雑貨と何でもあり ぼったくられるつもりから値切ればそれなりに楽しめますよ。夜はナイトマーケットになり楽しい場所です。

Great place to eat. Cheap, authentic food with so much choice. Just ensure you have some smaller notes to pay with as I think I was short changed one night but didn’t notice until later on. All tables are sharing tables so don’t be afraid so sit down near someone.

Amazing replica for relatively insane prices BUT some serious smells and out there produce in the meat section and the stall holders are pretty pushy. Have a good breakfast get your big girl pants on and you’ll come out with a beautiful product for a price you’re happy with and having met a really nice stall owner- just pick carefully. Quite the experience!

This French styled and build is universally know by everyone who stay in Ho Chi Minh City aka Saigon. The market is a destination by itself and where you can find everything from fruit to neat or fish to clothings and household items. The street around the market also offer many things to locals and tourists alike. There are 5 or 6 currency bureaux where you can change your money into the vietnamese $dong. You don't need to show your passport here and the rate is slightly better than in banks.
But less pressure from vendors. If it’s been made you can probably get it in this market. From a plastic chopstick to a 3 piece suit. Fun to wander but nothing we “ needed”
Smile and enjoy the experience, it's lots of fun! If you want a view of the general prices, then look around the fixed price stalls before going into the stalls where you need to haggle.

En bra marknad med allt mellan himmel och jord. Vi föredrog kvällsmarknaden då denna var ute under bar himmel. Man slapp den dåliga luften som fanns där inne.  Här ska du inte vara nådig när du prutar! Försäljarna är duktiga på att spela att de blir ruinerade, men går ni några stånd längre bort kan ni mycket väl hitta samma vara till lägre fast pris. Man tjänar på att gå runt lite och jämföra priser innan man slår till. Vi kom därifrån med ganska mycket. Även om man vet att märkena inte är äkta så är det ändå helt ok kvalité med tanke på vad man får betala.  Gå dit med öppet sinne, fynda och ha roligt! Glöm inte att äta den fantastiskt goda maten från restaurangerna som finns på marknaden.
Fun and pleasurable. Great bargain. So much to select. If you have time to spare, then you'll want to browse to the different stalls and start the bargain game.
Good place to go and buy something for your family. However. Be aware some thief there and your pocket will be gone .

This was a shopping paradise You need to bargain for cheaper price They are friendly people They will help you in all way to make sure you satisfied Just need to be careful on pick pocket HALAL restaurants is easy to find around here You need to book for Hotel earlier
Too expensive. If you want to buy something here, you have to dare to bid on items at least half the price offered. Because in other traditional markets, it is much cheaper.

It's been 10 years since I was here and if anything the locals are even friendlier. Yes they are very aggressive and we'll hawk you, but if you don't like that then don't go there. It's all part of the experience and as long as you are friendly and fair you will get great prices. Also good food and snacks to be had around the area.
You do get good gear, at keen prices. You might get fed up with the stall holders touching and cajoling you to buy after an hour or so. !
