Great market if you can get things for the correct price. At least halve the price they are asking. Even then it’s high. One bag started off at $75 I walked away 3 times eventually she offered $25. You really need to know what is worth before you buy. If not you will be paying more no matter how much lower you can get it for. Make make sure the bargaining process is fun. I made them laugh and you seem to get more from them😂. Great market but stock is repetitive.
This is the place to shop for local products but prices are expensive as mainly tourists are visiting. Remember to bargain to get a better deal.
大変活気があり、生鮮食料品、お土産品、衣料品と沢山のお店が入っています。 観光客が多く、ホッとします。 トイレが有料でした。4000vndでした。

The usual market where u can pick up cheap things. Temperature gets a bit high due to number of people and whether it is hot outside. Lots of Foreigners, locals not so many apart from the vendors! Bargain, bargain, bargain! Shops and restaurants bordering the market are also interesting. Less hectic at these shops. Some good lacquerware shops.

Very alive, colorful, seductive, and smelly place. I got coconut soap and we bought snacks for the next day train ride. Missed the seafood in the wet market because we got here mid afternoon. Someone one always greeting us and drawing our attention to their wares. I’d recommend.

Typical Asian copy market. Very good quality copy bags. About $35 aud. The night market we found had a better vibe and the stall holders more keen to bargain than during the day. Although there is traffic whizzing past you which is unnerving. Do yourself a favour and find the L’usine cafe close by and enjoy the devine food and coffee. The whole market could be done in 1-2 hours if you are a dedicated shopper. Bought a nice white gold necklace in a jewellery shop on the edge of the market too. Exchange your money in jewellery shops too - you get the best rate.

First time here! It was a great place for browsing and buying woman clothing. I purchased 5 gauze pants of assorted colors to bring home for myself and for gifts. Many vendors serving delicious Vietnamese meals’ A wonderful experience! A place you must visit. :):)

Some people will be rude or won't smile/make eye contact and of course some will be over the top. But great deals. The night market (outside after hours) is also good. Mixed variety but obviously less vendors.

It was a very interesting experience. People are nice and friendly. One needs to bargain..even after paying 25% of the quoted price you get a feeling you got cheated. Fun place. good for knick knacks.

It is really touristic now, but there are still s few good kiosk to find. Nice streetfood in here. There is also a money exchange point in here with best good rate comparinb to banks and other money exchange places. I usually do my money change here if I am in HCM

So ein Markt ist Geschmacksache. Es war unser erster Tag in Vietnam und auch etwas ein Kulturschock. Man muss sich auf enge Abhänge, laute Geräusche und gewöhnungsbedürftige Gerüche einstellen. Aber man kann gut in den einzelnen Ständen nach Souvenirs suchen und um den Preis feilschen. Das hat mir besonders viel Spaß gemacht. Es lohnt sich auf jeden Fall.

Il y a de tout à vendre mais il faut negocier surtout! A part sur les côtés dans le centre, les prix sont affichés. Donc plus facile pour nous les touristes. Nous avons voulu acheter des cadeaux pour la famille et les amis. C est simple et pas compliqué mais il fait chaud à l intérieur. C est typiquement un marché vietnamien très populaire. Il fait faire attention à vos affaires personnelles. Je vous conseille de le faire le matin ou bien le soir car il y a un marché le soir à l extérieur du marché. C est très animé. Vous pouvez échanger de l argent dans une boutique qui vend de l or à l extérieur du marché. C est sur le côté Ouest lorsque vous êtes du côté du rond point.

The largest and one of the rare surviving buildings of old Saigon (pre American war era). Nice to walk around, drink some coconut juice, have a coffee and/or buy some souvenirs. Just be careful. You will be hustled and bothered by vendors. Keep your calm.  P.S. Incidentally, I bought a pair of sneakers at a higher price that I would have found at COSTCO in the States. Well, c'est la vie!

Its a market that needs your close attention . You need to go through it lane by lane to get max out of it. .Shops are close to each other so be mindful of who the owners are. Bargining is a must, if you want to save your Dongs 9vietnamese currency) . I bought coconut shell bowls. Colourful and good souvenir to keep or give to friends for around 40,000 Dongs. If you have a nice picture of a dress or you want to duplicate one with different design , get one made. Generally 350,000 Dong to get it stiched from your purchased material . they make in 48 hours on level one of the market. Have fun and don't forget your fresh coconut juice in the outer part of the market to keep hydrated !
Binh than market is a really nice place to find pretty much everything. Really good price, good people and everything.
Semblables à d'autres marchés d'Asie (Thaïlande Cambodge) des vrais ds faux des souvenirs, du marchandage. Paiement par CB. Beaucoup de monde on peut se la jouer "riche" pour peu!

Trust me, you can haggle here and if you do and are persistent you will get a very good deal, if not you will be ripped off. Be aware most people in the market are rude and will touch you so if that's not your thing then don't visit. If not then go in and enjoy!
If you like a market you will love this one. It's busy and full of all different things to see and buy. Bartering is a must and clothes, shoes and watches seemed pretty decent quality.

Gar nicht schlecht um einen Überblick über die Geschäftspolitik von Asiaten zu erlangen. Allerdings nicht das echte asiatische Leben, sondern nur für Touristen. Der Nachtmarkt am Abend ist ebenfalls recht nett für Touristen.
Hotel was a 5-min walk to the market.  Prices quoted are sky-rocketed. Know you estimate price and be ready to bargain.

This market is nice to look look and see see to experience the atmosphere of the Vietnam style market. But the shops over there ask for very high price for their goods. It can be say almost 50% expensive compare to the fixed price shops that located outside the market that facing to the road. So I would recommend to visit to the fixed price shops first and then go into the market, so you will roughly know the price and can bargain with the sellers inside the market if you have time. Otherwise, just get anything yo want at the fixed price shops there, the thing at the fixed price shops almost the same compare to those shops inside the market.
The cleanest markets we have ever been in, huge selection and worth a visit, perhaps better in the evening when a little cooler.

Marknadsplats med massor av stånd. Försäljarna är väldigt på så fort du närmar dig och drar dig gärna i armen för uppmärksamhet. Är man beredd på detta så blir det en rolig dag. Pruta, skoja och le, då blir det härliga och roliga samtal med säljarna. Testa gärna Pho inne på marknaden, den var riktigt bra. Blir dock riktigt riktigt varmt i marknadshallen.
Many stalls selling lots of stuffs. You must have the necessary skills to negotiate your price downwards...

Let’s be frank - if you are looking for watch or sunglasses you will waste your money on crap. If you are looking for textiles - you will fond some cashmeere scarfs you wanna bring home for your relatives and friends. Bargain - you may reduce the price by at least 70%.
Dans ce marché il y a tous ce que vous voudrez acheter que ce sois un souvenir du textile de la maroquinerie une montre etc tout y est

Alot of purchases deem worthy to buy has to go to how you negotiate and bargain with the stalls. Some of them do have no bargaining cards though so do keep a look out. There is a nearby saigon square that also sells similar items so do compare wisely before purchasing

This is one of the bigger markets in Vietnam. 'Everything under one roof' experience. It is crowded and some of the aisles are a very tight fit. Once you show interest in an item by taking it to your hand or asking the price, the vendors will not let you go. They try their best to make a sale. They start high at 'tourist prices so one should not be afraid to quote a reasonable price. Therefore it's best to be aware of prices beforehand. The ladies seemed to be more aggressive in the manner they deal with customers than the men. Overall it was a good experience browsing through the wide variety of goods.

Went to the market to have a look around and was shocked by how huge it is. Stalk after stall, row upon row. Every person wants you to buy something from them so prepare to be shouted at and stood in front of, anything to get your attention. Lots of the stall sell the same stuff so definitely worth haggling. The range of fruit, vegetables, coffee and confectionary is unreal.

Be prepared to have storekeepers call out to you to visit their stores. The market is much neater than expected and is good for trying/buying local food. However, for other shopping, Saigon Square 1 was a more pleasant experience.

Et marked der er et absolut must, når man er i Saigon. Her kan med, stor fordel handles og prutte om prisen. 0 Mulighed for et let måltid, og en forfriskning. Vil man have syet tøj, er det også her man bør få det gjort.
Tips att äta mat på foodcourten som ligger i anslutning till marknaden. Var där kvällstid. Billig, bra mat och allt du kan tänka dig att äta. Perfekt när man reser med småbarn.

Amazing market - undercover with a range of similar products at different prices Be prepared to bargain as prices can drop radically (40 -50 percent) as you walk away from vendor Many good deals from shirts to trinkets and especially gifts for friends and family A must do experience

This place is pretty insane with what all is crammed in there. Food, merchandise, snacks, tourist stuff, clothing, etc. all in one place. I believe most if not all of the brand name stuff is fake so beware if they tell you it's factor seconds. They're fake. Even the good looking stuff...fake. Bargain with the merchants!! But a cool place to see if you're in that area. Be aware of petty theft like pick-pockets as you should be wherever you travel. Bucket list location in Ho Chi Minh city for sure.

"Big! There's everything on sale from clothes, watches and cameras to live fish and chickens. TIP: mark where you came in as soon every aisle looks the same. Some basic refreshments but good, modern WCs (pay). Good disabled access.

Ben thanh is a good place for shopping for cheap imitation good, local food and beer with price comparable to restaurants at back packers area. However discovered a food street nearby with very good local food and BBQ. The pork belly was delicious. Beer average priced. Open till midnight. Good place for a hearty meal and few bottles of beers. Address on photo attached

Bargains galore, take their opening prices and make half and go from their. Any nasty merchants walk by and don’t stop. Food is also there, but make sure you get what you order. We did not and then they acted like we were wrong. Lots of good prices and many items to choose from. Have fun, takes a few hours. Best way to get around this city is by grab taxi. Easy and cheap.
観光客向けの市場で値段が高いです。探せばお買い得品もあり、値切ることも楽しい経験になるでしょう。食べ物屋は比較的安くなってます。

Wow, the night market was an explosion of colour and culture. Pros: good variety of items to buy, easy access to local, western and street food, and heaps of atmosphere. Cons: very aggressive vendors, mopeds drive up and down the lanes and sidewalks with little regard for the padestrians. Tips: get there as it opens and get first customer of the night discount offers. Offer half of what their first offer was I was told a shot glass was 100 000 Dong, ended up getting it for 50 000, found it in a souvenir shop for 35 000 ;-)

You can buy almost everything here from sports wear, bags, souvenirs, nuts, fruits and cooked food. There’s also a fresh food market to check out what the locals eat!  Vendors can be rude when you bargain so just focus on the deals you want and ignore the personalities. :).

Il mercato Bến Thành di Ho Chi Minh City è uno di quei posti divertenti dove spendere un po’ di tempo. Si tratta di un grande mercato nel centro della città, simbolo della città stessa. Qui si possono acquistare dei souvenir da portare come ricordo a casa. Consiglio di farci una vista, magari spendendoci un paio di ore.

Marché assez sympa et très grand où on vous propose de tout Vêtements - peluches - sacs - baskets.......beaucoup de produits contrefaits évidemment Par contre les commerçants attaquent à des prix qu’il faut facilement diviser par 2 ! Après ça va ils sont assez sympas et normalement tout le monde est content à la fin de la transaction
정말 오래된 시장이고 에어컨도 없어 덥고 비좁고 하지만 구경하기는 재미가 쏠쏠합니다. 가격을 깎을 자신이 없으면 섣불리 사지마세요!

A good place to get whatever you want but be prepared to bargain!  Overall, most vendors are friendly and are even willing to point you to the stall that has what you want.  A bit stuffy at some places, but, it's a market!
胡志明市比较大的传统市场，什么都有卖，但因为是传统市场，所以比较闷热拥挤，夏天来要有心里准备，可以来买坚果类食品，以及咖啡豆，不过咖啡豆最好货比三家，算是不错的选择。

Nice market Good to buy souvenirs and small gifts and utilities like clothes,t shirts,pants ,track suits,bags,coasters and coffee etc Unless you like to bargain silly,it is best to buy from the outer most corridor shops being run by government Their sales personnel wear same dress and prices are fixed These assure a good price and quality both In-between when you are tired you can also move inside the market to have tasty food Good experience

Perus ulkomaantori-meininki. Sisällä melko tunkkainen ilma, iltaisin klo.19 eteenpäin ulkona mukavampi kuljeskella. Yhdellä kadulla parempaa tavaraa/vaatetta. Myyjät ottaa helposti marttyyri-asenteen kun tinkaa.
It is well worth a visit just to see local life and all the different things on offer. Probably not the cheapest place to buy things, but definately worth a look
A nice place to shop at. Bargaining is a must for good rates. Stuff is fine. But the shopkeepers are rude.

Sure worth visiting, much stuff to buy if all kinds of brands. Shirts, bags, trousers sunglasses, all. Food, too. But be aware the sellers can be quite pushing. Don't forget to bargain, half price or less easy to get. Crossing the large main road, there is another market, with more "high class" brand bags and stuff.
濱城市場內有很多店舖，很多貨物也可買到，琳琅滿目，但是買東西時，一定要講價，也有很多冒牌商品，但總括來說是美好的。

It's a typical medium sized walkable indoor market, with some stalls-cum-diners selling food and drinks, as well as the usual merchandise for taking pics or to buy. It's a relatively pleasant, and clean experience, with only a few odd hustlers. We had food and drinks there which were actually quite nice.

We walked to this place for some cheap stuff. Unfortunately many stalls started to close even though it was still about 7pm. During this hour, some stalls already chasing out tourists and some staff manning the stalls looked impatient when dealing with tourists who started to bargain for better price.
買い物は観光客だと、高い値段をふっかけてきますので、ドンコイ通りやビンタイン市場の方が良いかも、チェーの飲んで、休憩する場所に。
메인 복도말고 바깥쪽에는 이런 현지 식재료들을 많이 팔고 있으니 참고하시기 바랍니다 베트남음식에 드러가는 모든 식재료는 거의 다 있는거 같습니다

Ben Thanh Market is the famous one in HCMC but is becoming expensive as more and more tourists visit each year. I ended up buying two shirts after bargaining hard . There are many small food stalls where we enjoyed a meal. Prices were from about 40,000 dong and up. Beer was cheap here too. If you are looking for clothes it is better to go to Saigon Square which is about 400 m away.

Good place to see the wide variety of local produce and souvenirs. But could be rather stressful unless you enjoy bargaining. There are some government run stores at the perimeter where prices are fixed. Staff wear blue uniform and can speak English. We found T-shirts there that are cheaper by 20,000 to 30,000 dong than those which we had earlier bought from a night market vendor after some hard bargaining.

Dieser Markt ist auf jeden Fall einen Besuch wert. Wenn man gut nachgemachte Dinge gut findet und handeln kann, ist das hier ein Paradies!! Wir haben die anfänglichen Preise durch drei geteilt und oft (aber auch nicht bei jedem Laden) den Preis raushandeln können! Also wer einen guten Preis haben will, sollte gut handeln können und geduldig sein... Qualität der Waren ist dann auch nicht allzu schlecht.

A great place for bargains. The stands on the perimeter with the staff in uniform are government run, you cannot bargain with them. Only can bargain with the private vendors. Start at 60% of the asking price.

What ever you do ,do not agree on the first price that they throw at you. Bargain for the best price and look around for the other similar product. There are also some shops do have credit cards services.

Do not be afraid to bargain, they expect it. Also do not go here if you value your personal space or do not like being touched. Don’t be fooled, the special price they have for you is the same as they have for the next person and not everyone can be their first customer! Have fun!!

Great place to eat during the day ( inside ) and evening ( outside ). Clothes can typically be bought for 30 to 40% if asking price. Do check the sizes as they tend to be a little smaller than UK. If you’re keen on making some wonderful Vietnamese food when you get home, you can pick up stuff like their amazing cinnamon and black cardamom too

Le marché est plaisant. Plusieurs étales de fruits/légumes, herbes, souvenirs. Dommage que tous affichent un écriteau "fixed price" qui met un terme à toute possibilité de négocier. Les prix sont chers.
This was my first meal when I first came in 2013 and still my go-to spot for a delicious and cheap Banh Beo Hue fix. It can get crowded with both locals and tourists so be patient!
Ben Than is a Saigon institution Hundreds of stalls Friendly people Not overly aggressive But you must haggle very hard or you will be ripped off

What ever price the seller starts with make sure to counter that offer by HALF ..... this market is all about bargain since a lot of tourists go the prices are extremely high. I found souvenirs cheaper at the airport than in the market  Make sure to bargain here

A must- visit- place in HCM. Just keep an open mind and have a pleasant disposition when checking out this market. The salesladies could be all over you, but of course they just want to sell their stuff. Most goods have a fixed price.Got lots of textile which are so cheap but of good quality. Go to inner part of the market as sellers tend to be more patient in that area!

un monde incroyable avec les enfants ce n'est pas simple , les prix sont vraiment intéressants à condition de marchander. il faut faire aussi attention à la qualité et vérifier les tailles , car s'ils n'ont pas la votre ils changent les étiquettes ( c'est aussi quelque fois le cas sur les marchés en France). sachant cela on fait de très bonnes affaires. indépendamment du magasin le vietnam est un très beau pays, qui vaut le détour, les prix étant peu élevés cela compense l'achat du billet d'avion.

I keep on going back here at night because they have the best prices for local preserved and fresh tropical fruits. Aside from the good food court inside, there are a plethora of interesting items that are for sale inside the market. I bought a few souvenirs and a several practical hand made items made out of locally sourced materials. If you have the time, I highly suggest to take a few hours and go through the maze of this interesting market, you may find something unique to take home with you.

If you want a lot of things to look at this is the place, don’t buy stuff at the price they tell you it’s way too marked up walk around gets feel, barter hard for you money. After a hour you’ll get hungry go center of market get some good food. 👍😉🍽

Неплохой выбор: есть вьетнамская тайская, индийская кухня, барбекю на шпажках, смузи,стандартные рис, лапша с добавками, супы. Цены вполне умеренные: можно наесться за 40-100 тыс в зависимости от предпочтений.

Una tappa per noi obbligata in ogni città che tocchiamo viaggiando è la visita dei mercati in quanto riteniamo siano uno dei posti in cui è più facile vedere senza troppi artefatti pro turisti la vita del luogo ed incontrare gli abitanti intenti nelle quotiane attività. In questo mercato di Saigon si vende un po' di tutto ed i prezzi non sono fissi ma vanno mercanteggiati. Le vendite iniziano di primo mattino e continuano fino alle diciotto tutti i giorni della settimana, domenica compresa. Noi vi abbiamo fatto un purtroppo rapido giro prediligendo soprattutto la zona dedicata agli alimenti.
Just find a stall you are comfortable with and shop from a few shops. Most items are the same.. it's really getting a good price (bargain!) and getting the quality you want.

This place is definitely lively no matter the time of day as locals and tourists alike patronise the stalls for the many different types of goods that are being hawkered. Prices are definitely inflated as a result but still worth checking out.
市場は朝(午前中)に行くことを勧めます。朝は値引き交渉もしやすいく、暑さもピークになる前なので。 買いたい品物があれば写真を見せて交渉すると良いです。 ただ見るだけでも良いですが呼び止められてゆっくり見る事は難しいかもしれません。

Sehr typischer Markt mit vielen kleinen Ständen . Selbst wenn man nichts kaufen will, ist es ein Besuch wert. Achtung: die Stände aussen haben oft günstigere Festpreise. Je mehr man im Inneren ist, desto teurer wird es. Immer handeln - Ausländer zahlen sonst stets den "Touristenaufschlag".

Great local food stalls inside the main hall during the day serving good local dishes at market prices. £2 for a bowl of Pho which was really good. The main market is full of clothes and everything else you can think of. Bargain hard and there’s some good bargains to be had if that is your thing.  At night the inside market closes and the outside opens up. Good restaurant on the outside in the evening and a bit more relaxed than the frantic day

I am not sure about the order: Did the tourists' consistant bargaining make the local sellers jack up the price? Or the local sellers jacked up prices knowing that tourists will bargain?  There are plenty of nuts and coffee available which are cheaper than the airport. But you must not accept the shown prices. Many of the shops will show a high price but verbally offer a lower price as if they are giving a discount already. We saw a brand of coffee that could be found in supermarkets but are sold at 3 times the price here. We told the seller we will just buy it at the supermarket and we walked away. She came after us and kept lowering the price till it became one third of the original price she offered.  Do not settle at one shop. Walk around to ask for prices first.

It's a nice place for tourists because they sell all kinds of local stuff which you love to buy as souvenirs when you travel to Vietnam. They also serve good local food too. The only thing you need to notice is that you should bargain when buying stuff here.

Buka sampai malam disini. Jam 7 malam sudah pada tutup kios tapi ada night market di sampingnya, pasar tradisional sekalian menjadi tempat wisata. Kalo disini siap siap tawar jauh dibawah harga harus hilangkan hati gak enakan hahaha.
It's a lovely place with a range of products. Do bargain, the shopkeepers are themselves keen to bargain. Unless of course you are at a shop with a Fixed Price board.
Om op die markt te lopen is al een hele belevenis tussen al die kraampjes door te lopen die heel dicht op elkaar staan en wat ze allemaal verkopen je kan het gewoon niet op noemen zo veel
Besonders originell ist dieser Markt nicht; interessant allerdings sind die Lebensmittel- und Gastronomieflügel. Gutes Essen für eher wenig Geld.

Ce lieu est emblématique du quartier 1. Vous trouverez des souvenirs en tout genre (En laque 1er prix, en bois, etc), des tissus, des chaussures, des bijoux, il faut tout négocier. Il y a également des contre-façons, sans intérêt. Il y a des échoppes de fruits, de fruits secs, de café, thé, etc. On peut s'y restaurer. À faire.
You will find T-shirts, dresses, Electronics, Fast food and lots of other cheap things in this market. You got to have high negotiation skill to not to get fooled.

Très coloré ! super le matin pour les produits frais et les fleurs , les épices. Cela vous donne une idée de ce que vous pouvez acheter tout en sachant que si vous vous adressez aux vendeurs des allées centrales il faut négocier très très fort car les prix sont exorbitants ; pour vous en rendre compte il faut voir d'abord les prix avec les vendeurs officiels (en blouse bleue et longeant le marché de tous les côtés (là on ne marchande pas !)!
I stopped at a seafood stall and had some tasty razor clams, or the local equivalent. Decent value, 100k, but maybe I should have negotiated? If you like to buy stuff, you can probably find it there!

Filled with several food stalls and shops. Ideal place to pick up souvenirs and knick knacks pertaining to local sites/landmarks/culture. Be wary about the prices as the vendors at the market are ready to expect some heavy bargaining.

Definitely a goto area for all your shopping needs under one roof. Be prepared to negotiate for the best deal which usually starts at 75% of the asking price and moving towards 50% or at the amount you are comfortable with.
市民劇場から西に15分くらい歩いたところにあります。布・衣類、靴・サンダル、食べ物(乾物)、雑貨、なんでもあります。お土産もここで購入できます。とにかく市場が広いので、予め購入するものを決めてから行きましょう。同じ商品を複数のお店で扱っている場合が多いので、値段交渉はできます。先方の言い値の3分の2くらいを目処に電卓で数字を見せながら交渉しましょう。
I personally love visiting night market. This one was nice ! Cheap price and all, but there’s not a lot of shops so I am not sure if it’s under construction or something.

We came to the market a couple of times. It’s the usual tourist tat and fake designer stuff. We didn’t buy anything. The vendors are pushy and get on your nerves constantly hassling you to buy their goods( makes you unwilling to look too closely). There also a food market selling meat, vegetables, fish ,seafood etc which was interesting until I spied all the rats scuttling around! Wouldn’t rush back

A quick walk down the mall near the Bitexco Skydeck tower and a few streets (via google maps) and you come to these fantastic markets. Some say the marketers are aggressive, I think they are better described as keen business owners in a large market. Rather, they are attentive, keen, and smart. Be prepared to walk away to get the best offer. Their “best offer” is what they promise up front, but it NEVER is their best offer. US$5 to US$9 is the usual for most items.

Plenty to see and to buy..Be prepared to barter .Most stallholders quite good humoured about it. Enjoyed joining the locals for lunch.Plenty of variety of food to chose from.An experience not to be missed.

We reached the market in the early morning before it got crowded. It's a huge market with different sections for fresh produce, cooked food, dried food, souvenirs, bags, clothes and shoes. I understand that they also have a night market but we didn't have time to visit it.

What a great market. Rather large, so keep your witts about you as you might easily leave from the incorrect gate and get lost. Food stuff, clothing, shoes and lots of other stuff can be bought here at super low prices.
L'endroit a Ho chi minh pour trouver de tout: souvenirs, vêtements, décorations, ou même se restaurer. Par endroit beaucoup de rabatteuses

The market provides tons of selections from clothing to dried goods to coffee to street foods. As for clothes/shoe shopping, some owners though get annoyed when you just ask for the price without buying. Just be more patient with them but some are still accommodating. This is the best place to bargain I believe. Street foods are also pricier than normal. As for dried goods and coffee, the prices are almost the same as in the supermarkets. Overall, it's a good place to see variety of goods.

LE MARCHE a faire a saigon .Vous y trouvez de tout .Beaucoup de sollicitations des vendeurs .On est dans le marché touriste typique. Mais il y a aussi d exellents fournisseurs d epices qui vous donneront exactement ce que vous avez besoin pour réaliser le plat que vous leur indiquez vouloir réaliser.A faire si il n y en un seul a voir suivant votre timing .

This is an experience. You need to haggle. Quality excellent not a place for the faint hearted. Lots of stalls with people hassling you all the time but if you want souvenirs or to buy Vietnamese coffee this is the place to go. It gets very hot so visit in the morning

Fascinating market in the heart of Ho Chi Minh City. You should definitely check it out. Not sure the prices are the best. Be sure to haggle or you will easily pay double the going rates. I bought some lovely chopsticks at this market but found an almost identical set at the Phu Quoc airport for the same price - except the ones at the airport came in a nice box with a little stone to rest the chopsticks on.

It is a tourist shopping area in heart of hcmc ... local vietnamese garments and goods sovionours available but price are high many be 3 to 4 times more .... you can bargain here and get good prices for garments ...

I would say it is a market where it sells a lot of souvenirs , Vietnam coffee the most. There’s some restaurant to try too. The price is much more cheaper at Ben Thanh food street.  Reminder: You need to bargain for the things that you want to buy at the market.
One of the better markets. You can find good quality and very good prices. Lots of variety. Good restaurants. Bargain for what you want.

Good variety, remember to bargain bargain bargain. Always offer half of said cost, then bargain up, with a figure in mind. Never go over your figure. If store owner not happy with your offer, start walking away, store owner will quickly agree if they want your business. We were told a price of $1 million dong, went to another store and bargained and got items for $250 thousand dong. Same items.
Gostei muito da cultura. Das comidas. Das vendas! Das gentes! Muito comércio. Muita fruta! E a preços muito acessíveis.

This is a good place to buy souvenirs, fresh fruits and authentic vietnamese dishes.  You can bargain just about everything here.  At 7pm daily, this place turns into a night market where you can experience Street Food.  Definitely a must see if you’re touring HCMC.
ホーチミンの地元住民の方の生活用の市場と思いきや、完全に観光地化されていて、お土産目当てでくる観光客相手の場所でした。
It's a market be prepared to be harassed, it is a market and that's what they do. Watch out for some rats as well, but that's life
A good place to wander around and see local food, clothing, jewellery etc. Great for souvenir hunting too.
Como uno mas, aunque nuestra apariencia delata y nos atosigan un poco, se puede comer y tomar una cerveza por menos de 4€.

Another example of the local feeding frenzy. Many small businesses arranged in rows,offering a variety of local local:fish, soups,seafood,vegetables,cooked,steamed,grilled-you name it. Full of visitors of all ages,local and foreigns alike. At both ends-are sitting areas to enjoy the food and drinks.near the street side,a local band plays live music.

The market is very cramped, all the stalls very close to each other, on entring you are bombarded by the stall holders trying to sell you something, sorry we left very quickly, You could not look at anything, without the pressure to buy it.
A nice market with Vietnamese stuff i like Vietnamese market As it’s has its own style Bought some gift for My friends
During our trip to Vietnam last year we visited the markets, What an experience !!! Can purchase almost anything !! What a hoot, the hustle and bustle !
お土産を探しに立ち寄ります。いつも賑やかで楽しい市場です。コーヒーから雑貨、などいろいろあります。ただし、値段は多分微妙です。値ごろ感を調べておいて交渉しないと高い買い物になりそうです。
Beware of the greed and over-pricing of any shop. Check, compare and be cautious before making a purchase. Be daring to bargain with dirty discounts to avoid being scammed.

the market is very welcoming (sometimes too much). the prices are fixed only at the surroundings of the market - where you cannot bargain. still - the same things are offered for different prices as you get away from the entrance, so don't be seduced by the merchants at the entrance - be patient and get away. the inside stands sell less touristic souvenirs but you can (and should) bargain there to at least half the price. easy to get to from any point in the city

This market is one of the history in this city, and the must place to go when you come to Ho Chi Minh, but you need to deal with the seller if you dont want to pay a lot of money for your stuff. Everything is fine...

Dieser Markt bietet alles. Kleidung, Souvenirs, Essen, Spielzeug, Café, Tee, Schuhe, Sonnenbrillen usw. Es ist zwar sehr eng & etwas stickig aber ein Besuch eines Marktes darf absolut nicht fehlen. Dieser Markt ist der größte des Bezirkes und daher gibt es genug zu sehen! Zwar sind die Gerüche und die etwas aufdringlichen Verkäuferinnen etwas gewöhnungsbedürftig aber das ist typisch Asien & gehört somit einfach dazu! :)

After staying in Ho Chi Minh City for nearly a week and having bought almost zero souvenirs, I thought it time to head to Ben Thanh Markets to pick up something.  I went by foot and didn’t find it especially hard to find though was a little surprised by the relatively sedate exterior. That changed when I walked inside. I was attacked visually and verbally immediately from all angles. At first I responded to as many as I could but found that pointless. I then (rudely I know) pretended to not know English and ignored them.  I kept walking the aisles, some extremely narrow and found a place where the staff were busy so I stopped to look. Some good quality, some ok and some obvious cheap knockoffs.  I started to get me feet and purchased a couple of items. My haggling is terrible, so I was lucky if I got much of a discount but tried anyway.  After about an hour I headed to the food area. Once again, I must have look confused, as another hawker approached me to offer their menu. She was actually very helpful and I ended up with an awesome Chicken Pho and drink for about AUD2.50.  Revitalised, I returned to the shops and started to really have fun. I stopped at the places that either had something that looked different or their spruik was the best.  All in all, probably spent 3 hours there. Got bags of stuff and am certain I was ripped off completely. In saying that, I probably spent around AUD$100 in total and had a great time.  Well worth the visit, just hang in there and have fun.
ベトナム コーヒー、雑貨、コピー衣料品、食品、果物と何でもあれのマーケットですが言い値では買わない事です。ツーリスト価格で購入しない様、注意しましょう。屋台の生春巻などもチャレンジできます。

I love markets and could have spent an hour or more here. Unfortunately, I was there with two men whose idea of visiting a market is keep going right through and out the other side. If you stop, they say, people start to hassle you. Of course, I say, that's part of the fun! The stalls sell the usual tourist tat, also factory over-runs and samples of clothes, bags and shoes and dried foodstuffs. There are drinks stalls and street food, so park the men with a beer and go off and shop.
很多人都會來檳城市場附近的金飾店來換錢，我自己來的時候1美金是23160，可是換到第三個$100時，是23090，機場換錢一樣不需要手續費1美金是23000，這附近有很多的早市跟晚市，當地便宜品質的紀念品！也有一些吃的，但是晚上七點檳城市場內部就收拾了，只剩外面的夜市。

The Ben Thanh Market is huge, hugely busy and sells just about everything including live frogs (for eating). It is easy to get bargains but be prepared to be hassled, especially if you are carrying shopping. If you are squeamish, stay away from the food section. I loved it, my husband didn't (he doesn't like markets anywhere lol). It is well worth a visit.

Get a real feel for local market, not too touristy, I would think 80% of their business is locals, so see how they shop. Tight spaces but not uncomfortable, very little you want to buy but interesting to see what they sell.

This market has everything—food, coffee, souvenirs, clothes, and more. It’s a fun experience to walk around and take in all the sights and sounds, but be prepared for the crowds and the non-stop badgering by the stall owners. If you plan on buying anything, make sure you haggle otherwise you’ll most certainly be ripped off.

Tons of shopping, coffee, food. Pretty much every souvenir you would want to buy is here. It's a very high pressure sales market though. And you feel guilty if you don't buy something from them all. Certainly humbling. I was able to get 3 shirts and a few other trinkets for a great deal. the locals told me to haggle, I didn't really enjoy that though. Are you really going to try to bargain for a dollar savings? I only lasted about an hour. Very similar to Thailand markets.

Menginap di Grand Silverland Hotel, keluar hotel, belok kanan sdh sampai di ben Thanh market. Harga kaus di toko2 luar adalah fixed price dengan label harga jelas. Kalau di dalam, hrus nego, dan penjual buka dengan harga sangat mahal. Jadi lebih baik beli kaus di toko2 bagian luar yg sdh ada label harga. Ada Ban Phia, yaitu pia duren yg enak khas vietnam. Yg paling murah harganya VND 70 warna merah (tanpa telur asin). Yg warna biru muda (dg telur asin) harganya VND 90.

Allow half a day. Good market. Must know the reasonable price of an item how to bargain. We were quoted 150k VND for an item and We bought it for 15k VND. Within the market There are govt managed shops that sell at fixed price however the price still differs slightly from shop to shop.
We had lunch here, thoroughly recommend. All the locals seem to eat here too. Lots of cool stuff to buy if you're looking for souvenirs.

Leuk om je souvenirs in te slaan, ze hebben hier alles. Bereid je wel voor op het getrek aan je of je niet iets wil kopen: what are you looking for? You wan’t to buy something? Can I help you? Etc etc!
Very busy at night, fun for shopping and barter, I found best prices here in all Vietnam so if you like something here you can buy. Mainly cloths, shoes and bags.

Has everything here. Loved the food section. We had a mango smoothie everyday after our morning tour for about NZ$1.50. Be prepared to be hassled and you need to get good at bartering. Don’t pay more than 1/2 the initial price, often less especially once you walk away.
Leuke markt zowel op de dag als s avonds. Soms zijn ze wat te handtastelijk, maar hier kan je nog goed onderhandelen.
特に欲しいものがあるわけではありませんが、ぶらりと行ってきました。食堂で飲んだマンゴージュースは、美味しかったです。
If you like shopping. This is the place to go. It has everything. Shirts , pants, bags, chop sticks. All kind of souvenirs for everyone.
Put your bargaining powers to the test. Start with at least 50% discount or more! I found some great finds for home decor!

Busy market with lots of souvenirs, food and drink. Street food market connected but across the street was cooler, but this was also great! Having shopped in Hoi An and Ho Chi Minh, we actually bought a suitcase from the market to take additional bits home with us! 900,000 for a cabin case, rather than 4,000,000, which was the cheapest price we found in shops and department stores with sales on.

Noon the other day, I went to the market to buy things. Walk around for a while, for sightseeing stalls, then I finally stopped 1 stall. This stall sells some clothes that I'm quite satisfied with 😍, the owner doesn't look too aggressive like another stalls. I look at the owners of another stalls looking very fierce😰. But rest assured, there is always a guard team👮 inside a market, so it's rare for a fight to happen 😇.  I bought 3 shirts: The Starbuck & HardRock, I bargain with owner for a while, then reduced to: ✔️80k / 1 shirt (Initially shouted 150k / 1 shirt❌), Gucci: ✔️150k / 1 shirt (Initially shouted 250k❌). Because buying clothes in market can't as expensive as the shop + need to watch the fabric carefully (very thin fabric) + owner alway says this fabric is cool to wear (they just cheating you👎). It is recommended for you: 👉 You can deal with owner reduce 40% a price of a product , when I ready to leave, owner called me back immediately (because there are lots of stall in the market sell this shirts, easy to find another stall sell 😗 😏).  ⚠️ These are mainly to cheat tourist, foreigner, but not easy with local 😄  In addition, in the market there is selling food, wallet, backpack, other things are also cheap, if you know how to bid 😏. If you don't bid, you'll buy 100% expensive 👍.

This is a covered market and a touristy one. So you can go there on a hot or s raining day as well, but know well that have to bargain, and bargain more. What's interesting is that there are fixed price shops in the periphery, those are numbered and the sellers wear a uniform. I found it interesting because it's more organized and you don't need to haggle. So if you want souviners, go to those. If you want to explore more and are confident of your bargaining skills look up the vendors inside the main market. It's hard to say if everything there is made in Vietnam, I suspect a lot if it from China. The vendors do get pushy, so be firm and don't get offended! You can good quick food too and vegetarian options as well. So shop n have s nice avacado smoothie if you like!

Exactly what others had said....hustling, you get hassled lots, but can bargain on prices and get some great deals. If you go into the smaller aisles, just beware that some owners can be more pushy...you don't get chance to look at stuff without being immediately hassled. Also, we had one stall holder who got really upset and physically grabbed at us when we tried to walk away. We found a small street outside the market which had numerous sports stores (shoes, jackets, bags) that had better prices, no harassing, but less wiggle room on prices.

We planned to do our souvenir shopping for Vietnam here so expected the usual chaos and trinkets. A lot of the products are duplicated many times over so if you don’t get the price you want.. just ask next the next store over. It is hot and crowded so it was tiring for the kids pretty quickly. But we found most of our little trinkets and gifts and lanterns fairly quickly.
まさに東南アジアといった雰囲気。制服を着た店員のいるエリアがあり、そこの商品は全て値札が付いていて、強引な勧誘もされないので落ち着いて見て回れる。

We loved wandering this market which includes wet market, flowers, souvenirs, food vendors, leather goods. A great morning activity as it gets super stuffy and close. Friendly operators. Prices fair. Fun morning out.

כנראה השוק הכי מעניין בעיר עבור תיירים... שווה ביקור בהחלט .. בלילה הוא הופך לשוק שונה ודוכנים נפתחים גם בחוץ אז כדי ללכת פעם ביום ופעם אחרי החשיכה ...מוצרים מעניינים כיף לראות את המבחר .  הבעיה היא שברגע שאתה שם רגל בפנים כל המוכרים מתנפלים עלייך כמו להקה של זאבים... לא מפסיקים לקרוא לך ולפנות אלייך ואתה לא יכול לעמוד שניה בלי שיקראו לך לקנות משהו ... מי שלא בנוי לזה זה הולך להיות לו קשה

When you mention about Ben Thanh Market, people will automatically talk about the souvenir and fashion departmental store or stalls in the market or the night street market which it is popularly known for.  For me, besides the above, I also visit this market on another perspective, not into souvenir or fashion but it's fresh food market. In every of my visit to HCMC, I will surely comeby here on the last day to grab some fresh produces home!  Located towards the end (from the main entrance), there are a small area designated as a "wet market", where visitors can grab some of the local fresh produces, like seafood, meat, fruits and vegetables and even fresh flowers. It's good to be here to indulge into the lifestyle of the local people. Although most stall owners do not speak English, we could still communicate with them by pointing and using calculators! LOL. Don't worry, they are generally friendly and prices here are much cheaper than getting similar items from the convenience stores nearby or at the supermarkets.  You can see the things I bought from there, minus those items in my haversack bag!

Wow...my Boo and I wanted to check out the market and we got more than we expected. We picked up some coffees and made our way into the market. There were more little stalls and more people than I can explain. You can seriously buy anything here and they’ll bargain with you. It’s a fun experience and has all the souvenirs you can think of. It’s a fun experience
Took a Stroll to 'Ben Than Market' Great Bargains the Hawkers love to Rope you but it's Great Fun and you can get the Best look alike at Bargain Prices from 'Nike' to 'Underarm'

It's a big market, plenty of shopping - but it can get very hot in there! You can get some great bargains IF you bargain! It's a lot of fun and a great place to pick up shoes and memento's / gifts. Totally recommend a visit or two!

Good place to get souvenirs and you need to bargain. We stopped to have a meal here and were pleasantly surprised. You can get there by taxi which is cheap but only use the green taxis as the others are more expensive

It's a big market where you can find food as well as clothes, shoes, bags and all kinds of souvenirs. There is choice and also a lot of people. You can exercise your talents to haggle because the prices advertised to tourists are always very high. When you're tired, you can take a break from a Vietnamese coconut dessert or sip one of the many smoothies on offer. The market is covered so it is very hot. There are many counterfeits, more or less well imitated .... A good time if you like shopping!

In dieser riesigen Markthalle gibt es alles was man will, oder auch nicht unbediengt haben muß. Außen die Staatlichen Verkäufer, die wenig Interesse zeigen dir was zu verkaufen und festgeschriebene Preise haben, und in der Mitte die normalen Händler, die um jeden Kunden kämfen und pfeilchen. Abends nach Marktende gehen diese dann vor die Markthalle und verkaufen da weiter.
Sehr abwechslungsreiches Essen zu günstigen Preisen. Ebenso gibt es verschiedene leckere, frische Getränke, nach Bedarf auch mit Alkohol.

Un mercato mescolato tra cibo ed articoli di ogni genere, odori molto forti, trattative per la merce come nei paesi arabi, non riesci a capire se hai spuntato un buon prezzo o no!! Però è tutto molto bello da vedere anche se non bisogna perderci molto tempo.

Almost all shops accept Malaysian ringgit but found out from vendors that paying in Dong is cheaper.. Personally calculated that it is much cheaper paying in dong.. Things are mostly t-shirts and bags.. Similar almost every stalls.. One must bargain.. There are a couple of clothing shops with fixed price though.. Sales person are too friendly at times asking you to buy their goods.. And some will take out the things before telling you the price.. So be very careful..
Located in the middle of center, so you cannot miss it wherever you go in HCMC. Merchants are quite proactive to sell their goods. There are various goods, food, souvenirs, clothes, etc

Many have mentioned that Binh Tay is better. True, it is more authentic. However, this market caters well to tourists who want to buy souvenirs. The cashew nuts are well packed and look decent enough as gifts. This time, I bought 2 kg of cashew nuts and some fried jackfruits.

Good food Fresh tea and coffee to take home Lots of clothing and Nik naks. Be prepared to barter, do it with a smile and I seemed to have a good laugh with the locals .  The shirts I brought good quality

Markets were good, lots of items to choose from. Prices are inflated so barter away unless happy to pay the price. Preferred this market to Saigon square as cheaper. Kids enjoyed the fresh food side of the market. They liked watched eels get gutted. Watch out for the guys outside doing the rickshaw style bikes. Double check price first as they will argue once ride has finished.
It’s fun to go there and look around.  But when you buy something, you must bargain.  Cut the price 1/4 or 1/3 It’s a reasonable price!
食材からバッグ、食器にいたるまで、いろいろなものが揃う市場。空調がないので、暑いです。客引きがすごいので、初めて行く方は驚くかも。。。アジアを実 感します。

El mercado tiene durante el día los puestos , de toda clase de cosas, bolsos, ropa, comida... y de 19 a 23h montan puestos en la parte de fuera. Es un buen sitio donde comprar un souvenir. En el hotel nos avisaron de los "amantes de lo ajeno" como hay gente y los pasillos entre puestecitos son estrechos, hay que tener ojo con las pertenencias.

Decent market in the heart of HCM. There are some unique food and produce stalls but lost are clothes and souvenirs. Watch out, the vendors get a bit aggressive trying to sell you things. Worth seeing but I wouldn’t spend hours here. Better off going to the flower market
Covered market for lots of goods. Bargain with the merchants but go way down as they start very high. Check different stands and don't buy first thing but compare.
沢山の人が熱気の中で商品を販売している。 暑さの為、だれて見えるが活気をバンバンに感じる。  様々な商品があり、客引きもすごいがベトナムに来た感を感じるには素敵な場所。

Sheltered market for all things wet, dry and souvenirs... friendly faces greet u whenever you walk pass by.. mostly sells same stuff like coffee, green tea, pickled fruits, vegetables and meats, fresh fruits, and dry foods. There even a whole section of street food inside the market if u get hungry.. half section is also dedicated to clothes, bags, souvenirs, etc
Forget the trinket and fake good shopping. Check out the fresh food areas for a great experience that will get your senses going!

A large indoor market selling lots of general items where everyone wants you to buy from their stall. The more official stalls have items at stickers fixed price which is best as the stalls without marked prices will basically try to squeeze as much money out of you as they think they can get.  As you are probably aware you cannot buy a genuine Under Armour for €4 so you can draw your own conclusions about the genuinity of the items on offer. If I wanted a premium brand watch I would be more inclined to go to a jewellers, €30 does not buy a genuine Rolex!  As you will notice none of the photos show the actual market as there are quite enough photos of the market already available. Instead I have uploaded some images of various creatures encountered in India, Thailand and Vietnam. The market is a good place to spend an hour or two and pick up some souvenirs but stick with Tiffany's or Cartier for your watch purchases!

This place is full on hard sell! Forget leisurely strolling around. You will be challenged every step of the way! Great bargains can be had and there are some very good quality items to be had but you have to bargain like crazy. Definately not relaxing! On the outer rim are shops with fixed prices ( and not neccesarily dearer either!). You can recognise these stores by the ladies in blue shirts.

I'm not a big fan of markets but wandered in for a stroll. There's plenty of produce, clothing, coffee, etc. But it quickly feels like same same. Can definitely pick up some cheap fake clothing but be prepared to be haggled. Can obviously negotiate prices.

This market has anything and everything you could want day and night. It is bustling at night and bargaining is a must. The first price is always very high, so go halves and work to somewhere in the middle. It can be exhausting and exhilirating at the same time.Never buy on the first day if possible as you will pay very high prices. Great for all the gifts you need to buy anyone from family to special friends. Lots of designer copies that are very good. I have a bag I bought 3 years ago that is excellent.

If you want some cheap clothes/bags/souvenirs this is the place to go. It can be very busy at times. Make sure you bargain. They will offer high prices to begin with but if you stand your ground or pretend to walk away they will come down to your prices most often. If not someone at another stall will.
이 근처에 숙소가 있고, 쇼핑하기도 좋으므로 한번 쯤 들리게 됩니다. 시장 안 금은방에서 환전도 가능하고요 기념품 사기에도 나쁘지 않습니다

You can find all you need here, just remember to bargain down prices. Just walk away if it's too steep, plenty of shops sell the same things. Fixed price shops on the perimeter of the market with vendors in blue shirts. There's a fixed price shop that sells sweets and snacks as well.

Be prepared to be overwhelmed . Be prepared to bargain - go at least half the price quoted and stick with it ! Be prepared to shop till you drop . Be prepared to get hot and possibly bothered . Be prepared to have fun.

Had a quick walk through one of the side doors on our first day in Ho Chi Minh and then didn’t go back until our second last day. Like most markets, many vendors selling the same thing. Quality seemed quite good though for most things. Large food area and wet market at the front. Vendors grab your arm and talk at you. We went to a stall recommended to us and it was a lot easier. Best to know ahead what you want to buy and how much you will pay. Also take a tape measure if you plan to buy clothes as the sizing is varied. Also good to remember the number of the door you came in.
都内ツアーで来ました。グエンフエ通りより約１１分徒歩です。とても広く、色な買物商品が揃っています。客引きが多くため、静かに買物するのが困難です。実際の市民の生活を実感することがとてもよい観光スポットです。

Its a trully huge market full of stalls with various products, mostly aiming at the tourists. For 95% of tourists 1 or 2 visits will be fully sufficient to satisfy all of your shopping expectations in Saigon.  Just rememer to ask for the price in a few places, and for sure remember to haggle, as the initial poont is usually 200% of the decent price, so never say "ok" at once.

So if your looking for that Rolex, Yeezys, Gucci or branded T-Shirt then this is the place to go. It’s a labyrinth of stalls selling all fake designer goods alongside a food / vegetable market, and canteen food restaurants. It’s very much in your face and whilst you walk down an aisle you do have people approaching you with their products. It’s pretty much all the same items in all the stall. We just went for a walkaround ended up with some coffee and nick nacks. Please please if you go to buy bargain to death they quote prices 3-4 times what they are willing to sell for. If you say no and walk away they will call you back or you can go to the stall next door to get the price you want to pay.

The market has a lot happening with so many choices for visitors. We had a great time wirh a very friendly stall holder doing our bargaining. All done with good humour and we walked away happy with our purchase. The are many clothing, shoe, food stall etc. We enjoyed our time there.

Most people love the hustle and bustle of the city and market but I'm in a wheelchair so.. Anyway we do tackle it. Great atmosphere. Always barter, they always do. I go every year to one particular lady to buy a pure silk dressing gown and pure silk Vietnamese traditional out fit to wear in the house instead of pjs. Always been excellent quality. Don't know about other stores as I personally have not been to them. It's a coffee lovers heaven with plenty of choice. The market is definitely worth a visit on your trip but once a year is enough for me.

Whilst this is very much a tourist market it is fun to walk around and spot what’s on offer. I thought taking a small child around might be troublesome but vendors were really friendly and not overly pushy.
定番観光スポットのため、皆さん日本語や英語、中国語、韓国語で声をかけてきます。  価格ですが、はっきり言って安くはないかな。 ただ、お店が多いのでいっぺんにお土産を揃えるのは楽です。  大量に買うのであれば、そこそこ安いと思いますよ！

Es un mercadillo muy pintoresco, lo descubrí en una visita con un guía local y quise volver. Hay dos zonas diferenciadas, en las entradas, se encuentran los puestos regentados por comerciantes vestidas de azul que pertenecen al Estado. Ahí no se puede regatear, no es costumbre y los precios ya están muy ajustados. La segunda zona, si te adentras un poco más, consiste en mucho comerciante acosándote para venderte sus productos, a mí me resultó muy incómodo y además, muchas prendas de ropa me parecían de dudosa calidad al tacto aunque todas decían que eran de algodón. Te intentan vender en cada puesto, es difícil concentrarse y simplemente mirar.

I love this market... but it is a haven for Tourists and can be overwhelming for the unwary! A rabbit warren of 100’s of stalls all on top of each other and all wanting your business and trying to engage with you as you walk through. If you are just wanting to browse the stalls... well.. this is easier said than done with a plethora of stall holders yelling at you as you walk past “I have your size, I have your size, I have your size”.. be strong and walk on, but be polite. If you stop to look and ask about price.. they will almost certainly expect you to buy! If you are over haggling or you want to enter the haggling game but need to be sure of prices... around the outer area of the market are fixed price stalls!! These are great if you are overwhelmed or have had enough! But enjoy the experience, have fun with it, but try and be respectful and polite in your haggling and remember this is their livelihood and what you think you are saving in a hard haggle may only be a pittance in the scheme of things.

Visited the market quite early which was good as it was nice and quiet. We found a few things we really aimed for (tea set and a leather passport purse) and we got quite a discount.  All of our stuff is nice but We got completely ripped off buying our son a football outfit which the club logo came off already when we took it out of the bag. Besides, you also get really fed up bartering but then if you don’t, you end up paying way too much for the quality that you can buy here.  So be prepared to know what you want to buy, and then go for it to get a discount.
기념품을 사실려면 정찰제로 되어 있는 곳에서 사세요. 제일 저렴합니다. 시계는 품질 안좋고 뻥튀기가 너무 되어있어서 절대 사시지 마시고 지갑, 반바지 정도가 제일 가격대비 좋았습니다. 서로 웃으면서 흥정하고 장난치면서 즐기세요

I went here the other day and had a good look around and grabbed lunch. Plenty of clothes and tailors, but also lots of knock off bags and other brand stuff. Also plenty of food vendors and fresh fruit and dried fruit and coffee sellers Something for everyone!

On se disait encore un marche comme les autres. Et bien non il est plus complet et avec de tres bon prix si on negocie un peu. Nous nous sommes bien amusés. A deconseiller les vases en bois avec de la marquetterie magnifique. Au premier remplissage ils fuient, mais ils restent être de beaux objets. Nous avons trouvé des polos de tres bonne qualité magnifiques et a tres bon prix.
Cheap prices but only when you brave enough to bargain :) If they tell you 500.000 đ Pay only 150.000 đ some not really good quality.

Went there during the day (inside) and came back during the night (night market outside). Good variety of products you can get all over Vietnam, but clean and orderly. We bought a few things, you need to negotiate very tough if you want to get to their rock bottom price. But it’s fun, and worth a visit.

Både de stora och de små uppskattade marknaden. Barnen blev själva experter på att pruta och lyckades få ned priserna med mer än hälften. Gillar man sånt är marknaden rolig. Väldigt många stånd har precis samma varor. T-shirts, väskor och övriga kläder i överflöd. Främst kopior. Det finns även massor av mat. Jag fyndade lite tyger. De hävdade 100% siden, men när hon insåg att jag var bra på att sy kom ett erkännande att det nog var lite polyester i tyget också. Även kashmir-blandningar och bomullstyger till fina priser.

Den Ben Thanh muss man besucht haben, wenn man in Saigon ist. Hier kann man lecker essen und frisches Obst, Gemüse und alles andere kaufen. Aber Vorsicht: Kaffee und Tee sowie andere Dinge sind oft Fake Mittlerweile - leider.  Ab ins Getümmel und handeln, gucken, shoppen und essen !!!!
동남아를 여행을 많이 다녀봤다면 크게 특별한 것이 없다. 따라서 시간을 내서 방문하는 것은 좀 아닌 것 같다. 추가로 밤 7시 부터 야시장이 열린다. 규모는 그리 큰 규모는 아니다.
The Ben Than Market is larger and cleaner that most markets in Saigon/HCMCThere are many interesting things offered, but don't regard the prices as fixed. Negotiating is expected.

I found Ben Thanh Market kinda overwhelming a lot of stalls packed tight and some vendors pushy with prices. Not much chance of a bargain unless you really haggle hard. Food stalls looked good but I didn't try much as it was too hectic. If you want souvenirs maybe check other spots too.

A nice walk from the main District 1 attractions such as Opera House, Old Post Office and Notre Dame.  A real touristy location with really no bargains. If you talk to the locals, goods are cheaper elsewhere.  Nonetheless, great fun bartering with the score holders.
Burası Vietnam’ın Mahmutpaşası.zHer tür taklit tekstil ürünü,ayakkabı,çanta vb eşyalar burada satılmaktadır.Mutlaka satıcı ile pazarlık yapın.
This is a typical market of this area. Its worth visiting for a walk around and browse. The government stall are located in the outer ring and there is no haggle as they are fixed price.

Standard chaos of an Asian market. Lots of stalls selling the same same but different. Clothes, shoes, bags, linen, souvenirs, coffee etc  Feels very safe and a lot to the stall holders are very friendly. If batering is not for you. You can explore the outside lane where there are fixed prices.  Great street food stalls inside too

Our second visit to the large Ben Thanh market was a bit of a disappointment.  My memory of an earlier visit, some years ago was much better than yesterday’s reality.  There is a mix of food vendors and general giftware/souvenir/apparel/junk stands. All the vendors were aggressively touting for your business and it was all pretty crowded and dinghy.  Much better food option the surrounding streets.

Our second visit in 5 years and everything is the same except that prices are now fixed.  Stalls selling similar goods eg clothings, bags, coffee powder, food and shoes.  It was crowded with tourists as usual. We went there for rain shelter and did not buy anything. Its no longer as as cheap as 5 years ago.  Tourist trap.

Huge selection of knock offs of everything - watches, purses, clothes. Can be cheap or expensive depending on how you negotiate. Offer 30% of asking price. Lots of food vendors. Most places take credit card.

Even as you approach you know what to expect. Traders trying to flog you rubbish sunglasses and unfortunately beggars work nearby. Once inside the market your senses are hit hard. Masses of stalls in every direction. Traders constantly asking you to look at their stall, or asking what you want. It’s fine, just keep walking if you don’t want anything. There is a lot of repetition of goods with some familiar looking brands at unfamiliar prices! It is very popular and it gets crowded, but it’s fun to experience.
Hàng hoá đa dạng, đồ ăn ngon.  Lưu ý hỏi giá trước khi mua hangg hoặc ăn, trả giá và kiểm tra giá trên mạng. Cẩn thận bị chặt chém

Get ready for aisles upon aisles of street food and to haggle for quirky souvenirs, all while soaking in the chaotic charm of Ben Thah Market. It’s meant to turn into a night market, but both times we swung by around 8 PM, it was shuttered.  The only thing we ended up buying was a fresh coconut. However, if you want a souvenir and are new to haggling, start by offering 50% of the price. When they say "no," flash a smile, say "thanks," and start to walk away. Trust us, they won’t let you get far.
思った以上に、店の数がとこら狭しとある。 とても暑い日で中も蒸し風呂状態！ しかし、駆け引き面白い。 何点か購入した。 値段も自分のがよければいいのでは？ 2度いった。
Over priced & crowded. Very overwhelming - better off going to local street vendors. Tried to charge 950,000 for one singlet. Got it for 100 around the corner
Quite intense as you would expect but expect to pay half the asking price at the absolute max. Hold your guns and walk away, they will cave and you’ll get a bargain.

Good prices compared to where we are from although after spending enough time in HCMC you soon realise Ben Thanh Market is much more expensive than any of the other markets in the area, with the vendors being much ruder and more impatient. It’s worth a visit, but I’d recommend Saigon Square and shoe street instead, they all sell the same things anyway but you can get a better price at those.

Marché devenu plus pour les touristes pour ramener des souvenirs. On peut marchander les prix. Royaume de la contrefacon Au niveau des petits resto à l intérieur, intéressant niveau pris malgré une certaine augmentation depuis des années. C est quand même très abordable mais très cible touristes.
Shopping is average quality, hot and noisy covered market but great place to see life in a busy market in Saigon. Would not eat there. Quite an experience.

Probabilmente il più famoso mercato del centro della città, caotico come tutti nel suo genere. Solito cibo più o meno invitante e abbigliamento. Per gli amanti del genere (e non) vale la pena fare un passaggio

Lots of Vietnam souvenirs and cheap clothes but very busy and crowded. Worth a visit but a little high pressure with their selling. Starting prices around 3 times the price you pay but that’s half the fun of the visit.

I'm not a snob but don't find it alluring to shop or eat at the market, especially in Asia. They're usually in an old structure or on the street, hacking fake stuff, local specialties and cuisine (not sure all that healthy ...) If happen to pass by, I'll walk through it.   After lunch at Soul, I've 20 minutes on hand, too short to go else, so I went in. Nothing spacial. Really, just some fake stuff ...

If you are not here to shop and get knick knacks then best to avoid it. Way too hot and crowded to simply come and just do it for the experience. We bought some shot glasses here as souvenirs and just left. Just not the environment for me but each to their own travel schedules and how you want to experience it.

A huge market cramped and hot. But an experience. We got there early watched fish being filleted on the ground...meat bwing cut off big meat hooks and live frogs being skinned ready for sale. We brought nothing went there to look. Be prepared to be hassled to buy. There are marked areas that provide fixed discount prices with no barter. Lots of junk but if you have a cheapo purchase in mind you should find it

As someone who loves exploring markets, I make a point to visit them wherever I travel. Unfortunately, Ben Thanh Market didn’t quite live up to expectations. It felt more like a hub for fake goods and souvenirs than a must-visit attraction. The food section was unimpressive, and the meat section had an overwhelming smell that made it unpleasant. In my opinion, there are far more interesting and worthwhile things to see in Saigon.

Lots of local produce, souvenirs and also food and plenty of places to eat. Compared to the neighboring Saigon Square market, there are much simpler goods here and also more intrusive vendors. There is also no air conditioning here and it is very hot and there is a very strong smell in the neighborhood of the eateries and fish stalls. However, as a historical market, it is more authentic than the Saigon Square market.

Crowded and noisy! Prices are highly inflated and tourists who come from countries where bargaining is not a way of life are likely to be caught out! We wanted to buy a pair of Nike shoes and they wanted 1,200,000 VND! We offered 700,000 but they refused. Our final price was 850,000 VND and they reluctantly agreed. (The trick is to pretend to walk away and let them come after you!) Some of the sellers were rude and refused to discuss price unless we tried the items on.
It is kind of a big market. It is full of stalls of souvenirs and clothes. The prices are exorbitant and you have to be a good negotiator.
It is a very busy place traffic none stop very difficult to even cross a road .The market was undercover so very cramp and hot.

‘Ben Tahn is famous in Ho Chi Minh’, says our hostel receptionist. I can see why, it’s HUGE, selling food, clothes, and souvenirs. You get hassled quite a lot, and most stalls sell the same things (for example, we say at least 5 stalls selling fake football and basketball jerseys). If you’re looking for some souvenirs to bring home then pop in here for a few minutes, but beware it’s crowded 24/7

It’s lost its charm. The price here are at least 2x and 3 x and then you should have the capacity to bargain. Plus the shop keepers are not so friendly and they try to Huss you. Better to go to Central market
お土産探しに行ってしまいました。個人的にはゴミゴミしてるので好きではないです。 ただ街中に少ないので。以前に比べて綺麗になったような感じはしますが変わっていないかもしれません・・観光客は少なかったです。ベンタインの近くにはレートの良い 両替所もあるので行ったついでに必要ならおすすめします。
Tôi có đi vào chợ bến thành để đi tham quan và mua đồ nhưng có lẽ tôi nghĩ ở đây chỉ dành cho người nước ngoài vì giá rất mắc đã thế khi giá quá mắc mình k mua thì mng tỏ thái tộ 😡

Si vous allez pour acheter des souvenirs passer votre chemin. Les prix sont exorbitants. Un monde fou et une chaleur étouffante. Les vendeuses sont agressives . Du côté nourriture, l odeur est insoutenable et les marchands ne vous lâchent pas. 👎

Don’t take your kids it’s literally a very small labyrinth of rows within the stalls, larger people would find it hard to get around I knocked things a few times with my backpack and anyone with a walker or mobility issues wouldn’t have a chance. Expect every stall opener calling out harassing you to buy stuff- pretty much with all the same products competing against each others for a sale. Good bargains are possible if you want to work for it. It was extremely overwhelming very loud so many things literally everywhere in your face I just ran for what I wanted and got out it’s very claustrophobic for anyone who doesn’t like small spaces you are very blocked in once you walk down a row!

If you’ve never been to a market in Southeast Asia, this is a cultural experience.  The market represents an idea of what a market can be, with small passages/spaces to walk at times and vendors selling all types of items and food.  That said, it’s VERY commercialized, can feel claustrophobic to those who don’t like crowds or cramped spaces, and we felt is geared more towards tourist (aka, a tourist trap).  While worth a visit, you don’t need to spend too much time there.
It’s worth a trip, not really cheap. The stall holders are aggressive but they chilled out a bit in the evening  Go for the experience but not for the shopping

We were a group of 5 friends who visited Ho Chi Minh City in May. We stayed in Hotel Winsuites which was about 2 mins walk from the hotel. This allowed us to explore the market in the evening.  The market pretty much has everything from food, to souvenirs, household things, clothes, bags etc etc... Cannot comment on the quality of things here... but you pretty much find a lot of things.  Bargaining is definitely a thing. If you are good at bargaining you can pretty much reduce the price by 75% of the asking price.  Even if you don't buy anything it was interesting to see the market for sure.

This is still the place to visit to buy your souvenirs. There are corners where the prices are fixed but everywhere else, the prices can be haggled. It's very hot so bring a fan. Always haggle and keep looking. If you're not yet sure about a stall, ask the price and leave. Otherwise, they will not make you leave and force you to buy their products. It's not dangerous or anything. It's just their hustle.
Although it is a market, food prices are too expensive compared to restaurants. The food is not handled cleanly, the seller is not friendly.

Nice taste of Ho Chi Minh City. There are many booths with owners trying to get you to indulge in their wares. I didn’t particularly care for all the yelling they did to try to get your attention, but I understand why they do it. I preferred the calmer, fixed price areas. You like their price or you don’t buy. Easy peasy. In fact, I purchased all my souvenirs in these areas.

Came here twice with two friends on separate occasions and spent only like around half hour to 45mins each visit, didnt finish all lanes as many shops are selling similar items. I didnt get anything, only a juice. Overpriced and expensive. Cramp walkways. It is convenient to get souvenirs, thats about it. Would prefer a small local market.

This is a market that sells just about everything, and most shops sell the same thing so do not be in any rush to buy anything in the first 30 mins… otherwise you’ll be ripped off so spend the first 30 minutes just walking around getting a gauge for prices. My recommendation is to ask for the price, when they give you the price just walk away..do not respond. They will call out after you the lowest price that they want to start it. Go to the next stall, do the same thing, etc etc that will give you the baseline price and then pay no more than 66% of that price, ideally, 50%, and you’re probably not getting ripped off too much! As an example I was after basketball singlets, and they started initially at 400 or 500,000 dong, I walked away you get them down to calling out 200,000, you pick them up for Max 150,000, down to 130,000 was the lowest I paid.
A little on the touristy side and I'm not sure about the quality of the merchandise....but all in all a fun little walk through if you're looking for souvenirs and such....

中心部にあり利便性の良いホーチミン最大の市場で観光客向けのお土産物のほか、日常生活用品や食材など色々なモノが揃います。店員の押しが強く、じっくりと品定め出来ないこともありますが、交渉次第で半値になる商品もありました。私はアジアの市場等での値引き交渉が好きで、日本語の通じる店員さんもいますので、複数まとめて買うとか、他に探している商品などを相談してみると値引きしてくれました。お目当ての欲しい商品が決まっていれば予め写真を撮っておいて、それを見せるとスムーズです。怪しい商品が欲しければ店員さんに言えばどこからか持ってきてくれます。市場近くの9月23日公園内にある地下街のセントラルマーケットや、高島屋脇のサイゴンスクエア、またロシアンマーケット等と比較しながらショッピングを楽しんでください。でも価格交渉は節度をわきまえて楽しみたいです。なお、ベトナムではハノイにあるドンスアン市場より品揃えは勝りますが価格はこちらのほうが高めな印象でした。少し歩くと高級ブランドショップ街や観光地も点在しています。市場内は特に週末は混雑しますのでスマホや貴重品には十分注意してください。また日本語で話しかけてくるヒトや靴磨き等にもご注意ください。お帰りは近隣の大きなホテル内の客待ちのタクシーを利用すると安全です。

C (24.03.10 ) Ho Chi Minh City's representative market is large. Personally, it's not impressive because I don't like shopping, but it's a place worth a visit. There are various products and many stores, and it is a popular place for foreigners. Even if you don't buy anything, it's a place to stop by.

Sehr überzogene Preise, zum Teil Shirts für 50$ angeboten bekommen. Das Meiste für 30-50% des angebotenen Preises erhalten, vieles danach dennoch woanders günstiger gesehen. Sollte man definitiv besuchen, zum Einkaufen gibt es bessere Märkte (Pham Ngu Lao Street).
Too many shopkeepers trying to swindle us The prices I was able to bargain: Non La (hat painted) 50k Bamboo cup 65k Silk Ao Dai 350k Basic unisex t-shirts 80k Fridge Magnets 50k (pack of 6)
High pressure selling with over priced items. Go for the experience; fresh fish, crabs, meat, flowers, fabric and tat!

this market is a mixed bag... they have some good things and some good prices but the sellers are obnoxious! and there is too much of the exact same thing at many stalls. the bargaining gets old real quick - just tell me the best price and let's move on but no, tell me something which I know is too high and we will settle for something less or not. I bought a great bag at the "fixed price" aisle. also bought some CZ earrings for $12 (she started at $22!) that I prob could have gotten cheaper elsewhere but they were cute and it was an ok price.

You can find almost anything here. From souvenirs to nice artwork. Will not recommend eating here if you are a vegetarian or vegan. There are not many options. Food was not great. Overall good place to shop for souvenir’s

All the odds listed below by other travelers are true - inflated prices, pushy and aggressive sellers, unpleasant smell,...but that's how every market (specially a tourist one) in Asia (as well other third world's countries) works. You come here to buy cheap souvenirs, nuts and local dried fruits, coffee, presents for your friends, fake clothes and bags,.... And, you come with your haggling skills. If you buy for the quoted price (or only 15-20% cheaper) you're a fool. You must cut the seller's price dividing it by 7 or 8, maybe even 10 and stick to that, rising it only a little bit. Pretend you're not interested and you're about to leave. They will call you back and sell it to you for your price (sometimes grumbling with anger) as long as they have profit - if they don't call, you it means you exaggerated with cutting the price, so you should raise it if you want that. If you manage to do this you'll come out with loads of cheap and believe me, pretty good stuff. If you're unable for that simply avoid, as you'll ruin your day and experience.

A must visit experience when in Ho Chi Minh City. Very busy, hot and bustling with the market zoned into different selling options. You must haggle or you will be ripped off. do not pay more than 50-60% of the original asking price. A great place for material, cheap clothes, fake handbags etc. and tourist gifts. The food stalls offer some excellent street food. Closed at night but there is a night market instead with excellent street food again. If you hate crowds and aggressive selling I would avoid but you can just enjoy without buying.

It’s interesting first time but after that it is wearing and you get pestered turning a 5 minute stroll through the market into a 15 minute slog. The price of everything that was quoted was ridiculously high, then they pick up a calculator to start bargain . The prices were so high to begin with I couldn’t be arsed to even start negotiating so unless it’s something you really want then don’t start negotiating as you are going to be there forever.

went to these markets today this was my experience 1----- every shop will look at you and give you a price , then you will give them a lower offer , example looked at a item they quoted us 1.3 million and i said 200k then they kept dropping it until we bought that item at 350k so this gives you a idea what sort of pricing they are first quoting to what you actually end up playing i think if i was a local i would have paid only 200k for that item but i guess 350 was ok as you compare to what it is really costing you in dollars so it always sounds cheap. 2--- most of the suppliers will act rude i guess they come across so many travellers everyday then again they can be polite but they dont choose to . 3--- dont believe a word what you get told about the quality make your observation and then best way i found walk through the market first and then get a idea of what that item you are looking for is really worth and then go buy from that shop who is real with the pricing . 4--- was looking at some fake Rolex and omega watches , i believe what i have seen all over the you tube i disagree what qualities they have in ben thang market are all very bad quality fakes , went through few jobs heaps had them in stock but none are good quality so my question was how many days would this watch actually last , may be there are some other shops in the city where there sell better quality fake watches but ben thanh no they dont they will tell you come inside and i will show you better but i seen the so called best fake watch rubbish. 5---- i have to say again massive bargaining is required and after even you set the price i felt i paid more because it ,makes yo wonder when they started with 1.6 million and now within 5 minutes' they are happy to sell that to you for 200k so what was the real price for that item makes yo wonder . 6--Heaps of shops to go through , most have the same stuff and will try to convince the other supplier has inferior goods and they goods are better quality the usual tricks .  that was my observation today. gotta be smart or else you will they will take you for a ride ... and they are good at it they get a lot of practice everyday ha ha.

Shopped a vietnam t-shirt and a fake Michael Kors bag here as souvenirs on Dec-28,2023 evening. This is a typical Asian shopping market, with multiple stalls packed next to each other & has multiple entrances and exits & we started & finished at gate 8/15, next to which is the paid restroom.
Marché à voir si vous êtes dans le quartier. Effectivement, il faut négocier les prix bien que la négociation semble de plus en plus compliquée…
Un marché pour touristes, les vendeurs sont très insistants,voir désagréables. Les prix sont élevés par rapport aux autres marchés
