色々なお土産など売ってます。値段はぼったくり。腕は引っ張られるし付きまといます。 交渉好きさんはいいと思います。 お土産にTシャツを探していました。 最初1枚300000VNDと吹っかけられました。 ほぼ100%の店でぼったくり値段を言われましたが、最終的にはTシャツ5枚で450000VNDで購入。 色々買いましたが全て交渉しないとだいぶぼられます。

It's your standard Southeast Asian market scene – loud, bustling, and always a bit overcrowded. It’s not particularly unique and imo not a must see spot in the city. The place is a maze, and browsing isn't the easiest, but it's okay if you're up for the chaos. You can find most everything from food and coffee to clothes and souvenirs. Be prepared to bargain hard, otherwise you’re going to get ripped off.
A lot of shops, lot of people you can find every tu big from bags, watches, clothes and food. I really like Vietnamese coffe there. A must seen place in Saigon

Ben Thanh market is definitely worth seeing but we walked through a few times and didn't really stop. The vendors are very very pushy so you don't feel like you can even look at anything without being badgered to buy. The food options weren't appealing and the fresh food part of the market had a bad smell to it like rotting meat. Great to experience but we weren't keen to try to eat anything! Other wet markets we visited in Cambodia and Vietnam seemed much more hygienic than this one. We spent a lot more time at nearby Saigon Square for bargain shopping and found it much better than Ben Thanh.

Vendors here won't negotiate much since it's a tourist centred market so expect steep starting prices. Most from my experience are willing to settle at one third of their asking price. Most vendors are friendly and the market's decently designed so there wasn't much issue with overcrowding or ventilation. Worth a visit.

Went here a couple of times, as the girls were keen to get their hands on some fake handbags. It’s a typical Asian market, full of all kinds of imitation goods. If you’re looking for high end, quality merchandise, this isn’t for you. It’s, however, fun to haggle with the vendors and are ok with being constantly hassled by them, you can have a good time.

It is the low season, but still rather hot and stifling in the mostly narrow aisles even with few people.  Nothing really too new here. Over pestering and too much of same being sold by too many over eager sellers. They are not angry though if you politely decline.

Im Hotel wurde uns bereits bei der Ankunft gesagt, dass man bei diesem Markt 20 - 30% runterhandeln muss. Die Verkäufer setzen die Preise viel zu hoch an. An unserem letzten Tag haben wir jedoch erlebt, dass die Verkäufer auch aggressiv werden können, wenn man ihren Preis nicht zahlen wollen. An einem Stand haben sich auf einmal 4 andere Verkäufer eingemischt. Somit gehören die irgendwie alle zusammen. Da sind wir dann auch weitergegangen, weil sich in vietnamesisch fürchterlich aufgeregt hat. Am nächsten Stand hat die Verkäuferin den Taschenrechner einfach hingeschmissen und uns stehen gelassen. Ist auch eine Methode nicht mit dem Preis einverstanden zu sein. Und an einem letzten Stand wollte die Verkäuferin uns irgendwie gar keinen Preis nennen, nur wenn wir dann kaufen würden und dann ging diese auch wieder. Bei den Laternen sollten sie wirklich aufpassen, diese werden sehr teuer verkauft. Falls sie eine Rundreise machen und noch nach Hoi An kommen, dann kaufen sie diese lieber dort. Da sind die Preise ganz anders als in Saigon.

The market itself is very busy and stiflingly hot, there's actually a smaller market just down the road a little which is not only air-conditioned but has pretty much all the same stuff at much cheaper prices... this place is popular with tourists which likely drives prices up considerably, still I got my Vietnam flag and hammer and sickle tee shirts I wanted but you really need to haggle hard here to get a reasonable price... we bought another 3 shirts at the market down the road for half what it cost us here and minimal haggling as the price was pretty fair to begin with. One warning watch the coffee shops in the market, I bought a coconut iced coffee from 3Ro coffee for VnD 40K paid for it with a 200K note, got handed 60K wrapped up in the receipt and the thief legged it fast before I realised what he'd done... and no one their suddenly understood english anymore... watch your money carefully most folks are good but there's always some that aren't.

This place was underwhelming. People speak about this market like it’s huge and a real buzz where you will be harassed by stall holders and enjoy the experience of a real Vietnamese market.  I wouldn’t bother going if we went back to HMC, it’s like Sydney’s paddy’s markets, there was no vibe.
All type of shopping in one place,old bazar type cluster of shops for every type of customers from Textile,fruits soveneirs name any thing

Crazy, chaotic and hot experience but absolute fun as long as you keep your sense of humour. Haggling is a must, start at least 70% lower than their asking price. We started at 2 million dong and shook hands at 500,000. They’re great actors and have seen it all so don’t think you can dupe them! To be honest the goods are not the best quality but look good from a distance. Go, enjoy, engage with the traders, life is for living so go and have some fun.

Everything you need to take home as presents and mementos is available here. Negotiating is required since they opportunistically overcharge and costs have decreased by anywhere between 30 and 80%. They are aware of their limitations and will either pursue you or let you keep the transaction. clothing, crafts, food, footwear, and accessories. It's everything here, really. It is hot, crowded, and not for the faint of heart, but it is still enjoyable for a few hours. The only thing that didn't particularly appeal to me was when vendors tried to grab me.
Great market but recently they have jacked the prices right up I recommend shopping around the stores close to the market

Kayak pasar modern BSD. Jujur sih cuma mau mampir sebentar aja karena emang gak niat belanja, tempatnya rameeee sekali buat belanja ini itu, mulai dari makanan, tas KW, suvenir, segala macam ada. Tapi namanya dari sesama Asia Tenggara, isi jualannya sih enggak terlalu aneh di mata saya, alias banyak juga lah ada di Indonesia, hehehe. Tapi alasan utama enggak belanja sebenarnya karena Ho Chi Minh cuma perhentian pertama, biar kopernya enggak terlalu berat dari awal.

After the initial shock of being harassed by stall owners it becomes a little easier to put up with. Bought a tag watch from one of the larger watch stalls and it has as expected failed within a month...look great but expect that they are rubbish and you wont be disappointed when they stop working. And the warranty is a farce....no paperwork and they know tourists wont come back...so dont expect a repair or replacement Bought a backpack and handbag, pay a price you are comfortable with knowing that they are locally made. Overall do it for the experience, do some haggling and enjoy the experience.

Like each and every indoor market across SE Asia....all human life is here. Nothing you could possibly need and everything you may think you want is here in abundance. We stopped for a coconut and a little people watching.

Another market selling the same stuff as all the others. You need to haggle with the prices as they start of ridiculously high. Be careful if you are buying bags, they will tell you leather when it is not. They will even do the lighter test. It is imitation leather, they got very annoyed when I told them it was not real but then reduced their prices to a fraction of the price. The food stalls were excellent and we ate there a couple of times.

A huge traditional market. All branded items are fakes. If you are not good at haggling then do not go.Chances are you will be worse off.No quality stuff there. The shopkeepers have a team who hunt in packs.And their haggling skills are very well honed.

We have been to a few other markets in Vietnam and find most are similar and sell the same products. Most of the stall owners are quite aggressive in their sales pitch but you learn not to react or engage unless you are actually interested in an item. If you don’t know roughly what you should be paying it would be easy to pay way more than the items are worth. Haggling the price is part of the experience and I found usually the end price was a quarter of what was originally asked. Worth a visit especially if you haven’t been to a market before.

A very interesting experience. We went there to grab some food and got absolutely harassed to buy something from all the vendors. The food was average and slightly overpriced, the street food across the road is better and much more friendly vendors. It also has a very strong smell in there so be warned. We stayed nearby as we thought we’d go lots but after the first time didn’t go back. If you want to buy lots of clothes/goods it would be okay but get ready to be yelled at.

The market is a great hype It is full of counterfeit products In case you are interested to buy it pls bargain hard & also be ready face aggressive behaviour of vendor The market offers varieties of materials like garments clothes souvenirs food product live vegetables & plethoras of meat & sea foods It also provides sun glasses watches luggage etc
ツアーで訪れました。ツアーコンダクターの話によると、衣料品、カバン店、靴店、時計店、食料品店、飲食店など約２０００のお店が入っているそうです。声かけ、客引き、スリに注意が必要です。販売しているカバンや靴、服、時計のブランド品は全て偽物で、本物は売っていません。ちょっとした物を買う時は、値段交渉次第で、どんどん価格が下がるようです。見る価値はありますが、買う価値はありませんね。

An archetypical market for tourists which is worth a visit for an hour or so when in Ho Chi Minh city. Dozens of stalls with somewhat pushy sellers selling the same redundant fare but, that said, it can be a fun experience if one doesn’t take it too seriously.
The Saigon, Ben Thanh Night Street Market that surrounded the covered market at night is no longer on. I'm here now 5 June 2023 and nothing we are he for a month I will keep you posted.

ホーチミンシティでは最大のマーケットで、地元住民だけでなくベトナム各地、さらには日本など世界中から観光客で1日中にぎわっている。徒歩圏に深夜まで営業のマクドナルド、22時ころまで営業のケンタッキーフライドチキンもある。 バンコク/タイのチャトゥチャック市場よりは売場面積が広くないものの、敷地内は通路が狭く店舗が多数ひしめき合っている。 衣類と雑貨などを豊富に取り扱っているが、どの店舗も店員が日本語で執拗な客引きをするので注意が必要である。

As you would probably expect from a market. Sweaty Smelly Aggressive stall holders  Prices weren't that cheap, really, even with haggling. Unfortunately, the meat section gives a smell that lingers throught the whole market.
food and shopping! hot though. use your bargaining power to the max! best if you already have something in mind to buy.

Ben Thanh market was not a pleasant place to shop. Constantly hounded by merchants in a stuffy, smothering market is not my idea of fun ( my buying wife enjoyed it more). Go only if you are a masochist for negotiating.
Marche couvert ou en trouve de tout et surtout du made in China… Section fringues, section objets, section nourriture… Tout y est si vous n’avez pas le temps de trop vous balader😉

The sellers are not too aggressive for the most part, you can just walk away if they get oo much. Have an idea on what you are prepared to pay so that you dont get overwhelmed. They can get pushy and just tell them to stop - be polite but firm and it seemed to stop them going hard at you. Felt like seaguls on a chip! Worth a look through though and around one side they seem to have marked prices and all wearing the same colour t shirt - wondered if that was more regulated.

It's an interesting market, full of souvenirs. Be prepared to bargain a lot, the vendors are pretty pushy. The food area has a lot of variety that is more expensive than local restaurants, with very pushy vendors as well. It's worth walking around for the experience but there are not really any bargains to be found.

Busy, crowded but good market. Don’t ask a price unless you are serious. The vendors are aggressive and pushy. My son was dragged into stalls and felt intimidated. Items selling for 850,000 VND could be bought for 200,000 VND

Cool to see but it’s just a tourist trap so expect to pay a large tourist tax. Easy to negotiate massive discounts and it’s frustrating that the vendors try and charge so much.  Opening times published are just a guess too as they close as and when they like.  If it’s labelled up, pay it, if it’s not 1/2 it. Much different to the markets in Hanoi which still apply a tourist tax but not as high.
Op zich een leuke markt, maar het is er erg druk en ze verkopen geen hele bijzondere dingen. Ook zijn ze vrij opdringerig wat het lastiger maakt ik wegens rustig te kijken.

This market is hot. Vendors pushy and some can be extremely rude when you don,t buy from them. Make sure you make a note of stall you are purchasing from as they all look the same. I brought a bag and when i wasn't looking they have switched it out with inferior quality bag. Didn,t notice until i got back to hotel. So be ware. Average prices

Дорого, но можно сторговаться. Мы смогли договориться о снижении цены в пять раз на футболки, хотя и не так сильно на красивые, но более дорогие магниты. Поскольку фрукты очень дорогие, предпочтительнее покупать их на другом рынке поблизости.

Stor marknad, mycket prylar, kläder, kaffe och mat. Man måste pruta rejält, de kan tex säga att en grej kostar 900 k dong men man kan få den för 200 k dong. Vi köpte rökelser, trä och sten souvenirer.

The market frankly is a tourist trap. Lots of knock-offs that are not really great quality, knick-knacks and souvenirs. The stalls sell the same things, so if you seen one, you seen all. Of course, bargaining is a must. We were quoted very different prices from different stalls. No need to waste any time if you sense you will be fleeced; just move to the next stall. Lots of pushy and touchy stall owners. Though it was during noon time, the heat was bearable. But then again, we are from hot sunny Singapore.

You have got to be a great negotiator when you're here. They sometimes more than triple their prices. We've walked away several times and they'd pull you back in and agree with your counter offer. Some are very rude and I know this first hand since I speak Vietnamese and can understand them (they don't know that). We spent almost a whole day there -- even had lunch and a snack at that market. It's a fun experience but you have to have a lot of patience.

Este mercado es bastante conocido, con un enfoque importante para el turista. Tiene buena diversidad de productos y souvenirs para comprar y dura hasta tarde. Recomendable para comprar algo.  TIPS: -El primer precio que te daran siempre será muy superior, por lo que debes regatear para bajarlo. Es una practica común en este lado de asia. Por lo menos bajar del 20 al 40% del valaor inicial que te ofrecen. -Puedes usar calculadora para ir regateando si hay problemas de idioma.

Ben Thanh Market is a great place if a tourist wants to get some shopping for local Vietnamese items. However, just like all tourist related places, the vendors just have to try selling at the highest price possible and make people take so much effort to fight for a good deal.
Just another market. Absolutely no different from any other. If you want something different which is for locals, go to Ton That market.
土産を買いに行きました。 呼び込みに価格交渉と相変わらずの市場。 似たような土産物、衣服、コーヒー、屋台などなど見ているだけでも時間が過ぎていきますがメチャメチャ声かけられます。 価格は果物以外は大体半額になりました。 丸い春巻きの皮ではなく長方形の春巻きの皮は水に付けずに食べれるので喜ばれました。

Bonito mercado con todo tipo de productos de cómoda, recuerdos, ropa y mochilas de imitación, etc Algo muy destacado es la venta de café, el tema es que lo venden bastante caro y supuestamente es de Civeta, pero según se escucha no es de verdad. El café de Civeta cuesta muchísimos dólares el Kilo y aquí no lo venden. Al igual que el de Coconut y otros, que lo impregnan en aceites que es lo que le da el brillo y el olor que cautivará tu nariz…. Pero es Café normal.
Glad we went but wouldn't rush back. It was unfortunately very quiet so it meant we were targeted by all the street vendors. You can get some good souvenirs and other items but you have to haggle.

I had a walk around here and wasn't really looking to buy but wanted to see how it is since last time I was here. Sellers are alittle pushy but not too bad. There's plenty of shops mainly selling same sort of things like clothes, wallets, jewelry and souvenirs. Some food shops look nice but I didn't eat. Worth a look if you are in the mood for some shopping and bargaining.
Go here if you have lots of patience. Lots of bargaining and time is required. Variety is enormous right from food items to home items to clothes. Interesting experience of street shopping

どの旅行ガイドにも必ず紹介されているであろうベンタイン市場。先ずは独特な匂いがこの市場を強烈に脳にすり込み、しきりに話しかけてくるお店の人、腕をつかんで引っ張ってくる人、無視すると「ばか」と叫んでくる人に少しウザさを感じながらも市場ならではの値切りを楽しんでこそベンタイン市場である。コロナの影響で中のお店もかなり入れ替わったが、相変わらずの客引きでございました。  あらかじめネットなどで相場を調べてから買い物をする事を強くオススメ お店のプライスそのままで購入なんてのは、本当にカモになるだけである お店にもよるが1/3価格程度から交渉を始めても罰は当たらないと思う  夜に市場周りで開催されるマーケットのほうが市場より安い場合もあるので 夜のマーケットも見に行こう。もちろん値切ってね！（コロナ禍中は休止中）

It is a tourist trap though, and you MUST haggle for the price. Don't buy coffee from here because they are super expensive, more expensive than the coffee sold in gourmet coffee stores which is a LOT in Vietnam (Highlands, Phuk Long) . For trinkets, go to the cheaper post office or areas outside Ben Thanh at night.

Lots of stores selling same or similar things, also can't walk past without them trying to convince you to buy something which can get quite annoying after a bit. If you do decide to buy something, prices are negotiated.
Bon marché à Saïgon. Il y a quand même le choix entre le textile, l’alimentation et les souvenirs. Si vous êtes à Saigon, c’est toujours intéressant d’y aller faire un tour.
ベンタイン市場は、他の方が書いてある通り高めの値段設定になってます、が自分で価格交渉できる方にはお勧めしますが、全く海外特にアジア系がはじめての方にはおすすめは出来ません、 それでも思い出の一つとして価格の安いものでお買い物されてはいかがでしょうか、 ホーチミンには買い物出来る所がたくさんありますよ、

Having been to plenty of markets around Asia this one was nothing mind blowing. I usually love bartering and exploring each individual stalls but i found the majority of them selling near identical items and nothing that I wouldn't be able to get back home albeit at a higher price. If you have been to other markets in HK or SG, I wouldn't waste my time here.
A typical market. Testing your negotiation skill for a reasonable bargain. Please be sincere when bargain if you are keen to buy.
동대문 시장과 가락 수산 시장은 비교가 안 될 정도로 엄청난 호객 행위와 바가지.  바가지 쓰는 것조차 여행의 일부로 즐기시는 분들께는 추천해 드립니다.  정가를 알고 있어도 정가에 구매하는 것은 불가능에 가깝습니다.  시장 중간에서 할아버지가 파시는 생 과일 smoothie는 정말 맛있습니다.
Nothing special about this market, constant hassle as soon as you step through the doors. Many stalls/shops selling the same goods. Worth a look though .

Di tutti i mercati visitati in Vietnam questo è stato il peggiore, mi avevano già avvisata che a Saigon bisogna contrattare ma la mia esperienza è stata bruttina. Innanzitutto non ti è quasi permesso guardare i banchi perché non appena vedono un potenziale cliente cominciano a starti addosso, e se non li stai guardando ti chiamano e ti cercano di vendere qualsiasi cosa. Se guardi un banco devi dirgli cosa stai cercando perché se non lo hanno te lo vanno a cercare per potertelo vendere loro. Non è stata un’esperienza positiva. Purtroppo ho dovuto acquistare qui perché era la mia ultima tappa e per questione di valige e peso avevo deciso di prendere tutto a Saigon. Rispetto al mercato di Hue c’è molta meno roba. Cercavo una bambola tipica vietnamita, la ragazza al primo bancone mi ha bloccata dicendo di stare lì ad aspettarla che me la andava a prendere. Poco dopo è arrivata con la bambola e mi ha sparato un 380000 VND (15€!!) assurdo! Per poi vendermela a metà prezzo.. per chi odia contrattare e non ci è per niente abituato è un’esperienza orribile. In un altro banco volevo prendere dei biscotti.. sarò stata lì mezz’ora perché dovevano per forza farmi vedere mille cose. Sono stati molto (troppo) insistenti.

Lots of stores haver closed since the lock down but it's picking up.  Drink stores are friendly and worth it on a hot day 20k.  Tops range from 50-150k for the sportswear, anything over that, someone isnt as honest as you like.  My favourite snack/nuts store is still there.

Mercado enfocado principalmente para turistas. Cuenta con muchos puestos que te ofrecen todo tipo de productos locales y de imitación. Ropa, relojes, gafas, artesanía... Es muy caótico y agobiante. Los vendedores no te dejan en paz en ningún momento. Te aconsejo la máxima precaución posible con la cartera, tarjetas de crédito y pasaporte. Viajamos seis amigos y a una le robaron la cartera y la documentación mientras estaba comprando. Afortunadamente, gracias a Luxury Travel, nuestra agencia de tour vietnamita, todo se solucionó a tiempo de continuar el viaje. Quiero dar las gracias a nuestra guía Tam por todo su esfuerzo y dedicación. Sin ella nada hubiese sido igual.

Before pandemic 😷 crowded by tourists visiting city and buying souvenirs. During these days no tourist or very little local ones coming. Place without people looks very strange even when all sellers are at their stalls.

If your just visiting Asia then sure, we all love a deal and some knock off things. But to be honest I didn’t think this was any better than those found in other Asian countries such as Cambodia and Thailand. Impacted by Covid, but the range is all very same same. If you are on the larger size or shoe size of 43 and above, don’t get too excited as the options will be limited
Nói chung năm nào cũng đi coi vải ở đây. Vải mới có nhiều, cập nhật xu hướng nhanh. Có điều giá hơi cao nếu người mới đi mua, chưa biết cách nhắm tầm giá sản phẩm. Vải cao cấp có nhiều
traditional. lots of variety of things to buy. but the shop owner can be a little cranky. buy souvenirs here. it is cheaper than in other place

Nothing to get too excited about in this fairly typical market. We bought some coffee and a few bags. Since there were very few tourists when we visited in early March, the prices were quite reasonable (although we had to negotiate as the starting prices were ridiculous in many cases).
Nothing special if you’ve seen other markets in Vietnam. Easy to find, easy to walk around, still have to wear face masks on entering the market

This market has become quite the tourist draw, and as a result, the sellers are very pushy, trying to attract the most attention and the most sales. You won't get far inside the market without getting hassled. However, I noticed a distinct difference between the "inside" sellers and the ring of stores on the outside of the market. Seemingly more of a department store, there is a wide assortment of clothing, bags and suitcases, shoes, flowers, and souvenirs, and signs everywhere noting "fixed pricing". Therefore, no negotiations and no hassling. There are attendants in each section, but they are not pushing for a sale, they are there to help. As someone who gets easily overwhelmed in stressful situations, I found this option much more enjoyable, even though it may have cost me slightly more.

We were really looking forward to going to the Ben Thanh Market, and it was really interesting, however we just found it was super busy and you didn't have time to really look at anything as you were constantly being hasseled by the sellers. We were going through to look, and also wated lunch, which we did get from one of the restaurants theres, however in the end we just decided to go for whatever one we could as we we so flustered. I'd suggest visting, but maybe when you've had a little more experience in the country and not like us on our first day.

Everyone says this is where you have to eat authentic street food. Every white tourist seems to be here, which I found a little irritating. This was like Camden Market in London, lots of food counters and communal seating area. There didn't seem to be many Asians or locals here at all. More expensive in this market than at most independent street sellers.
Marknaden är öppen trots att mycket är stängt i HCMC! Ett fåtal turister endast i dessa tider med Corona o munskydd...

I like markets while traveling, especially if they cater mostly to local buyers. This one does, but seems to really target western tourists very aggressively.  Fruit, veg, and meet were all very exotic and certainly not up to USDA standards for display and sale. That said, all that I tried was awesome. We visited at the on-set of COVID, so there were masks everywhere (except on western visitors)... there was a bit of uncertainty in the air as this was late Feb.  I suggest you go early (it smells a bit later in the day as it warms up)  Try not to make eye contact with vendors you don’t want to interact with, you’ll enjoy it overall.
観光客が多いかと思ったのですが、意外とローカルの方も来ていました。 ただ、ベトナムにしては値段を高めに言われるので絶対に交渉してものを買うべきです。 服とかは交渉次第では安く買えますが、交渉が苦手な人はあまりお勧めできないです。 しかし、市場の雰囲気は、エネルギッシュで熱気があるので、楽しめるとは思います。

Found the market to be overwhelming. It could be a great market but in the areas where there is not a fixed price the sellers are on top of you. I personally hate this type of selling. As soon as it looked like you had the slightest interested in any product they were literally on top of you. I spent 1/2 hour there before having to get out.

Walked through Ben tanh market today with my wife. We were harassed by the vender ladies selling clothes. They grabbed my shirt and my shorts and said what you looking for, you buy from me? My wife couldn’t stand it so we left, I have been living in Vietnam in recent years and the ladies have been getting more aggressive of late, They are driving customers away with their behavior and higher prices. Many have told me they feel ripped off at Ben Tanh. Not good.

You have to haggle with the salesperson. A few can be convincing but buyer beware. Goods appeared cheap if you haggle. Mostly made in Vietnam or China. We bought a few items like coffee, and tea for personal consumption. Language is a barrier. Not many people speaks English. Most people are pleasant and friendly. Strong arm tactics by some salespeople. Just be firm. If you think your cost is a win-win, buy it. Comparisn shop first. Enjoy the market.

Don't go if you are at all claustrophobic or sensitive to the smell of rotting vegetable and fruit. The market is open all day so dont wait for 6pm. Nothing remarkable but if you like markets off you go....

5年おきぐらい、3度目の来訪で、1度目よりも2度目は客引きが多くなり、2度目よりも3度目は売っているものがつまらなくなりました……。  客引きは本当にしつこくて、全然集中して見られない！！ 強く引っ張られたりして怖い思いも。  しかも価格が高いです。昔はここでお土産を大量買いして、楽しかったのですが、今はラッキープラザのほうが安いし、落ち着いて見られます。  雰囲気はあるし、好きだったところなので残念です。よくなって欲しいですね。
Lots of clothing and food. Interesting to see but had to leave after being accosted every 5 seconds by the next stall.

I walked around here in 5 or 10 mins. Some people calling me over to their stalls. I didn't really need anything but I just wanted to have a look. All the usual market stuff. I think I've found better deals in Hanoi shops.

The place has many options but you need to be in the right mood to go. People scream at you trying to ger your attention, and try to overcharge tourists a lot (sometimes they start asking for 4x the fair price). Very stressful. Would have bought many more stuff if it wasn't so hard.
More of the same. Almost every store has the same items as the one before. So really nothing special.
迷路の様にお店が混み合っています。同じ感じのお店が多いので、品揃えは豊富と言えますが、値段が書いてないので交渉が必要です。

Como estaba cerca, pase a ver de lejos el El Palacio de la Reunificación, lugar donde el año 1975 se hizo entrega del poder al caer Saigón, cuando por sus puertas entró un tanque norvietnamita. En realidad no quería saber de guerra, por lo que me fui a tomar desayuno al mercado Ben Thanh, lugar donde encuentras de Todo!!! Jajjaja te wevean una y otra a vez para que les compres souvenirs y te dicen un precio y te lo bajan a la mitad o menos, es un chiste. Rusos y franceses comprando como locos.

The place is a bit sketchy, so you have to be aware of your belongings. Fun to check out as they sell a variety of interesting things remember to negotiate with the seller on the price or else they’d try to overcharge you as high as possible.

Lack of soul, over inflated prices and touristic trap: definitely a waste of time! I was so disappointed from the market: I was expecting a vibrant place (a little bit like the Old Quarter in Hanoi, or the market in Hoi An) in little streets full of surprises and charme instead of we were greeted from a covered market were everything is the same and without soul....no no no!

To be honest we found this a bit overwhelming, if you like look at 6,-7 types of stalls repeated throughout then it's for you. The stalls and sellers in the middle of the area are so close to you every single one asks(some tap you on shoulder) to see if you will look and buy. Which is fine but after 50th time it's annoying difficult to ignore and in effect they loose trade just because its not comfortable just to look and browse on please so you end up mot buying anything. If you do we were told by a guide to go upto 70% off the price they originally start with. I have to say thus is probably true if the couple of things we did buy this is about the discount we got.
本当に日本では考えられない程の臭いが… ヤバイです！ でもドライフルーツやパンプキン、ひまわりの種は安かったです。 値切ったら値切っただけ安くなりますね！ そのまんまの値段では買えないですね！ 品質はわからないけどベンタイン市場の後のナイトマーケットのほうがﾀﾞﾝｾﾞﾝ安いですね！

Bags and clothes can be gotten up to 25-40% of original quoted prices. Meaning 60-75% discount if you shop around and bargain hard.  Dried goods too can be gotten up to 50-80% of their original quoted prices or discount of 20-50%.  Easier and cheaper to buy in the market during the day than at the night market outside at night. Prices start higher at the night market.  Course there are so many stalls, take note the stall and location you may wish to come back to. Ask for their business card or take picture of their stall number and friendly faces.
Ehh. There are hundreds of markets like this over SE Asia, not bad but nothing special. Careful of pickpockets
Oftmals Fixpreise, unfreundliche Verkäuferinnen. Für Kleidung besser den Russian Market aufsuchen. Bei Kaffee immer handeln was geht und sagen, dass man das in Deutschland billiger bekommt.

Busy market but got fed up of being hassled , couldn’t look at anything because you would get asked comstatsnky if you wanted it and how much for. All the stalls repeated themselves and sold the same stuff . Ok but wouldn’t rush to go back .

A large square indoor market, inside you are met by row upon row of stalls selling many different things, mainly clothes in the part we entered. Each stall there is someone begging you to look and buy, they aren't aggressive or chasing you but after a few stalls it's all very repetitive. The stuff is so cheap and you can barter for a better price. It's big, hectic and its busy but make time to experience the place

Price are tripple higher then usaual..even u already make a bargain most place hve aggressive attitude to make u buy thier stuff..the only thing that stress me out shopping there..the rest is ok eating price still cheap

It was ok. It is entertaining to try and make your way through with all of the aggressive vendors. It is the firs market that I have been to where people actually grab your arm and try to get you into their stall. There are limited number of products. They are basically the same things in each stall. Most of it is made in China. My personal choice is to buy things that are really made by local people.
There wasn’t anything interesting, the price was a little higher and the stones were ok, they were all the same. The food was good tho.
You should be clever enough to purchase anything in this market , otherwise go directly to the fixed priced part as they mentioned to the prices without bargain .

Melkoinen paikka, tavaraa on tarjolla aivan törkeät määrät. Jos nauttii tinkimisestä ja kusetetuksi tulemisesta niin tämä on juuri sinun kohde. Jos maksat yli 1/3 pyyntihinnasta olet hölmö. Silloinkin maksat enemmän kuin pyyntihinnat ovat muualla maassa.  Toisaalta mikään ei voita sitä fiilistä kun tinkiii kunnolla ja jättää tuotteet kauppiaalle. Sitten vaan seuraavalle kojulle. Mikään ei ole niin kateellinen kuin kauppias.

Ben Thanh is an institution and absolutely must be on your list. However, beware that most prices you see at the market are 2-4x the costs you’ll find elsewhere in Saigon, making it a bit of a tourist trap. That said, I still recommend going and buying something for the experience.

This is busy marketing ! We saw a lot of tourists , obviously enjoying the crowded environment, and the food is very cheap ( not to mention the $1 cans of Heineken!) The sales pressure is a bit tiring, maybe I’m just not as into bargaining about prices as I used to be .......& there is only so much stuff you can buy and fit into a suitcase ? One visit was enough for me .....

Chợ Bến Thành ngay trung tâm quận 1 nên khá nổi tiếng với du khách nước ngoài nhưng từ quần áo, đồ lưu niệm, đồ ăn đều như các chợ truyền thống khác ở Việt Nam. Kể từ khi bạn bước vào, bạn sẽ bắt đầu cảm thấy muốn chạy trốn càng nhanh càng tốt vì quá đông, chật chội và mấy người bán hàng cứ cố gắng bám theo để bán bất cứ thứ gì cho bạn. Giá cả mọi thứ đều bị đẩy lên cao 60-70% do tâm lý bán cho người nước ngoài.

This is quite a large daytime indoor market (closes 6pm) - massive range of items to buy - clothing, bags, luggage, gifts, china, food etc etc. Be prepared to bargain and walk away, you will find the same item fo sale a few stalls away. There is also a “fixed price” area, however we found that as we walked away from the stall .prices came down quickly and they were open to offers
Large choix mais beaucoup d’arnaques si on est pas prudent. Prefere aller dans le district 5 bcp moins mais il faut savoir parler vietnamien
Ok fake market, nothing more or less. If you like to bargain and to get some souvenirs this is the place. Quality is not the best I’ve seen for bags and t-shirts.

Some vendors here are VERY aggressive! Make sure you are REALLY interested in the item before asking for the price! I spotted a couple of beautiful Buffalo horn serving spoons that caught my eyes. I asked the saleswoman for price. She happily took me inside her shop and showed me the price tag: “One is 600,000 ($26), but NO! I give you CHEAPER! How many you want?” I told her I wanted three. “Ok, Ok, I gave you three for 1,500,000 ($65)!” It’s more than what I wanted to spend, so I politely declined and was about to take my leave. Here is where thing got interesting -- she BLOCKED my exit! Her shop was piled high with merchandise, with only a narrow walkway in the middle. And there she stood. I was never afraid because my husband was just outside, but her action irked me.  “How much you want buy?” she demanded. I said three for 500,000 ($21.65). I saw her flinch. “560” she said grudgingly. Now the difference of 60,000 VND is only $2.65 and I would’ve happily paid it, but this woman was getting on my nerves. “500” I repeated. After some hesitation, she gave in and stepped aside to bag my spoons.  I felt bad that she wasn’t smiling any more, but I was happy! (Each spoon is carved out of single Buffalo horn and is heat resistant. I can use it in cold or hot dishes.) This exchange is what I remember most from my visit to Ho Chi Minh City back in December.
Passa på att fylla resväskan med kläder och sovernier. Bra prutmån 1/3 av priset inga problem, börja gå därifrån och ni får till en bra deal.

The thing about Asian tourist markets is they are pretty much identical. Same same but different. I like to browse quietly but they like to give a hard sell. So I dont often stay and end up avoiding most of it as a hassle. Go if you need to get gifts and go to the non fixed price placescand bargain hard.
雑貨や飲食物、飲食店など様々なものがあります。一つの市場に色々とありますが、値段は観光客価格なのかお得感は薄い。下げれるところまで下げたのですが…街中のスーパーで買った方が値段交渉もしなくて済むし、値段も同じか少し安かったです。
It is a market for souvenirs and other things but you will have to bargain hard.Not a very satisfying experience.Do a lot of enquiries and then buy.

Es un mercado sucio, lleno de malos olores y obvio lleno de gente. Es justo lo que debe ser un mercado de estos. Se va porque es parte de los imperdibles de la ciudad. La parte de las carnes y mariscos llama la atención por la cantidad de cosas que se ven.

Kann man einmal besucht und gesehen haben. Es gibt viel Bekleidung teilweise auch in akzeptabeler Qualität. ( Plagiate ) Es ist sehr eng und man wird jeden Meter von irgendwenn angesprochen. Eine jede Menge Schnickschnack und Kram welchen man an jeder Ecke bekommt. Wer auf Kitsch steht, ist hier gut aufgehoben. Empfehlenswert ist der Abendliche Nachtmarkt, dieser hat nocheinmal einen anderen Flair. Gekauft habe ich nichts aber ich war jeden Abend dort um das Treiben zu beobachten.
Bra marknad med massa krims krams och kläder i en salig blandning. Det mesta som finns är väl importerade lågpris kläder.

とても広い市場です。街中で売っているお土産と変わらないものが売っています。客引きが多いですが無視すれば回避できます。足を止めてしまうと腕を掴まれたりするのでゆっくりと歩いて回るのがいいかもしれません。同じものが売っている店舗が何件もあるのでぐるっと1周したほうがいいと思います。欲しいものはまとめ買いで値引き交渉しました。強気な値引き交渉でも少し時間はかかりますが以外とこちらの提示金額で受けてくれるお店が多いと思います。実際私は提示金額の1/3くらいの金額から交渉を始めました。日本語が通じるお店がほとんどです。なので値引き交渉も日本語で大丈夫です。市場内は半分は衣料品や雑貨、半分は食品でした。食品の方は匂いがキツいので苦手な方もいるかもしれません。私は匂いがダメで食品の方は行きませんでした。

I do not care for this type of shopping. The foods area was interesting due to the great variety and difference in items from the ones we have at home. Beyond that it is crowded noisy and intimidating. I do not care to be hassled to buy something and despise trying to bargain. It is as if the seller does not want you to pay a reasonable price. This market was included in three of our excursions. Once was more than enough.

It was an experience to visit but very warm and crowded. It is split into different sections which was helpful (although I only read this after we had been). Generally has everything tourists might be looking for. There are stores with fixed prices around the outside of the market, I would suggest checking the prices here so you know the minimum price to pay inside. Make sure you pay at least half of the price they tell you inside the market. I purchased a pineapple shirt and shorts, they initially told me 600k but I ended up paying 180k!! If you don’t like bartering then walking away is an effective method! The smoothies and juices were nice but we received old and dry ‘fresh’ spring rolls which was disappointing

Grande mercato coperto al centro di Ho Chi Min. Forse tra tutti quelli visitati nelle capitali asiatiiche è quello che abbia o trovato meno interessante. Prezzi non proprio bassissimi e sinceramente poco caratteristico. Vale la pena visitarlo ma senza aspettarsi troppo.
吃的、用的，什麼都有的市場，但名牌商品都很明顯的不是正品。到這裡吃東西，買零食手信還可以，但只是個到此一遊的地方，不宜逗留太久。
Skip it, so much more to see. Alternatively only shop at the Blue badge fixed price stores around the edge.

A crowded place. A lot of goodies. Don't accept the first price they offer, keep bargaining, that's how it's done here! Be very very careful of your belongings. A lot of bag snatchers. Try not to take a big bag with you.

Neither of us are much in to shopping, so we’re probably not the target market! We picked up a couple of t shirts for gifts, but it’s the same touristy stuff you’ll find anywhere. Generally seems to be about triple the ‘real’ price as a starting point. You will be grabbed and harassed to some degree, like in most markets in SE Asia, so be warned. If you’re around here, check it out, if not, you won’t have missed anything🤷‍♂️
少し有機がいるようなところでしたが、活気ああります。 ドリアンを買って初めて食べてみました。 微妙でしたが、やはり美味しかったかな。

Haggling was quite challenging and the price are quite expensive to start with, but can get only a lil cheaper if you purchase at the fixed price section. Some vendors are actually quite friendly and easy to do business with. Would recommend their stall but can't seems to remember their location.

C'est l'inévitable marché central de la ville : vêtements, souvenirs, café, restaurants, etc. Tout y est mais il faut savoir négocier âprement si on veut faire des affaires.  Les restaurants testés étaient plutôt bons dans l'ensemble.  A faire rien que pour l'ambiance typique qui y règne.

This is a market frequented by tourists. I was told it is a popular symbol of Ho Chi Minh City. It is a combination of wet and dry market. You can buy many things (local food, clothing, souvenir, handicraft, etc) but expect a higher price since visitors are mainly foreigners.  Don’t expect easy communication with the sellers so a calculator will come in handy. What’s unique about this place is that in some areas, price is fixed; there is no room for haggling.
９月２３日公園からベンタン市場近くの宝石商ハタムで両替をしたあと、市場に立ち寄る。いつものことながら特に買い物をするわけでもない。正面の入り口から入り、北口へと抜ける。客引きの間をぬって歩く。北側の花売り場やコーヒー豆売り場を覗いてみるが、やはり何も買わなかった。
시장의 중심부는 딜을 해야하는 가게들이 있는데 의류, 식품, 장신구 등 다양하다. 시장의 외곽은 고정가격의 가게들이 있는데 딜 하기 피곤하면 갈만하다. 여기서 컵받침 살려했는데 4개에 450만동을 부르더니 350에서 250 까지 내려갔음. 근데 현지에서 살았던 친구에게 물어보니 컵받침 개당 10만동이 실제 가격이라 함 참고 하길 바람
I guess it's inevitable there's such a market here. Many stalls and goods all competing for your tourist dollar. Sore that I ended up paying twice the amount (outside) for some coffee.

Positives - you can negotiate a looot. But the stuff is the same as everywhere else. It's very overcrowded by stalls and tourists and loosing the character. I like the night market outside the hall much better.

Lots of copies of big brand items, souvenirs and also some local textiles.  Be sure to haggle - you shouldn't pay more than 50% of original asking price and may do as well as only paying 30%.  Be aware, if you don't like being accosted this probably isn't the place for you!

Nice market to wander around if you are up to it, lots of average souvenirs at a fixed price. Very touristic, we have seen nicer markets in other places though.  Leuk om in rond te struinen als je van markten houdt. Veel prul souvenirs, afdingen is er niet bij. Wij hebben elders leukere markten gezien.

Same kind of market as all over south east Asia, fake goods, trinkets and general tat all while being hassled just for looking in their general direction,but still ended up as always coming away with stuff. Place to visit if you want souvenirs to take home.
Det er noget man skal opleve. MANGE boder til gode priser, men hvis man er træt af “hello madam, hello Sir” så skal man ikke tage derind. De vil sælge så derfor er de meget pågående.

All sorts of items from clothes,pants,bags,accessories and souvenirs sold here. Before buying know roughly the real price because the sellers there will give you ridiculous prices for it. They will also hassle you as you pass by their shop. Don't be too imposing coz they will curse you if you decided not to buy. I can understand that they are desperate to get sales but at times it seems scary. I have to admit that I had quite a few good buy although I didn't really bargain. Some sellers are quite nice and compromising.
I found the markets to be typically crowded and stuffy . Food section was where most of the hassling was done . Over all it’s a market so you get what you expect

Very hot in day and close at night. But outside is night market, very rowdy n crowded. Careful with bag n wallet. Bargains place, but they dont like ppls touching, can be quite annoying and aggressive sales persons.

Nearby many hotels. From Platinum hotel it's about 5-10 minutes walk. They sell food and drinks. Many souvenir shops. Late afternoon and night is the best time to shop. Shops there sell souvenirs and imitation bags. There is also a wet market beside it. Don't settle for first price. If you walk away they give you a lower price.  My friend bought some fake pearl earrings.
Merce contraffatta su decine e decine di banchetti tutti uguali, insieme a negozi di argenteria, cibo e souvenir. Se interessati, contrattare e scendere anche della metà rispetto al prezzo richiesto.

Ben Thanh Market is a very touristy place but worth a visit if this is your first time in Ho Chi Minh City. There are plenty of food stalls that serve local cuisine. Prices are relatively more expensive than shopping malls food court.

Fake market with that quality. The price/quality ratio is not bad (if you get more than 50% discount minimum) but don't expect the original quality. I've bought a backpack and the zip had broken after 2 days. I have experience with the clothing section only.
Very disappointing and quality not very good - when you were actually allowed to view, as hassled so much to buy, before you even looked !!!
Hot and touristy. Many food stalls but they are offering more or less similar dishes. Should perhaps have more variety in terms of local specialties.

Came on a Sat night around 6pm and everything was closing up. We went to eat at the street market and had a massage then came back to walk the night market. Lots of cheap junk, pushy vendors and honestly, not much to buy. We love shopping in the night markets of Asia but honestly, this was just garbage. Walked 2 streets, bought nothing and went back to our hotel.

Thanks to the digital age, with all the write ups, photos and videos of the place it has generated interest and created a overhype of the place, but in reality there is nothing really special about it.  It is just a typical wet market, where there is a live meat and fish, vegetable, dry goods (including coffee, preserved food etc), clothing and food sections all nicely layout in their specific zone. Such markets used to be a norm and can be found in many Asia countries until the arrival of the supermarkets.  But it has become too commercialized especially at the clothes and souvenirs section where stall holders will actively engaged visitors to purchase their goods. Do be careful with your purchase as some product’s quality are questionable. Here you may not always get the best deal if unfamiliar with local pricing (having gotten the best bargain over a price with a store owner may not get you the Best Buy)  All being said it is still an eye opener for those that have not experienced such ‘organized chaos’ or just wants the thrill of haggling over a price of a product. The place will hit all your senses at the same time with its sound, smell and sight. But it definitely should not be on a Must Do list.

This place is absolutely huge it's so many market stores crammed into one massive building we found the shopping not to be that good unless you are a size 8 there were a lot of things to look at but after a while they were all the same same
特に買い物がないのであまり楽しくはなかったです。市場の雰囲気はいいけど現地の人も注意しないとスラれる危ない所。
生物やお土産、衣服などなんでも揃う市場。 最初はかなりお値段高めを言ってきますが、交渉で下がっていきます。 まとめて買えば交渉しやすぃです。  お店によっては17時頃閉め始めてしまぃます。
東南アジアのマーケットです！観光がてらと言った雰囲気！！ サーーーっと見れば雰囲気味わえます。中に食事できるエリアと、衣類エリア、食材も細かく、乾物エリア、コーヒーエリアなどなど別れてるので、目的を持って行かないと疲れてしまうかもしれないです^_^
For Tourist Many Shops with all what you want Bags food flowers Open morning Prices ok but water bottle 20 coconut 20

Si vous cherchez du textile vous êtes au paradis cependant si cherchez de l’authentique avec de la nourriture et autres ce n’est pas le bon endroits. Cette endroit été à voir mais nous avons été un peu déçu du lieu

most stalls sell much of the variety of imitation stuff (like Under A**, Suoer**, Adi**), local food & drinks, dried foodstuff like nuts & coffee powder, fruits. nice old building if you are a keen negotiator, plenty to buy. even a money changer inside but there is a popular money changer just outside.

Went 2 times while in Ho Chi Minh in early December 2019. Aaron our guide from a previous tour recommend that we end up paying approximately 60% of the vendors quoted price and it actually worked. Vendors will markup non food items with the anticipation that you'll want to barter. Don't be afraid walk away because their are many vendors selling the same things. Down side is all the begging outside and around the entrances. If you have a hard time saying "no", just carry a bunch of 1,000 Dong notes. It's not much, but it will help you on your way. Have fun!

Local Market with food, drinks, fruit, clothing, suites, belts, caps, polo’s, t-shirts, shoes, Vietnamese souvenirs ... you name it.  So, this is the place to test your Test your negotiation skills.  The Ben Thanh Market is Saigon’s oldest landmarks. And a fun but crowdy place to visit.  Tip: Carry your backpack on your chest. Beware of pickpocketing.

This market is very similar to many other Asian markets that sell knock offs. It's not the largest I've visited but has everything you would want from fake purses to souvenirs to tacky clothing. Banana shirts and pants here sell at 100k vnd each piece, but they start at 350k when they tell you the price.

Fun shopping experience- but be ready to keep moving- high pressure selling which we found overwhelmingly as we weren’t prepared for the onslaught.  There is a fixed price area which we found on the perimeter where we were not hit on.  Too highly pressured to buy to enjoy browsing.  Visited other markets In Saigon & didn’t experience such high pressure selling. I guess it’s very touristy.
물건은 사지는 않았지만, 과일을 사는데도 고객에게 현가에 50% 이상 판매하는곳.. 알아서 조심을하던지 딜을 잘하셔야합니다..개인적으로 반은 먼저깎고가심이 나을거같습니다.
Classico mercato turistico come se ne vedono tanti altri in asia. Niente di particolare. Un giro vale la pena farlo.

The stall holders are very pushy, the prices start high but you can bargain down, if you don’t like the price don’t worry there will be a similar stall close by. There is a Malaysia street just outside that sells the same souvenirs cheaper in RM. Avoid if you are claustrophobic! Some of the knock offs were decent quality.

Wir waren auch diesmal auf dem Ben Than Markt auf dem es wirklich alles zu kaufen gibt und man handeln sollte. Diesmal waren wir super genervt, nicht ein Schritt ohne Anfassen, Ansprechen und Hinterherrufen. Wäre auch kauflustig gewesen für spezielle Dinge, aber es war so nervig, dass wir geflüchtet sind, nie mehr wieder.

The shops are open till sunset and within no time of closing ths shops, another market pops up in the perimeter of the market. Most of the shops bargaining is the norm; however, some shops have fixed prices of items they sell. No bargaining board is displayed above those shops. The place is congested and not very clean. The traffic adjoining the market is very congested and terrifying to the visitors.

A good place to visit the local market in Ho Chi Minh City. It offers a wide range of products from Vietnam starting from food materials to fabrics and coffee. Its a place where u need to bargain a lot.
東南アジアの市場の雰囲気を味わえます。 所狭しと並べられた、衣類や雑貨。 値札がついておらず、言い値はぼったくり価格。 必ず交渉して下さい。 市場で飲食も可能です。

The markets aren’t huge but they are big enough to get a feel for the chaos of sounds and smells that a good market provides😌. A plus for us was that we were able to set our young teens free with a small amount of money and an hour. We knew they couldn’t get lost and they had a great time. I agree with the other reviews that it’s overpriced and even after haggling hard and getting an item down 75% you pay more than you should.

Having travelled some of SE Asia already, this was just another market to tick off. In comparison to markets such as the Ladies Market in Hong Kong, this was at best average. Of course if you're just visiting HCMC alone then I'd say it's worth going for the experience, but if you've visited other markets then you'll find that Ben Thanh is even more-so crowded and overpriced than many. There was hardly room to move between stalls and everyone is very pushy with trying to get sales; don't expect to be able to browse anything peacefully for more than two seconds. And as aforementioned, prices were too high in my opinion, even with haggling, items such as generic key rings would start at 150K VND for a single one?! I could get a key ring cheaper in central London... Even after haggling, many sellers would only go so far as about 50K VND; in perspective, I got the same key ring as the above prices quoted for 20K VND away from the market (from a street stall). In conclusion, go if you haven't experienced the Asain markets, but if you're well travelled then don't expect anything too special.
ホーチミンの名物市場です。 とにかく人と商品が多いです。 食品と衣類のお店が多いように感じました。 地元の方によると、ここで食品は買わないほうがよいとのこと。それは賞味期限切れやギリギリのものが多いからと言っていました。 特に買うものもなく、ひやかしで見て回りましたが、想像通り客引きが凄かったです。 市場の近くにはバックパッカー街があります。

Pasarnya luas dan menjual dengan bisa di tawar setengah lebih harga, hanya saja semua pedagang tidak sopan karena menyentuh dan menarik-narik kami yg berkunjung dan membuat tidak nyaman. Tp banyak produk premium dan bisa di beli disini

Loved the range of goods available, but found it hard to browse because of the insistent stallowners. Could only stay for an hour or so before having to get out. The night market outside was a lot more enjoyable, there was a different vibe and less crowding.
Un marché dans le secteur 1 donc bien situé mais rien de particulier. Alimentation et habillement, souvenirs. On n’y trouve que de l’ habituel
Good market, fairly moderate pricing can be negotiated here. People not as pushy here as they are at night market in Hanoi. But it’s not as big as Hanoi’s market either

Didn’t love this market - a lot of the same things and the sellers were a bit more aggressive than anywhere else we had been in Vietnam. It was also a little more expensive too and a lot of the stalls were the same

These markets had a good range of products but were very expensive compared with Hoi An. Even when you barter with them, hard to get down to comparative price with other areas. Also need to be safety conscious here.

This market is CROWDED and has tons of cheap stuff. Some sellers can be a bit aggressive. Many stalls have the exact same items so if you don’t get the price you want, just walk away to other 15 choices. Say, mah-quah (too expensive)! Raw food not in US sanitary conditions. Meat not kept on ice, flies on meat, shrimps washed in buckets of dirty water. We did not eat or buy this food. Dried spices are plentiful and nice. Lots of pretty tea choices.

有名なベンタイン市場ですが、市場のおばさんと価格交渉が好きな人は良いと思うのですが、私には面倒です。結局は価格提示された1／2か1／3くらいの価格まで下がるのですが、コピー商品にそんなにエネルギーを注ぎ込まなくてもと思ってしまう様です。気がついたことですが価格をドンとドルと円で言い分けてくる強かなおばさんがいます。特にドルと円は若干の差が生じますのでドルも持っている人はよく考えて買った方が良いです。お釣もドルで買ったらドルでお釣りもらった方が円をドルに両替時の手数料を考えると良いのではと思います。

Was fun walking around here looking at the myriad of stalls selling all manner of things. But the market is very touristy and the initial quoted prices being initially very high with the expectation of bartering to a lot lower final price, but to be honest, for most of the tat that is sold here it’s not worth the time or the effort.
Il mercato centrale di Ho Chi Mihn. Grande e caotico non lo ho trovato particolarmente interessante. Rimane un posto adatto per acquistare ricordi.

As other have said this is just one market and others maybe more suitable. Price is fixed around the perimeter as you walk in; identified by blue shirt sales people. Inner core makes up much more of the market and you need to bargain and bargain with knowledge and be firm. Be prepared to walk away, especially if you are just getting started. Be aware that other stalls are offering the same stuff so you can go back and forth to get a lower price. Some products are left overs from previous seasons. Knowing this helps with lowering the price. Be prepared to walk away and even give up the purchase. Some vendors will come back with a better deal.

First time i saw this market like pasar pagi in my hometown, many product you can find, like souvenir, jewellery, food, etc If you want to buy something, you must bargain, but just my advice if you want bargain, you can said : "can i get the price for 2 items, what offer for me" Why, you said like that, cause just make a seller not mad for your bargain the price 😊

Grand marché avec une multitude d'articles alimentaires ou non des vêtements et de la contrefaçon ! Des fruits de l'arbre ou de mer ,des fleurs,des objets artisanaux.. Comme touriste vous vous faîtes racontés à chaque stand mais souvent en anglais et avec le sourire...énormément de choix!

This market sells everything. But is actually just another souvenir market and everything for shoppers to bargain. For tourists, it's up to you how to negotiate the price. In term of the place itself, it's quite warm and crowd. Perhaps you can bring your own fan hand. The seller will persuade you to buy and quite annoyed but it's not a big deal for me because I don't touch anything that I don't want to buy. In fact, I ended up not buying anything from this place. Different country, different culture so I don't really mind with the seller behavior. That's how their way of selling things anyway.
I give average... Full of many souvenirs But you must haggle... Crazy sellers trying to cheat you constantly... Price is a bit high... You must bring it down

A couple of colleagues of mine invited me to join them going on a shopping spree to the local market, and I did joined. The market is quite big relevant to the shop sizes, which average 2m by 2m with a handful of shops that are much larger than that. Haggling for the sellers is a must and a sport, however some sellers may pull your arm if you turn to leave, but the majority respect your personal space.
Esta bien para ir a pasar un rato. Comida, souvenirs y ropa, imitaciones, etc. en todo el pais venden lo mismo, a las afueras tambien hay tiendas que luego a la noche las mueven a una calle.

お店がひしめきあっている感じです。 おねーさん、お土産あるよー。 何ほしい？って、どこ歩いてても話しかけられ ちょっとウザいかも(´･ω･ `)  好きな人も多いみたいだけど 交渉がめんどくさい人はNGです。  ドンコイのお店は プライスカードがあるので安心かも。。  朝早く行くと 朝市の様子が見られました。 新鮮な野菜、魚、肉など 地元の人が買い物しています。 こっちのほうが見てて楽しかったかな♡  でも行く価値はあります。

I’m from South east asia, so this market seems to be the typical markets I’ve been too. A bit pricey and too touristy. I felt like I was over charged for everything I bought even after I bargained. Would recommend any convenience store or supermarket. Pretty much the same product except better packaging in supermarkets

Found the markets overwhelming with people constantly grabbing you and shouting at you while squeezing your way down the walk ways. Found other markets throughout the city were slightly cheaper and much easier to barter with. Worth the visit but certainly wouldn’t be expecting to buy much!
Have been here many times and it is not the bargain it used to be. And unless you know how to barter it is not worth it.

Tourist trap with lots of typical souvenir stuff many l stalls that sell the same stuff with purses, food ,and so on, price s is marked very high and you really need to bargain , very pushy When asking price.

広大な建物内には何でもあります。市民と観光客で溢れています。市場内での食事も、まさにベトナムを感じる事が出来ます 。肉・野菜・果物・雑貨等、何でも調達出来ます。市民の日常を感じる事が出来ます。ホテルから2～3分で歩いて行けたので 、果物を何度か買いに行きました。深夜の暗がりは要注意です。特にスマホは狙われるから注意するよう、ホテルスタッフからアドバイスがありました。市場前のカフェで飲んだ、アイスライチティーが美味しかったです。

I could have been asleep for 20 years and still wouldn’t know what has changed at Ben Thanh markets. Same trinkets, same cheap coffee including weasel coffee (fake) same BaBaBa beer t-shirts and of course polo shirts. But it’s a bit of fun to walk around and sometimes there are some bargains to be had. Maybe tradition should prevail but it would be great if there was something new and exciting. If it’s your first visit to HCMC then go but if you’ve been before it’s same same same.

Auf diesem Markt kannst du viele Klamotten, Schuhe und Souvenirs kaufen. Mit durchschlendern und umsehen ist hier aber nicht. Man wird durchgehend angesprochen und aufgehalten was zu kaufen. Uns war das zu anstrengend.

Lot of fake products. Go for a Super market. Don't spend ur money in this place. Good for visit. Not for purchase. If you purchase you must double check the price. Also remember to calculate balance money.

Probably a good rainy day activity. Crowded vendors hawking goods. Which seem to be the same after awhile. Lots of clothing, shoes, bags, electronics, watches, etc. Cheap quality. Vendors are constantly asking you to buy something and it becomes annoying. I found it hard to browse and ended up buying nothing. Mostly merchandise and very little food (perishables and non-perishables). Watch your wallet as it can be quite crowded.

I visited this market because is in the list of the placed you "must" see, but I was not impressed, the market has dozen of similar shops and the prices are not the best, you can find cheaper shops in other section of the town. Looking inside the market is seems most visited by tourist than local people. One hour maximum walking through is enough to get the sense of the place.

Good for some souvenirs with good prices. They try to pressure on you to buy things all the time. The corridors are very tiny and sometimes you have to get some patience to cross between the stores and the people.

Le marché alimentaire reste correct... les boutiques de souvenirs en revanche sont à éviter. Contrefaçon proposée dix fois le prix où ont vous les vends après 10 mm de marchandage. Et quand vous regarder de près vous les laissez sur places tellement elles sont grossières. Vendeuses agressives contrastant fortement avec ce que peut offrir ce magnifique pays en terme de gentillesse de ses habitant. À traverser très vite si vous passez par la, si vous le manquez n’ayez pas de regrets

This is not for the faint-hearted. I was OK in the Cu Chi tunnels but this place began to make me feel claustrophobic after a while. It is crowded and hot, you have to bargain hard and it is not uncommon for a vendor to grab your wrist (way too firmly) and not let go. This can feel very confronting but it’s all just part and parcel of venturing in here. On the upside, it is colourful and noisy and can be fun if you take it all in your stride.  The buying procedure seems to follow a fairly standard process at all the stalls. They will nominate a laughably, ridiculously high price to start with, then ask you how much you want to pay and whip out a calculator for you to tap out a counter offer. It’s best to have already decided what you want to pay before you even approach the stall. Next there will be a bit of back and forth. If their price is still too high, then walk away. At this point they will either call out another price or follow you. If they don’t, you’ll know your price was too low. After bargaining, we found that we paid probably between 50% - 30% of the original asking price.  Most of the stuff here is the same as other markets. Expect to find copy handbags and backpacks, fake Polo shirts, T-shirts, belts, wallets, sunglasses, caps and Vietnamese souvenirs, especially shell and lacquerware. The copy bags are not good quality. Zippers and linings in the bags and backpacks are cheap and and probably won’t last long. It’s worth taking that into account when deciding how much you are happy to pay. You will find lots of Vietnamese souvenirs and gifts to take back home. My favourite purchase was a whole lot of bookmarks for my book club.  There is a large food section, although I’d recommend going outside and finding the Ben Thanh Street Food Market, which is a separate venue with great food just up the road.

A must do when in Ho Chi Min City but be prepared for the chaos and pestering by market traders. Very crowded with little space between stalls selling the normal cheap stuff and plenty to pick from. Traders can be very aggressive so it pays to take a hard line and keep walking if not buying.
많이들 가시겠지만~  흥정은 상인이 잡지 않을정도로 깍으시고~ 반팔은 10불이하 스포츠백은 30불 이하.  상인이 공격적 성향이 있으니 놀라지 마세요.
買い物、食事が楽しめます。 観光客はすごく高い値段で最初言われるので、値引きは絶対にしてください。半額以下になる事が多いです。 店員も日本語も堪能な方が多いです。
Good for local souvenirs but not real friendly and no one seemed interested to bater. I'm sure there would be better places to visit in this city. Do your shopping in Hoi An prior to this city
Targ jest istnym labiryntem wąskich uliczek, gdzie można kupić dosłownie wszystko! No i koniecznie trzeba się targowac to podstawa!
Interesting market, was defenetly worth a visit. But a lot of items were expensive unless you are good at bargening
食べ物、洋服、雑貨なんでも売っています。客引きは意外とあっさりしていてそこまでしつこくありません。値切り交渉は必須ですので相場を調べてから行った方がいいです。相場を調べずに適当に値切ったのですが、もっと値切れたと後からわかりました、、。ベトナムドンがなければ円でも買えます。
Sono ben altri i mercati asiatici da visitare. Questo è mercato per turisti. La maggior parte sono bancarelle di abiti contraffatti
市場裡面賣許多各式各樣的乾糧，不外乎越南有名的腰果，水果乾，還有許多衣服跟紀念品，第一次拜訪越南的人可以來看看，但如果真的要逛街就不推薦了！

HUGE flea market with everything. (8am -6pm). Designer dupe bags, perfumes, jackets, & more. You must price negotiation, they start high.  This market is indoors with no air conditioning. It was HOT & the lack of ventilation became overwhelming at times. The areas around food court smells terrible.  Recommend finding a good vendor that gives you good pricing, and tell them what you are wanting to purchase. Get their stall number and tell them you will be back the next day. They will go find the stuff, negotiate good prices and have it available for you when you return. Just remember the Stall Number so you can return to the same person. This method worked well for me and prevented a massive heat stroke.
On est alpagués sans arrêt par les vendeuses et difficile de s’en défaire. Y aller pour voir l’ambiance mais privilégier des achats plus relax dans les rues voisines.
Pasar tradisional di Saigon. Suasana ramai. Segala macam barang kebutuhan hidup ada disini. Harga relatif murah.
A pushy and sometimes overwhelming market, but you can find decent bargains here. It can get very hot, unlike Saigon Square nearby.

Lots of variety of dried foodstuffs like nuts, fruits, herbs, and others. Good place to go if you want to get nuts, here offers reasonable price. Lots of bags, shoes, clothes, cloths and accessories but quality is subjective. Have to bargain. Love the variety of cooked local food and drinks offered here.

1st time in Saigon went to the much rave about Ben Thanh Market. came across shop 1059 gate east no 12.I think owner name is Diem. Lady boss was very friendly to my mum thus thought ok buy a set of coffee from her. Asked for the price she quoted me 120000 vietnamese dong. As market was just opened i ask if can discount to 100000 dong she said no. With purchase in hand i walked out and otw out to entrance to GRAB back to hotel, i saw a stall selling exact same product at 64000 dong. I went back to the original stall and lady boss insisted it was different until no choice i had to wait for her friend who speak better english but he was serving another customer who was buying alot of coffee. I was very tempted to tell that guest not to buy from this dishonest stall but decided to hold my tongue. Stood there waited for HALF AN HOUR for the person to complete transaction and came back to me. The young chap said it was different but i showed him the picture, the gram and number of packets were the same how different can it be. Knowing that they were in the wrong, he say he give me back the money in full. Of course i gave him back the product and went out to buy from the other stall.
Il mercato risulta essere un insieme di bancarelle con prodotti molto simili tra loro e non sempre di buona qualità

Passage plus ou moins obligé. Trop touristique... A noter qu'il est en "2 parties" : zone intérieur où il faut négocier (diviser par 3 ou 4 pour commencer), couronne extérieure avec ses prix fixes (reconnaissable par la tenue bleue ses vendeurs).

Doesn’t have that much variety, almost everyone sells the same things.  You can buy T-shirts, dried fruit, coffee sneakers, hats, magnets, wallets and straw wallets, all the usual stuff. They sell “hard rock” T-shirt
Tourist will be dragged and chased to buy items that is extremely overpriced but good to look at the local delicacies, need to haggle for discounts even up to 50%. Too crowded as well in the morning.

For starters, if you’ve been to one Asian market, you’ve kinda been to them all. What makes the Ben Thành market different, is its outer ring of shops. These are the government shops with much of the same merchandise that’s in the inner market. The catch, there’s no bargaining and items are clearly marked with prices. I would suggest walking the outer ring first and pricing items out. Then walk the inner market and bargain for a better price. As with most markets, start out around 50% of what they ask and go from there. The prepared food there is good and what you’d expect to find in the market or street side.

Not one of the best in the world, but okay for cheap stuff. Too much hassle from stallholders, and street vendors. Grossly inflated prices for tourists, if you can haggle, then good. If not, walk away. It is a big place, so many vendors to choose from. Very hot and humid inside.

A great place to get souvenirs, but be prepared to co stably be heckled by the owners to stop in and buy something. There were, however, good food options and plenty of fun souvenirs choices. We found the prices on the outside of the market were lower than actually going in to the building.
アジアらしい活気のある観光地化している市場です。 客引きは特にしつこくはなく普通程度でイヤな気にはなりません。 最初は値段をふっかけてきます。 まとめて買えばディスカウントしてくれます。 広いですが扱っている商品は大体同じなので一度立ち寄れば満足という感じです。

This market is now more designed for tourists so expect to pay more then other places in the country. There are some locals but your better off at dan sinh market or other markets for a better price!!

Marché surprenant où l'on trouve de tout, nourriture comme des souvenirs. Il fait très chaud a l'intérieur. C'est un grand marché de fruits et légumes pour les locaux d'un côté, des stands de souvenirs de l'autre et de la restauration où on peut manger des phô à prix pas chers et bons. Seuls inconvénients, des que tu mets un pieds dedans beaucoup de rabattage c'est assez pénible.

Be prepared for hawkers outside market particularly the men who ride the Xich Lo (rickshaw bike)! They will offer to take you to the Hong Kong Market which is a 3.0 klm ride from the Ben Thành Market! If you want this experience make sure you bargain the price for return trip or just the single trip to the Hong Kong Market  Ben Thành Market is busy and hot but you can still have fun bargining for all sorts of goodies!!great for souvenirs

Ganz netter Markt, aber mit viel zu hohen Preisen für die angebotene Ware. Wer von Ho-Chi-Minh noch weiterreist, sollte sich den Kauf von Souvenirs hier wirklich überlegen und es lieber in Hanoi oder Hoi An versuchen. Ansonsten kriegt man hier sicherlich einen Eindruck vom regen Treiben, insbesondere die Obst und Fleisch/Fischstände waren interessant. Von fermentierten Bananen bis zur gut gefälschten Handtasche bekommt man hier alles.

the place to go if you want to get souvenirs. you can get a variety of goods here. from nuts, coffee, tshirts. even a meal too! dont be afraid to bargain.  stall holders are more willing to offer discounts at the start of the day as the first sale is supposedly good luck.  stalls in the middle are cheaper than those on the outskirts
東南アジア的な市場です。 東西南北の入口にそれぞれ装飾がされています。 雑多に品物がうず高く積まれ怪しげな雰囲気を漂わせています。食品も食あたりしそうな気がして手が出なかったです。 ガイドさんはスリやひったくりに気をつけるように忠告してくれました。 交渉上手やタフな人向けだと思います。

So many things to see here but I was somehow nervous to ask questions because once you do it will be hard to leave empty handed even if you don't want to buy.  I asked for the price of the buffalo horn spoon and fork. Said it was 350,000 VND. Haggled and bought it for 120K but the seller was not very happy with me. Another seller was willing to sell for 110K and even chased me screaming ok ok 110K!

어느나라를 가든 그 곳의 시장을 방문한다는 것은 재미있는 일입니다. 그 곳의 특산물, 기념품, 공산품 및 주요 농산물이 무엇인지 알 수 있으며 그들의 생활상을 엿볼 수 있는 곳입니다. 이 곳 벤탄시장도 동남아 다른나라 또는 베트남 다른 도시의 현지시장들과 비슷합니다. 호치민이 대도시이기 때문에 벤탄시장도 규모가 큰 편입니다. 기념품과 특산품을 사기에 좋은 곳이며, 좋은가격으로 흥정하시면 됩니다. 사람들이 붐비는 곳이기 때문에 가방과소지품을 잘 간수하셔야 합니다.

Das Gebäude war unserer Meinung zu überfüllt, stickige luft ect. Und durch sehr aufdringliche Verkäufer wird der Aufenthalt schnell sehr anstrengend. Trotzdem wars nett diesen markt mal gesehen zu haben :)

Wide selections of food, local products, souvenirs. Price need to bargain like to a quarter price.  Tourist area price on high side. One hour oil massage cost 300k while other place just pay $370k for 1.5 hour.  You will be hassled every shop you passed by.
저렴한 의류 등을 구입할 수 있고,  주로 가짜메이커 상품들을 판매함.  다낭 한시장 대비 가격이 20%이상 비싼건 단점.  그래도 국내가 대비 저렴하며, 처음 직원이 부르는 가격보다 50% 깍아야 정가를 주고 사는느낌.  정가로 표시된 의류의 경우 20%정도 깍으면 많이 깍는 수준.  재미로 몇개 정도 구매해보는 것도 좋을 듯.

Worth a look and to pick up some fair quality fake brand goods if your prepared to bargain. Some vendors aggressive and a couple abusive when said no thanks which was pretty disappointing, but overall as expected from a market.

If the market was cooled with more fans perhaps our experience would’ve been better. If you walk into the clothing market multiple vendors will harass you by selling you clothes and shoes. It’s a bit overwhelming for the non seasoned tourist.

Amantes de las compras este es vuestro lugar, pero cuidado cuando vais a comprar. Estos vietnamitas son muy cucos y tienen distintos precios segun el dia. De lunes a jueves por la mañana es el mejor precio. De noche doblan literalmente los precios, y los fines de semana que saben que vamos turistas suben un tanto en comparacion por semana. Aun asi encontrareis productos a buena calidad. Fulares de seda, mochilas, ropa de deporte y los tipicos souvenirs.

We visited this place and enjoy the Market. To many bargains but not sure about quality of goods and how much you can haggling to. To busy and have to watch your bags and pockets all the time.It gets very crowded and can push you around. The smell of food and drinks can disturb you in buying clothes. We felt pressure from get go. Couldn’t go again as crowded place made me un comfortable . Unless you looking for fake goods at very reasonable prices than worth a visit.
ほかの市場に比べて観光客向けに吹っかけてくるので買わないけれど、活気があって元気を貰えます。 でも立地が最高に良くておみやげ物がなんでも揃うので、値段交渉を楽しみながら買い物をするのであれば、ベトナム旅行の醍醐味が味わえます。

A eye opener for shoppers looking for imitation branded fashion wear, souvenirs, foodstuff, local products n fruits. Suggestion for budget travellers - don't leave until the last day to shop here as u may realise u may not hv enough Dong to spend as u may be spoilt on choices.

This market is huge and packed with the usual fake handbags and clothing. As you walk through its maze of tightly packed aisles the stall holders try to get your attention by touching your arm, offering buy one get one free offers and asking what you are looking for. I bought a hat and a wine bottle holder and used my excellent bargaining skills. I was told by a local to use the 'walkaway' method if they won't drop the price and it definitely works. There is a small food section displaying the usual crabs, fish and other creatures.

If you have traveled in Asia this is nothing new with lots of stalls but a lot of repetition in terms of what is on offer. The colour, sights and smells are great but be prepared to be hassled a little as the vendors can be a little pushy. Also note that as they have to pay ground rent prices are high so be prepared to haggle hard or walk on by. This review is for the indoor market only not the food court outside which is amazing and easily scores 5/5 for great cheap food from around the world and very cheap cold beers.

if you have travelled around india and asia quite a bit, you will find nothing special about this market. its a large market with great bargains. none of the stuff you will find here are very exclusive or extraordinary but you can pick up some cheap souvenier. make sure you bargain a lot.

This is a typical sprawling tourist market selling everything you do or don't want or need. Higher prices ( all relative) than other markets. Prepare to bargain. If you have seen other Asian tourist markets, give it a pass.

Would recommend if you want to experience the intense market culture! Prepare for no personal space and lots of colours and noises. We walked there from our hotel, stayed for about 10 minutes and then left because we had had enough. We did a market tour in the Mekong Delta and it was a lot more pleasant because it was more spacious, had authentic Vietnamese produce and was a lot less touristy. If you were going to do a market in Vietnam I would recommend one in a more rural area
洋服、アクセサリー、食べ物、コーヒー、本当になんでもありますが、クオリティは・・・といったところかと思います。あまり気にせず記念にというのであれば良いかと。 あとは、やはり勧誘というか、売り込みがめんどくさいので、ゆっくりは見れないかと思います。

평균 시세는 잘 모르겠지만 확실한건... 가격을 표기한거에서 반 이상 깍지못하면 호갱 당하는것이다. 필자는 벨트를 200k에 구매,가방을 500k에 구매 했는데 처음에 벨트를 500k, 가방을 1200k를 불렀다. 이 공식에 따르면 처음가격에 40%까지도 깍을 수있다. 참고로 밤낮 가릴것 없이 비싸니 다 깍아라 옆의 자라 의 신발이 싸게 느껴지는 초기의 가격 충격과 공포 그자체다.
売っている洋服は観光客にとっては特に珍しいものはなく、おみやげも街中にあるものばかりです。ただ、まとめ買いしたい場合は良いかもしれません。

A good market to get a bargain and some cheap knock offs. The food here is plentiful but there are better places around the city. Barter hard with the store owners, they frequently start 4-5x higher than their final price.

טוב אז השוק הזה הוא לא ממש גמו שווקי הלילה שאני מכיר מהמזרח, פשוט כמו שאנחנו רגילים לראות בשוק רמלוד או הכרמל, דוכנים של בגדים ושעונים ובשמים מזוייפים אין משהו מיוחד חוץ מהאווירה של השוק וההרגשה של התיירות חוצמזה שום דבר לא היה מיוחד בשוק הזה, אתם צריכים לראות את השווקי לילה בטאייפי בטיוואן כדי להבין על מה אני מדבר...

We were told and read a market not to be missed, but found it very overcrowded and stall keepers calling out to buy continually. The prices we found weren't cheap and one needed to barter hard. I knew the price of Sketchers (made in Vietnam) in Australia and was shocked when they originally stated the same price so do check out the price of whatever you are buying before you go and barter hard. One can often buy the same and cheaper at a small shop around the corner from your hotel or a Mini Mart.
구경 할것은 많고, 흥정하는 재미에 마음에 드는 라탄 가방은 하나 구입했습니다. 착즙해서 주는 사탕수수 쥬스 추천하구요. 그런데 일부 상인들이 흥정중 자리를 뜨려니 손목을 아주 꽉 붙잡고 못가게 하는것에 기분이 나쁘고 무섭시까지 했습니다.

Although, this market is more geared towards tourists it’s still a Vietnamese market much like any other Vietnamese market!  It sells an array of tourist tat mixed with counterfeit clothes bags etc...  Prices start high naturally but offer half the asking price and go from there!  If you’re after better counterfeit goods try the little indoor market in Saigon Square but warned the prices are higher in Saigon Square and they are less likely to haggle and let you walk away.  There’s also an array of stalls selling food which I found to be tasty and fresh

If you’re looking for cheap clothing, shoes, linen, food or souvenirs, then this is the place for you! We found the stall owners way too pushy and you definitely have to talk down the price or they will take advantage. The stalls on the outer perimeter where the prices were “fixed” were much less hassle. In saying that, we picked up heaps of great bargains to bring back! Well worth a look if you’re in Saigon and have room in your suitcase.
호치민 관광 시 한 번 들려보기엔 괜찮지만 물건을 실제로 구입하기엔 별로인 듯. 관광지 근처 기념품가게보다 훨씬 비싼 것 같음.
그냥 한 번 구경하기에만 좋은 곳 같습니다. 같은 마그넷도 중앙우체국 앞 노점 가격보다 몇 배는 비싸게 부릅니다. 물건을 구입하고 싶다면 흥정이 필수 인 것 같습니다.

I am sure that the actual price of everything is less than half of the first price you are given.  Only ask the price if you are super interested other wise you will not make it through the first row of stalls. Remember for these guys selling means thAt they will earn something maybe put food on the table government support is not a thing here.  In saying that you have to be tough only bargain if you want something. I am told that they expect to bargain so don’t worry about being tough.  There is a lot of the same at this market which is frustrating.

Most annoying vendors I have struck. They get in your face and hassle you constantly. Lots of “same same but different”. We had a nice bahn mi in food area but again we got harassed by every vendor till we sat down. Bargain hard
Alle lokale specialiteiten zijn hier te vinden. Het is alleen niet mogelijk om even rustig te kijken, want de verkopers blijven aan je trekken.

You will find everything you need here to bring home as souvenirs and gifts. Bargaining is de rigueur as they overcharge opportunistically and prices came come down by anything from 30-80%.
