They know their limits, and either they will come after you or let you walk away with the sale. Clothes, handicrafts, snacks, shoes and accessories. It really is all here. It is hot, crowded and not for the claustrophobic, but a good few hours of fun. Only thing that really didn't appeal was vendors reaching out to grab me.
A fairly predictable tourist market - though there are some decent looking clothes makers and food stalls.
裡頭商店很多 空氣沒有很流通 所以白天滿熱的 順路過來的旅客可以逛逛啊！ 晚上旁邊就是夜市 只是東西實在是很普通 沒什麼購物慾望  倒是換錢的匯率 這裡很多家都不錯 可以參考參考

When my husband and I visited this market just 7 years ago we were able to walk by the stalls at our leisure unbothered. Today, before both of our feet were inside the building, sellers were yelling at us to come to their stall to buy things we weren’t interested in. After saying “No Thank” too many times they moved in to the next foreigner. Unfortunately the same held true for the food, the real reason we were there. The next time we visit HCMC we will exclude a stop there.

Sehr touristisch, es gibt fast nur noch Souvenirshops im Markt. Wenn einem noch Zeit in Ho Chi Minh Stadt bleibt, kann man schon durchschlendern. Es gibt allerdings nichts anderes zu kaufen als in den anderen Märkten auch. Etwas nervig fand ich die Sonnenbrillen- und Fächerverkäufer rund um den Markt, die sehr lästig waren. Meiner Meinung nach kein unbedingtes Muss.
Street food was good! Goods and produce sections were interesting. Some big roads to cross on the way there. Cross with the locals!

I could have done quite a bit of shopping here but felt it not worth the effort. You can’t really browse as when you so much as look at someone’s stall they pounce on you, even to the point of grabbing your arm and not letting go. Be prepared to haggle as the price will change for everyone.

Good for those who wants to try out the local cuisine. Not always the best but for the price It’s ok. As for Shopping Prices are reasonable. But be prepared to do some bargaining. Do wear comfortable clothings. Very warm in the market

A big covered marketplace (daytime) where tourist can find their souvenirs, textiles, handicrafts, local foods, coffee powder, nuts...etc in Ho Chi Minh city - District 1. 1) Branded T-shirt, bags, shoes, backpack etc: the quality / material of their t-shirts, pants..may be different from the original (could be imitation or smuggled) but some of the quality is acceptable. 2) Coffee Powder: They sell many types of Vietnamese coffee powder. 3) Food: The seafood noodle in the store near gate 11 “Sap 1301” is not bad. 4) Bargains: i)Most of the items can be bargained except the one with the sign of “Fixed Price” hang down from the ceiling. ii) Can start from >50%. Or, prices which you feel is comfortable. You can walk away to other store if you don’t happy with the price. Sometimes they may call you back. iii) Not easy to bargain. Need to spend more time if you want to have a good deal. 5) It may NOT necessary to be an ideal price after spending so much effort on bargaining which compare to an online purchase, eg. A handmade basket bag initial price 350,000VND/about RM64 or more, pay at 170,000VND/about Rm31 & can find at Rm37.02 on online sales. 5) Small change / note: Prepare some small note. Sometimes they don’t give back the balance of small change. Eg. they supposed to give back the balance of 50,000VND to my friend but they didn’t. 6) Light clothing: Wear light clothing. It’s hot inside that place. Bad air ventilation. 7) Shopping tips: don’t go back to the same store after purchased. They may quote at higher price. 8) Direction: There are signage hang down from the ceiling in front of the entrance/exit gate & in front of every store.

Well as a tourist looking for some souvenirs this is a place to find some. Lots of cheap rip offs or seconds purchased some shorts, backpack, some trinkets. Loads of haggling quite over the ‘how much do you want to spend’. Don’t enjoy being harassed and not having the space to just browse, but that is just the way it is. They speak very good English. Earlier we met a chef at the food market section where we purchased fresh produce and then went to her restaurant and cooked a three course meal which was superb. Lots of fresh food available and we learnt about different herbs and produce. My husband got pick pocketed when a couple of people trying to get him to go on a tour and a lady with a baby was begging distracting him. So just beware.

Stopped by the market for shelter from a sudden downpour.  The market has a dizzying array of everything : t-shirts, knock off bags, clothes, snacks, shoes and pretty much anything else that can possibly be made in Vietnam.  Vendors have a great command of English and will be calling for you to stop every few centimeters. Bargain hard!  Good place to buy all manner of souvenir.
물건 종류도 엄청 많고 사람도많고 길은 좁다 관광객 대상으로 하는 시장이고 흥정하는게 피로해서 암것도 안샀다. 유명한 곳이니 한번 가본걸로 만족함
市場の中は広くてたくさんのお店がありますが、置いてある商品は同じものが多い様子。 海外の市場の雰囲気を楽しむのにはいいかもしれませんが、一度行けば十分。

Strolling around Ben Thanh Market is fun, not fun if it is raining though. If you are kind of person who can’t stand wet road and pile of trashes laying around everywhere, this place definitely not a place for you.  They sell coffee, candies, 3D Christmas cards or any cards basically, souvenir, fruits, fake brand bags 🙃 and they even have an aisle especially for halal food and muslim clothings.  We had durian for 80k, and as durian lover you know it is not a durian season, but oh my, it still taste very good! The ladies even give us plastic for hand so we don’t dirt our self, she doesn’t have a stall but instead selling it on the bike, so we ate it while standing. But she is very very nice, she taking care of our trash and even offered us to open the lid for us.  You kind experience lots of kindness here, and once you bargain and you think the price is too high, just leave, they will drop the price more than 50%, (at least based on my experience)  Unlike other comments here, they don’t pull me and touch me, they keep enough distance and don’t really pushing me to buy things.  Overall was a nice experience!

You know you’re a tourist, they know you’re a tourist, so take a deep breath and immerse yourself in the experience. You can buy almost anything here, and everyone is hawking their wares. It’s aggressive but good natured, just keep smiling and keep moving.

I really didnt like this market it was very much targeted to locals and a lot of the stall holders were pretty unhelpful to westerners. I was happy to spend good money there but did not really find much of interest.
口コミでスリが多いとか、あまり安くないとか、店員がしつこい等を多く見たので警戒したこともあり足早に雰囲気だけを見て帰りました。 ザ！アジアの市場感を味わう事が出来ると思います。 ですが、確かに色々な物が売っていましたが、大きなスーパーの方がゆっくりと見て安く購入できそうでした。

If you don't have to walk, don't go. Shop assistants are ambitious, sometimes unpleasant, and usually touch you and ask where are you from and what your name is, just to delay you. If you have to see this market, be prepared to be patient and always negotiate. Coffee for 150VND actually costs 40VND etc.
アジアにありがちな市場です。日本とは違う感じかするので、旅行に来たなと思うことができます。でも雰囲気だけで、実際に買う様なものはあまりありませんでした。
I don’t really enjoy this place as I found few items are more expensive, would still prefer Bin Tay Market.

Couldn't find any placeto eat here. some stalls with food but looked a little risky. Ended up eating some unknown items in soup. this is about 20 minutes away from Bui Vien Street by taxi. About $5 dollars USA by Taxi. Sort of in China Town. Iffy area. Better market wasthe one near our hotel in district One. name escapes me right now. there were tons of things to buy.got some great shirts for $3 to 8 dollars. I do have the lady's card for this market where i bought some shirts. She is located on second floor. Speaks good English.Wish i had bought more shirts from her. Can't find her card. maybe i can add it later.

We arrived the market around 1pm and the place was crowded by most of tourist. All of the bags was fake and sold with high prices and we had to deal to them. We saw many spot sell local food but it seems dirty so we had not try it. If you do not know where to go, this could be an option.

Visiting the market was what we expected, set up for tourists so be expected to barter. There are better cheaper markets the locals use such as those in the Russian quarter for a better deal. You may find a bargain and you may pay more than you should, being for the tourist trade they are experienced in making sure they don't loose when it comes to money. Be careful if paying in US dollars and are handed Vietnamese dong in change, unless you have a currency conversion app on your phone you may loose more than your aware. Whether you go for a bargain or not, it's worth seeing as it is well known as a must do when in Vietnam.
Ok shopping but very hot. Good place for Saigon or Vietnam souvenirs. Prices are pretty good on some items.
The market has every piece of clothing you would ever need and at a fraction of the price, only if you’re prepared to haggle which can be quite fun!

Rows and rows of teeny stalls. We bought the 4 wise buddhas - starting price 300k, we paid 150k and later found them at a fixed price shop for 70k 😂. So bargain hard. Food area looked great but we were a bit nervous to try.

The market is swarmed by sellers selling fake items for the price of gold. Fake bags were being sold by million dongs and if you chose not to buy anything, be sure to get attitude from the seller. It is overall an over hyped market with nothing interesting.
People here are expected that the tourist will bargain. Sometimes u can get the things with 1/3 from their mention price. Use calculator as ur 2nd language here.
People there are aggressive when it comes to negotiating price. They will rip your money off if they know you are not local.
The seller is friendly in aggresive way. You need a friendly bargain skill...if not..you hv a nightmare.
大きな市場で何でも売っています。広すぎて全部見て回るのは大変です。しかも売り子さんがしつこい感じて、腕を捕まえられたりするのでゆっくり見ることができません。値切って駆け引きを楽しむお買い物が好きな方には楽しいと思いますが、とても疲れました。10円か100円ぐらいの違いなら、ドンコイの冷房が効いたお店でゆっくり買い物する方が良いように思います。短時間で市場の雰囲気を楽しむだけで充分でした。

The market is actually close to Saigon centre and many hotels nearby. The market itself offered varieties of souvenirs and clothing. If not interested, just smile and walk by. Some hawkers may touch you for attention, just ignore them and go straight.

If you like knock off bags and clothes then this is the place for you.. the sellers are pushy the place is hot and super crowded..I preferred the stalls that are set right out of the market at night in the open air..the place is worth visiting even if you don’t buy anything..

They have everything you want and don’t. But very aggressive and insistent stall owners. I would very much recommend you shop in the stalls that surround the outer wall of the market and those immediately inside the outer wall as they are all fixed price and there is no bargaining. By way of example the inside stalls start with t shirts at VND850,000 and outside fixed price are VND62,000 for same shirt. A difference of VND800,000 and you will get them down to about VND100,000 so why not go fixed price.

It was a very hot humid day we visited this market and it was extremely crowded which apparently is pretty standard. Lots of tourists trying to bag a bargain but even with some good barter skills there are not any 'real'bargains in my opinion. Stall holders are pretty aggressive as they are in most Asian markets I have visited. The same products are every third or fourth stall and the quality of everything seemed pretty doubtful. I bought a pack of underpants (was running a bit short of clean ones) and they were good for one wear.. Genuine knock offs they were too! There are numerous genuine shops in Saigon for every product known to man so if you want something to last a bit the markets aren't the place to buy. If you want a bit of fun and have not really experienced similar markets go for it.. Caveat emptor

Found the market crowded and stall owners very pushy. Grabbing hold of you and pushing to sell stuff. Very high price as first offer, found offer half and work from there, you can always go next stall to buy same item.
Bargains to be had but you need to barter! Nothing great to buy just like any market in Far East.Rats running all over place !!!!

This is flooded by touirists. Locals won't shop there. Haggle. And don't apologetic about it. Walk away if you dont get the price you want. Don't be surprised, AFTER you've got your bargain price purchase, the next stall owner wil offfer the same, but cheaper. hahahah. Before that, the price will be the same. And btw, if you lose interest in buying, some can suddenly very cold towards you. Not all, but it happens.
濱城市場就在胡志明市的是中心，市場只有一層樓但蠻大的, 裡面有舖售賣各式各樣商品，吃的喝的穿的等伴手禮都可以在這裡買到但外圍商家的價格比較貴，可以走到內排的商家好好的殺價，附近則有知名的換匯銀樓可以來這順道換匯。

After a few weeks in Thailand I expected more about local markets. This is not local , full of tourists and price aimed to foreigners. Things was not that attractive and the air was really bad inside the market
I read a lot of reviews about this market. Nothing special when we got there. You gotta be good in haggling the price thats all.

This is a huge market that is certainly very vibrant. It is very hot, humid and crowded inside and the vendors tended to hassle us in an effort to make a sale. You can bargain hard in this market. I found that there were so many repetitions of the same merchandise in the stalls
Pour les vêtements, Que des copies ! Diviser par ou 4 si vous voulez qq chose. Au centre beaux rayons alimentaires fruits légumes.... des smoothies au top, mangues sechees...
Typical city market selling the same stuff as usual Plenty of food stalls, I had a nice noodle soup dish with plenty of flavor. Worth a look.
Please don't straight away buy stuff in the market as it can be more costly. There is a small mini mart in the market which is nearer to the wet market and it sells cheaper goodies.

When you are in HCM this is definitely a place to visit. Many stalls with food and shopping stalls. Seems like all the products are made from the same supplier but when you ask why some stalls are more. They will say "same same but different."
This is the place to come if you are looking for cheap knock-offs and other bargains. I come here to buy nuts e.g. cashew, and pistachios. Good quality and at good prices.
The market is more frequented by tourist and goods are generally sell more expensive. For nuts especially, may be able to find them much cheaper in Binh Tay market near Chinatown.

The market is packed with 100’s or stalls pretty much selling the same things. Fake athletic wear, runners, bags, bags and more bags. Each vendor will try get you to buy, not a relaxing way to shop. At the end of the market there’re some tasty little food vendors
Watch out and bargain hard. Don't be fooled by initial attempts to charm: when you walk away without buying you will see a distinct personality change!

The only good thing about this place is that it’s a one stop shop for whatever’s worth shopping in Vietnam. But bargaining is a must as the price starts very high. You have to consider what price you feel the product is worth and keep insist as much as you can on that price. Slashing the price to 50% practice is not always good as the initial price often starts ridiculously high. Don’t be afraid to command that price because I don’t think locals can afford the price they are offering. If tourists doesn’t buy, it would be difficult to sell. Generally, great place to buy nuts, dried cuttlefish, souvenirs, handicrafts and brand inspired t-shirts and hang bags. If your interest is mainly on T-shirts, apparels, handbags, shoes, it will be better to check out the comfortable, air conditioned, yet better price Lucky Plaza compared to here. However, if you want to buy handicrafts such as lanterns, and nuts, this place is the best place to buy.
A place for shop and getting more understanding local culture & foods. Shopping must have good bargaining power, otherwise will buy expensive things.

Everyone knows this famous market, but don't expect amazingly low prices. The seller starts at a high price and then the bargaining starts. It's at least double what should be paid. if you walk away they will ask you what you want to pay. Why they can't give you the last price I will never understand. It stop people offending each other through bargaining. Some other shops in the same area are cheaper.
なんでもありの空間でした。お店の前にはお姉さまが座っていて、日本語で話しかけて来ます。面白い場所です。何も買わずに帰りましたが。

The building the markets are in is stuffy and on a hot humid day, you won’t want to be in there for too long.  The stall holders can be very rude! Particularly at the end away from the food where it is mainly clothes. They will physically grab your arm to suggest you buy from them. Even if you are walking by to get to an exit, they will shout at you even once you have walked by. I always find it off putting if you try to look at an item and they are constantly speaking over you and nagging you to buy. This for me mostly results in a no sale for them.  This is nothing new in regards to markets like this, but well worth noting before you venture in.

There is a lot of nice stuff here, but the vendors are much too aggressive. They will follow you around and pester the c**p out of you. It seems to be a Hochiminh city trait: and it happens far too often here. Spoils some of the experience.

Al centro del distretto 1 di Saigon, questo gigantesco mercato assomiglia ad un bazar turco: mille stradine da percorrere con i venditori che ti offrono la loro mercanzia. Si trova di tutto: dagli alimentari, ai falsi di abbigliamento e pelletteria, alle cineserie fini ai classici souvenir. Se volete acquistare, ricordatevi di contrattare: potrete prendere la merce offrendo anche solo il 30% del prezzo iniziale.

There is a lot crammed into this market. Every stall holder will ask you to buy from them. It can be very hot, crowded and overwhelming. Be prepared to bargain hard. Keep a tight hold of your bags/wallets. I would recommend for the experience if nothing else.

Went in to check out the place, but walk out pretty quickly. This place is not that comfortable to be in. Way hot and the sellers are pushy and aggressive. I really Recommend shopping at Saigon Square much better. More relaxed atmosphere. And Having air conditioning also makes all the difference in a place like this. You still need your bargaining skills at hand. And make sure to check for defects and such of the things you buy.

We visited this market at the end of our 3 weeks in Vietnam so had experienced the bargaining and bartering that is part and parcel with a market. Tourists who don’t feel confident with haggling should not come here. It’s essential as the prices are grossly inflated. We started at 70-80% lower than what they asked for and were successful the majority of the time.  The sellers themselves can be rude and aggressive, particularly if you ask a price and then don’t buy the item. Make no mistake, they may laugh along and ask you questions about where you’re from, but they really don’t care. They are there to make a profit so haggle hard and don’t be afraid to walk away. You can pick up some decent quality fakes like Nike, Prada etc and overall we felt we did well but you need to be firm and confident. Overall it was an ok experience, as long as you are aware of the kind of atmosphere you’re entering into!
Beaucoup de Made in China, beaucoup de solicitations de toutes parts pour un endroit très peu authentique... même si vous trouverez vos souvenir de fin de voyage très facilement ici.
I went or passby there almost everyday even there is nothing much I can buy. I feel this place have all the things we need such as food, souvenir, snacks compare to other market in Ho Chi Minh.

Even on haggling right down, the t shirts are still way cheaper on the edge of the market at fixed price stalls. 50,000 VND vs 100,000 I paid after a starting price of 650,000. If you don’t like in your face selling then don’t visit. Everything from clothing to souvenirs to food.

I was really wanting to explore the market.I did manage to get in there but it was so crowded and getting in between those small lanes with shops on either sides was so difficult because there was hardly any place to move around. even if I would manage that there was a step to get from one place to another. the entrance of the market is wheelchair friendly no doubt but then you cannot do much there. It is so crowded that memory has the time to show you anything peacefully.i was happy not doing any shopping there because I went to saigon square which was a much better place to shop probably with same pricing and little bit better quality and somehow or the other you can find the ramps to get onto the shops. The market was walkable distance from my hotel so that was good even though the roads are not so wheelchair friendly
Posto prettamente per turisti ove non si trova nulla di nuovo e di particolare. Un giretto si può fare ma non perdeteci troppo tempo.

Decided to pop into the market to check out the scene there. And yes, as many have suggested, it is more or less the same as all the markets in Vietnam. More of the same stuff that you see at all the other markets.  The difference though is that perhaps some of the store vendors appear to be more aggressive and somewhat disrespectful despite us being respectful of them.  Haggling for a lower price throughout most parts of Asia is part of a cultural game that the vendors play, and some buyers like myself quite enjoy..😁😁. It is meant to be fun and perhaps provide the buyer with a sense of achievement.  At the end of the day, the vendors will never sell at a loss so if you end up buying something, it will be a win-win outcome as you too would only pay if you like the purchase price.  Unfortunately in Ben Thanh market, the vendors do not play by those rules.  Firstly, they try to guilt you into buying at a price you have not agreed to and secondly, on a few instances, they actually agree on a price and after they pack it in a bag for you and you get your money to pay, they say another price that is between 30k to 50k above the agreed price. If you then say forget about it because they are being dishonest, they will call you all sorts of bad things and verbally abuse you. In the market scene in Asia, I'm sure most of you will have to agree that this is probably one of the poorest behaviour for a vendor in markets.  The solution is really to be stern and tough back on them, but it's really easier said than done especially for the less experienced buyer.  The trouble is that the sellers know that most from a western culture find  this situation quite confronting and often just pay that little extra just to get out of there. They know this and they deliberately prey on this which is what I find extremely disappointing.  Apart from the shopping, there is food. Right down the middle section of the market lies a little gem of an area to try all sorts of local food. A lot of locals eat there and you can find almost anything to eat there. Tourists quite often overlook this spot in favour of the Ben Tanh Street Food Market which caters for tourists. But if you take a chance and have a seat in the food area, you could be pleasantly surprised. Eat with your eyes. See something you like or something that looks interesting..sit down, have a taste 😁.  It's hot and sticky in there during the day, but that's expected in most markets in Asia.  Overall, I'd say it's a nice place to visit for food and shopping, with the experience only ruined by the behaviour of not a few, but many of the vendors poor selling and negotiating style....  I will still love shopping at markets but was disappointed feeling as though I did not want to visit any more markets this evening as I left Ben Tanh Market...  A little friendly advice 😁, don't head off to another store to do a price check of the same item. Enjoy the win that you felt that you got when you agreed to buy. Quite often, you might only find that you could've gotten a better price... heheheh

A great place to look for souvenirs and have a taste of vietnamese food. The horrible experience I had was when I was buying stuff, the seller took all my money from my hand, trying to sell me a bag and 4 shirts I wanted for VND3.5 million . I really insisted to take back my money but she’s trying to bargain until she sold all of it for VND800,000.

The market is huge, so much to see and so much to buy! Food, silk, souvenirs, clothes, sport brands, jackets, watches, jewellery, shoes, sunglasses, belts! You can get items really cheap but they make you work hard for it and even guilt you when you barter too low.  The experience is quite overwhelming with people trying to get you into their stalls. Sometimes even grabbing your arm if you try to leave. If you look interested in something, they will pressure you into buying it. They start the price too high for tourists. I paid too much for a pair of knock off Nike runners because they knew I was a tourist and I didn’t start low enough on my first visit into the market. You have to appear experienced and start 60% lower in most instances, otherwise they take advantage.  If you are interested in something, the sellers try their best to please you and are happy to fetch you the size, different colour or a similar option which is helpful.  I preferred paying a little extra in the fixed price section as I didn’t have to guess the price or be pressured.
You can find some decent deals if you are willing to negotiate and then walk away. It’s hot and humid. I’d rather get clothes back home for better quality.

We went to benh thanh market after a visit to the street food market nearby. It was decent; overcrowded and lots of touristy souvenirs with too much bargaining to do. The stuff is the same that you get all over vietnam. We also saw the night market which was a bit more of the same.  If you are not into shopping it can be skipped. But decent place if you want to get some food or pubs

Bon Marché où vous avez la possibilité d'acheté des vêtements locaux officiels controlé par le gouvernement ou des vêtements à d'autres marchands "classiques" donc attention aux contres et mal façons pour ces derniers.

生活市場とおみやげ物屋が同居。午前中早い時間は、魚や肉などの生鮮食料品が並べられ、東南アジアの活気ある市場の感じ。 他は狭い通路に山積みの土産物屋と強引な店員。価格交渉は当たり前、いらないと言ったらがっつり腕を掴まれ、「いくらなら買う？いくつ買う？」そりゃしつこい。気の弱い人だったら「買うから離して」と言ってしまいそう。 すぐに1000円と言ってくる。日本円で言うのも彼らの手口。ドンで言うより数字が小さいので安く感じてしまう。さらに、「じゃあドンに直すといくらです」ここでもまた為替をごまかす。なかなかしたたか。見るだけ気分で行くのは良いが、お土産なら他に同じようなものを扱っているところはたくさんある。

While the market is vast and impressive in sheer size, the product offerings are largely similar. If you’re there to shop, you’d likely be able to find what you’re looking for within the first 10minutes of exploring the market, before finding replicas at every other store.  It’s a nice experience, but there’s really no need to stay too long here.
Always a bit of fun bargaining to get some clothes/bags, but it’s sometimes a bit overwhelming with all of the merchants asking you to buy things and is always really busy with tourists.
Very big and busy, the stall holder can be very very pushy. Worth a visit. Goods are not that cheap so haggle with the vendors. The fresh food market is pretty cool.
観光客のみのターゲットっていう訳ではないので、ある意味色々あって良い。ただ、コーヒーなんかもどこでも売ってるものを売ってるし。観光客は間違いなくボラれる。というより、ぼってる意識は店にはない。観光客だから当然といった感じ。いつも行く良心的なスパイス屋に人に行ってもらったら、いつもの3倍くらいの値段で買わされてた笑
Packed with tourist and the price is too high. Must bargain before purcase. Go to Binh Tay Market instead if you want to find cheap nuts.

Even if it's a must see in Saigon, the market is not that big and unless you're in expert in negotiations you will overpay everything. If you looking for fake bags and branded tshirt then you should be happy

Probably worth visiting while in Ho Chi Minh, but be prepared to haggle as all initial prices are way too high. Each stall holder will try and make you buy their wares, so be firm and walk away when you’ve had enough. We went at 10.30am which was early for this market, but not so crowded.
라탄백 사랴고 했는데 가게마다 디자인 거의 똑같구요 그냥 맘에드는 디자인 찍고 여러군데 다니면서 흥정하시는게 좋을 것 같아요. 과일은 망고스틴 샀었는데 안읻어서 결국 못먹었었다는,,,

Huge building jammed packed with hundreds of stalls all selling the same items. Clothing is mostly all brand name items like nike, Adidas, under amour, ck etc. All items are way over priced considering they are mass produced counterfeit. Can be quite confronting and annoying if you're not used to having people in your face and touching you as you walk by. You will need to drive a hard bargain in order not to get ripped off. I would suggest to start at half what they offer and be prepared to walk away. I personally found it very frustrating to try to look at things or even point at something without being latched onto.

Overdekte markt is niet heel bijzonder. Er worden veels te hoge prijzen gevraagd, dus afdingen tot meer dan 70 % van de vraagprijs. Pas op voor zakkenrollers. Ze zijn heel brutaal. Bij mijn zoon probeerde een oud vrouwtje de rits van zijn rugzak open te maken.

We visited the Ben Thanh Market on our first day in Southeast Asia. Everyone said it was the market to visit. We found it more of a spectacle to observe than an actual place to do any shopping. It was interesting to walk buy the tables full of fish, brains, intestines, and the stalls of fruits, vegetables, and goods. So it was a great place for photos, but the actual shopping experience was disappointing. We understood going in that most of the name-brand products were likely knockoffs (what you might expect to find on a street corner in major cities). We didn’t expect the vendors to be as aggressive as they were. Each one would call “madame’ or “sir” and try to push their product. If you show any interest, the price negotiations would begin. If you want to purchase, be ready to bargain. We preferred shopping at the more low-key markets we found on our trip.
Maybe is something interesting if you are new around but if you have already been in Asia is just another market. They sell the same kind of things and souvenirs all around.
Ok is you are looking for fake stuff, otherwise not really special. Nice to walk around though. Not something you really need to see, would not take a taxi for this.

This place is worth to take a look. If you are looking for cheaper lunch around the area, you can consider the food stalls inside. They are much cheaper. However watch out for the stalls sales people selling things, they will approach you for sales when you walked past their stall. It’s an experience to try if you are in Ho Chih Min

Deze markt was in buurt van het hotel. Niks bijzonders en dezelfde spullen die ze overal verkopen. Je moet goed opletten voor de hard rijdende scooters die over de markt rijden ongelooflijk dat dit zomaar kan.

I came here twice to buy cashew nuts and the price is quite reasonable to me compared to back home. I didn't bargain much because I'm not good at it and the seller can be quite intimidating and aggressive. I have no complaints with the stuff I bought. If you come here, research well and bargain because most things do not have written price except food items like nuts and coffee. I had a good avocado smoothie here as well.
Demasiado turistico , bien para verlo o comerte algo , pero las compras no las reconmendaria hacerlas alli , es todo mas caro .
这里的东西有许多当地特色，但是普遍比较贵，据说砍价可以砍到一半购买。这里的水果种类很多且齐全，但价格很贵跟国内有过之而无不及，建议去附近的一个当地菜市场购买水果，推荐里面的果汁，不贵用料也挺足的，用餐就不必了！

This market was very disappointing as there was minimal authentic street food available. It offers a 'sanitised' version of Vietnamese food ie grilled chicken, meat skewers and spring rolls. Prices were high, for example 35,000 Dong for one medium sized spring roll vs 40,000 Dong for a full plate of genuine street food. Although this market is not a true representation of street food, it is clean and good seating is available.

Go for the experience. Do not go to try and shop. Very very crowded market, aggressive sales and sone will try and physically grab you to buy. Items are extremely over valued and you are expected to haggle hard. Always take the price offered and take 80% off. If they son’t cone to the party, walk off as the next stall or two down will have heard and will want to do the sale. Alternate walk across the road to the Aircon of Saigon Mall and good advertised prices.

Taxi tried to cheat us going in from the port and again travelling back to the ship  All the vendors are constantly in your face trying to sell you fake stuff  The speciality coffee is all fake, they are very good at doing that using chemical treatments and additives.  It vaguely air conditioned and the heat really gets you down.  The aisles are, in places, very narrow and hard to navigate  There is some interesting fruit and vegetables in the market
I found it quite expensive for vietnam. Even the fixed price. There are a few cheaper shops nearby though. I got a nice tshirt for 50000d
Unless you want to shop for clothes, shoes, souvenirs and coffee this market requires a short stop. It can get hot in here so be early or late or risk a heatstroke
Shoe polishers and eyeglasses sellers will Chase you around disturbing you. The products are much expensive than other market's there

Know what you’re looking for and how much to pay. We were here 2 years ago and it has definitely become more aggressive since last time. I was pulled into a shop by 2 women and they yelled at me when I “escaped” my partner tried on shorts and they didn’t fit right, when he left she yelled “OH MY GOD” and yelled at us again when we walked past. I was about to buy a handbag from another shop, when the woman rips out a lighter and yells “it’s leather” ran the lighter across the bag. That put me off, I know it’s not leather. Saying that, the chaos is fun! I got a “leather” bag for $20, had some delicious Pho and did get some bargains!!
Beaucoup d' attrape touriste, pas très typique. Je n’y retournerai pas. Beaucoup de monde, pour la nourriture ça peut être intéressant.
Aggressive sales people targeting tourists. Walk out of he market across the street to find the same deals with more friendly reasonable people

Better buy item with "written" price on it. Seller on this market is 'unbeliveable'. They give ridiculous price for item which lead us to turn around right away. Even if we know we can bargain, but when the price was set up too high we loose intention to continue. They should treated visitor better. We are not a bunch of human from other planet who can not estimate the price of Earth Item.

Vendedores são rudes... se você olhar e dizer que não vai querer, eles ficam extremamente bravos. Uma mulher chegou a chamar a gente de "crazy" kkkkkk só porque recusamos uma bolsa. Não são todos os vendedores, mas aconteceram situações assim pelo menos em 3 bancas, então não conseguimos nem olhar mais nada. Tomamos 3 sucos que estavam muito doces. O preço das coisas estava caro pq eles diminuem mt o preço na "conversa".

We were very disappointed with the market. We learned that the government controls the market now. There used to be bargaining over the price but now the government has fixed the price. The cost is still low but you must remember all of the products are nock offs.

夫婦2人でフォーと焼きそば、ビールを頼んで10万ドンくらいの会計。50万ドンを渡してお釣りを受け取ったところ、妙に少ない。50万ドン渡したと抗議をしたところ、20万ドンしか受け取ってないとシラを切り続けられた。30万ドン、約1800円を騙し取られた勘定。市場の店などでは50万ドン紙幣を使っちゃダメ、たとえ使うにしても紙幣を渡す時に50万ドンを渡すから、と声に出すべきだったと反省してます。かつてイタリアで20ユーロを渡してやはり釣り銭を誤魔化されたのを思い出しました。今は電子決済が主流なので、釣り銭詐欺も減ってはいると思いますが⋯

東南アジアの市場はいろいろ行ってますが、ここはあまり気分いい場所ではなかったです。 旅行最終日だからもう換金したくなかったので、この中の屋台でクレジットカード使えるか聞くと「OK」との返事で食後に支払いでカードを出したら、別の店員が「NO」と言い先の店員は知らん顔。最終的に、また別の店員がカード使えると言ってきて、金額にカードの手数料的に50,000D上乗せされました。「300円くらいちょっとでしょ」と言ってくるのがムカつく。他の服屋でもそうでした。カード価格と現金価格に差があるので気をつけてください。 ゾウパンツが欲しくて値段交渉している際に、まず、ありえない金額を提示してくるのでこちらも1/4くらいで提示すると、あからさまにぶすっとした顔で電卓を商品の上に投げて無視される。なので立ち去ろうとすると、引き止められ希望価格を聞いてくる。最初の価格の1/3くらいで買うのが普通と知っていたので、先ほど提示した価格より少し上げて再提示すると、向こうもありえない価格から少し下げてくる。でも「NO」と言って立ち去ろうとすると、こちらの言い値でいいと言ってきて交渉成立する。 そんなパターンを2～3回やってください。それでも十分儲けているいるのだし、もっと愛想よくすればまた買いに行こうかなと思えるのに。

Ben Thanh market left much to be desired. It is stacked high with fake designer goods and many aggressive sales folks who will attempt to grab you (and your money). It is a great place to get cheap mass produced souvenirs but that’s it. It can also be a bit claustrophobic once you stray from the main pathway. You’re better off going to Book Street or Bui Vien.
どんなものか見に行く程度には良いかと思います。交渉好きな方はいいと思いますが、いちいち付きまとわれるし、袖は引っ張られるし、価格交渉をあとでひっくり返されるしと、いろんなハプニングがありますね。 ここを狙ってホテルを決めるのはやめた方がいいと思います。

Go there just to look at whatever they're selling - nothing more. They jack the prices so high then they give you a discount which still makes it a lot higher than its real one. The coffee is soooo expensive you're better off buying beans from the coffee shops around the city like Phuc Long, Highlands, Trung Nguyen, etc.  One thing I strongly suggest is scout the prices in Ben Thanh, take a cab to Binh Tay Market and end up buying a lot lot more.

I was in and out. It was souvenirs, fake luxury goods, folks promising clothes in 24 hours again and again and again. Too many people aggressively selling, some even touching me as I walked by, and I was just done. This was a pass for me.

Ein grosser Markt spezialisiert auf Touristen. Mondpreise werden zunächst genannt, die dann mindesten auf die Hälfte herunter gehandelt werden muss. Dies gilt teilweise auch bei Artikel. die schon Preise enthalten. Da gibt es andere Märkte, die preislich nicht so überzogen angesetzt sind.

Heard so much about this market and the many good reviews which made it a must for me to visit when in HCMC. BIG mistake! I went a stall sending coffee and snacks. Decided to buy 2 different brand of coffee for my friends as a gift. I was quoted VND 250k for brand A and VND 100k for brand B. After much bargaining i was offered VND 200k for brand A and VND 90k for brand B. Later in the day, i happened to pass by a local supermarket located in the basement of Vincom Center. Saw the same product i bought at the Ben Thanh Market earlier but the prices were VND 90k for brand A and VND 70k for brand B. I checked carefully and both were the exact type (size and weight). I think it is a good place to feel the vibe of the local market (you can try the cooked food and drink there) but my advice is to avoid buy anything there unless you are ok with the big price differences. You will be better off buying from their local supermarket.

On peut acheter de tout : vêtements, légumes, fruits, chaussures, gadgets. Peu d’artisanat, surtout des produits industriels. Au centre, il y a des stands pour manger. Je déconseille d’y manger : c’est bondé et stressant et ça sent fort. On y mange de la cuisine de rue plutôt chère en y étant mal installé. Les vendeurs sont collants.
Personnellement vu que j'habite ho chi minh, j'évite le marché de ban tanh car réservé aux touristes (surtout les prix qui sont multiplier par 2 ou 3)
Found it cramped, with the same items everywhere. Hassled continuously. We would not recommend it. Across the road at the Saigon Centre was far better.
Brace yourself. This is not a casual stroll looking at produce. This place is a war zone. Haranguing by stall holders, outrageous prices for $2 shp rubbish Stay away! You have been warned!

Expensive with rude merchants. I would have rated a one, but I want to make sure I am being fair; I really liked the market in Hanoi much better. I also had someone try to pick pocket me. I’m not even sure it’s worth a visit. So many more interesting, safe and exciting things in Ho Chi Minh City to explore.
Overly hyped and congested market. Prices on higher side, though haggling can be done a bit. One can buycClothes, bags, dry fruits, souvenirs etc.

There are better places to shop. This is more of a tourist spot and they know they can get away with jacking up the prices. Like 350k dongs for a Nike shirt?? That’s more than my custom dress in Hoi An. Just go to the alleyways near Ben Thanh Market, you can a Nike shirt for 50k dong.

The market prices are very high comparing the other stores out of the market. I found store holders were very pushy to sell something. Also, prices very high, if you bargain and give your price and start walking away, all off a sudden they say yes okay to your price. Example, I wanted to buy a hat, the salesperson said 350 thousand Dong, I said 70 thousand Dong, price started to go down, as I started to walk away, the person accepted my price. When they said a price, I always said less than one fourth. Example, if they said 400 thousand, I would say 100 thousand. Don't go up or slight increase from my price is okay.

Large market in the city center. There are hundreds of little shops. Difficult to walk between the shops. Full of products for tourists. Attention, you should always discuss the price. Always offer at least a fifth of the asking price. It's not a very interesting place. Just a market for tourists to see. There are many more stores in the city, much better and with better prices
Packed, busy and very smelly. It was very hot and the smell of the food was overpowering so we did not stay for long. There are constant offers to sell things.
Can’t see the point of visiting. Unbearably hot in the daytime as the roof and fans just make it feel like you are in a fan assisted oven. Goods seem to be priced for tourists.

I think this market is overrated. It’s absolutely packed full of cheap rubbish and if your’e travelling solo you get harassed non stop. If you stop walking you cannot get two seconds of peace to browse. Food looked good but couldn’t stop to look properly.
Horrid place, people are so harassing and not nice. We bought nothing. Complete con artists and a bit aggressive. They are charging 5 times what things are worth.

Our tour guide advised us to bargain 50% of the starting price offered by the seller. Even then the discounted price is much more expensive than stuff I can get at home.  Come here for the experience and photos but don't bother buying anything there. It's an energy draining process that leaves you exhausted and still you overpaid for the items.

Fate attenzione i prezzi sono esagerati, contrattate perché nei negozi fuori dal centro si trovano gli stessi prodotti al 50% in meno. Per alcuni articoli addirittura -75%. Esempio fogli di riso per roll nel mercato 80000, in un negozio normale 20000. Borse taroccate 500000, in altri negozi fuori paese 150000,200000. Questo vale anche per i prodotti da mangiare... mentre i ristoranti hanno prezzi economici, ma si mangia dove la notte sono passate blatte e topi. Visti con i miei occhi

This Viking excursion to the Ben Thanh Market was ruined by an impatient Asia Overseas tour guide who discouraged Biking passengers from shopping and rustled us through the market like cattle. On the bus to Ben Thanh, I asked if we would be able to shop and his reply of “this is not a good place to shop” was stunning to me, my wife and several of the people on the bus. When I asked why we were going to Ben Thanh. His reply was that we did not know how to bargain ! I have formally notified Viking of our displeasure.  Before going to Ben Thanh on a tour or excursion, inquire how much time you will have to shop. This applies to all shopping areas you visit.

These markets are really overwhelming. And sellers harass you badly. If you're wanting your typical market item, these are the best markets to go to as there is everything under the one roof, however, I Couldn't wait to get out of there! One of the most hectic experiences of being here!

The prices of things here are very obviously inflated, but if you can bargain well and handle the aggressiveness of the vendors who try to approach you, you can get some nice things. Be aware that different vendors will make up different credit card fees though for random reasons (not a problem if you can pay in cash). I had one particularly bad experience with a food stall connected to a smoothie stall where the woman said I could pay with card before I sat down and then when I tried to pay she took me to another stall where they tried to charge me 12% to pay with card. If you’re foreign and you let them they will try to squeeze every last penny out of you.

Went to this market. Not the best one I've ever been too. It is rather smelly (dried fish) preferably take a mask with you. Some OK things to buy. Went once and did not go back as they are so close to you and would pull you in which annoys me.

Genial para compras la verdad, eso si regateo salvaje te suelen pedir más del doble pero no pagues más de.lo que crees que vale que será una cuarta parte de lo que te pidan tienes de todo para comprar
A touristy place with lots of tacky things for sale. Not worth the time or effort to get there. Salespeople are pushy and constantly accosting you as you walk around.

If you're going to buy small souvenirs like magnets, string bracelets, post cards, bookmarks etc do not buy them from here. Absolutely insane prices when you get the exact same things from the Ho Chi Minh Post Office. I bought a bracelet for 50k dong from the Post Office, exact same bracelet cost 350k in this market.

Way too much harassment. I would not go back because I found the experience too stressful. Not just the vendors inside but all around as well. Can someone tell them that people like me would like to shop there but the aggressive selling is just too off putting.

I wouldn't recommend a visit unless you're *desperate* for last minute souvenirs. This is a tourist market. I can't comment on the food vendors that are there, but with the other sections I lasted about five minutes before I had to get out.  Many (many) of the vendors are aggressive. Not just subtly, but obnoxiously. I don't think they quite realize that if any of them just left me alone for 10 seconds I would look at their stalls and probably buy something to take home. The part though that I really couldn't stand was how many thought it was their right to try and grab my arm to get me to buy from them, which made me leave asap.  If that sounds fun to you, go ahead, but it's not likely you're getting a deal either. At the non fixed price stalls they are asking severely inflated prices, and even negotiating to less than half will have you paying more than what you would elsewhere.  I respect people trying to make a living, but this one was one of my least favourite places in Vietnam. Other days may be different, but the day I was there really soured me on the experience.

I recently visited this store and while they had a good collection of products, I was disappointed with their pricing strategy. I found that for products worth 200000 VND, the sellers started with a price of 1 million VND, which is extremely high. Additionally, even after negotiation, they did not come down much from their initial asking price, which was disappointing. Moreover, during the bargaining process, I found the sellers to be quite aggressive and rude. At one point, they even shouted at me, which made me feel uncomfortable and disrespected. This kind of behavior is unprofessional and unacceptable, and it definitely put a damper on my shopping experience. While the quality of the products was good, I would caution other customers to be prepared for aggressive bargaining tactics and potentially disrespectful behavior from the sellers.

Huge place, very hot and clautraphobic. Someone I went with felt suffocated in there and had to get out of the market early. Too cramped and vendors can be very pushy .  For someone who doesnt like bargaining, dont go as you will be paying way over the odds for your items. I reckon the mark up is 300% or more. The best I managed to do was reduce the price of one item to 50% of the original asking price and even that, I reckon the vendor made a good profit on t. Personally I didnt enjoy the experience.

Viele Stände mit Souvenirs, Obst, Kaffee und ähnlichen Dingen vorhanden. Die Verkäufer sind aufdringlicher und Preisen ihre Ware aggressiver an als in Vietnam gewöhnlich. Es gibt auch klassische streetfood Essenstände. Wir haben es nicht probiert, daher kann ich dazu keine Bewertung abgeben, es sah jedoch sehr touristisch aus.  Die Preis sind orbitals hoch. Z.B. Kaffee ( e nach Sorte zwischen 80.000 und 250.000 d pro 100 Gramm). Die üblichen Preise liegen bei 18.000-45.000 d.  Der Markt eignet sich für einen ersten Eindruck, laufen sollte man jedoch an wenigerer frequentierten Orten.

Not sure how this gets as a 'must do' for HCMC, but anyway. It's a big market, with tons of stuff... all the usual stuff you find in all the other markets. Low quality T-shirts creating the garment fabric waste of tomorrow, and plenty of tourist tat. The FOOD area was good though. Variety and good prices.

Extremely aggressive and pushy vendors, you can't even look at anything without being harassed. It is certainly not an enjoyable shopping experience. There is, however, merchandise around the perimeter with fixed prices. The items all have price tags as well. These prices are actually lower than what you can get after a very stressful bargaining session with the vendors, and you can actually look around in peace!  So, if you decide to go, just stick to shopping around the perimeter inside. The ladies there were polite, friendly, and very helpful - they get 5 stars!
Könnte theoretisch ein netter Markt sein. Verkäufer und besonders Verkäuferinnen sind hier noch aufdringlicher als sonst wo. Unglaublich nervige Erfahrung. Wir sind nach wenigen Minuten abgehauen.

Si vous avez le temps, aller y faire un tour mais sans plus… Les vendeurs vous accoste voir même agresse pour vous faire acheter. Si toute fois vous avez envie d'acheter, négocier le prix, parfois ils font X10 sur le prix initiale. Vous y trouverez de tout.

I love visiting markets on my travels & love to browse. Unfortunately, due to the aggressive & pushy nature of the sellers, browsing & having a pleasant walk around Ben Thanh is out of the question. So many great souvenirs & handicrafts for sale ( which I love) , however you lose your train of thought with the constant harassment and end up looking to find the nearest exit to get out of there.  It is next level, which is a shame because if they let tourists browse in peace and be able to move from stall to stall in their own time , they would generate more sales. It is still popular and the place to go, however hubby and I spent very little time there and didn't return during our week in Ho Chi Minh.
Overpriced and aggressive vendors. My least favourite shopping in Asia.  No bargains here and the t-shirts are poor quality.

We we’re stopping over in Ho Chi Ming & wanted to go and explore the night market as we were staying in a local hotel. The area and market is super popular with vendors and tourists making it quite a busy and bustling place (hold on to your bags). The main market area is indoors with lots of vendors and stalls selling mostly souvenirs, big bags of nuts and clothing/bags. There are also some small food vendors inside too. Great to see, but probably wouldn’t go again or recommend to a friend 😊

Attended this market today to walk through for kicks and giggles. However, be aware you will be harassed by vendors, particularly the group of magpies BETWEEN the EAST ENTRANCE DOORS. They should be kicked right back "into" the Mekong river for the good of the rest of the vendors and the tourists that are there to support the normal ones. EPIC FAIL!!!! Hopefully, this post gets traction and the magpies get the point. Had to have at least 2 stars in there for the legit vendors. All that to say, avoid the EAST wall.

We went there to appreciate the diversity. We quickly realized that there are mainly three type of stalls: clothes, nuts, tea, and repeating each other. Same clothes everywhere at the same price. Same stall of tea/coffee next to each other. Overall, the offer of produces is low. It's quite disappointing considering the size. Made limited purchases and got a huge turn-off when we saw rats running between stalls.
Not too bad...but a little expensive compared to what I know similar markets in China...anyhow bargain play a good role on it ..

Certainement pratique pour ramener les derniers souvenirs dn groypant les achats( textile, café, bibelots...) Mais il faut diviser les prix annoncés au moins par 2. Marché fréquenté uniquement par les touristes... Beaucoup de monde, c'est le souk Vietnamien et ses accotés!

Having travelled through most of Asia, this is one of the worst markets I have been to. This is purely because of the vendors. The vendors are aggressive so be prepared to be hounded to buy anything and everything from the kitchen sink. They highly inflate the prices when they see tourists. When you refuse and bargain with them, the prices are still way overpriced. They are not friendly and only care to sell their items. There are some nice things however like quality souvenirs plus bags and loads more. There are also the usual junk stalls. The market has a nice atmosphere. Go earlier in the day, morning or afternoon. Post C-19 and the market is less active at night. If you see something you like and want to buy it, then remember where the stall is or buy it straight away. It is easy to get lost browsing through the many stalls as a lot look the same or sell similar items. The food area was reasonably clean. If you want cheap local cuisine, food and drinks looked to be OK but we didn't try it. Most of the market is covered so this is a good idea for a rainy day. If you want to buy dried fruit or sweets they told me that they only sell in bulk. I just wanted a small quantity and they refused. I bought one fresh mango where the lady wanted to sell it for more than what I would pay for it in London. I refused and she agreed to a more reasonable price. Most vendors however don't bargain. I saw a scarf I really wanted. I asked the vendor about the price a few times. She kept avoiding the question, instead telling me about the quality, the colours and other information. What she was trying to do was look me up and down, trying to see what I could afford to pay and how many scarves I wanted! I pushed her again and then she asked me what I wanted to pay. I just walked off and then she shouted some high price. You would think they wanted business especially post C-19. A lot of people were browsing but I saw very few buying! Be careful with bags and purses especially when busy. Better to keep small change. Even when walking outside, watch how you go as motorbikes pass by close to the pavement. They are known to snatch bags.

Les marchés sont tjs une expérience à faire pour s’imprégner de la culture locale ! Mais ce marché est une arnaque et vous ne croiserez que des gens qui achètent de la contrefaçon de grandes marques de luxe… pour les produits tels que thé café épices les prix sont beaucoup trop élevés malgré l’incessante négociation … beaucoup de vendeurs vous sautent dessus pour vous vendre tout et n’importe quoi avec agressivité… vous n’êtes même pas considérés comme acheteur mais comme billet de banque … ça a perdu de son charme…

Mercato coperto, pieno di prodotti copiati (alcuni come le maglie da basket non visti cosi spesso altrove), souvenir e cibo. Attenzione a questi ultimi perchè vengono venduti a prezzi molto superiori a quelli riscontrati nei mercati delle altre città come Hanoi Hue o Da Nang. Una confezione di tè avevano provato a vendermela a 250,000 dong (10 euro). L'ho comprata in un negozio locale con prezzi esposti a 30.000 dong (1,20 euro). Peraltro dopo un pò che girate rischiate di rimaner sordi a causa dei numerosi "what are you looking for?" degli espositori

Waste of time. Full of knock off Chinese goods.  There is a food area but very crowed and hard to determine the good from not so good.  Be prepared for hard sell, grabbing you by the arm and not letting go.  There are better places.

It wasn't worth the hype. Selling same fake products. Very pricey for a cheap items, even though you just asked for the price they will immediately get the item for you and assume that you will buy. If you will not buy they will say 'give me your budget' and when you give me them your price they get mad and started shouting things at us. Very rude sellers especially at the center. However, the sellers on the outer, or just right at the entrance are good and friendly. They will tell you they haven't sold anything for the day just to guilt trip you in buying their products.  One seller even shouted at us 'don't scam me' just because I bargained for a FAKE bag. Go to night market instead.
be careful with the thief and pickpockets here, you may lost your phone and bags. so sad for the experiences here

This is a very big and varied market for a lot of shopping, including food. However the intensity and continued pursue of sellers make shopping very uncomfortable and unpleasant. Better to go to to Saigon Square Market. Pretty much the same clothes, watches, etc. but waay more relaxed.

I have travelled a lot in SE Asia and like to haggle. Even when I bought a bunch of t-shirts and clearly displayed I had no money left in my wallet a woman approached me from another stall and said no problems we just go to ATM. High pressure sales from 5-6 people at once. I will never go back and I am sure they plan on this so gouge as much as possible at the time (I am a fair person and believe in small business making a profit so I am not a cheap Charlie). Greed will be a hindrance to you in the future Clothing Operators!

There is nothing special there! If you are looking of corse for some spices or products for kitchen yes you can go there but be aware price are quite high for tourists. But if you are looking for clothing or something typically Vietnamese, avoid this place!

Hồi xưa mới lên sài gòn thấy ai cũng hô vô chợ bến thành chơi. Mình thì cũng đi thử cho biết. Mà vô trong đó thấy bán đồ nói thách nhiều quá. Cái áo 500k mà trả 350k cũng bán???được cái không gian cổ kính, tại di tích lịch sử mà với tây quá trời tây

En este mercado encuentras toda clase de artesanía y souvenirs de todo Vietnam. Es un lugar cerrado y no tiene aire acondicionado, lo que lo convierte en un lugar sofocante. Tiene siempre mal olor. Los vendedores te acosan todo el tiempo. No tienes un minuto de tranquilidad. Si eres extranjero y preguntas el precio de algo, te dirán el doble del valor real. Si no te gusta regatear precios, no vayas porque te estafarán. Solo dile “gracias” y sigues caminando. En segundos estará el vendedor detrás de ti dándote un precio menor y suplicando que le compres. Si eres astuto, espera y regatea un poco más hasta que llegue a la mitad del precio inicial. Así es como funciona. Si estás dispuesto...visítalo y conseguirás precios al por mayor si compras por docenas. Si te abruma una experiencia así, no te lo recomiendo.

If you walk through the Market, smells sometimes so ugly. Most areas so dirty. The seller are so pushy ( not all ) different goods. Depend on "Store" you get good replicas from all, some have really the cheapest versions. Prices 50% to high. If someone talk about 10-20 % Discount, only go. You will get the 50%.
食料品、衣料品などなんでも揃う大きな市場です。お土産を買うことも可能ですが、あまり良いなと思うものはなかったです。どこを歩いてても声をかけられるので雰囲気を味わうにはよいかも。

Les stands vendent les mêmes objets, on vous poursuit pour un tee shirt. Vous êtes non stop arrêtez si vous avez le malheur de regarder, donc en fait vous regardez de loin. Pas intéressant sauf si vous souhaitez des souvenirs made in Vietnam ou plutôt de Chine et des contrefaçons. Les vietnamiens de ce marché devraient être bien plus aimables. Nous ne nous sommes pas senti aimés sauf si vous achetez. N'allez pas dans le coin nourriture car là on vous tire par le bras et en plus les plats ne sont pas frais du tout.

We went to this market and found the sellers very difficult to deal with chasing us down the aisles. We ended up at the Saigon Market, on Google maps, looking at the title, it is called Mercado Ben Tahn. It is where to locals shop and most is at a set price of 200,000 VND. There isn't the foodstalls, mainly clothes. The people are much more friendlier and the choice is actually better and have different things. We found it difficult to find at first but the best guide I can advise is to go to Saigon Square 1, then walk along the street Duron Le Loi towards Ben Thanh market. At the end of this block, cross the street and on your right is a non-descript opening of little stalls. It is much smaller but we went back many times. Do definitely go to Ben Thanh market, but more for a look.

I don't know if it's because tourist numbers are down because of the corona virus outbreak, but the vendors were extremely aggressive and almost desperate, clutching at you and following you. You dare not ask the price of something because then they won't leave you alone. Not a pleasant experience.

We were looking forward to visiting this market, but it turned into a nightmare. The end with the produce was fine, but the clothing end...I am used to looky looky men in Tenerife but the stall holders in the clothing section took this to a whole new level. Every stall you passed thrust the same thing at me. I didn't want it from your neighbour, why do I want it from you. After two aisles we gave up. We couldn't take it anymore. So if you are happy with constant hassling, give it a go, otherwise try the first aisle and see for yourself!

Just an ordinary, Asian market...This one has raw food and garments all together...Spent 10mn and left right away...It's not touristy at all, nothing crafty...Don't bother going there if you have limited time in Saigon..
Eine strasse weiter hinten ist ein anderer markt mit den selben artikeln, wo. Man aber nicht alle 2 sekunden angesprochen wird
If there is a definition of a Tourist Trap, then this could be it. Pushy sellers selling stuff at inflated prices. Best avoided.
Een markt zoals zovelen in Azië. Alleen dan met agressieve verkopers. Voelde onveilig. Het aanbod was ook absoluut niet de moeite waard.

HOt and stinky, if you want to put up with rude shop keepers then go here, if they don't make any sales they will be cranky and rude.. it a lot of effort to haggle.. they expect you to, so they jacked the price up by 500%, so basically if they said 1 mil Dong you will have to haggle it down to 200K Dong.. the night market is better, it was much cooler and the stall holders were a lot more chilled and not as rude.
ik had het daar al snel gezien. bij elk kraam spreken ze je aan. neen niks voor mij. 2 steegjes doorlopen en dit was het maar ik begrijp dat er mensen zijn die dit wel leuk gaan vinden

ツアーで立ち寄りましたが、市場と言いつつ観光化されすぎているので庶民の台所って感じはまったくしません。 観光客目当ての土産物を売る店が大半ですが、正直街の路面店で買った方がまだ安心かなと思います。（食べ物系はちょっと怖くて買う気がしません）客引きがしつこいのでぼーっとしてるとまた声をかけられるので早足で進みましたがそんなに時間を割くべき場所でもないように思います。蝙蝠のはく製の標本とか物珍しさはありますが、みるだけって感じがします。カラフルなチェーを売ってる屋台とか見たら「ベトナムらしいなあ」と思いますが、そこで実際に食べるかというと、勇気があると思います。ホーチミンではお土産や民芸品を買うべきお店はここじゃなくても沢山あります。

This place is good to check out maybe once for about 15 minutes then you'll want to get the hell out. Almost every vendor is overly aggressive, not just asking you to buy something but they will literally tug on your shirt or tap you and say, "buy t shirt?" even after you just acknowledged them, smiled and said no thank you. Honestly, you can't even look at anything at any of the shops without them coming onto you and already trying to negotiate a price. If you like your personal space, don't bother coming here. I personally would never come back.
Pasillos de 80 cm. Y tiendas de 2x2.ellos comiendo y durmiendo en esas cajas de cerilla, indignant. la gente acosandote. Muy agobiante y los precios no son gangas y mala calidad
Une légère impression de Marrakech. Il faut tout marchander on se fait agresser en permanance. Tout est cher, il faut diviser les prix par 2. Lieu sans grand intérêt
The Ben Thanh Market is worth exploring and it is convenient but I would recommend further exploration as there are many other markets in HCMC.

We spent an hour walking around Benthanh market and looking at goods, asking about price and then we realized we shouldn’t buy anything (Food, shoes, clothes,...). Normal quality products but prices were so high. If you aren’t good at bargain, you’ll certainly pay much more than necessary. However it’s interesting to experience and take pictures.
Tack markets have never been our thing, and Ben Thanh just reinforced that for us. The food market was ok, but the rest is just row after row of junk and imitation bland rubbish.

I didn’t like this market. The smells really bothered me and it was incredibly dirty. If you want to buy souvenirs, this is a good place to do it. Don’t forget to bargain but I couldn’t eat here because it was filthy
구경해도 볼 것 없고 동대문이나 남대문 짬뽕해놓은 느낌인데.. 바가지도 심하고 흠 그냥 한번 슬쩍 보고 시간을 투자 할 필요없는 곳 같네요

Well since you walk in you will start feeling like you want to run away as fast as possible from there!!!ha ha The vendors are trying to sell anything to you and are super pushy and sometimes super nasty.They alway come out with crazy price that you have to knock it down by around 60-70%!Coffee super expensive don’t buy there go to walking street in backpackers area!They don’t understand sentence “I’m just looking”.Good luck there as after 5min being there got massive headache.
It's overrated market with a huge amount of fake clothing. It doesn't worth a visit. There are better markets in HCMC.

Vietnam es sin duda un país muy barato, y así son también las compras de artículos artesanales. El Ben Thanh Market es sin duda un espacio donde comprar muchos artículos. Eso sí, hay que regatear ya que este sitio no tiene precio fijo y se puede sacar todo mucho más barato. Además de ello, visto dos pasillos ya tienes visto el resto del mercado, ya que se repiten bastantes los artículos

This very busy place is designed with one thing in mind: getting as much money from the tourists as possible. The sellers are the most agreasive i have ever tried. Also the prices have received an upwards bump compared to the other markets of HCMC 🙂
Tourist trap. You need to have mad skills at bargaining to get anything for a decent price here Find a local shop and buy what you need there instead.
Big market. Unfortunately selling only typically mass-made tourist stuff. Very busy though. A guy at another event I did said "don't by anything there, over priced "...

I am not a great fan of markets, but prefer those that are visited by and cater towards locals, as they give the greatest insight into the everyday life of a place that you are visiting. Having said this, this market seems to be one that is really tailored towards tourists and is more focused on souvenirs, is too crowded and a bit of a rip off. I could have happily missed this stop on my tour of Saigon/Ho Chi Minh.
Too crowded. Sellers set the price on (Probably fake) Adidas shorts 7.250.000, ready to lower price to 500.000, when you can get the real thing in the mall for 400.000.

I am not a fan of over crowded areas where people hassle you ever two seconds with their products. I wanted to look around at my own pace, but with everyone shouting to buy their products i just didn't feel at ease and left. I preferred roaming the streets and going to local shops. Take 5 min to go around if you're in the area, but that's about it. (it is close to the Ben Thanh street food market and that is worth the visit!!)
여행객들에게 바가지 엄청 쒸움. 특히 한국인을 개호구로 생각함. 일단 흥정은 기본. 흥정도 70프로 깍고 시작하면 50프로 정도 깍음 참고하시길

The place smells horrendous. I wouldn’t dare eat in the food market. The stalls were all selling the same (clearly fake) crap and the vendors are so tired of tourist after tourist not interested in buying anything and so have zero patience yet almost all of them will hassle you on passing

เคยเจอประสบการณ์ แม่ค้าด่า ไม่ทอนเงิน แถมเดินหนีอีก ทำให้เราไม่อยากซื้ออะไรจากที่นั่นเลย เครื่องดื่มอร่อย แต่ราคาสูงกว่าข้างนอก ถ้าเดินซื้อของให้ระวังกระเป๋า ทรัพย์สินมีค่าให้ดี แม่ค้าที่นี่ปากไวมาก แค่ยืนดูของ ไม่ได้ซื้อก็โวยวายแล้วค่ะ

Un mercato come tanti altri visti in Vietnam e in questo caso molto costoso, prezzi per turisti. Non ho ancora visto altri mercati nella città per fare confronti ma questo non mi ha particolarmente entusiasmato
您可能買到偏貴的價格 以及商品的重複性很高 多賣一些休閒服飾及飾品 有一些小吃飲料攤販 有喜歡的需要貨比三家

Outer ring is set price. Interior is where you can haggle. It’s quite boisterous and an assault on the senses. Personally I don’t believe it to be a place for deals. Seems more geared toward the tourists than locals. If you want a better sense of the markets...visit one away from the tourist zone.

Too many bandits taking the piss with their prices.One Adidas was quoted at 300,000 dong when I knew it was 100,000 at most. When vendors back was turned asked another vendor and got it got it 90,000 dong.They really are taking the piss.Also if they know you bargain too low or have no interest in the sale they will remove you from their stall. Try the street perpendicular to the market if you want shoes. They were a third cheaper with less stress. These kinds of places put me off Vietnamese unfortunately.

We love market and was looking forward to going to Ben Thanh Market but I found it was very touristy. My husband and I rented motorbike and go around other cities in Vietnam and enjoyed all kinds of market for shopping, just see culture, food etc but Ben Thanh Market is for only tourists and people just try to sell with aggressive attitude. But when it comes to negotiations, they point out their sign says “fixed price” which is not the case. Yes it is cheaper than buying airport but I don’t like aggressive sales but not negotiable or they start with double or triple price than real price.
베트남 여행중에 자주 들리는 곳입니다 호객행위 엄청 심하고 품질도 안좋은데 바가지 요금이 심해요 상인이 부르는 가격의 삼분의일 가격이하 에서 흥정해야함 특히 시장 주위에서 대기하고있는 택시는 무조건 피하세요 엄청난 바가지 요금을 지불할수 있습니다 비나썬이나 마일린택시만 타세요

Besides food, most of the international items are either fake or shoddy products. I would only recommend getting their local products like bamboo, ceramics or food and please do not accept the first price at face value. Bargain with some strategy in mind.  We went with a shopping list to get their Vietnamese ceramics (Bat Trang), handmade stuff and dried fruits. Items are priced probably 2-3 times than the price you would be paying. For a hand embroidery bag, we went around asking their best prices and it was quoted from a range of 50-80K dong, but when we bartered it down should we buy 25 pieces of it, it was 30K. When you refused to buy after looking through a while, get ready to be reprimanded for wasting their time or according to them, messing up their stuff. You cannot really shop in peace as the vendors are so ravenous for your money. Atrocious behaviour that really spoil the image of their country.  You would be better off shopping at their supermarkets like Coopmart or at shopping malls like Saigon Centre or Takashimaya. What you see is what you can reasonably pay for.
Too crowded, very dirty and price at least five times more than normal price. Don't do shopping just look around.
처음가면 어거지로 가격을 부릅니다 거시서 계속 깍다 보면 판매가가 됩니다 개인적으로 흥정하는걸 싫어하시면 비추천드립니다

It's very hot/humid and crowded. Strolling around the food stall you'll get pulled and jumped on by the stall workers. I was unlucky and met a very bad salesperson that seem to hate her job/life. I can communicate in vietnamese and was there with a group of friends. At a stall selling souvenirs I helped my friend to ask for the price of rice hats. The girl at the stall was not kind and barely service minded. When I asked for the price she sloppily said 30K dong, where there were different sizes. So I asked again, while pointing at both sizes "so these are for 30k dong?". She then said "are you retarded? how can this bigger one cost the same as the smaller one. You must be retarded!". Even if she took it as I was bargaining for cheaper price there's no reason to be rude when I had a respectful tone when asking for the price and the workers at the market should be used to tourists bargaining if she took my question for bargaining. I translated to my foreigner friends what she said to me and walked away. While walking away she kept calling me retarded. The exact words translated from Vietnamese that she said to me was "you lack brain". Her bad attitude ruined our visit. It was the souvenir stall at the beginning from the main entrance to the left. Sorry cant remember if each stall had a name. As for the other stalls they all tried to scam us. If you do visit this market be sure to know how to bargain and handle these types and have a strong stomach if you eat there. Some of us got stomach ache.

It's an average market with a lot of options but everything is over priced and the shopkeepers are extremely rude and aggressive.  I would recommend going to Saigon market which has better pricing and shopkeepers are nicer.  The restaurant district is good but very pricey to what you can have in other areas.
You will get hassled at this market. Seriously hassled. Products are pretty shoddy. Much better, hassle-free, air conditioned and better quality products next door to Saigon Central mall.

It’s a good job I arrived in the north first and travelled to HCM City. Then I know that the people of Vietnam are kind and friendly- but not in this place.  Walking through the market every stall has practically the same produce. Probably made in China, but not sure. The products are not of a high quality and if you are looking for something better then go in the shops closer to the 5 Star hotels. These have much higher quality Vietnamese souvenirs.  What I found disappointing was the attitude of the market traders. You bargain a price and when it is still too expensive and you decline the sale the traders get obnoxious. I lost my temper a few times with them. Even if I was gentle in my negotiation they would still be rude when I didn’t want to buy as I thought it was too expensive. I even observed a couple of girls being very demanding to an elderly woman who had American Dollars that were too dog eared and old, they wanted new crisp notes.  Maybe they’ve been spoilt by too many tourists there?  I don’t need the aggression.
Bien plus chère que les autres Marchés. Bref un spots à Touristes. Mieux vaut aller à Bình Tay Market.
Rất đông khách thăm quan dù hàng hóa ở đây không có gì đặc biệt, lại khá đắt. Đường phía trước Chợ rất hay xảy ra cướp giật điện thoại, túi xách.

The Ben Thanh Market was an interesting experience. We went here on our first day in Vietnam and was pretty overwhelmed. It is packed with stalls and is huge in size. If you’ve been to Bali or Thailand I’m sure you understand being haggled etc but this was something else. Stall holders didn’t mind yelling, following you down the aisle. Prices were also sky high at the start, I didn’t have the strength to barter them down but I’m sure it could be done. It was a must-do in HCMC however we found another market called Saigon Center not far from the Ben Thanh Market. It sold similar items but the owners were less aggressive and prices were set and reasonable from the get go - my advice would be to venture there to buy some bargains!

２年ぶりに訪れましたが、お買い得を求めての買い物は無理でね。ツアーの添乗員の人が”今は決して安くなく、ドンコイの店の方が安いですよ、見るだけに”と言っていたことが本当だな、と実感しました。 市場で買ったサンダルがドンコイの２倍でした。それもしつこく値切った結果の値段とです。暑さと値切りの労力を考えたら、交渉を楽しもうの人以外は、市場は雰囲気を楽しむ程度にして、お買い物はドンコイで、、、をお勧めします。

Ich habe den Markt heute besucht, ich wurde von jedem angesprochen oder angetatscht und nicht in Ruhe gelassen. Zudem kam dass sie völlig überteuerte Preise angeboten haben. Ich würde definitv sagen es ist totale abzocke. Sehr viel Auswahl aber fast jeder hat das selbe. Nur besuchen wenn man die nerven dafür hat.

To watch this market is very nice, but be aware from the prices! For clothes they count sometimes more than 6 times more then normal. If you really need clothes go to market in district 5 or if you are western people, better go to the russian market, they will have your size, better quality and cheaper price! If you want to trade in Ben Thanh market, find an vietnamese friend to help you!

Nous avons daja fait quelques marches notamment à Bangkok, mais pas apprécié du tout la Ben Thanh Market. Marchander avec des prix 3x plus cher... pas agréable du tout. Et les boutiques en périphérie à "prix fixe", n'ont pas les meilleurs prix! Nous avons trouvé des tee-shirt au moins chers ailleurs. Chaleur insoutenable.

Although this market is open day and night and there is a lot of variety of everything you can buy, the locals are very agressive and will do anything to get a sale. If you try to bargain they will speak amongst each other and it does not take a scientist to figure out that they are talking bad about you. Mind you they will always price something ridiculous and there is no way I would pay that much, hence I try to reasonably bargain. The locals will yell at you and physically drag you to their shop/eatery, and they will hold signs literally in your face to get your attention, which in my opinion is the biggest deterrent. If you are used to the markets I guess this is normal, but being a famous tourist market spot, we didnt enjoy it as much as we hoped for.
場外の食材売り場と花屋は、少し注意したら良い買い物ができます。 しかし、場内での買い物は要注意、ロ－カル市場の10倍近い価格の場合もあります。

On est pas rentres que l'on a été harcelés par des vendeurs énervés et énervant. Une seule envie, en ressortir! La seule partie agréable c'est les allées a prix fixes ou on ne peut pas partager...quand aux vendeurs de nourritures ils sont ignobles...

Slightly tacky and less than average market. There is no character here, but there is a sense of disappointment because this is regarded as one of the sights of Saigon. Walk into any market and you'll see the same things, and generally you'll get a better atmosphere. We wandered into a number of markets while travelling around Southeast Asia, including several little known markets, and all of them managed to delight in some way - either because of their charm, their quirkiness, the range and type of goods, or the picturesque quality of the market. But this one had little of that. I would put it down as average but for the sense of disappointment at the place. We didn't stay long.

If you are looking for a good bargain and a great deal this is probably not the place for you. The cheap imitations and the constant : do you want to buy ? You look Miss ! Are pretty annoying.... but when in Ho Chi Min you should walk through it once . The night market around it is more interesting and more fun !
Didn't liked the quality and variety of the products You get what you pay for ... Better option saigon square

We visited this market late having visited Saigon Square and felt really intimidated. Every seller would hold things in front of you, try to grab you by the arm, and every corner you turn, there is a seller in your face trying to sell you something. We did find a nice lady and purchased a couple of T-shirts. It was when I purchased some dried fruit and the lady tried to scam me. I must admit the notes looked similar so I was always checking my money and noted. I spent 40VND on some dried fruit and paid with 200VND note. She gave me the change and I counted it in front of her and only saw 60 change. I looked up and she sheepishly handed me the 100note which she tried to keep hold of. Perhaps she didn’t think I would check and would just walk off , which would have been harder to challenge had that happened. I didn’t like the feel of this market and it was quite sad as it was the last stop in our Vietnam trip. I felt nothing but friendliness and fantastic hospitality up until this point. I’ve travelled to many places but i didn’t appreciate the aggressive selling.
