Our guide was Beti and it was certainly the best part of the tour. Fluent English , with good pronunciation what is important for tourists from other languages . Quickly solved problems that arose Usually avoided scheduled excursions, but after reading I saw that there are few possibilities to do it on their own It is a very touristic circuit, visiting places that personally do not interest me (like candy is made or take photos with a snake etc) but it is what everyone does It certainly had, and has had expectation about the Mekong and it was not fulfilled

The best thing about this tour was our guide Kang! He was so friendly, positive and helped us so much. He always had a smile and worked very hard to make our trip enjoyable. We sampled lots of fruits and interesting foods!

Tourguide: Our Tourguide, David aka Big Dave, was very great! He told us a lot about Saigon and the Mekong delta. Also he helped us organize our further travel to Cambodia. Thanks a lot :)  Tour stops: The tour itself was interesting but packed with too many stops. This resulted in only limited time for each stop and therefore rush-in & rush-out sight-seeing. Also, many stops (floating market, islands in Mekong Delta, etc.) were crowded with tourists. In the future we will try to attend tours which focus on less touristic spots. However, these circumstances cannot be influenced by our tour guide and are rather set by Vietnam Adventure Tours.

We did the 2-day Mekong Delta Tour as a fivesome. Overall, we found the tour very diverse and varied. I can 100% recommend our guide Kynie. She explained everything perfectly and always had an open ear for our questions and wishes. What was nice was that she got involved in the activities herself. Of course you are a tourist here, but you still get a little insight into the area of the Mekong Delta and the life of the people in this area. In summary, a clear recommendation for the provider and our guide Kynie.
It is an essential experience if you have to go to Vietnam. The guide Kevin has made it very easy and has explained it very well.

Duc was a great tour guide! He explained everything well and made us laugh. The floating market was worth getting up super early for. The river is quite busy with Vietnamese tourists, but we always had a boat only for our group.

We had a very nice trip with our guide Betty. She was always friendly, caring and highly motivated to give us a lot of informations. The food was really tasty & we tried fruits we have never eaten before. Also the stay in the hotel was satisfactory. The Mekong Delta was definitely worth a bist. All in all we were really happy about our booking, thank u.

I give 5* for our tour guide Steven. He gave a lot of insight in the way of living in the Mekong Delta.  My personal highlights of the tour were the cooking class at the homestay in the evening of the first day and the visit of the floating market in the early morning (6 o'clock). According to my experience this is a perfect time to visit the market.  I'd like to advise other visitors to go there with an empty stomach, in order to taste all the tasty food, you can buy on the boats.  The program of the first day (e.g. fruit tasting & honey making) seemed to me more as a touristy set up to sell additional products.  For the tour itself (activities organized) i give 3*, but because of the insights from Steven, it results in a 4* rating.

We take 2 days Mekong tour, and Sally made the tour so funny, and the tour is nice, but Vietnam has a lot of trash on everywhere, the 2 stars hotel was good but the room doesn’t have any windows, but after all, Sally made us have a nice time in Vietnam!!!

We have just come back from our 2 day 1 night tour to the Mekong Delta. I was initially very skeptical as I had read so many mixed reviews about the Mekong Delta tours however, it turned out to be an enjoyable few days. We had an amazing tour guide Ms Dao, who was very engaging and informative and had excellent English. I think the guide really makes the tour in these type of touristy trips and she really was very professional. We were picked up on time from our hotel and she kindly waited for us to finish our breakfast. Bus was comfortable and not overcrowded so there was enough room to take our luggage. Driver was excellent, friendly and safe - apologies I can't recall his name. Tour ran to schedule and to the itinerary provided, although we were back a bit early than expected (3.30) but that suited as we had to go to the airport later on. The tour itself is very touristy as expected and the river itself isn't quaint or in the countryside per se. There are two short sections (1 each day) where you go in a hand rowed boat but only for 10-15minutes each time. Lots of stops at touristy spots selling different products but no pressure to buy. I would have liked a bit longer to cycle around after lunch, only about 20 minutes allocated so it was a bit rushed however, the guide was flexible and gave us sightly longer than initially planned and just made the trip to the coconut candy workshop shorter. The hotel was very nice (3star option), the floating market was interesting and authentic, although again dont expect anything quaint. Food at lunch was good although there was a too much food, so I suspect a lot of waste. The pollution of the river with rubbish is really awukd. Both the locals and the government need to do more to tackle this. It is so sad and depressing to see and might ruin the trip for some. Our guide did acknowledge the problem and I know it is a work in. progress so hopefully it improves so that the environment can be salvaged. The company could improve their eco credentials slightly. A water filter at the restaurants/on the bus would be good to replace the individual water bottles handed around. I would also lose the wet wipes, as they are wrapped in plastic and not compostable. Plastic straws provided in the coconuts could be replaced by paper or reusable bamboo or metal straws. Overall a worthwhile trip but don't go with very high expectations of an authentic experience. And ask for Ms Dao!
Well organized excursion that has met expectations. The hotel was well located and the food was good.

We booked our trip with Viatnam adventure tour. We had a lovely guide DAO DEP (girl in pic.) Speeks very good English. Told about vietnam en what they do and how they live. She made everybody feel comfortable. We had a nice tour at de delta mekong... A lot to see. We had a 2 day tour. And stayed at a 3 stars hotel wish was perfect. And very clean (we had the option to select 5 or 3 star hotel) It was a good tour and we had en very nice group.

Ken- we enjoyed the guide and the tour overall. We left Ho Chi Min in the morning and now we are headed to the airport! We visited temples, pagodas, the delta... the guide makes many jokes that are not funny but put a smile on your face.
Great Tour with guide Sam. We Can recommend to others who wants to visit Mekong and the local people. Stayed at the 3 Star hotel which isn’t that good unfortunately.

We had an interesting tour where we saw and learnt a lot. Betty not just did a standard tour but explained a lot, was super friendly and did from our opinion more than we know from others. The Tourguide has a big impact and we are lucky that we had her with us these two days. Unfortunately all these tours become more and more touristic, however this is business and necessary but also makes it sometimes a little bit „too much“. Thanks to Betty for everything 😎

Firstly, our tourguide Steven was amazing. He did a great job making the tour enjoyable for us. He was able to tell us a lot about Vietnam and the Mekong Delta and his english is very good too. The tour itself was ok. We did a lot of boat trips which was nice but the Mekong river is very dirty so scenery was often not so nice. We visited many local businesses (fruit farms, bee farm, rice noodle production etc.) which on the one hand was very interesting but on the other hand was very touristy. The spots we visited where clearly made for tourists only. The food we had was very good! We stayed in the 5 star hotel which is the vinpearl in can tho. Amazing hotel!!! Unfortunately we only had very little time there and arrived in the evening so we didn’t had time to enjoy the hotel to its fullest. So I guess choosing the 3star alternative is better and a cheaper.

We went on a 14 people tour with the tourleader DAO DEP . She has made us feel very comfortable and the visit very interesting.Dao is very professional and gives a lot of good information about Vietnam. I highly recommend the tour. We stayed in a 3* hotel in Cao Tho and it was good. The delta deserves a visit but the time you spend in the boat river should be longer but in conclusion it was a nice trip.

It was very nice but too commercial for us. On the first day the boat trip went only to sales events and the rowing boat tour lasted only max 10min. Group size was good, bus was good, hotel 3stars ok. Our guide Dao Dep was great, great, very nice Dao gets 1000000 stars. Lunch was great.

The tour corresponded to the description. Everything was well organized and structured. We had a really funny and nice guide, Fa, who let us dive into the culture and „lifestyle“ of the people in the Mekong-Delta. We were introduced to ricenoodle production, were showed some typical Vietnamese fruits which we got to taste. All meals were authentic and mostly delicious. The big floating market of Cai Rang is a market for tradesmen with big loaded boots. All in all the tour contains many stations but they could have been more diverse. Nevertheless we had a great time also because of our guide Fa. :)

Just back from the 2 day mekong delta tour with Hang as our tour guide. Hang was a wonderful guide. She was so friendly and funny and she gave us great information about Vietnamese culture. There was a great variety of activities on the trip. The hotel we stayed in overnight was lovely also. We really enjoyed our trip to the mekong delta.

Just completed a 2 day trip to the mekong delta with Hang. The trip was very interesting and fun, packed with a lot of activities. Hang is a great guide, she is knowledgable and enthusiastic, and her contribution made the trip all the more fun.
The tour responds to what is offered. The guide's fine. The food didn't love it and the 3 star hotel is not bad.

We booked a night at home stay. The stay was pleasant - good conditions and always took into account any dietary restrictions that anyone in the group had. The visiting guide, Kung, was always with us and was quite helpful.

Ben was great and his English was excellent. He went out if his way to make the tour enjoyable for all. We went to lots of different places and he did a great job of explaining the history of Vietnam and the current and traditional practices. The trip was well organised with enough free time to explore places before moving on. The floating markets were interesting, less crazy than expected but very enjoyable.  The only thing that could improve was the standard of accommodation - the 3* hotel was fairly basic although not the worst we stayed in on our three week trip. Given the option, we would have paid for an upgrade.  We would highly recommend this trip fir anyone wanting to see the Mekong Delta.

We did the two day tour and our guide was Amy. She is a really nice girl who took us to a lot of nice places, the second day even very early to watch the sunrise over the floating market. The experience overall was very good, good guide, good accomodation, good food.  So why only 4 stars? Beacause even though we had some discount we felt that we overpaid a bit if you compare it to the expenses the company has for this tour. Also, at the booking office we opted for a bigger group because it would be cheaper, the salesman told us this would be approximately the same price, and that a big group would not be the same expierience e.g. You would be rushed through the sites etc. but we highly doubt if this is the case. We saw a group that day from the same company with 17 people in it and it looked like a nice group to be part of. But anyway, we had a nice trip and a great time :-)
Locky made my day:)  It was a nice trip, and the homestay in the Mekong delta is 1 of the top places tot stay.

This weekend I had the opportunity and fortune to carry out a two-day tour of Mekong Delta with this company and the truth is that I enjoyed it very much!!!  Everything started very well from the punctuality with which they passed us to the hotel, Bunny our guide is a quite nice person and in which you rating that he likes his work because he explains everything in detail and always looking for no doubts!!!!  The places you visit during the Tour are quite nice and everything is very well organized so you waste absolutely no time at all!!!!  Without a doubt I recommend this activity and this company to carry it out!!!

Probably more tourist boats than actual market boats - still it’s a nice ride on the water in the cool morning air watching the sun rise.  A relief from the sweltering midday sun!

The tours will insist that you start at an ungodly 5AM!  You’ll be racing down river with all the other party boats with tourists in bright orange life vests 😂

But it’s Can Tho and you Have to do it right?
Just book the cheapest tour ‘cause for the locals it’s all about the money not the actual market!  And enjoy the boat ride!

Cai Rang Floating Market is one of the most popular tourist attractions in Can Tho. We woke up early to enjoy a pleasant boat ride to the market. Although it now caters more to tourists than serving as a traditional market, it was still enjoyable to see a part of the traditional lifestyle in the Mekong Delta, where people sell food and fruit directly from their boats.

🛶🛶 Floating Market tips 🛶🛶
We stayed in Can Tho and were quoted extortionate amounts of 1.5 mill dong for a trip to the market, noodle factory, canal and orchard.
Along the front of the river near the night market there were friendly ladies selling boat tours the exact same as our hotel for 300k per person on a private boat, leaving at 5am. We were slightly sceptical, however after seeing groups at the above attractions on our tour we realised they would be having an identical experience. The tour itself was okay, I wouldn’t hold any expectations. The main enjoyment was the actual boat and being on the river. After a 4 hour tour, we felt that 550k for 2 people was a fair price, anything higher I would have felt ripped off.

To experience the fun of the river area, you have to get up early and travel by boat for about 30 minutes from the center to the floating market, the market sells many fruits, as well as specialties of the western river area, the prices are reasonable. The people are very friendly and cheerful

An enjoyable experience. Market seemed smaller than expected with not too much activity other than tourist boats driving around. This may have just been the time of day or the day of the week that we went, but it was still worth seeing and experiencing.

Good experiences, very cheap price for tropical fruit and nice sunrise. I went there very early in the morning around 5:30am so I caught the beautiful sunrise. Foreigners should take different tour so you guys could evenly the most authentic floating market. And don’t expect a lot ( like what you see in the advertising 😅). …

The market is not as crowded as expected. Very few boat and trading activity happend. Tourist even much more than locals😆 But still it is a good experience. The people here friendly. You may need to refuse some offers from your boat driver if they recomend you to go to some shop or others places. …
Great experience even though you can tell it has been adapted for tourists. If you overlook that fact, the insight to local life is very interesting.

Interesting place to understand the culture of people living by the bank of Mekong delta. Since there was no road connection to city they used boat for transfer, going to market daily trading food, fruits… nowadays coming not only vision difference type of products selling and how to sell but also can enjoy fruits, coffee right in the Mekong river
interesting to see but shocking to see how people to do business to survive
Best to visit it during early mornings, a vibrant market on the Mekong.

It was a lovely experience to see this floating market in the early morning when the market is the busiest time in a day. Many boats are selling water melons, pineapples, sweet potatoes, pumpkins, fruits etc.
The good time to set off is from 6am  to 7am in the morning, i do not like starting too early.

With so many roads being built these days, farmers don't need to use the waterways to sell their products. Although the floating market is only to promote tourism, I really enjoy it. It's a glimpse into the old days.
Interesting, help you get a brief insight into the southern life. Should go on a cloudy day to feel better.

Interesting to see especially if you have never been to a floating market and it gives a good insight of what life on and along the river is like.  It seems that these days this is mainly a wholesale market but there are still smaller boats around selling fresh fruits, vegetables, cooked foods, coffee and other drinks. Very touristy!  is not necessary to take a tour if you just want to see the floating market. We rode the motorbike from our homestay to the area and there are many locals offerring boat rides through the market for a fraction of the price of a tour. Make sure to bargain for the price.

Due to development of Road and Bridge. This floating market isn't busy like before. Almost boats are working for tourist service. But you can understand a part of culture South West of Vietnam in the past.
One of the best tourist attractions in the South of Vietnam. To visit the market, you should come early between 06:00 and 07:00.

I think it’s a must-see if you’re in Can Tho!
There’s only a few floating markets left in Asia and they probably won’t last long because it’s a very difficult job to do!
Tourists can buy fruits and vegetables there but also drinks and food!
It’s a good way to get closer from the local people!
My only concern is that the river is way too polluted unfortunately!
Locals throw away everything in it, from rotten fruits to plastic cups, plastic bags, garbages...
So don’t be surprised to see LOTS of plastic!

Cai Rang is a wholesale market so there are not many tourist-related boats. Go there early in the morning, around 6am. I believe it’s not worth the 1,500,000 vd (50€) tour that hotels try to sell to tourist. You can take a taxi to the Cai Rang Bridge and take a boat from there for 50k (weekdays) or 60k (weekends). If there are less than 5 people on the boat the price is 150k.
It was a nice experience. But the market was not busy as I thought. There were many more tourist than the local traders. May be just the day I went
Getting up early, taking up a small boat to Cai Rang Floating Market and having breakfast with traditional rice noodle. Good place for experiencing

Typical local floating market. A lot of tour with small or medium sized boats from 4$ to 35$ depending whether an English speaking guide or not, the journey, the time spent around, the trip schedule, etc...
It's a must see if in the area, but normally we come here to enjoy this and other markets. Better to leave at 5 in the morning to enjoy the sunrise, max 5:45. If not included, possibility to get coffee, juices, fruits and noodles from the floating boats.
The boat is nice but not so much to see on the floating market. The fruit boat is for tourist only and price is super expensive.

It is a great experience to get here early in the morning and to sightsee on a boat and to buy something as fruits, coffee from other boats, foods from restaurant boats along the rivers are not good and not cheap.

It was great to explore once.  Had the bun rieu. It was just ok.  Iced coffee on a boat was nice too.  Our boat driver was so nice and it was an awesome
Experience starting at 5am and finishing at 10am.  The coconut candy factories were fun.
Imagine! You can buy fresh delicious fruit with many kind, coffee, eventhough noodle soup on boats. An unforgetable experience.
Wanted to experience live market. Tried this around 5am when boats bring fresh catch.
Came back after 5 years and it felt short on my expectation. Pretty much all shops are set up for foreigners so if you look for a more authentic experience, go for another route.
It a must place to go visit when you are in Can Tho. 410K per person including the tourist guide speaks in English. Good experience.

Good experience but the floating fruit market is barely in existence anymore. Only 2 boats selling fruits. All other boats are full of tourists. I paid 350k VND for 2 people on a private boat when I got there. One side of the river has private boats, the other side is commercial boats.
Must see market whenever you are in Can Tho. Be early better. I've been here sometimes but still always love that.
Nice floating market. Not big, though. Mainly for local wholesalers
It’s an amazing experience to see floating market. You can learn more about Vietnamese culture when you go there.
Early morning visit is a must for the area to witness the routine of purchasing fruit an veg. Fresh pineapple on a stick so good
I visited it on a rainy day. The weather was so bad so there was not much thing to see.

It was a nice experience. We visited it as a part of a trip booked at one of the small "agencies" close to the wharf. We only paid 400k in total for 4 people, so don't bother booking this trip at your hotel voor 2 million +. It was nice, we had a coffee and a pho on the water, quite the experience. But you can't really buy anything at the market as a tourist.
A must go place in Can Tho, don’t forget to try “Pizza Hu Tieu” :)
cool. follow to the river and listen the guitar. that's perfect feeling
I came there late so can not see the market at the morning. But it is still lively, dynamic

You can buy ticket around night market with reasonable price. You should start the trip at 5:30AM so you can enjoy dawn on river,
Having breakfast on floating boat will be a interesting experience.
This local floating market is huge and lifely. It similar to what we have in Thailand.
An interesting experience with the flooring market. This is something we will consider coming back.
Nothing special, a few boats on a dirty river, can't see much
Not much thing to enjoy here. But once for your experience.
Very large river, good place. But this water market is just for visitor

Mostly a tourist attraction almost if not al the products were to be sold to tourists.

Worth a visit but don't expect a real genuine experience of locals and a living local market.
Take an early trip ~5h30 and you can enjoy sunrise on  the river.. A bit cool giving you fresh air... take breakfast with some dishes of typical taste of Mekong Delta..

Used to be wholesale market, but only a few left since trading on land is much cheaper. Its just a tourist attraction now. With a quick tour of 5$, you can see many merchant offering their goods if there is vnmese (they cant speak anything else) and visit food boat/ sourvenir boat. If you are in Can Tho already, you might try once.

Stopped by this floating market early in the morning. There wasn't a lot of places to buy things, but it was definitely easier for the boat to navigate since it wasn't crowded. Best part about it was when I could hop on the fruit boats to order straight from the vendor.

If you look closely at the edges of the river, you'll see a lot of litter!

Real market, not setup for tourists meaning no hassle. Nice to stop by some boats and sample food. Relaxed atmosphere. Went on a small slow boat with only two passengers feels more intimate than big tour boat.

Start the tour before 6 at least. 5am is even better. That's when the market is lively. Great collection of fruits and vegetables and a excellent photo location. Take a small 3-4 person boat to make the experience more interesting.

This is Cai Rang floating market.
I enjoyd it, but to my opinion it is at least a little bit overrated.
I was lucky enough having got a boat and my coconut captn on my own. We had breakfast together on the boat and afterwards she took me back through sideway canals.
Always a great place to visit, watch the traders being busy, eat some Soup and drink some Ca Phe which is offered by small boats cruising between the boats. If you in Can Tho, do visit. Great place
In that floating market you can visit once if you are exploring to Can Tho city, and if you needs some foods and vegetables, you can go and buy it.

Unique experience you have to see when you are in Can Tho. Try to go early in the morning to avoid the heat of the sun. Renting a small boat costs 500.000vnd/$20. The entire trip takes about 2/3 hours if you leave from the dock in Can Tho.

Apartments are available around the market, a choice of AC or fan. A small market but enough to tickle curiosity. Locals do not speak English. Some young people know some words though. Boat ride is around 150k dong. Food is great and much cheaper compared to HCM.
A must visit place when you are in Mekong Delta. We booked a private boat for 2-5 people for 350k. You should go here around 5am and enjoy the market.

For tourists who want to experience the old style of market in the Mekong countries. Should go early, from at least 6 am. An Binh market on the river bank is ok with reasonable price and no need to bargain. You should buy fruit and accessories here!

Its more of a wholesale floating market for locals to buy fruits and vegetables. The boat ride is nice and you can stop at floating shops to eat or buy fruits . Make sure your boatmen stops for you. Get the boats at the jetty area you get a much cheaper  deal.

Essentially, you’re going to watch people do their groceries. But it’s well worth doing. Don’t expect to see hundreds of boats, unless you go very very early. We went onto a floating café there and had some breakfast with undetectable ingredients. We later found out it contains pork blood cubes, stomach and heart. And it was very tasty! We also got to buy lots of fruit ourselves. They sell these half pineapples that you can eat like lollipops, and massive bags of lychees for about 50p

I guess it's okay to experience one time. We took a boat ride from Ninh Kiều wharf at around 4:30 AM. Saw many boats but about half of them were tourist boats. All the tourist boats made stops at places that sell stuff to tourists and wouldn't exist otherwise. Felt like a tourist trap. I didn't see much trading going on other than between tourists and vendors that sell stuff to tourists.
good food
Interesting local market best visited in the early morning
Should go early in the morning for more choices, and ofc to avoid hot sunny day

I have been here recently and was very exciting. It is because of many tourists and hawkers. It was crowded and noisy. I was surprised what hawkers to drive the boats in and out. I will be back if i have a chance.
Touristic place, I’ll prefer Phong Dien Floating market instead
Felt very 'touristy' as there were almost more tourist boats on the water than produce boats. Nonetheless it was an enjoyable morning and an experience worth recommending.
Large-ish wholesale market on the Mekong Delta. Interesting to see. Best seen at about 6-6.30 am. There's small coffee and noodle boats between the big ones offering breakfast

Getting too tourist oriented and what it seems less for its original purpose. After few years there will be no floating market anymore beside selling bits and peaces to tourists in case they can persuade them coming there.
Fine foods with fine prices. Easy to reach by boat.
The place must to visit to knew more about the life of living on the board
Floating while eating, drink things you like :)
Interesting to see a relic of an obsolete form of commerce, but variety of food and goods available is limited.
If you never ever visiting Cho Noi... floating market in Can Tho... You better make that trip experience yourself. Good to go
A little over rated with the tour package; ; and the tour for stopping on land is not good just wasting time to stop especially at the fruit garden.
Small boats of fruits & vegetables. Worth the experience if you buy from small boat. Tourists can stop in land market to eat at cafe. Buy a drink & borrow a knife for your watermelon.
Recommend. But take a small boat and be there early 6-7am. It's okey to bargain if you buy more than one fruit per vendor.
Water, boat and enjoy local foods and fruits
One of the shop in Can Tho Floating Market.
Fascinating to visit. Early morning is best. Various local produce.
Can tho floating market is a famous Tourist destination for you to experience.
Very different market place than any other place in the world. Real market floating on the river. Nice experience to go around on boats and shop.

You can tell what each boat on the floating market is selling by looking at a stick with a piece of the fruit or vegetable tied to it🍍🍉

Other than fruit and vegetable boats there are coffee boats, laundry boats, restaurant boats and even a karaoke boat!
Fantatics! Very exciting in Cai Rang floating market. This is beautiful activity in “Mien Tay” ò Vietnam.
Market is a nice idea. Just all the sellers are surrounding you like crazy that is a little bit annoying
Mesmerizing experience and humbled by the hardship of the boat residents but they are always so cheerful, so much for us to learn
Not overly touristy, like the markets in Bangkok.  Come early to beat the heat and see the locals doing business
if you can reach this market during the sunrise you can enjoy the best moment for visiting this place
Great local floating market fascinating to see how it all works
Great experience. I had even tried noodle soup and coffee for breakfast on the boat
A must-try if you come to Can Tho City. Very local and special experience!
It is interesting in the morning. It a traditional market in Cần Thơ province
The beautiful floating market sells a lot of food
Awesome Floating market
The 4 times visit to Can Tho and Cai Rang Float Market, still exciting!!!
Try go early. The place is nice
An eye-opening experience. Visited the floating market on a private speed boat.
Very unique experience.
An early start but worth it, great to see the trade.
Worth doing but too many other tourists
Tight and somewhat dirty, but, fish, fruit & vegetable  here all fresh
Worth a visit. Beers on the river are always good
Not a lot of crocodile products and that’s a great thing!
Nice boats drive on the river
no one cares about the environment here, but that's just the way it is
Very nice tradition market in mekong delta
Wonderful experience
Wicked........bought beer and pineapple on a boat market
Super.
Nice & unique experience for tourists
interesting place
Another perspective other than the conventional trade exchange
A Special type of market in Mekong delta VN
Interesting boat trip for tourist
An exciting experiment !
Just traveling on a boat for fun.
Nice to go early morning

Nice city, i like it :)
Unlike anything I have seen before

Interesting place to visit.
Overhyped
