Username: Cty TNHH DV Photocopy In ấn Trí Nghĩa
Comment:
Chợ nổi Cái Răng họp khá sớm đó nhé từ khoảng 4, 5 giờ sáng các ghe xuồng đã bắt đầu cập chợ vậy nên bạn cần đi sớm một chút, tính toán dư thời gian để có thể tới chợ lúc đông vui nhất.Phiên chợ sẽ họp đông vui nhất vào khoảng 6 giờ sáng và sẽ vãn dần vào khoảng 8, 9 giờ.xuống tàu rời bến Ninh Kiều để tới chợ nổi lúc 4 giờ 30, khoảng 5 giờ tôi đã có mặt tại đây. Lúc này trời vẫn còn hơi tối, mặt trời chưa ló dạng cộng thêm gió trên sông hiu hiu thổi khiến tôi khá dễ chịu nhưng cũng khá buồn ngủ.

Thật may là tiếng buôn bán của khu chợ đã khiến tôi vượt qua cơn ngủ và trở lại với “cuộc vui”. Hãy chắc rằng bạn không ngủ gật nhé, vì bỏ lỡ chợ nổi sẽ buồn lắm đấy!
Rất thích thú vì sự nhộn nhịp của chơ. Ăn các món ăn như bún, phở, hủ tiếu,... trên sông nước của chợ này đúng là một trải nghiệm khó quên.

Chợ nổi Cái Răng cũng là chợ đầu mối chuyên mua bán sỉ các loại trái cây, nông sản của vùng. Hàng hóa tập trung ở đây với số lượng lớn. Mỗi mặt hàng đã được phân loại cho đồng đều về chất lượng, kích cỡ. Nếu như dân địa phương và các vùng lân cận thường sử dụng các ghe, xuồng trung bình chở các mặt hàng nông sản đến đây tiêu thụ thì những ghe bầu lớn là của các thương lái thu mua trái cây tỏa đi khắp nơi, sang tận Campuchia và Trung Quốc.
Thường thì mua bán gì thì sẽ treo thứ đó lên cây sào được cắm trên ghe, chợ nổi giờ không còn tấp nập như xưa, đi muộn thì hầu như không còn gì để xem, chủ yếu ngắm sông và cầu Cần Thơ là chính. Cũng có thể kết hợp thăm quan chợ nổi và mấy vườn trái cây gần đó nhưng là vườn khai thác du lịch nên cũng không được đặc sắc lắm.

Các tiểu thương bán hét giá quá nghe cái muốn sỉu ngang.giá cao cũng cao vừa vừa.có đâu mà cao hơn thị trường gấp 3_4 lần vậy sao dc.làm vậy mất hình ảnh đẹp truyền thống của người miền tây chúng ta hết.
Không còn bản sắc thực sự của chợ nổi miền Tây, khách du lịch nhiều hơn người họp chợ, dịch vụ xô bồ, ít giá trị khám phá!
Khởi hành từ chợ nổi vào 5h sáng, ra tớ chợ nổi ăn sáng trên sông nhưng mấy người bán trên này bán giá rất mắc, bất kỳ đồ ăn gì cũng mắc hơn trên lộ

Chán thái độ phục vụ của lò kẹo ghê.
"Ai có khẩu trang thì đeo vô, ai không có thì đi về"
Ủa rồi không biết bán khẩu trang cho người ta sao?
Chợ sinh hoạt sớm, tấp nập người mua, kẻ bán, tham quan đến từ các vùng miền sinh động náo nhiệt; Nên đến cho biết.

Pas une vraiment une fabrique de nouilles, car la pâte des nouilles est dans des sacs de 50kg.
Plus une attraction pour touristes.
船費一人十萬小孩五萬 我去時 真沒什麼可以看 逛。或許是疫情期間吧
Не е като по филмите. След построяването на моста пазара е предимно за туристическа атракция.
Mang màu sắc thương mại khá nhiều không còn nét dân dã. Nhiều cò chèo kéo đi đò.
Đi trước 6h để ngắm nhiều tàu thuyền hơn
the floating market has disappeared, no more small or large boats, replaced by trucks on the roads or almost, a radical change in 10 years

The poor review is mainly because of the guide's (Beo's) attitude, which is one of the worst I have experiences in Vietnam. He spends the entire tour telling you you should smile at people because South Vietnamese people are so kind etc but if something happens to you on the tour, don't count on his help. I fell off a bike into a small river because the company takes you on this ride on old and non-functional bikes on a path that is full of very steep little bridges and instead of asking me if I was OK, Beo treated me like a nuisance disturbing his schedule. He even expected me to ride on the bus for 1 hour in completely wet and muddied clothes and when I insisted on changing my clothes he got upset at me. Another participant told me she had raised a health issue with him at some point and he was completely not receptive.  It's a pity because otherwise the tour is very interesting and Beo's English is very good so he can tell you interesting stories but with this kind of attitude you just don't feel safe.  PS. Like in other reviews, worth noting that the floating market is not what you would expect, it's just a few big boats, you barely see any products being sold. I would also scrap that bike tour, which is not safe and is clearly a filler in the schedule.

The hotel in Can Though was nice, but 3.5km from the night market. We also arrived too late to see any of the local sights on our own. We started too late on the second day to have a good experience at the Caí Rang floating market. Our group voted on a start time, which unfortunately i lost the vote. So we predominantly saw tourist boats and boats wanting to sell to us, but no locals buying from each other. I think because of the late start we also missed out on a couple of other experiences that were part of the itinerary.
Unfortunately, you hardly see anything of the delta, you don't learn anything about the history and the food is bad. The tour isn't worth it.

It is not worth the money you pay $123, so many bus hours, let alone waste two days traveling in this wonderful country. The first day was fine, but there is a day trip for less than $25 that does and you return to Ho Chi Minh. It was a lot of road to visit a floating market with 10 local boats. The West Hotel where we were housed was very old, and the walls were full of moisture; you entered through an alley through a ruined building. We were told it had 3 stars but I doubt it ever had even one.

The river is quite polluted, and traffic at the floating market has decreased quite a lot compared to past times. Altogether this tour felt very "touristy" and oriented towards selling things. It was the worst tour we did in Vietnam and we kind of regretted spending two days for it. Our guide was not very helpful and did not provide any useful information—he mostly just read out signs and pointed to things that we obviously saw... Unfortunately, we really had a bad time with the guide, partially due to a unique misunderstanding between him and Vietnam Adventure Tours. A 1-hour yelling match on the phone ensued as we traveled to the Mekong Delta. Objectively though, he did not provide us with much additional information and also pleaded with us not to give a bad review. I think he was aware that his contributions were... sub-optimal. I don't know in which capacity you could organize it yourself but there were definitely not "hidden gems" or highlights we wouldn't have thought of ourselves. I think it was just a bit easier having everything organized for us, but as mentioned, it might be suitable to do something shorter or to try and go to the places of interest by yourself.

The tour is very designed for tourists. You first stop at a restaurant where you get fresh fruit and tea. Meanwhile, Vietnamese songs were sung to us and a basket for tips was put on the table. Continuing we took the paddle boat to the next stop where we got honey tea and dried fruit. Of course we could also buy these. After that we went to Coconutcandy production. Unfortunately rather small and manageable. Apparently, the sweet is also only made and sold for the passing tourists. We haven't really seen much of the Mekong Delta. We liked the trip by paddle boat the most, although it was very short and the river was full of other boats. After that we had a good lunch (fish, rice, vegetables). Afterwards we went on to Can Tho to the hotel. Since there was so much traffic, the journey took 4 hours instead of 2. The hotel (2 stars) was clean and the breakfast was ok. The next day we went to the floating market in the morning. There, the sellers were not travelling with their fruit in paddle boats, as you know from the colourful photos, but with motor-powered boats. Our guide explained to us that paddle boats were used 30-40 years ago and motorboats are easier for sellers. Afterwards we made a short stop at the local market and continued for lunch at the beautiful "Mekong Resort Stop". There was good fish, soup, rice, vegetables and fruit again.  We imagined the tour to be a little different. 2 stars there because our guide was nice and effortful, the mini van comfortable and air conditioned and the food was very good. Also the driver was very friendly and drove very pleasantly. Perhaps you will find a tour where the organizer is more concerned and not the usual tourist route is followed.

We hired a stay at Homestay and immediately after starting the trip, we were ‘convinced’ that it was better to stay at 3-star hotel, we were sold as an improvement when the price was the same from the beginning, all because we were the only ones in the group who had chosen that option. The room in Can Tho, no windows and not very clean. The group was 14 people when 10 maximum. Otherwise acceptable, the food delicious.

I am sorry but there is only a market on some boats for tourists. Don't believe the pictures they show you! Pictures on the internet from a river full of colorful small boats are from the past. We woke up early for nothing what we did could also be done at 10 in the morning. Use your money for something else.

Very disappointing it’s just tourist boats sailing around a marina. A few barges are carrying produce to be unloaded and a few small boats selling coffee and water. It has its charm as all marinas do but avoid sitting on a boat breathing in diesel stay in bed it’s not worth it
It's sad here...pretty empty and not much to see. Food is not great either....every broth is sweet.  The only fun part was the hu tieu factory where you get to watch and make your own.

We were a little disappointed with this floating market for some reason - I’m unsure exactly what we expected but it wasn’t this. I was extremely hungover too and could barely withstand being on the boat so perhaps this had something to do with it BUT the 4:30AM wake up was definitely not worth it in my opinion.

The idyllic floating markets that you have in your head are not this one. They’re just some cargo boats with men throwing fruit around and few of them at that. Certainly wouldn’t advise anybody pay a boat man or tour guide to visit here as you can also watch it from the side of the river and have the exact same experience. The only positive was boating down the ‘small canals’ which were tranquil, though nothing I’ve not seen before!

See the images for a fuller picture…

Not like it used to be anymore. Way too commercialized. Everyone trying to sell you boat tickets.  The boat moves way too quick to enjoy the experience. They want to take you to floating restaurant and attractions that sell you more stuff. Kids under 15 do not enjoy it at all cuz it so early in the morning. Ok for adults who want to experience it.

To us this market did not live up to the hype. It looks nothing like the pictures online. This market is shrinking now that roads and bridges are being built. We went on a 7 hour small boat tour and it was way too long. Take the earliest tour you can find for 3 to 4 hours max and you will see it all. Other than the sunrise it was not worth the early wakeup for me.

We, family of four, rented a small boat from locals at Can Tho wharf today to go to the floating market of Cai Rang. Renting such a boat is a must do, slow travelling.
Either the season is wrong for a visit or the market no longer exists? Floating restaurants we did see, feeding the mouths of the dozens of large tourist boats, splitting through the water like speedboats. Cai Rang floating market, unfortunately, is being consumed to death and, it seems, will soon become a tourist trap.
And that's not so good news for the locals who, on a small scale, live off the floating market. The small boats we tied together to eat and drink coffee, the surprise in our plates, not understanding each other but always communicating, isn't that what's Cai Rang floating market is about?

Unfortunately, this floating market is not what it used to be.   Don't be fooled by the photos advertised online and through tour agencies. All old photos of the floating market. Can Tho is known for this famous floating market but it is dying so dont waste your money. We went 10 years ago and  it was such an amazing experience and wanted to share the experience with friends. We recently went again, and it was very disappointing - there was only a handful of boats,  the vendor were rude and there wasnt much to see.

Cai Rang has been overhyped.  It’s not hundreds of small boats and a busy market as pictured and described, it’s majority tourist barges and a few local barges which don’t really do any actual commerce.  There’s a lone small boat making coffee, one noodles, and then a couple of floating restaurants for tourists.  Most tours will take you to a fake rice paper factory and a shop in the hopes of selling you local stuff.  The water is littered with garbage.  The prefecture is trying to keep this iconic attraction alive, but at this point it’s a gimmick for tourists and not worth the multiple hours drive down from
Ho Chi Minh.

Quite a disappointment to be honest. Paid a local 200k vnd for an hour on the water (they started with a 400k price). Got dropped off on several locations to buy stuff. No thank you.
The amount of waste (plastics and styrofoam food containers) in the river is painful to see.
Go very very early in the morning before the sun goes up. More sellers to tourists than an actual market. Fun if you are already there - not worth a trip.

A trap for tourists! We arrange a boat tour with the hotel at 5:30 and basically the recap was:
- the driver removing plastic bags from the propeller every now and then
- boat trying to sell things specifically and obviously made for tourists
- a couple of boats selling fruits and vegetables (really really few)
- breathing a lot of engine exhaust gas (as always 😂)
The “guide” then just drop us in a floating platform full of tourists and shops selling packed stuff again 100% made for tourist.
We decided to go there, knowing that it was a sort of scam, just to see with our eyes how bad this market became, sadly 😔 I wouldn’t say I don’t recommend it, just be aware what you are going through, live the experience as it really is and not as they want to sell it!
Really small market made for tourists. disappointing
More tourists than vendors. Conventional markets moved to streets as everyone has scooter/bike/car, floating market kept only for tourism

Way overrated experience.  There were more tourist boats than local boats selling anything.
We got pestered to buy orange juice and other fruits etc...was nothing like the pictures we saw before going.
Waking up at 4am....pointless. They took us to a smaller market first, which was literally 4 boats...nothing else...and we made it to Cai Rang by 7am. Do not recommend this tour...:(

Unfortunately, the floating market seems to be dying since covid. Went there with a small boat at sunrise and it's basically just a tourist attraction - some boats selling coffee, fruits or soup with 90% tourists and almost no locals shopping there.
Absolutely not worth it in my opinion, especially not if you're coming from HCM just for this.
If you're passing by Can Tho anyway, I guess it's still okay to check out, if you're really interested - otherwise save your time and money.

Very disappointed. Since the new bridge was built, the market is loosing its flow. There are just a few big boats with a just 1 kind of fruits and some little boats that buy from them.
Not really big. Maybe we expected much. Renting a boat and driving through the small canals was nice.
Can Tho was pretty fun at night but the floating market is a bust. Mostly just a bunch of larger boats selling fruits to smaller boats. Not worth the trip, sorry.

In 1995 a very special experience, in 2020 most boats are tourist boats and a few locals selling fruits. Not much of a market anymore, times changes. Update the guide books! Take a trip through the canals instead.
No doubt this was one magical, but there are now more tourist boats than market boats.  Save you money and just take a ferry ride across the river and back to see a bit of river life.
It’s nice to see some boats selling fruits and vegetables. However it’s unbelievable the amount of trash floating in the river.

Can Tho market was the confirmation that our days in the south of Vietnam were a complete waste. Touristy, people will try to rip you off in any way, poor connections.
The market was original, yet not suprising after having visited the whole country.
Just some ships selling fruit in bulks and lots of tourist boats - i didn't think it was very special. There must be more beautiful, smaller ones.
Can't buy any fruit or veggies here due to not safe to eat. Been soaked in chemical before sale in the floating market. So not sure which one is ok.
I went to Cho Noi one day a week, so disappointed, too few boats, not at all like the show on TV, hope the weekend will be better
Not worth visiting. There very only a few boats selling fruits and most of the boats were tourist boats. I expected an authentic and lively market and I was very disappointed.

Einen Aufenthalt in Can Tho legt man ja eigentlich nur wegen den berühmten floating market  ein. Deshalb war auch ich dort und bin vor 5 Uhr aufgestanden um, letztendlich, Nudelsuppe schlürfende Touristen zu beobachten. Mehr gab's nicht zu sehen. Kaum noch Boote der locals und wenn dann sind sie umzingelt von Touristenbooten die Kaffe oder Nudelsuppe kaufen. Der einzige Farbtupfer sind die vielen orangenen Schwimmwesten...Der Markt ist tot, eine Touristenfalle und man kann besser ausschlafen. Wo die farbenfrohen Fotos von mit Obst und Blumen beladenen Booten entstanden sind bleibt ein Geheimnis. Für mich eine Enttäuschung, aber da ich mir ein privates Boot genommen habe und keine Tour gebucht, habe ich wenigstens einen Einheimischen unterstützt.

Doch, hier findet aktiv Großhandel und Einzelhandel statt. Hier leben ja viele Menschen auf dem Boot. Man hat aber tatsächlich den Eindruck, dass es mehr Touristenboote als Einkäufer gibt. Und da es in Can Tho gerade fast keine europäischen …

En effet le marché flottant n’existe quasiment plus ! Si ce n’est un défilé de bateaux remplis de touristes ainsi que quelques bateaux qui viennent vous accoster pour vous proposer cafés et soupes de nouilles à des prix bien plus élevés qu’ailleurs (pour des quantités faibles). C’était quand même sympa de prendre son petit déjeuner sur le bateau pour l’experience.

L’arrêt à la fabrique de nouilles n’a vraiment aucun intérêt et l’arrêt au verger encore moins. On essayera de vous faire payer 30k supplémentaire par personne pour entrer et visiter le verger qui n’est absolument pas entretenu et vous faire déguster une assiette de fruits sous cellophane (qui ne viennent certainement pas de leur verger 😂😂).

Si vous voulez voir les locaux faire leur vie et leurs courses, il faut dorénavant se diriger vers les marchés terrestres de la ville (ou plutôt marché en scooter), ce que nous avons fait après être revenu du bateau vers 8h30. C’est beaucoup plus photogénique et authentique !

Si vous tenez à y aller absolument, négociez au plus bas et ne vous attendez à rien. C’est le seul moyen d’être peut-être agréablement surpris.

La balade en bateau vaut tout de même le coup car c’est toujours sympathique. Nous avons payé 150 000 par personne pour une petite barque de 4 personnes et n’avons pas payé la dégustation au verger donc seulement la visite gratuite après négociation.
Café à 20k et soupe de nouilles à 50k (en réalité cela devrait être plutôt 30/35k).

Trải nghiệm thú vị khi đi trên sông ngắm bình minh, nhưng chợ nổi không tấp nập như quảng cáo. Giá vé vô chừng và các bên làm dịch vụ tại chợ đêm hay chèo kéo khách, kê giá, không có bảng giá công khai được quản lý bởi 1 đơn vị nào cả
Các điểm tham quan làm dịch vụ chưa tốt, quán ăn trên nhà bè không mấy thân thiện, Lò hủ tiếu là ám ảnh nhất, khu vực nhà vệ sinh ở đó rất khủng khiếp. Tôi sẽ không quay lại.

Lạ lạ một chút nhưng bán buôn gạt gẫm. Về miền Tây để tìm lại chút gì ngày xưa nhưng thấy thất vọng quá.
Mua đồ mấy cái ghe rượt theo cặp thuyền thì coi chừng bị tráo đồ tào lao nhé bà con

Chợ nổi Cái Răng giờ du lịch hóa, trái cây bán mắc hơn trên đất liền, ăn uống cũng mắc hơn trên đất liền, chợ nổi giờ chỉ có 1 đoạn nhỏ chỉ phục vụ cho khách du lịch là chính, không nhộn nhịp cảnh mua bán như trước..., nếu ai chưa đi thì cũng nên đi để trải nghiệm khi đến Cần Thơ nhé!
Als wir heute vor Ort waren, gab es quasi keinen Markt wie es zu erwarten gewesen wäre, dafür ausschließlich auf die Touristen ausgerichtete Verkaufsstände. Enttäuschend!

Trãi nghiệm lần đầu tới đây.
Cách chợ nổi tầm 1km có 1 anh xe máy dẫn chạy vào bãi xe.
Giá thuê 1 tàu cho gia đình 8 người lớn, 4 em bé hơn 1tr. Giá ở bến Ninh Kiều là 700k.
Trả giá 700k.
Tàu xuất phát. Cảnh ghe chợ đìu hiu chỉ có vài chiếc tàu chở trái cây, vài chiếc ghe nhỏ chạy lại chào hàng, vài chiếc ghe bán đồ ăn sáng giữa sông, vài nhà bè bán đồ ăn sáng.
Nhìn chung có 1 buổi trãi nghiệm quá thất vọng

Không giống như hình ảnh trên mạng, rất chán, không có không khí họp chợ, chủ yếu là các thuyền bán trái cây, nước, cà phê cho khách du lịch, có thuyền bán hủ tiếu. Giá khá mắc, hủ tiếu 50k, cafe 35k. Đông nhờ khách du lịch.

Leider ein Reinfall, wir stellten uns viele kleine Boote vor mit Obst und sonstigem. Leider sind es nur noch ein paar wenige und vor allem große Boote. Zusätzlich wird man zu größeren Verkaufsshops auf dem Wasser geführt. Auch mit dem kleinen Extraboot dauert es ewig bis man dort ist. Sehr, sehr schade für uns  war es leider ein Reinfall und der unnötigste  Ausflug seit langer Zeit und das morgens um 5.

Di chuyển: Di chuyển đương nhiên là thuyền, và thuyền thì đủ loại thượng vàng hạ cám. Tối cứ đi dạo bến Ninh Kiều sẽ được chào mời nhiệt tình, tham khảo khoảng 10 thuyền rồi trả giá và lựa chọn. Chú ý hỏi kĩ về giá tiền, lộ trình, thời gian đi về và phụ phí... Tốt nhất là bao thuyền nhỏ nếu đi đông cho thoải mái.
Thời điểm bắt đâu nên là 5 giờ hơn để tới nơi lúc sáng sớm. Khung cảnh chợ hơi hụt hẫng, không nhộn nhịp như mong đợi. Các thuyền thưa thớt và phần lớn là phục vụ du lịch, không còn nét tự nhiên nữa. Một nửa số thuyền bán các loại trái cây và chào mời dọc đường đi, ai muốn có thể mua ăn thử, chất lượng ổn. Một nửa còn lại là bán đồ ăn và quà lưu niệm, nhìn chung là đắt hơn trên bờ khá nhiều, chất lượng thì không hơn, có thể lên tham quan và một số loại bánh đặc trưng.
Tóm lại chợ nổi Cái Răng chỉ còn là hoài niệm.

Rất ủng hộ du lịch Vietnam phát triển nhưng 10 năm đi chợ nổi Cái Răng nhiều lần nhưng vẫn k thấy thay đổi mấy
Không gian ảm đạm ko có màu sắc, thuyền nhỏ cập vô đa số chào hàng buôn bán, mạnh ai nấy làm
Nên quy hoạch tổng thể chỉ dành cho buôn bán du lịch, trang trí màu sắc tạo nét độc đáo riêng cho chợ nổi, sắp xếp lại hàng quán, biển quảng cáo

Chợ không có tàu thuyền tấp nập buôn bán mà chủ yếu là tàu chở khách du lịch tham quan.
Mình có ăn hủ tiếu trên ghe do ng dân bán vs giá 45k. Nước dùng ngọt, chỉ có vài lát thịt vs giá đó mình nghĩ là bán cho khách du lịch chứ k phải cho người địa phương.
1 ly cà phê sữa đá giá 15k mình đánh giá ổn.
5h sáng mình có mặt ở chợ An Sinh được một cô giới thiệu cho đi ghe 2 người 200k, có thể tấp vào bất kì đâu hay mua gì đó, chở đi 1 vòng CHỈ trên chợ nổi.

Quá buồn cho một cuộc tình
Mình đi vào giữa tháng 04, mình đặt tour bên Dịch vụ Tuấn gì đấy, ngay đầu bến thuyền 50k/ người sẽ được đi chợ nổi Cái Răng + làng hủ tiếu, cốm. Sáng 5h tàu chạy, mình đi có 4 người nên phải đi ghép với đoàn khác. Từ bến thuyền Ninh Kiều tàu chạy cũng khá xa mới đến chợ nổi. Đến chợ nổi thì có vài thuyền nhỏ đến gần bán nước, cf, trái cây,... Xong thuyền tấp vào 1 thuyền khác là cửa hàng Bún - Phở ăn sáng. Chị chủ khác vui tính, "Sáng nay chúng ta được ăn bún lắc, lắc quoa, lắc lại.." Khách nào có hát karaoke thì được free, free cho trẻ em dưới 6 tuổi. Giá 1 tô bình thường là 40k, nước lèo khá lạt, nói chung là buồn tập 1. Đi xuống thêm 1 đoạn nữa là khu trung tâm chợ nổi. Mùa mình đi chỉ có dưa hấu và ít thơm/ khóm với xoài thôi, vô nhìn ít, buồn lắm, mặc dù là mình đi rất sớm. Xong rồi thuyền quay lại bến Ninh Kiều. Tổng thời gian chuyến đi chưa được 90 phút nữa @@ Buồn tập 2

Chợ nổi không còn xôm tụ như hay thấy trên hình vì chỉ có 1 khu nhỏ ghe hàng thôi và cũng ko có cảnh buôn bán gì nhiều. Đa phần người dân đã chuyển lên chợ trên cạn gần đó. Đi hơi trễ chút thì sẽ thấy ghe khách đông hơn ghe hàng, họ chạy vòng quanh khu ghe hàng nhìn cảnh tượng thiệt lộn xộn. Nếu bạn chọn đi xuồng nhỏ, lúc này sẽ dễ len lỏi giữa các ghe hàng, có cảm giác hơn về chợ nổi nhưng bù lại đoạn đường về khá chậm.

Ăn uống là 1 điểm nhấn thú vị trong chuyến đi. Mình mua ghe nào họ sẽ móc neo vô thuyền mình cho khỏi trôi. Mình ăn 1 ghe bán hủ tiếu khá ngon, giá tầm 40k, ngồi chòng chành ăn là kỷ niệm đáng nhớ. Cafe, trái cây bán trên ghe khác, đủ món, dễ lựa và nhớ trả giá.

Ở đây rất dễ tìm được ghe để đi, từ lớn tới nhỏ, đi riêng hoặc ghép đoàn đều có, bạn chỉ cần dạo bến Ninh Kiều coi chỗ nào hợp hợp thì lựa thôi. Mình chọn chiếc xuồng nhỏ để đi riêng cho tiện theo ý thích, giá năm 2023 là 300k/chuyến 3 người, đi đủ nơi.

宿が手配してくれたツアー（4人で1人あたり300kVND=約1800JPY、川上り、果樹園、米麺工場とセット）で訪問。昔はなかなか面白いところだったらしいが、事前情報通り、今はもはや観光客のボートと観光客目当ての物売りボートばかりでかつての地元の人による賑わいはない。ツアーは必ず水上の土産物屋で降ろされるが、ここも大したものはない。早朝5:30出発で行ったのだが、そこまでして来る価値はないと思う。

Chợ nổi Cái Răng chỉ còn toàn là khách du lịch ngắm nhau. Hủ tiếu thì dở hơn hàng quán trên bờ, tôm chưa làm sạch, trái cây thì mắc, người bán người mua không thấy, chỉ có khách du lịch tự ngắm nhau. Gọi là thăm làng nghề hủ tiếu nhưng thực chất chỉ có cái máy làm kiểng, họ dừng lại cho mình mua những đồ có trên đất liền với giá gấp 1,5. Vậy là hết tour rồi đó! Thà qua làng nổi Tân Lập còn được chèo thuyền ngắm thiên nhiên yên bình.

Chợ nổi Cái Răng là chợ nổi chuyên trao đổi, mua bán nông sản, các loại trái cây, hàng hóa, thực phẩm, ăn uống ở trên sông và là điểm tham quan đặc sắc của quận Cái Răng, thành phố Cần Thơ.
46 Đường Hai Bà Trưng, Tân An, Ninh Kiều, Cần Thơ

Khi xưa, dường như mọi hoạt động mua bán đều diễn ra trên những chiếc ghe dập dìu trên dòng sông hiền hoà bao đời chở che. Ngày nay, khi những con đường bê-tông được mở rộng, hàng hoá trao đổi thuận tiện hơn thì hoạt động mua bán trên sông vẫn diễn ra tấp nập như một nét văn hoá đặc sắc của vùng Tây Nam Bộ.

Kinh nghiệm đi Chợ nổi Cái Răng của mình:
- Bạn nên đặt vé đi chợ nổi trước một ngày. Tham khảo các đại lý uy tín hoặc tuỳ chọn ghe tư nhân nếu các bạn thấy giá cả phù hợp nhé. Nhưng đừng để tới lúc đến nơi mới tìm ghe vì có thể bạn sẽ bị “chém” đẹp đấy. Giá 1 ghe riêng (từ 1-4 người) đi tham quan 3-4 điểm khoảng 300k nè.
- Nhiều người sẽ nói với bạn rằng bạn nên đi từ bến Ninh Kiều tới chợ nổi, nhưng mình nghĩ nó sẽ tốn mất 1 tiếng cả đi và về mà trên đường đi cũng không có gì đặc sắc. Thay vào đó bạn có thể tới chợ An Bình và xuống ghe ngay bến ở gần đó.
- Chợ nổi họp rất sớm, khoảng 4h tới 8, 9h là vãn nên các bạn tranh thủ đi sớm. Vừa có thể ngắm bức tranh lao động trên dòng sông lúc trời còn chưa hửng sáng, vừa cảm nhận được sự khoan khoái của buổi bình minh trên dòng sông hiền hoà, e ấp.

Có hai điều làm mình cảm thấy tiếc nuối nhất sau khi tham quan chợ nổi Cái Răng. Thứ nhất là cảnh họp chợ đã không còn tấp nập như xưa, hoạt động chủ yếu nhất trên sông là đưa đón khách du lịch mà thôi. Hai là tình trạng ô nhiễm trên sông đang ở mức nặng cũng như ý thức bảo vệ môi trường của người dân chưa được tốt làm mình rất buồn. Hy vọng trong tương lai tình trạng này sẽ được cải thiện.
Rác quá nhiều, khách tham quan đông gấp mấy lần người bán cả người dân và du khách đều rất thiếu ý thức bảo vệ môi trường. K biết sau vài năm nữa thì cảnh quan sẽ như nào. Thất vọng
Chợ nổi rất chán. Đi trúng mùa không có gì. Ko đáng tiền thăm quan

Chợ nổi Cái Răng là chợ chuyên trao đổi, mua bán nông sản, các loại trái cây, hàng hóa, thực phẩm, ăn uống ở trên sông và là điểm tham quan đặc sắc của quận Cái Răng, thành phố Cần Thơ.

Chợ Cái Răng thường họp khá sớm, thường từ lúc mờ sáng và đến khoảng 8, 9 giờ thì vãn.

Khách tham quan nên đi vào khoảng 6-7 giờ là tốt vì có thể tham quan đúng vào lúc chợ hoạt động đông đúc nhất. Các ghe nhỏ thường đến chào mời khách du lịch. ( các bạn đừng bao giờ mua ở đây vì sẽ bị hôa giá và bị cân điêu từ 400g đến 600g do cân của người bán)

Chợ không hoạt động và hoạt động rất ít vào các ngày Tết Âm Lịch (mồng 1 và mồng 2 Tết, Tết Đoan Ngọ (mồng 5 tháng Năm âm lịch).

Do nhu cầu của người đi chợ nên không chỉ có các xuồng trái cây, nông sản phẩm mà còn có nhiều loại dịch vụ khác: phở, hủ tiếu, cà phê, quán nhậu nổi... Các xuồng dịch vụ (thường là thuyền nhỏ) len lõi phục vụ khách đi chợ và cả khách tham quan lạ.

Giá vé tour bán ở Bến Ninh Kiều là: 300k cho một ghe riêng hôm mình đi 3 người tính ra 100k người
Nếu đi chung tour thì 50k người

Siamo stati floating market, alloggiando di fronte, dalle 5.30 del mattino alle 6.30 come consigliato dalle guide. Purtroppo è stata una vera delusione come avevamo letto in altre recensioni, ma abbiamo voluto dargli una possibilità essendo ancora molto riportato sulle guide! Ci sono pochissime barche locali che scambiano merce e per lo più barchette, comunque poche, che cercando di vendere ai turisti bibite e altro. Sono più le barche dei tour per turisti che le barche locali. Ormai è una tradizione scomparsa. Lo confermano i due barconi con souvenir e poco altro.

Worst tour operator we’ve ever had. They are awful. Beware! This was a bit of a disaster to start with. I booked our trip through Viator well in advance and received confirmation from someone at the company called Ms. Hannah. She listed a What’s App number and said to reach out with any questions. We wanted to see how much it was to upgrade our hotel and also wanted to return to a different location in District 1 after the trip. I sent Hannah a What’s App message on Dec 8, Dec 13, Dec 23, and Jan 1 (for a Jan 2 tour). She only responded on Jan 1 and did not answer my question. I also emailed the company twice and called Hannah on her number twice without a response. As a last resort, I called Viator. The person I spoke to did not understand English very well and just told me that she would send a message to the company. The company never responded. I figured that upgrading would be nice, but not a big deal and I would work out the drop off with the guides on Jan 2. I was a little worried about the tour in general as I have never had a company that ignored questions. The day of the tour, the guide was 20 minutes late to pick up and when they arrived, stated that we had too much luggage . This was a group tour of up to 20 people and because it was overnight, we all had a medium suitcase. The van they had for us actually had no luggage space. Other customers had large backpacks and had to place them on the seats. How is this alright? The guide spent 30 minutes trying to figure out what to do and they finally said we would have to cancel or drop off our luggage at the tour office (wherever that was) and pack an overnight bag instead. The tour operator I spoke to on the phone that morning stated that it was my responsibility to let them know before the trip that we each had a suitcase. How ridiculous! They expected us to pack travel backpacks without actually informing us that was the case. There was no information in the confirmation email about this nor was there follow up information regarding suitcases. In the end, my husband finally said he thought he could get the bags in the van and he did. After that, I didn’t bother asking about the hotel upgrade, just happy the trip was starting. The trip itself was our least favorite in Vietnam. We thought the Mekong and floating village would be more authentic and a throw back in time. That was not the case. Calling it a floating market is kind of a joke. The tourist boats and tourists greatly outnumbered the people who live there. In fact, there really isn’t a market except for the tourists. It’s now just a tourist trap and the people who show you their method of making coconut candy and rice paper are just trying to get through as many tourists as possible which was extremely evident when they stuck numbered stickers on the arm skin of every tourist walking into the “market”. The river itself has tons of trash and you see people actively polluting (not caring about the river at all). At other times, boat operators would demand tips before you even get done with the ride. Our tour guide was enthusiastic, but did not know enough English to answer our questions or explain the background of what we were doing or the plans. When we would eat lunch, he would just sit us down and say when we would leave, never explaining the fixed lunch menu or how that related to local culture. When food was placed on the table, the servers could care less about you and didn’t seem to care at all about their guests. Most of the restaurants we went to also seemed to only cater to tourists - we rarely if ever saw locals eating there. When we went on a 15 minute bike ride, it was unclear what we were seeing, and there were tons of mopeds driving on the path which made it a bit chaotic and dangerous. That was how it was the whole trip. It was also a long way from HCMC and although it was broken up well on the way there and back, it does eat up time. I would not recommend this excursion, or more specifically this actual tour, to other travelers.

They called me less than 24h before and cancelled my tour because they don’t work on Jan 1st (I had booked this tour almost 3 months ago). When insisting, they told me that they overbooked and that they were going to try to arrange something for us and that they will call back. I didn’t receive a call so I tried to contact them on whatsapp. Whoever was in charge of the whatsapp account confirm that the tour was going ahead and that they were going to pick me up at 8 am the next day.

The next day came and no one was here to pick me up at 8 am, so I called and they told me that the tour was cancelled and that they couldn’t do anything (they could reschedule but we had the flight the next day so we couldn’t do it). We were now checked out of our hotel and with no hotel that night cause it was included on the tour.

We decided to go to their headquarters office and try to talk to someone there. When we arrived, at first, they didn’t believe us (the costumer), because they called Lucy the person that told us that it was cancelled and this person said that they sent an email (never received a written email or response saying that it was confirm that was cancelled apart from the initial call).
Then, they told us that the tour didn’t go ahead because we were only 2 and no one else booked the tour that day (a different reason from the one that Lucy gave us the day before). After showing the whatsapp messages and the tripadvisor messages, the person in the counter believed our situation and tried to accommodate us a Mekong Delta day private tour for the same price (our tour included the accommodation and 2 days of tour, and we still had to find accommodation in HCMC for that night and last minute during NYE holiday season which was 3x more expensive).
We decided to try to find accommodation and find a day tour that was cheaper on the next day (we couldn’t do it that day as it was already 9:30 am and all the tours had left.

Conclusion, if you can avoid this tour the better, they are not reliable and they are more expensive than others that you can find while walking around HCMC.

We have just returned from the two-day Mekong Delta tour and - don't go! Here are a few reasons that speak against the provider and the tour:
First of all, we were picked up 45 minutes later than planned. The guide could not be reached on the telephone number provided. The tour therefore started at 8.45 a.m. The bus provided was a disgrace - it smelled of a solvent that stings your nose the whole time - apart from the health risk, this is unbearable on a long bus journey (5 hours in total).
The tour includes several stops, all of which are designed to buy things - a classic coffee trip and tourist trap. Of course, nothing about the program items is mentioned in the description.
Day 1 can be summarized as follows: Visit to a temple - here we were thrown off the bus and had 20 minutes to explore the place, there was no background information about the place, the temple and the history. Then we went on to a singing performance - and promptly the "collection box" came and we were asked to pay a tip. The next stop was a bee farm - here unfriendly and bored staff made us tea in a mini glass - and of course the very "friendly" ladies then came with the products that we were supposed to buy. We continued with a short rowing boat trip, about 5 minutes - here the guide told us that we should pay a tip because this was appropriate (there was no alternative, i.e. refusing the boat trip). Sometimes the boat drivers demanded a fixed tip of 150,000 VND - that is an absolute cheek. Then we went on to a small "factory" where sweets are made from coconuts (they are actually delicious). Then there is a detour to a kitchen where puffed rice is made. Of course you can buy something everywhere.
Day 2 starts at 5:50 a.m. with breakfast - here you can choose from 6 options on day 1. I had decided on bread and fried eggs and got them, but they were cold, as they had probably been cooked some time beforehand. So breakfast was cancelled. We set off at 6:30 a.m. We took a boat on the Mekong - it takes about half an hour to get to the floating markets - once you get there you spend about 45 minutes before moving on to activities that are not in the description. Noodle factory, orchard, market visit, small "cooking class" - what is referred to here as a cooking class is actually the opportunity to make your own pancake (the mix for this is already there - we didn't learn anything). After eating, we took a 5-hour trip back to Ho Chi Minh City in a smelly bus.

Our conclusion: you don't get to see much of the floating markets and the traders - and that's exactly why you book this tour. Basically, it's two very long days in which you experience a lot, but not what you wanted - namely, finding out how and with what the people in the Mekong Delta trade, what history lies behind it and how the Mekong Delta has changed over the years.

Our tip: Book it yourself!
The bus ride from Ho Chi Minh City to Can Tho costs €8, a hotel (depending on the facilities) costs around €20, then you can get a tour guide on site (it's best to ask the staff at the hotel).

Overall, the tour is far too expensive at a whopping €106 per person!!!

The first day was long
3 hour drive and the town visited and all the stops were disappointing not only by me but from all the passengers on our tour. The tour guide mainly spoke of topics he was interested in. We stayed overnight and was not given an opportunity to choose our hotel. We then went to floating markets and witnessed only 3 or 4 boats selling fruit. Other spits on the way back were not really interesting. I am sorry to leave such a poor review but believe there are much better places to visit throughout vietnam then the mekong delta

There are several things that clients should know before signing for this tour and pay (for anything) to this agency.

First, you should be aware that this is not really a floating market, Mekong delta or any kind of experience tour. 90 percent of the time you spend visiting different shops and stalls and are presented with goods to buy. You will spend more time in each noodle shop, honey shop, fruit shop, candy shop etc. than in the floating market, which gets in total around 20 minutes. The situation is so extreme that you start to feel that the agency should pay you as they must be getting a cut from the sellers.

Second, the accommodation provided by the agency is horrendous- black mold on the walls, blood and poop stains on the bed, gecko in the toilet, ants on the breakfast plates and smell of the wet market (rotting meat and vegetables) in the hotel. It is not acceptable to accommodate paying clients in this type of accommodation. Just to repeat and underline, they claim this is a LUXURY TOUR.

Third, they claim (even in their leaflets) that the group will consist of a maximum of 10 people. We were 17. If they are lying about something so basic, how can anyone trust them?

With this in mind, if you don't mind paying USD 99 (booked directly in the office of the agency) for a shopping tour and unsuitable accommodation, go with Vietnam Adventure Tours. Otherwise I recommend you to search further.
Overpriced it's just food sitting on boats waiting for tourist to give them money and if you don't want to buy something your quickly pushed away not an experience I would recommend

The trip description is misleading. It suggests that you swap the streets to waterways. In fact it is 5 hours drive to Can Tho city where you stay overnight. There are several stops in local villages where you are provided with food and encouraged to buy local stuff.
The market itself is very sparse and limited and it’s not worth the effort such a long drive. Note that Hi long bay cruise is totally different and beautiful.
It is not guide dependent. Our guide did his best to smile all the way.
I don’t recommend it.
All five stars reviews look fake.

They should change the title of the tour, they take you to Cai Rang market when it is already closed, so you can not see the market activity. We were told that at 6 am it was very full and it was better to go at 8, when we arrived there was no activity in the market. We complained but the guide didn't care. What if they take you, is to several product factories for you to buy. If you want to see the floating market, this tour is not worth it.

I was also on this tour, as reviewed by the previous individual ‘Marcus Simpson’. I am fully aware that the Cai Rang is the biggest floating market in the Mekong Delta, however when we got there it wasn’t just ‘not crowded’ it was in fact closed. Not only was this disappointing for all of us on the tour but our tour guide completely lied to our group, saying that 8am would be the ‘best’ time to get there, failing to reveal it would actually be closed when we got there for 9am.

Upon arrival to the floating market, we asked our tour guide why we didn’t arrive earlier than 8am to actually see some activity, in which he responded by saying ‘no boat would take us at 4-5am in the morning’ again another lie - from reading previous reviews of this tour we can see other people have arrived at the market around 5-5:30am. I think it is extremely unfair that everybody pays the same price for this tour but some get a much better experience while others are left feeling disappointed due to a lazy tour guide.

Not only have we been lied to by the guide on this tour, but this company have shown bad customer service, failing to reply to any emails that we have sent, instead replying to us only on a public domain where their reputation may be damaged (reply picture below).

This activity is not as advertised, and we were extremely disappointed. The pictures are not true to what you do on this tour.

Our main reason we booked this tour was to see the activity of the floating market, as pictured on this tour. Our guide (Paper man) said on day one that a 8am start to go to the market would be a good time, however when we got there the next day, we were told it was all shut after 6am.

We left feeling extremely disappointed as there was nothing to see, and we felt lied to by our tour guide (paper man) as he said it was not possible to go earlier. Since seeing reviews on here this is clearly not true.

The rest of the tour was underwhelming and rushed. There was not enough food provided at lunch times for the whole group and we spent more time in a mini bus than doing activities.

Overall we feel frustrated and scammed as the tour was not as advertised. We would not recommend this tour, as we feel the two days was a waste of time and money.

I went on tour with your tour agency on the 26th and 27th of April( Cai be-Vinh Long-Can Tho, 2 days 1 night) Our tour guide was Betty. I have just realized that I have been charged twice for that tour.
We booked and and paid the tour on the 24th.
The guy at the front desk was very confused. He kept on passing our cards on the poss several times. He also charged my husband and he voided the payment. Now I found out that he also charged me twice.

We have seen exactly one boat selling pineaples and one selling coconuts. Don’t waste your day waking up at 5am. There is no market anymore, all trades happen on land.
We were so glad we only went to see the market from the shore. There were 3x more tourist boats than seller boats. A lot of great Banh bao stalls on the way to the riverbank walkway.

This place is so bad. It's not an original floating market but just for travel. Things are so expensive compared to other places. The driver takes us to places where he gets the commisions even though everybody doesn't want to.

The floating market wasn't all that special. It was organized and designed only for tourists. Real river markets are probably only found further inland, and I don't understand why they need to leave at 5:00 in the morning. I really don't see the point, given that when we returned, we saw tons of boats leaving, and it was 7:00-7:30. If you have to come to Can Tho for this reason alone, I wouldn't recommend it.

Disappointing - sailing around in trash. 5-10xx more tourist boats than floating markets boats.
Pictures seen beforhand long way from reality.
Narrow canals even worse - even more garbage - so sad to see they dont take waste seriously.
Everthing ends up in our oceans !!!!

Doesn't cost anything and gives an authentic impression what locals really do. If you want to see a real local market, go to An Binh market right next to the floating market. It's worth it!
Everything was ridiculously expensive, and even the service was terrible.I have no idea why this bowl of bún riêu is 90,000.

Nothing special tbh. You will mostly see asian tourists eating noodle soup on boats. The river is very polluted unfortunately (thanks to us humans). Boat traffic, more tourists than vendors and exhaust fumes from the boats. Can be skipped. I dont understand the idea of a floating markets in general anyway.

Book a tour with local guy through AirBnb and was told to wake early 5.00am , ushered to a small boat ( can fit 4 ppl only), the ride was horrendous with choppy water splashing us in the boat. Was not even provided life jacket until i insist after having being bounce around and spashed.

At the end, there was NO floating market in Cai Rang like in n most advertising picture/video. Tour guide told us the vendor  stop selling since several years ago and there is no more floating market like in add picture. There were too many big tour boat that causes the river water waves too strong and conducive for having meals on small boats.

Totally disappointing..what was the point waking up early. We stop at noddle making factory and took taxi back to hotel. Avoid this and if you deal with local guide, make sure they give u exact specific details of tour and what can be expected.

Total scam. There’s no floating market anymore. Some boats are there to make it seem like it but its staged and the pictures you saw in advertising or travel books do not represent the reality AT ALL.
Total waste of time
Just a tourist destination now. More tourist boats than local boats. Don't bother

Considering the price of the boat and distance from HCMC trip to the floating market is not worth it. Number of tourist boats is even higher than the number of merchants’ boats. Also be prepared to wake up around 5 am. I would not go again.
3 fruits boat. Many toursit boats. No point to come here!

We traveled from far away only to see a few big, sad boats—none of them carrying any cargo. If it ever was a bustling, colorful place, only muddy water and plenty of tourists remained, with no locals in sight. We went at 5 a.m., as recommended.

Very overrated place to visit... When the tourist big boat 1st stop at this place.. All tourists will went up to 3 or 4 big building on the river.. The building itself is selling local products and the prices may more expensive on the land.. If you really intend to visit just go early in the morning by yourself.. your agreed with me.. I went 1 day b4 evening 6.00pm and then early in the morning 6.00am on the next day to survey the place by motorcycle from Central when all the tourist boats departures 👎👎👎.. the further journeys just visit local farm or factories or small rivers.. Nothing much special.. This place you may visited once and will never never visit again 😊😊😊

Over.... Belong to history. Should now be renamed Tourist boat market. What you see are 100 tourists boats and some 10 real local market boats 😳
End of an historic place …

Honestly, not worth it...even if you are in Can Tho anyway.

There are two ways of getting there - "large boat" of about 20-30 people or "small boats" of 2-6 - these are smaller sampans/ short long tail boats which can use either motor and oars. Smaller boats are slower to get there from the tourist dock (and you have to stop several times to remove plastic bags from the propeller - it's a heavily polluted river), and can get a bit choppy on the water, but feels less like a 'tour group' and can move around by paddle once there. We got to the dock at 5am "to beat the rush" but nothing prepared us for the massive deluge of people and sea of orange life jackets. By the time we got to the 'market' there were 70-80 tourist boats (>90% large boats), so ~2,000 tourists, circling around (we counted) just 22 boats with produce on them. It was a tourist circus. It's a wholesale trading place so you'll just see the occasional boat offloading pineapples or plastic bags of vegetables from one boat to another, but there's nothing really going on and can't be described as really photogenic. The traders must wonder why they get a tourist deluge each morning. There's a couple of floating metal sheds on the water catering as shops for tourists (which we gave a miss to). Smaller boats can go up the canals (basically one canal that is just covered in floating Styrofoam containers and plastic bottles) - it was so bad we had to look away at times, but it was good to see the village wake up. They all stop at a 'noodle making factory'. It's really a tourist shop that also sells noodles (so best not to get too excited!). It's nice to be on the river at sunrise, but if I had my time again, I'd just catch a Grab/ taxi to the south side of the river with a coffee and watch the melee from there.....and not get up at 4am.

Market does not longer exists. It came down to maybe 100 bigger boats, no small boats left and nothing to buy. Beside the 100 bigger wholesale boats you can find 100 tourist boats. We came on two different days but same scenery. Save yourself the time and money or try to find an authentic still functioning market in the Mekong delta.

Actually, it Does not exist anymore. Only a couple of merchant boats still there surrounded by dozen/ hundreds of tourist boats, that s sad to see .The Land market ( just nearby) is much more interesting : big, living and only local people.

Its definitely not worth paying for a tour to come here. It's 90% tourist boats crowing the area with almost no local sellers or buyers. Would not recommend. If you do want to come here anyway make sure not to overpay for the ride. Anything more than 100k is too much.
A definitive no go, specially from Ho Chi Minh City. We spent 3 hours in direction to see nothing but few boring boats and a dirty river, and of course we had to pay quite an amount
Out of everywhere I've been in Vietnam this was by far the most disappointing. 90% of the boats were tour boats and the actual market has apparently declined from 5-600 boats down to around a dozen.

The market does not really exist anymore!
Only a few boats, no trading at all, only selling stuff to tourists. In the past the floating market seemed to be a lot bigger, that's probably where all the pictures and recommendations come from. I would not say it was worth getting up so early. You should definitely know what to expect before you go!
Waste of time. The market no longer exist. It’s 95% tourist boats and 5% coffee/fruits vendor. No need to wake up at 4 to see this non-existing market. We came 50 years too late…

Just a tourist thing. Need to go there around 4am if you want to see they trade. Go late, nothing to see, just few boats are there. The boat driver try to sell their services (1 round about just 400m totally). The floating market in Bangkok is much interesting....not much thing to do in Can Tho anyway.

Extremely disappointing trip. Basically paid 500k for a 2-way boat ride. Had only encountered two boats during the ride , One sold coffee and the other sold noodles. Didn't bring us to see any noodle making session as initially claimed. Not a bustling scene as portrayed by pictures. Very poor experience of Cai Rang floating market.

Floating markets used to be a thing. Now they are not. It is much easier for people to sell their stuff on land, and their customers come by moped to buy. Floating markets are just tourist gimmicks now. And this one isn't really worth missing breakfast for.

Was there in July.  What a waste of time & money!  There ere only tourist boats.  The normal vendor boats are all gone!  They made stops at 2 float shops to buy candies, souviniers, other weird stuff and that's that.   I heard they all moved to Cho Noi Cai Be in Tien Giang.

Don’t take the tour. It gonna waste of your money & time here. They’re going take you to their arrangement boot shop with the price at the sky. We start the trip at 5am and arrived at the market when still dark. They ask going to the boot shop  first and then come back to the market when sunrise. But all the way back, They didn’t even slow down at the floating market as they said and just go straight back to the main boat dock. Such of waste of time and money. DONT TAKE THE TOUR THAT ARE SELLING ALONG THE NINH KIỂU RIVERSIDE. Asking the local and choose the private boat if you can.

There's no floating market, it's just small VERY touristy shops (booths not really floating next to the river), selling all the same things, especially do not book Mekong tours, they are constructed for mass tourism and really nothing positive or nice to see!!
An absolute tourist trap! The floating market no longer exists, just tourists floating around watching each other with a few boats selling overpriced food and drinks. Avoid!!!
Privat boat costed 200.000 vnd. But market itself is very touristy place. Not much local stuff. Better to go on the market in the land nrar by to get more authentic feel
As of 2024 there is nothing here. 3 hr boat ride but all you see are some motorboats docked. Better time spent elsewhere!!
This Floating market dosnt exist any longer. Those are 2-3 boats only. Since bridges are built, there is no need for a market on the river.

What a disappointing experience, i do hope someone from the market management reads this. We will no longer be using any partners who offer to send clients here. Let me explain the process (and Tet is not an excuse)

Two people collected at 5:50am, total cost only 200,000VND for two! Great!

The market is not a market! It is a very small collection of small boats, about 4 in total we counted. Selling the most useless fruit, whole pineapples, whole coconuts! No where did we get offered sliced pineapple, that you can eat on a stick (as in Thailand), no sliced watermelon (that would be perfect as a single serving). No Chom Chom, no apples or orranges, not even bananas!

Instead you are shuttled (and all the boats are sent) to a floating cafe to buy food and drinks! Then you are sent directly to a floating souvenir shop! These are the typical toursit trap! They are not a tour!

Then its off to a bee keeping garden. There is no education at all on keeping bees, no demonstrations etc etc. Oh, and i speak Vietnamese so this was not a case of misunderstanding or cultural differences! It was simply a case of buy some honey! After 30 mins here you are taken to a fruit garden, this is the best part of a terrible experience and one that is quite frankly a SCAM! The fruit garden is quite nice andyou can relaxe well here for about 1.5hrs.

Why is it a scam???? Because taking boat loads of tourists at 6am stright to a floating cafe and then directly to a souvenir shop and then to a honey shop is a scam! Tourists are not here just to hand over their wallets to you! You need to give something in return! Some information, an unforgettable experience etc etc!

Sadly if this continues only the local Vietnamese will suffer as people will slowly but surely start ti give Can Tho a miss from visiting. This has happened to other places in the world before, lets hope this is not a lesson vietnam will have to learn.

So in short, if you are traveling to Vietnam and are thinking of adding this to your itinerary, i would say dont, its very far ane their are better optionsto spend both your valuable time and valuable money!

Not worth waking up early in order to race here if you are expecting higher quality fruit and produce. This market is created to just lure tourists for boat fares and poor quality few fruit (over riped pineapples, coconut, mangosteen) and produces.

Horribly overrated. There is nothing authentic about this market anymore. Just a bunch of people doing boat rides and selling things to tourists, expecting you to believe that it is a ‘floating market’. Nah nah.

we went on a tour from Kim’s travel , that sent us to a tour from sunrise travel group , the tour was really short (less than 20 minutes
At first when we finished the tour we thought it was a joke from how short and lame it was
You go around in a big boat that contains about 35 people
The river is full of trash and is gray- brown looking , the locals and Vietnamese costumers treat the river like it’s a trash can and it’s a shame to see
When you’re on the boat some little boats will try to sell you fruits or drinks but you barely interact with them because the boat is big  Then you will get on a little cabin which is the floating market
There’s just coconut candies and some food to buy and really in general nothing interesting
The place is so crowded and small that you can’t even breath
I wouldn’t recommend going there and don’t have expectations at all
Nothing to compare to the Thailand floating market
They have nothing here. Few boats, that's all. Do not use bigger boats from agencies!
There is no tradition/culture left here. It is basically a tourist place, with everything doubly priced.
Doesn't exist at all, photos with many boats full of food/vegetable fake.
A bit of a tourist trap. I don't recommend the Mekong trips. You see the floating markets for about 30 seconds and drive straight past them. Interesting to see but not very engaging.
There are more tourist’s boats than the market itself. There is nothing to see here just don’t go to Can Tho it’s a waste of time.

THIS IS NOT CAI RANG FLOATING MARKET.
It is a pier where you can grab a boat which could take you there (30-40min).
Extremely disappointing trip; very poor experience of Cai Rang floating market that looks nothing as advertised.
Just for tourists, locals don't use it anymore
Looks nothing like the advertisement. Very disappointing
Not much happening, more tourists than vendors.

This boat ride sucks. Food is terrible

Totally disaster!!!!
Rip Off!!!!!!!

Non vale la pena venire fino a qui per questo. Ormai ha perso tutto il suo valore. È fatto solo per i turisti, ti portano a spasso con una barca, più o meno grossa in base alla tipologia di tour, si affiancano altre barche con caffè o noodle, ti portano a vedere dei produttori o i canali e poi dopo un paio di ore ti riportano al punto di partenza. Sconsigliato! Lo scambio, il commercio, non lo si vede neanche! Piuttosto visitate il mercato coperto a terra, sicuramente più tradizionale, puro, caotico.

En stor skuffelse! Ikke verd prisen eller det å måtte stå opp klokka 4 på morgen.
Elveturen til markedet er i mørket, så man ser ikke så mye. Underveis kommer 3-4 selgere i egne båter bortover og selger enten frukt eller mat.

Turen tilbake er fin hvis man klarer å overse enorme mengder søppel som flyter der.
Selve markedet er 4 større båter der de selger litt dill-dall. "Pastafabrikken" er i en av disse båtene og i utgangspunktet bare er 2 damer med en vedovn midt i rommet.

Det koster 100k pr person i en større båt, og da stopper man halveis til "markedet" og går ombord til en flytekafé der man kan kjøpe seg frokost.
For 200k pr person er man i en liten båt, slipper kjøpepress og er litt friere i når man vil dra tilbake eller hvor man skal slippes av.
Vi valgte en liten båt, båtførere var hyggelig og dyktig, så vi følte oss trygg.

Det ble ikke den autentiske opplevelsen vi så for oss. Det er ikke et marked for lokale og eksisterer nå kun for å lure turister dit.
Det finnes andre markeder på land i Can Tho som er mye mer autentiske og også rimelige.

Du kan også dråpe båttur i kanalen. Det er bare søppel og elendighet. For en strandrydder er dette et forferdelig syn.

Siamo venuti a Can Tho solamente per quest'esperienza che molte agenzie e "influencer" sponsorizzano come attività autentiche e fuori dal comune. Il Tour è costato quanto 6 ottime cene ed è stato soddisfacente solamente per quanto riguarda la produzione dei noodles di riso. Tuttavia sto qui a recensire il mercato e non il tour di cui ho usufruito e devo dire che è ormai inesistente ed è solamente per 4 barchette di turisti. La sporcizia a cui abbiamo assistito, ha livelli veramente alti anche per il Vietnam. Le buste di plastica bloccavano la nostra elica ogni 3/4 minuti. Tra qualche anno sarà impossibile per loro continuare a  navigare i canali se non cambiano mentalità purtroppo.

Không nên đi vì không có gì đáng để xem. Nếu cố chấp đi thì ko nên đi lúc 5h cho mệt. Vì 6,7,8h thì vẫn thế thôi. Không có câu chuyện thuyền bè rau củ trái cây tấp nập đâu. Mà có vài chục thuyền bán buôn. Họ ko bán lẻ. Và 5,7 cái xuồng nhỏ bán hoa quả đắt lòi. Đồ bán vùng miền khác cũng đắt gấp rưỡi, gấp đôi giá trên bờ. Dưới này ko thuê nhà, ko thuế má, nhẽ ra bằng giá trên bờ cũng ổn rồi. Nếu tiếp tục ntn thì chợ này se, càng ngày càng vắng. Dân ko quay lại và đặc biệt sẽ không bao h mua bán gì hết
Tôi đã đến điểm này 1 lần và sẽ không bao giờ quay lại nữa luôn. Chợ này giờ chả có gì là sức hút du lịch nữa. Thuyền đò đi thì mỗi người làm một giá, chả có ai quản lý về giá.

Barcos llenos de turistas y 4/5 barcos con alguna fruta y paradas en sitios para comprar souvenirs o comida típica (nada que sorprenda, cosas que puedes encontrar en cualquier supermercado de Vietnam). De verdad, ahorraros venir a Caí Rang y me atrevería decir que a Can Tho, ya que lo único que tiene son los mercados flotantes y no valen nada la pena. Una decepción muy grande.
Plovouci trh jiz neni, je to jen fraska pro turisty. Bohuzel, casy se meni a dobu nezastavime… Je to skoda, ale je to tak. Za me zbytecna navsteva…

Đề nghị cty TRẦN TUẤN coi lại nhân viên... trải nghiệm khó chịu vô cùng.. nhân viên đi làm không được trả lương hả...ra tới chợ nổi... ăn bún riêu chiếc ghe màu hồng...lái tàu ăn xong trước không trả tiền... tôi đi cặp đôi..2 tô 90k.. lúc trả đề nghị trả lun lái tàu.. gòy ăn trả không nổi hay gì mà ép khách trả...xong kết tuor về bến..đề nghị rất thẳng thắn chi tiết yêu cầu khách trả phí riêng cho lái tàu... vì sự xin trực tiếp .. chúng tôi vẫn đưa vì lịch sự.. khó chịu vô cùng...chợ nổi là tuor không nên trải nghiệm nhất...cò thì chèo kéo.. đùng đẩy nhồi nhét khách..vòi vĩnh tiền khách du lịch trắng trợn

Mình sẽ nói đây là Tourists Trap!!!

Lần đầu mình đi Chợ nổi hồi dịp lễ 30/04 năm 2015, mọi thứ vẫn còn rất dân dã, thiệt thà và đẹp kiểu Miền Tây Sông Nước. Và chính những kỷ niệm đẹp đó đã thôi thúc Gia Đình mình quay lại thăm sau hơn 10 năm.

Lần này đi Mùng 3 Tết 2026 thất vọng 100%. Vé tour lên gấp đôi 100k / người, tour ghép đi như kiểu lùa cừu non dô chỗ mua đồ đặc sản giá trên trời. Rồi qua bên chỗ ăn sáng 55k / tô thiếu điều về ăn mì gõ đầu hẻm 20k còn có giá trị hơn. Chặt chém du khách quá mức.

Không còn hoặc cực ít cảnh các Cô Chú, Anh Chị lái vỏ lãi bán đủ thứ tấp dô kẹp sát hông tàu để khách thưởng thức món ăn, thức uống trên sông, cảnh kẻ mua người bán nếp sống sinh động vốn dĩ từng diễn ra.

Xuất phát 5:00 sáng bận đi còn tối om như mực chả thấy cảnh gì chụp hình. Về lại bến khoảng gần 8:00 là xong tour.

Với cá nhân mình thấy bây giờ ghé thăm Cần Thơ mà đi chợ nổi là phí tiền phí thời gian. Cần gấp sự cải thiện về mặt thu hút du lịch. Vấn đề nữa là rác nổi lềnh bềnh khá nhiều.

Cần Thơ vẫn rất đẹp nhưng Chợ nổi đã bị phá nát bét rồi!

Pas un marché mais une attraction touristique. 3 vendeurs qu'un se transbordent des Ananas ou pastèques pour donner le change. À part ça on vous emmene manger une soupe assez chère avec les autres touristes sur un bateaux restaurant. Les fruits qu'on vous vendrait viennent du magasin d'à côté sortis de l'emballage sous le pont à l'abri des regards (voir vidéo).
Les agences vous vendent un petit canal - qui est juste une poubelle d'arrière cour et pour le séchage des pâtes, là encore du mensonge vous verrez 3 galettes sécher au soleil au milieu d'œufs kinder et sucreries.
Évitez, même histoire de, c'est juste nul.

Il n’y a plus de marché flottant à proprement parlé. Vous y verrez plus de touristes que de bateaux vendant des produits. C’est une arnaque à touristes. On vous fait lever à 5 heures du matin pour vraiment pas grand chose. Les petits bateaux présents sont uniquement là pour vendre aux touristes des plats de nouilles, et des noix de coco.
Si vous voulez voir un vrai marché, pas besoin d’aller là bas. Le marché terrestre de Can Tho de fruits, légumes et poissons est mille fois mieux.

Leider auf ganzer Linie eine Enttäuschung. Wir hatten eine Tour auf getyourguide gebucht. Fahrt zur dem Markt mit anschl. Reisfabrik und kochkurs.  Der Markt ist rein für die Touris noch da.Wer wildes gewussel erwartet wird enttäuscht.Ein paar Boote auf dem Menschen noch wohnen und eine Handvoll die Essen servieren, die sind aber nur wegen der Touris da, weil Essen im Programm dabei ist. Ansonsten viel fest installierte Boote mit riesigen  Essens plätzen. Die Reisnudelfabrik entpuppt sich als verkaufsraum mit schnickschnack und einer Vorführung bei der auf einer heissen Kugel  alles aufgetragen wird; abgezogen.;getrocknet; und in Streifen geschnitten wird. Das wars. Die Fahrt zum kochkurs durch kleine Kanäle ist nicht schön; überall Plastikmüll auf dem Wasser,in den Bäumen usw.Der kochkurs sah so aus das man 5 winzige Spring rolls Rollen durfte und ein Teig in eine heiße Form Gießen durfte.Das wars. Beim anschl. Essen wurden noch 3 Gerichte aus der Küche serviert. Getränke gehen extra.  Der Spaß hat 54, Euro pro Person gekostet.
Die Bilder im Netz sind uralt.

Siamo rimasti fortemente delusi dall'esperienza al mercato galleggiante. Abbiamo deciso di visitare il sud del vietnam principalmente per questo posto e abbiamo trovato la desolazione. Un mercato non più attivo, o meglio attivo solo per turisti e come attrazione turistica.   Non è un mercato reale, ricco di vita e autentico. L'unica cosa che abbiamo trovato è tanta tanta immondizia soprattutto plastica che galleggiava ovunque. Vergognoso.
Sconsiglio vivamente di andare in questo posto apposta per non rimanere deluso oltre che abbastanza disgustato.

Der schwimmende Markt ist nicht mehr wie er war. Die Händler waren nicht soviel wie früher und kaum noch zusehen. Mehr als die Hälfe sind ans Land gezogen. „ Lo Hu Tieu „ wurde direkt am Ufer verlegt. Früher musste man mit dem Boot in den kleinen Wasserkanal fahren um in dem Dorf um Lo Hu Tieu zu besuchen. Man konnte außerdem noch vieles wie Landschaften, Palmen am Fluss entlang, Obstplantagen, Traditionsmusik hören und einiges zusehen.
Heute geht es mehr um schnelles Geld zu erwirtschaften. Echt Schade. Im Jahr 2019 habe ich 300000d und 2024  für 600000d für ein Privatboot gemietet. Sonst 100000d pro Person.  (2024).
Mein Fazit: Es ist nicht Wert zu besuchen. Es geht hier nur noch um schnelles Geld zu verdienen.

Vi kørte fra Saigon og hertil, blot for at få mine bange anelser bekræftet.

Vi blev mødt af en mand på scooter 1/2 km. fra Cai Rang og han foreslog vor chauffør at han bare kunne sige 8-900K til os for sejlturen og dermed snyde os for 3-400K, blot han betalte 500K til bådføreren. Det sagde vor chauffør heldigvis nej til.

Det hele er en stor turistfælde og en time i en båd til 500K og at se alle de andre turister i orange flydeveste gjorde mig trist.

Det er selvfølgelig lokale, der skal tjene penge, og sådan er det.

Heldigvis var vor chauffør fra Saigon god til at advare, når varerne var for dyre.
Kein echter Floating Market mehr. 80% Touristenboote mit Schwimmwesten und praktisch keine Einhemischen mehr. Lohnt sich meiner Meinung nach gar nicht.

Quelle déception!
Certains ont du avoir la chance de voir ce marché il y a quelques années mais de nos jours il y a que quelques bateaux perdus entre les bateaux des touristes! Vous verrez donc essentiellement la couleur orange des gilets et des moteurs bruyants partout. Vous n’avez pas besoin de booker un tour ( si vraiment vous comptez y aller !) les berges regorgent de bateaux en attente pour vous amener voir quasiment rien, puis vous proposez des visites de fabrique de Nouilles ( que j’ai refusé de faire)
Un peu plus loin, sous le pont géant , il y a d’une part et de l’autre des marchés authentiques, quasiment sans touristes, qui vous enchanteront beaucoup plus que cette « attraction », hélas, qui n’existe plus.

Μια δραστηριότητα που προσδοκούσα πολύ αποδείχθηκε μια δυσάρεστη εμπειρία. Το μόνο που μας έμεινε είναι η στενοχώρια και η απογοήτευση από τους τόνους πλαστικού και σκουπιδιών κάθε είδους που επιπλέουν στο ποτάμι.
Δεν υπάρχει τίποτα ευχάριστο στο να κάνεις βόλτα ως τουρίστας σε ένα βρωμικο ποτάμι που καταστρέφεται καθημερινά (και) από τους ίδιους του κατοίκους που εξαρτώνται από αυτό. Η αγορά δεν είχε κάτι εντυπωσιακό όσον αφορά στον αριθμό των βαρκων και το θέαμα.
Ισως παλιότερα να ήταν αλλιώς, όμως αυτό που έζησα εγώ ήταν μια στενάχωρη εμπειρία που δεν μας προσέφερε τίποτα.

Chính quyền địa phương quy hoạch cho phục vụ ăn uống trên những bè lớn nhìn rất công nghiệp, làm đánh mất đi nét đẹp tự nhiên và trãi nghiệm vốn có của chợ nổi là được ăn uống và mua sắm những sản vật của địa phương vốn do những người nông dân, tiểu thương buôn bán trên những chiếc ghe nhỏ, lênh đênh trên sông nước.

Với lại chất lượng của những món ăn trên các bè lớn rất tệ, nấu nướng nêm nếm cẩu thả, không đúng hương vị như tên gọi của món ăn vốn dĩ là đặc sản miền tây như: Bún riêu, hủ tiểu, bánh canh. Giá bán thì đắt 55k/phần, nhưng chất lượng chỉ ở mức 20-25k. Và điều tôi muốn nhấn mạnh ở đây là chính các bè lớn này đã mất đi vẻ tự nhiên vốn có của chợ nổi.

Tôi mong chính quyền địa phương quy hoạch làm sao để du khách có được những trãi nghiệm đáng nhớ và chân thật nhất khi đến đây tham quan. Với tình cảnh hiện tại thì tôi sẽ không quay lại.

Ormai del mercato galleggiante non è rimasto più nulla. Le poche barchette rimanenti sono lì solamente per i turisti. Come ci ha spiegato la nostra guida, il classico mercato galleggiante nelle prime ore del mattino non esiste più. Esisteva quando ancora la gente del posto veniva da lontano, remava tutta la notte per vendere frutta e verdura anche alle 3 o alle 4 del mattino. Ora quelle persone non esistono più perché tutti hanno costruito le baraccopoli che costeggiano il fiume, quindi non hanno necessità di svegliarsi presto la mattina, tra di loro non c'è più intercambio di frutta e verdura ma solo con i turisti. La qualità del prodotto è calata drasticamente. Non ci sono norme igienico sanitarie. La guida ci ha spiegato che per la loro " " " " cultura " " " " (così ha giustificato lui) non sanno dove buttare la spazzatura quindi gettano tutto nel fiume che è diventato una cosa così schifosa da non poterla descrivere. I poveri venditori che vivono la giornata, nelle baraccopoli, appena vedono il turista nel barcone avvicinarsi, si gettano alla carica per venderti qualsiasi cosa... anche nella fabbrica di noodles, per capirci, vendono giocattoli in plastica per bambini e souvenir.
Không còn là chợ nổi khi xưa, giờ chủ yếu là phục vụ du lịch, chỉ có ghe thuyền ghé tàu du lịch, chứ không còn là cảnh mua bán trên sông (ra dáng một cái chợ) nữa.

Quá dở, nếu ai đi từ 1-6 ng thì nên đi ghe nhỏ, đừng nên đi ghép. Nếu đi ghép thì bạn sẽ không có lựa chọn, khó thoát khỏi vấn nạn chèo kéo khách, chặt chém về mặt giá cả. Một số điểm mình không hài lòng như sau: hôm ấy mình đi ghép với 1 đoàn, ít ghe đến cặp thuyền để bán đồ, đến khu vực ăn, 1 tô có giá 50k, thật sự là cái chất lượng nó chả hợp lí, cực kì dở, quá tệ, nhạt nhẽo, bạn tưởng tượng bạn đang uống nước mưa thì nước lèo của tô bún bạn ăn ở đây giống như vậy đó. Trước khi ăn nên hỏi giá kĩ, đừng như mình, khuyên bạn nên ăn trước, chứ đừng như mình, kẻo nhận lại 1 thất vọng ko đáng có.

Địa điểm du lịch đáng thất vọng:
- Rất nhiều người chèo kéo, giá cả bát nháo
- Không nhộn nhịp như tưởng tượng, chỉ có 1 vài hàng quán bán trái cây, hủ tiếu. Số lượng khách du lịch còn nhiều hơn người buôn bán.
- Các thuyền đều đã thoả thuận với mối để dẫn khách đến chỗ cụ thể, mình muốn tấp vào một vài hàng để mua đồ nhưng tài công cứ lơ lơ.
Mình không khuyến khích mọi người đến địa điểm này. Với cách làm thiếu chuyên nghiệp và thiếu nội dung thì dễ hiểu tại sao du lịch miền Tây không thể phát triển.

Grande déception. Ce marché flottant est devenu une jungle de bateaux à touristes, beaucoup plus nombreux que les bateaux de commerçants eux mêmes. Une flotte de touristes tous vêtus d'un gilet de sauvetage orange dont les organisateurs et les guides ne s'assurent même pas que ceux ci soient bien sanglés, les bateaux de commerçants se faufilent entre les gros bateaux de touristes quitte à risquer de se rentrer dedans. La rivière n'est pas vraiment préservée, des bouteilles vides et boîtes en polystyrène jetés par les touristes ou les commerçants eux mêmes, flottent sur la rivière du Mékong, un spectacle désolant à voir.
Tout est organisé pour développer le tourisme de masse, même le soit disant lieu de production de feuilles de riz et de nouilles de riz et légumes est une reproduction des techniques, intéressant à voir mais il ne s'agit pas d'un atelier de fabrication. Je ne recommande pas cette visite du marché flottant. Le marché terrestre est bien plus intéressant et moins polluant que celui de voir des centaines de bateaux de touristes à moitié vides et polluant la rivière de leur carburant et déchets. La ville de Can Tho gagnerait à préserver sa rivière et interdire de jeter les déchets dans l'eau.

Chợ nổi không còn như xưa nhìn buồn và vắng vẻ, vài chiếc ghe nhỏ bán trái cây và đồ ăn sáng chứ không có gì đặc sắc. Nếu muốn đi thì buổi sáng đến thẳng chỗ gần tượng ông Hồ sẽ có tàu lớn, còn ở ngay bến ninh kiều cầu đi bộ sẽ gặp một chị chèo kéo nói đi ghép nhưng sáng mai lại kêu ko có người đi ghe riêng rất nhỏ và chậm ra sông gặp sóng lắc lư rất mệt, nguy hiểm. Ko nên vào chỗ làm hủ tiếu và vườn trái cây vì sẽ chặt chém đẹp. Tư duy khôn lỏi không bao giờ sử dụng dịch vụ trở lại.

Völlige Katastrophe! Wir waren anfangs sehr gespannt und freuten uns auf einen der besten floating markets in Vietnam. Um 5Uhr sollte es los gehen, mit etwa 30min Verzug startete es dann um 5:30 Uhr. Die Reiseführerin sprach durch ein Mikrofon auf Vietnamesisch(kein Wort Englisch) viel zu laut und einfach unaushaltbar. Nach 20min Fahrt durch den stinkenden und verdreckten Fluss kamen wir dann zu dem „floating market“ ca. 40 andere Touristenboote und 5 Verkäufer mit Getränken und Obst. Dann bekam jeder auf unserem Boot eine Suppe(außer uns natürlich?!) auch nachdem wir aufstanden und uns eine Suppe holen wollten wurden wir nicht beachtet. Nach ca 20 min rumstehen ging es dann zum Ufer wo weiter Lebensmittel erworben werden konnten. Nach 10 min ging’s auch schon wieder zurück zum Pier. Hört bitte nicht auf die anderen Kommentare! Es gibt so viele schönere Orte in Vietnam die man gesehen haben muss. In zwei Monaten Backpacken ist das unsere schlechteste Erfahrung.
Kein guter Markt! Menschen unhöflich und respektlos!
