We brought tickets in advance on the app as we got 3 day unlimited access to Vinwonders And the Safari. It was fast queue jump as we had a QR code ready. There was no queue to buy tickets on the door.  You can't bring in outside food and drink. The aquarium was really enjoyable and the rides were fun. There's lots of food restaurants and market stalls here but the market stalls only take cash. There's a arcade part too close to the entrance/ castle for the kids and big kids to chill at. We got queue jump only for the water rapids as the other rides hardly had a queue. In the aquarium you can feed penguins that cost 500,000 vnd which is expensive but they give a cuddle penguin bear that was 520,000 vnd in the gift shop. For the evening show at the castle, it got very busy so we got VIP seats for about 150,000 vnd each and they gave us a complimentary seal keyring. There's a buffet restaurant by the castle/ to the right of the water park that charges 350,000 vnd per person and had good food options- it includes water too.
The water park, we didn't enjoy. The staff weren't as happy or friendly compared to the theme park, there were a lot of rides you had to bring your own float up, very strict on weight limit on loads of slides, lazy river was good and you could find squirt guns on bridges. There is a wristband place to put money on and use it around the waterpark but this needs to be set up elsewhere and many places dont like using it. Better to use cash for options on food and drink. We prefer the water park in Sunset Town. But the aquarium and theme park is worth a visit. Note: starbucks is outside the park.

Today our family visited VinWonders Phu Quoc from 11am to 3pm, focusing on The Sea Shell and Fantasy World. For families with a 3-year-old, this combination feels just right—calm and scenic in one area, fun and interactive in the other.

✅ The Sea Shell (Aquarium):
The aquarium was cool and comfortable, with a relaxed pace that worked really well for a young child. Watching the fish felt genuinely magical—like discovering an underwater world for the first time 🐠 It’s also a great place to start if your little one can get overstimulated, because it doesn’t feel too loud.

✅ Fantasy World:
This area was the highlight. The ball pits were a big hit, and the colouring activity was such a cute surprise—after colouring, the artwork appears on a screen, and you can literally see the pride on a child’s face 🎨🥹
Fantasy World also has a “fairy-tale village” vibe with familiar stories like The Three Little Pigs, Red Riding Hood, and Beauty and the Beast. Walking past the themed houses and story corners was fun for adults too—very nostalgic.

Quick tips for families:
• A 4-hour visit is totally doable if you focus on your must-see areas.
• Food and drinks aren’t allowed from outside, but it’s not a problem—there are plenty of restaurants and convenience marts inside.
• Bring hats, suncream , umbrella as its sunny and hot

Overall: The Sea Shell is calm and beautiful, and Fantasy World is a dream for young kids—plus adults will enjoy it too. Highly recommended for families 💛

Vinwonders in Phu Quoc is a fun and unique experience. It’s a themed amusement park mostly geared toward kids—about 60% of the attractions are for children and 40% are enjoyable for adults. The park is quite large, so you realistically can’t cover everything in a single day.

There are plenty of rides suitable for kids, and the park is well-maintained and entertaining. One important tip: if you visit on a sunny day, it can get extremely hot. Bring an umbrella, a fan, or something to keep cool, because walking around the park in the heat can be exhausting.

To get the best seats for the “Once Show,” especially at the front, arrive early and secure your spot in advance. The laser show is impressive, with great lights and effects, and definitely worth watching.

Overall,vin-wonders is a one-time experience worth visiting if you’re in Phu Quoc. It’s a full-day outing, enjoyable for families and adults alike, with rides, shows, and entertainment, but plan ahead for the heat and seating to get the best experience.

Vinwonders theme park is a fantastic place to spend a day in phu quoc. The place gives you the French n venice vibes . The theme park is divided into 3 sections  namely Adventure World, Typhoon World and the aquarium.

Adventure World has around 10 games that are both fund and a little scary at the same time.The roller coaster ride (wrath of Zeus) is especially thrilling ,but unfortunately it was under maintenance during our visit.we particularly loved eagle warrior and skate rides.

The next section , typhoon World , features about 30 activities (including children section). While some of them are average , a few are really enjoyable.Tje Hawaiian drop, Hidden sea Snake,Fiery dragons, mighty drop and insanity drops were fantastic ! However the main downside is that you have to climb alot of steps,which can be tiring even before approaching the starting points of these rides. Another annoying thing is that they check your weight before each ride (every time too) and have strict weight restrictions. Even after paying hefty entry fee , we couldn't  enjoy the wrath of Zeus (maintenance) also access to some rides were denied due to these restrictions .The staff's are really nice , after completing each ride they will clapp for you and smile just to make you feel energetic.The theme park has plenty of toilets/restrooms ,food stalls to grab quick snacks . The locker is on paid basis they charge 150000VND for one l locker ,they will repay you with 50000VND once you return the locker key .The shower is free for all the guests.

The aquarium at VinWonders, shaped like a giant tortoise, is a true underwater paradise! From adorable penguins to playful Nemo fish, graceful rays, and even small sharks, every corner is magical. 🐠 Kudos to vinwonders for creating such a beautiful aquarium. Again the mermaid show was called off without any prior notice which makes you angry.

But as the time passes All the crowd  gathers near the centre of the arena .yes it's for the Disney show .The Disney-themed show at VinWonders was absolutely fantastic — full of energy, colors, and amazing performances! We truly respect Vietnamese culture and traditions, but it would be great if the management could also arrange seperate shows in English and Vietnamese ,as not everyone understands Vietnamese.

The vinwonders theme park has free buses from popular places of you have the entry ticket so utilise it in order to reach the destination. The outside food is not allowed in this theme park. Overall it's a wonderful experience but there is alot of room for improvement.

A theme park meant to spend the whole day from opening to closing.
There's a water adventure park, an aquarium and few other thrill rides zones. Depending on how much time spend at each zone and whether you used the paid service for bungee, might not be able to cover everything in a day. Worth going if into rides, have kids 8-10yrs and above, and for couple dating.
Catching the various shows to recover the ticket price paid. Bungee service is good choice to add-on when buying ticket, for faster travel btw zones (which are nice walkable distance apart) and in case of rain.

A beautiful and amazing water dancing show. It’s almost worth it. Arcade machines are mostly degraded. These needed to be serviced. Why not turn on the bells and whistles for them?
Too much concrete and asphalt on the ground.  I suggest to substitute with interlocking so that it’s not so hot.
Food here is a bit too pricey.
The stairs at one of the attractions is dangerously loses!!

We have been to VinWonders in Nha Trang so this time we came to Phu Quoc to visit this location. It wasn’t as great at Nha Trang imo but it was still a must visit. We went on a Wednesday morning and it wasn’t that busy. The guests did pick up around midday and by the night show, it was very busy. As you can also buy a ticket just to watch the 6:45pm show.
The aquarium building was a turtle which was cool to see. The aquarium had a lot to see. There was also a few diver/mermaid shows though we didn’t watch them. There’s sections for kids to crawl through & a walking through section with glass panels above. For an additional small fee you can feed specific fish.
As you require to walk such the large park, they have an additional cost for buggy services that drive by pick up locations if you want to jump on.
There is a height limit for most rides, my 2 year old was able to ride a little train. That was the only thing he could ride on however in the Egypt section there is a building just for children which included a big slide & ball pit.
There is a small arcade with all games included. No tickets to be won but just games. Basketball, driving, shooting, air hockey & more.
The waterpark section was defs the busiest towards the second half of the day. I have been to quite a few water parks & this one had the best one for children & toddlers. They had a lot of mini slides; 1 metre or less roughly.
There are restaurants around the park and one buffet. The buffet was pretty good, bit pricey compared to Sunworld BaNa Hills < They offer 3x more & is half the price. But the food here was still good & had a vegetarian section, I only noticed as we were seated near it.
Towards the afternoon/night all the food stalls on Main Street section open up and are quite busy as everyone is around eating for the night water show. The show itself is water, lights & fire.

Seemed to be a very quiet day. No line ups for any rides. Wrath of Zeus is by far the most extreme. There’s also a super steep fast one at the water park.
We stayed from open to close. Long day with the kids but they last till the end of the light show. Stayed at VIN holidays so made going back to hotel quick.

One of the biggest theme parks.  Divided into 5 sections.  Go early to cover everything.
Don't cover both vinsafari and vinwonders in one day.  It was not possible to cover entire park in half day.
Chekc vinwonders app for timily shows that happens around the park.
Mermaid shoe is must watch.  Water park is great and roller coaster mind blowing. Must try

And don't miss the final show that happens in evening every day.  What an surprise.   Must watch to end the day with bang.

Overall nice park to spend a day. Make sure you go in the low-season. I wouldn’t queue for any of the rides. It has some cool roller coasters though.

Don’t forget the swimming suits, they have water park as well. And stay for the evening show, it was impressive.

Had Great fun in theme park and water park. They were strict with height measurement for kids to allow for rides, minimum 140cm. All staffs were very kind and helpful.The waiting time for rides was minimal and the aquarium was impressive. Sea aquarium has huge variety of fish and other see creatures. Kids enjoyed it a lot.Definetely mermaid show is must watch.
You can include free buggy rides, Food vouchers included in the ticket. Had lunch in maharaja lounge, but the food was poor quality.
We went for a day, which was enough, but during our visit due to rain they have cancelled all rides in afternoon. Kids were not able to enjoy much in rain so we enjoyed only water park.
We watched the evening show with lights and fire. But show was in Vietnamise language, so we didn't understand anything..🤣🤣

Amazing..
If u r with kids they will enjoy..
Rollercoaster is must.
Rest rides are average.
2-3 shows are good.
Take a buggy for 1L dong which will not make u tired.
Note- please take a raincoat or extra clothing as some rides will.make you wet..
There is also water rides which we avoided but if u wish u can go for it.
Overall nice experience

Very fun amusement park, huge area to wander and explore. Various rides ranging from thrilling to casual. The most recommended ride is "The Wrath of Zeus", a thrilling roller coaster that accelerates to speeds of 110km/h with twists, turns and loops.
Went on a Friday afternoon in November, barely any queue, express pass not required.

The staff here is super friendly and give thumbs up with funny face when you finish your slides 👍👍🤣 Must be because of our face when coming out from the adventurous fast slides. The place is nice, many activities to do and food for filling up your growling stomach. Price quite cheap to compare with other neighboring countries 😍 worth coming here if you are visiting Phu Quoc 💪👯‍♂️

Visited on a Sunday.
It was a huge theme park,with an indoor big aquarium.
Some rides are for young children,some for adults,and some of these machines actually looked "old"..?
Preferably,the whole theme park's decorations can be improved,to look more lively,like Universal Studios or Disney land.
As it rained in the afternoon,most areas were flooded,and everyone who were wearing shoes had to walk barefooted in the water.
Overall,it's still worth the visit,price-wise alike.

Very nice place and affordable theme park. U can spent whole day. Morning till they close the park. A lots of attractive games to try. We go on weekdays. No long queue. No wait. For muslim, there is Halal Certified cafe inside. U can lunch there without hesitate. Its Opai Greek Restaurant. Not bad at all.

Must to visit place at Phu Quoc… total fun with family … be it shows, games, fantasy shows, thrilling rides, adventure zone…. Etc… it has all of them …. Even a full day will fall short to cover entire 6 zones…

Good theme park with good selection of rides, food vendors and things to take pictures of. Can buy tickets in advance or there. The wait for the rides was at most 10 minutes. Nice to do if you have a spare day. There is also a water park inside the park but remember to bring swimwear to go on these rides.
Vin Wonders was very good, especially for kids. I went on Sunday and had a great experience. Lots of fun activities and a nice atmosphere. Definitely worth visiting with children!
Lots of fun, can't take your own food. Water park and amusement park. Get the two day pass if you can, one day for amusements and one day for water rides.

When I came, there were several places that were closed due to reconstruction. and there were also many places that my child could not enter. maybe the height difference. maybe in terms of cleanliness of the place, it could be improved further. many places were mossy. Overall, this place is 8/10. oh yes, the night show at 1830 is very interesting
For adventure, these is only 1 or 2 rides under 130cm tall kids . But swimming pool is great to kids. And also game room is very good place to have fun even if it’s very hot weather.

Amazing experince.
Though we missed the largest ride as it was shut due to maintenance.
But fun and great experience.

Excellent attractions! So much fun, even for me, 34 years old. The staff is friendly, thank you 🙏

The only minus: it is impossible to find good food here. Finally i spent around 500k, and threw all food to the bin. And then can only eat snickers and instant noodles.

Good amusement park in phu quoc. All the rides and attractions no queue. Fantastic. Rides are not too bad , but as my kids are young (<1.4m) cannot take most rides.

Huge fish aquarium with lots of fishes and tons of penguins. It is an Aircon enclosed section with a large fish tank viewing gallery. Real concern is safety, absolutely no crowd control in place, enter at own risk.

Mixed experience. We liked the place a lot, but unfortunately 50% of the rides were closed and the park looks quite empty. 2 rides broke when we attempted to ride them :D. Not sure if this place will survive for long. Nevertheless, aquarium was fantastic.
A joyful place for all ~~ please rest well one day ahead, fully charged, get ready for an entire day excitement.

Very fun would recommend going when there is no rain so most of the rides wont be closed. The marble floor gets pretty slippery when wet so be prepared.

I would say my favourite ride would definitely be The eagle warrior, it is very fast and goes up and down a lot but the screams make it more fun. Overall the experience was great and really fun.

Reasonable price (850,000vnd or $53aud) with heaps of activities. Not too busy either. The only thing tho, when you went to the waterpark, you had to pay 130,000 for 1 small chair or 200,000 for big chair to sit on the man-made sand by the wave pool. I thought this would have been included, but that’s ok. The rides were good and well maintained. The amusement park is huge and took whole day to get through every ride and attraction. Our kids love the Once Show starting at 6:30pm

Ok here's the go... absolutely recommended buying tickets in advance if you can ... can buy on the way there in the taxi at roadside stall not online if you wish.. have a plan because it's gunna get busy... get there early as possible... go straight to your most preferred rides or attractions.. not all rides will operate in the rain.... even tho you are told they will 🤔.. big roller-coaster is the best ride here... and then trap door slides IMHO... do not buy food and drinks at small places inside... near the entrance is a mini mart... way cheaper... use your 300k voucher for the buffet it has multiple times during the day...drink plenty of water..the show at 645 pm is worth staying for but book a taxi in advance so you can leave asap..must see the huge aquarium too.. hope this is helpful thanks 😊

A must visit when in Phu Quoc. I bought same day tickets on Klook which included a food voucher. It was such a good deal. Food voucher was 150K per child or senior or 250K per adult. We were a family of 7 and were able to split it between 2 restaurants. It was more than enough. They also offer a buggy service for 100K per person when on site. This was worth it for my family that included kids and a senior. We took it 3 times. It’s very convenient and it came often.

No physical maps were provided. So take a photo of the map once inside.

The 2pm mermaid / merman show is hugely popular. We came on a Tuesday. If you want a seat, make sure you get one on the steps by 1pm or earlier. Any later, you will be standing and it’s going to be chaotic. There is also a viewing area upstairs. The show lasts about 15 minutes and it can’t be missed. It was amazing minus the crowd. They should limit the amount of people in. If there was a fire or an emergency, a lot of people will get trampled on and will get hurt.

The Once show is okay. I’ve seen it twice. The first time one of the towers had a broken projection so it ruined the show. I’m glad they fixed it when I revisited. The dialogue is in Vietnamese but the singing is in English. For this show I would also find a seat at least an hour before.

The water park section was also nice. Great for kids. Bring your own towel and shampoo as it is not provided. You can also find some massage chairs.

If you stay till closing, it’s difficult to get a Grab car.
It’s really fun place to visit with kids , the aquarium is the best

A great way to spend the day. This Disneyland inspired amusement park has everything - water rides, roller coasters, an aquarium and more.

The commute inside the place is rather smooth with shuttles, the rides are good too. Zeus’ Revenge was rather aggressive, short ride but all sorts of roller coaster elements added.

Most fun we had was at the shooting range. It’s a hoot.

Definitely worth a visit.

Park is large and full of exciting places and things. But... One star down for really dirty beach in the water park and for Aloha bar. The worst coffee in Vietnam(what is almost impossible because there is everywhere great coffee) and ants in my cup of lemonade. Overall very good ratio between price and performance.

Username: เจ้ส้ม ขายทุกสิ่ง
Comment: The huge theme park which you have to spend your time whole day. It a multi activities to do such water park, aquarium, kids theme park also adventure park for adult. We can't finish it in one day there are lot of things to do. For water park you can rent towel 50,000 VND.  Rent locker 80,000 VND . But the bathroom is so dirty by lot of water from  other room  while they take a shower. Aquarium is the best in here it big and have a good management.  Mermaid show at 2 pm is also good. I recommended you to buy advance ticket from their app. It easy and cheaper. I brought 2days inclusive it about 1,500,000 VND.

VinWonders feels like Vietnam's own Disneyland, and the Mermaid show was pure magic with graceful mermaids dancing amid colorful fish in the massive turtle-shaped aquarium.  Finding tasty Indian food there made the day even better, with spots serving authentic curries, naan, and thalis right in the park.  The evening laser show capped it off amazingly, featuring dazzling lights, fountains, synchronized music, and epic projections on the castle.
Some equipment are good and stuff are friendly. Just some equipment looks old and and proper maintenance. Others are good
We had a good day, enjoying the park. At this period of time (April) not do crowded, it's good to go. Don't miss the final show at 6:45 pm
Great place! Loved the this place with kids. Try on weekdays.  The staff were friendly and helpful. Can't wait to come back! 😊👍" …

The best part of the park is water park and aquarium. The rest is so-so. There are 2 dark rides, both mediocre, but the Eagle Warrior much better than the dragon one. The park lacks good themes for rides. The water park is cool though. Lots of slides, lots of fun. Though cool weather took away much of the fun. I still think the Aquatopia is a better water park though. And much newer. There is very little queing, as there is not much people inside. Overall it’s quite a small park. People who claim the park is huge has never been to a huge park, like Energylandia in Poland.

People smoke every where around here

Updated.
Thank you for your response and I do appreciate your effort to keep it clean and fresh for tourists. You should have specific places for those who smoke, not EVERYWHERE in Public as they can do now and MORE SIGNS of NO SMOKING around because I met some of them and they said they DIDN'T SEE any signs of NO SMOKING. And there is one or two very TINY signs of NO SMOKING at restaurants on HÒN TẰM, public areas on HÒN TRE, I rarely see these signs on Phú Quốc islands

Had a good time here. It has its own charm and the attractions are decent. It’s not on the level of the major international parks, but it’s a fun experience. Sea Shell Aquarium is definitely the highlight of the park.

Plxe is great and fantastic. You will be occupied the entire day and it's a great theme park like castle, video games, water park and great mermaid show.

But do please note that all show prices  are not included, it's a big area and they charge separately for buggie. I think this is a big disappointed for the tourist.
Their themes pamphlets and language only support locals and you will end up asking me every person where is next show.
Please do watch videos and get a good understanding before visiting the place and carry extra money as the ticket price is just a initial gimmick.

We were uncertain but actually really enjoyed going to Vinwonders. Recommend arriving early in the day. We were there for opening at 9am. We enjoyed going on the rides first, before the day was too hot and of course before eating! There are plenty of water activities and so many waterslides and rides to enjoy. The aquarium was amazing and another way to relax out of the sun. We were there for 5 hours and didn't see everything. You could easily spend the whole day there. You can hire towels and a locker, plenty of stores to buy things and many options for food also. You can buy tickets in advance on their website but it also goes super smoothly and easy if you do so right away when you get here. Recommend!
A beautiful place for relax in Phu Quoc Island. Good for water activities for fun and relaxing place. You can spend almost whole day with family. Must go in Phu Quoc Island.
Beautiful park, water park is great, amusements are mild.

This is the first park in my life where I did not have to wait in line for rollercoasters. So in 1 day you will be able to try all of the rides, also the whole water park. While the park was opened not so long ago some of the places feel like no work has been done on them since the opening. However I felt everything was safe to use. When riding the ferris wheel you will see that before Covid there were bigger plans for the park, however the surrounding of the park looks like a garbage dump in some places and is falling apart. I would not recommend eating at the street food trucks as the meat and other things are not thoroughly cooked (and still raw). Overall it is a nice park and I had a lot of fun. Hopefully they can recover and finish/ renovate things in time.

Overall, we had a good day at VinWonders.

Highs > The water park was brilliant, lots of different water slides and a big wave pool too. Adventure land had a decent amount of rides (but with specific opening times) and we managed to amuse ourselves for the whole day. The aquarium is impressive and I loved watching the ray feeding. The staff were all really friendly :)

Lows > It's so so so empty which results in a slightly weird atmosphere. Viking land and Fantasy land were small and with no good rides for adults apart from 1 water ride (but this might be a positive if you're coming with small children). The food quality is bad. The penguins in the aquarium don't have enough space and that area felt quite off and uncomfortable.

The park is amazing in terms of the rides — the variety and quantity are impressive. The aquarium is also stunning and very large.
What slightly ruined the experience for us was the “police-like” atmosphere in the park — extremely strict rules about weight and height. They measure everyone’s weight every time before going on a ride. Luckily, there weren’t many people, so it didn’t cause lines. The rules are much stricter than in any other park we’ve visited around the world. They didn’t let my 14-year-old son enter the family area with my daughter because he’s over 140 cm tall. As an adult woman, I wasn’t allowed on a mat slide because I weigh less than 60 kg… and there were several other similar examples.

Very interesting place to visit. This place surprised me because I though it would be like the other theme parks that I went to in Vietnam. But I was pleasantly surprised by the variety of attractions. The aquarium was a good surprise as well. The park is very well maintained and it is clean. I would say the entrance ticket is alright for foreigners and the well to do. Would recommend this place if you are looking for a theme park to visit.

not many rides to enjoy.
However, the aquarium and the show were very satifying.
Generally, here is rather pricy than its own facilities.
Fyi, recommend to rent a bicycle in front of the gate which helps you to enjoy easy.
It was amazing and show in the evening was really good but only 1 thing i didn’t like that was that we had to take inflatable tube all the way up other then that everything was amazing

It was a fun and interesting experience, reminds me of Disney World, except no queues and less people. The aquarium would have been enough for us (2 adults) but there is lot of to do and see with children. It was clean everywhere and food in the restaurant was good👍 …

Large theme park with many different games and areas. Some of them were closing before the park's closing time, at around 18 instead of 19 so be sure to check those out first if you are entering a little bit late.
The aquarium was also closed and the 'turtle' dome is still not finished.

We only found a handful of games that we liked, some are really geared towards kids, such as the fairy zone.

They also have an arcade with free games, plenty of two players shooting games which are nice to play when you don't have to worry about buying tokens.

The food choice is limited and terrible, overpriced instant noodles with some hard beef. Maybe go out and eat at the Grand World, there is probably more choice there.

It was good experience. Better to come on weekdays. That's what we did.
I would recommend to have more rides for kids under 100 cm.

But otherwise we enjoyed a lot.

It’s a massive theme park with so many sections. I’m unsure if one day is even enough. Doesn’t feel like you’re in Vietnam at all. It has everything you’d need to keep the family busy all day long. Water park, rides, different theme sections, kids and adults. Shopping and food.
Massive.

So massive amusement park in Vietnam

Important things to note-
You have to walk a lot,make sure you are aware about that. Kindly book your tickets prior.There are plenty of restaurants inside so no need to carry food.
Park closes at 6 pm.
We visited the place on a weekday so it was less crowded

This amusement park is amazing for kids and adults. A lot of difference types of activities to do, in fact so many that you won't be able to see the whole place in one day. We enjoyed the water park the most. The aquarium is also beautiful. The boat taxi experience at the grand world is meh, you can probably just walk around and take pictures.
Ok for kids happiness, but not muslim friendly when it come to food. no halal food at all, please fill up your tummy before come here, outside food not allowed

Fun Day with the family!! Staff were very helpful and friendly. The only problem we had during the day was 1 ride we were on (volcano log ride) stopped half way up, however, the staff quickly rescued us and walked us back to the beginning. Other than that, a very enjoyable day.
Have so much fun here. Not too many people and basically you don’t have to queue up for the rides. Have different shows to watch. Many food there. Good to spend the day here.

my review will mostly focus on a couple of the places i ate at

as for the rides itself: rides were fun. quite enjoyable however as not many visitors it meant less staff, and so some rides operated on a hour allocation. so keep an eye out on the allocations

as for food:
odins hall. give it a miss. not worth. its not a restaurant. outdoor eating alfresco area. cafeteria style. burger not worth
the Deep Sea restaurant though was decent. its indoors in their aquarium section (amazing aquarium), and ur seated right next to a massive fish tank! loved it. food is average. but at least its an actual restaurant (and not a cafeteria as the map indicates)

long story short:
rides is great. short wait time
aquarium area, loved it
food in general is meh. go to the aquarium restaurant coz its much nicer, indoors with aircon, and watch the fishies swim!

Half empty adventure park with different attractions for adults and also for kids. There was no waiting time for attractions and you don’t have to pay for attractions individually as it is already in ticket price. For us the most interesting was aquarium. The price of ticket was 900 000 VND included 250 000 VND voucher for food in some restaurants. You can also buy combo with entry to safari.

great place it's made on a huge scale It's a pity that there are not enough visitors Maybe there should be more advertising the staff is very kind but there should be more people It's very strange that there are interesting buildings built in a huge amount around the complex which can be seen as a decoration Although  and if they are real otherwise the Fun City itself is wonderful there are many water activities and different and other entertainment especially for children and In any case it is worth seeing
The aquarium is amazing 🤩 with a lot of flora and fauna the experience is wonderful
Good theme park and the aqua show in the aquarium was superb just the roller coaster ride had lots air pressure and too quick and scary over all it was fun
This place is very cool with nice performances
All are nice, the rides are scary, its just because when i wanted to ride the zues, electricy run down…

Great family spot.
Best of both worlds.
Play on the rides 1st half, then cool down in the water park side of the theme park.
Recommended for young families
Many rides were under maintenance, one stopped working while we were waiting in line. Still ,the whole park and the worling rides were a great experience.
Nice place but not Muslim friendly so much. No halal food in here, accept maharaja bistro.
You should definitely ride a Zeus roller coaster!! Because of that, I added two stars more. The others were not available or really childish.
Beautiful sceneries , electric bike for rent is very good idea, but just very few public WCs there

A little disappointing that many of the rides were closed. They shouldn't be charging full ticket prices. The one attraction that was very impressive was the aquarium, I haven't seen a better one is Asia so far.
Highly recommend spending a whole day there to explore every inch of the place! Worth the experience with friendly staffs.

Waterpark, amusement park, aquarium... Damn, wasn't expecting that xD. Service awesome and cheerful. Abviouslly some longer queues and pracier food. Cute fairytail-like end at 19.00 for kids and those who want to feel like one again xD
so fun and worth it
Visited it on a rainy day and got very few things to do, and shops run out of food all the time. But still great on a sunny day, worth a shot.

Amazing aquarium with a nice show featuring some "real" mermaids. Good quality rides, a lot of fun at the waterpark. We went end February. There weren't many queues. Amazing end-show at 7pm, which you should hang around for to see. Food not that good though.

We had a fun day here. Enjoyed the surroundings, the rides and the show. However, there were a few downsides - the ride in the Viking village is not worth the wait. When we went, the queue was not super long but it moved painfully slow because of the poor organisation in this particular spot. Also the shelves for personal belongings are left unattended here. Another minus was that one of the slides in the water park area isn’t planned well, people were constantly stuck at one point, which resulted in the stress of the visitors and formed a long queue too.
Other than that, if you enjoy rides, having fun in theme parks and are not afraid of crowds - recommend!
many rides were not available
Bad weather but still nice place. Can spend entire day here. Should use electronic bracklets for payments
Performance was good. Mermaid & mermen are beautifully swimming.

What a wonderful experience in this Vietnamese Disneyland. The entrance fee included food vouchers. We had a great time here and it was really worth the money. We spend the whole day here. Mostly we enjoyed the underwater world “sea shell”.
It is not only great for kids but also grownups.
The show really good, i enjoyed my time to watch it

A good amusement park.
A day is enough to use whole attractions.

The rides were nice. Food, well….i thought it would be wow but ok, room for improvement at Odin hall restaurant. Maybe try to go towards the evening as we went before noon. Lots of walking. Try the Hercules ride and go to the aquarium.
Nice place to go for a day trip
Some rides are very nice...there is hardly any queues ..water park is awesome ..just they should allow grown ups in some more water rides ..

The only downside if this theme park is shower rooms at wet zone. The clogged water flow hole on the floor caused the floor flooded with dirty waters, really disgusting that you have to take shower in such condition. Our visit to this amusement park, particularly its water park section, was filled with splashes of joy and excitement. The water park is truly exceptional, featuring a fantastic array of slides that cater to both adrenaline seekers and those looking for a more relaxed experience. Notably, the dedicated section for children under 140cm is a thoughtful addition, ensuring fun for the little ones in a safe environment.

One of the highlights was the minimal wait times for water slides, a pleasant surprise that allowed us to enjoy more rides without the dreaded long lines. However, the changing room situation slightly dampened our spirits. The need to change in individual shower stalls without adequate space to keep belongings dry was a bit of a hassle. This area could certainly benefit from improvement.

Beyond the water park, the amusement park offers a variety of rides and attractions that cater to all ages, ensuring that both adults and children have plenty of options to enjoy. Despite the wide range of activities, we found the food offerings to be less impressive. The quality of the meals didn't meet our expectations, so we'd suggest exploring local dining options outside the park.

A must-see is the farewell show, which captivated us with its spectacular performances and was a perfect way to end our day at the park. Additionally, the staff across the park, especially those working with children's activities, were incredibly friendly and helpful, making our experience even more pleasant.

Overall, this amusement park earns a solid four stars from us. While there's room for improvement in certain areas, the fun atmosphere, short wait times for attractions, and the exceptional farewell show make it a worthwhile visit. We recommend it to families seeking a day packed with entertainment and adventure.

VinWonders Phú Quốc is a world-class theme park located on the beautiful island of Phú Quốc, Vietnam. The park offers a wide variety of attractions and experiences for visitors of all ages, including thrilling rides, water slides, a wildlife park, and a cultural village.

One of the most popular attractions at VinWonders Phú Quốc is the Underwater World, which is home to over 5,000 marine animals from all over the world. Visitors can walk through a tunnel that runs through the middle of a giant aquarium, and they can also watch sharks, rays, and other fish being fed.

Another popular attraction is the Safari Park, which is home to over 2,000 animals from all over the world. Visitors can take a tram ride through the park, and they can also see the animals up close in their natural habitats.

In addition to its attractions, VinWonders Phú Quốc also offers a variety of dining and shopping options. Visitors can enjoy a meal at one of the park's many restaurants, or they can buy souvenirs at one of the park's many shops.

VinWonders Phú Quốc is a great place to spend a day or two with family and friends. The park offers something for everyone, and it is sure to provide a memorable experience.

With a pleathora of rides and activities, its a great place to visit, for the whole family! A nice and well-maintained place with great choice of activities, relaxation and fun-time shows! One single day- is NOT enough. One must consider getting a combo of two days ticket- to fully explore and enjoy this place the best. Then some time shall have be spared for the pared/ carnival and later in the eve- for the light- sound- fountain show! Yes, you even can find ‘Maharaj chai’ if you are a tea-liver from India. The buffet lunch at the restaurant is extensive- but not so good for ghe vegitarians, esp Indians. Try to take your own food as far as possible. (They do check at the entry, though.) The aquariam takes a good time to visit- and the mermain show is just awesome. Do manage to reach there timely. There is an exclusive water-park inside. We spent the day at VinWonders as a family of four, two adults and two children aged 5 and 7. While we had a fantastic day and thought VinWonders had some really interesting things to see and do, I wished we could have been made aware of the very strict height restrictions. I couldn't find these listed anywhere online before I went and even on the park map they are listed in the smallest print that is difficult to read. You have no way of knowing what the height restrictions were until you got to the ride. My 5 yo daughter who was 117cm wasn't allowed to go on a lot of basic rides which were restricted to 120cm. It was very odd because in the same trip we had just visited Disneyland Hong Kong where she went on multiple thrill rides so to not be allowed on basic rides here resulted in a lot of tears! My son is just under 140cm and could also not go on a lot of rides, however they also had areas that were only for up to 120cm (like rock climbing, which he easily could have done) that he was then too tall for so there were tears on his end too. The Typhoon World (the water park) is awesome except there's a kids side with slides which adults aren't allowed on even with their kids but then the adults side has 120cm+ restrictions (and they were so strict, we walked up 6 flights of stairs to do a four person family raft to just see if we could get on but they were straight on to my daughter being a few cm too short and wouldn't let us on). So basically there was nothing for the parents to enjoy in Typhoon World because we couldn't leave our kids alone and there were no slides that the family could enjoy together. We did do the wave pool together, however, they make all children under 140 cm wear life vests (no exceptions, the life guards were strict on it) which was very confusing for two strong swimmer children from Australia! Again, we have been to Gold Coast water parks in Australia and the kids have been on thrill slides so it was a bit confusing for us all. It's still a great place to go but make sure you research the restrictions first so you have expectations managed. We loved the Alice in Wonderland Maze and we also loved the river rapids ride as a family as well as the massive Ferris Wheel. Lines were really manageable too!

VinWonders is definitely Vietnam's biggest theme park as on day. Part of the larger Vin Group tourism complex in Phu Quoc, its waking distance from VinWonderWorld as well as VinPearl Resort, and also very close to GrandWorld and VinSafari. The park opens at 9 in the morning but the rides start at 10 and everything shuts at about 6 pm.  We had VinWonders entry tickets included in our 4 night stay at VinWonderWorld. This was duly noted by the hotel staff during check-in, our faces were scanned, and next day when we went to the park - we didn't have to stand in any queues, just scanned our faces at the gates and straight entry. Not that there were any long queues anyways at 9 in the morning.  The park is spread across a fair bit of land so be prepared to walk as you move across the 6 zones - European Street, Viking Village, Fantasy World, Adventure Land, Sea Shell Aquarium & Typhoon World. The first 3 zones contain the more kiddish rides. The real thrill starts from the Adventure Land rides - some of which are really scary. But what was a let down was that the biggest roller coaster in the park was shut on the day we visited. A few other rides were also shut due to operational issues - now was that the truth or just cost saving measures on a low occupancy day?  The Aquarium is average but the Colours of Ocean Show at 2 pm is a must do. I have seen some of the best aquariums in South East Asia but never a show like this. It was beautifully choreographed and it was quite intriguing to see so called mermaids dance around marine life. Though the show being in Vietnamese meant we were hardly able to understand anything. Everyone seems to be waiting for this show so finding a spot to sit is difficult unless you plant yourself well in advance. And don't miss the penguins - they are always fun to watch.  We went on a lean day when there was hardly any waiting for most rides. Only the rollercoasters and more thrilling rides had some queues. We were able to do the ones we wanted to just in time for the Colours of Ocean Show. After the aquarium we came back to the European Street for lunch. There is Maharaja Maggi Point there which serves piping hot Maggi & Momos which transport you back to India. The ere are plenty of snack, drink and meal options around the park but limited vegetarian food.  Post lunch it was time for the water park Typhoon World. There are lockers, towels and showers available on rental but strangely the locks are not number locks but actual physical locks which means you have to carry the key around with you tied to your your wrist. They also have a rule wherein if you don't return your locker keys by about 6-6:30 your security deposit is not refunded. Typhoon World was a serious let down because it was the exact copy of another water park in Kuala Lampur in terms of both the layout and the rides. Also the wait time was really long as om most water rides only 2-4 people can go at a time. And once you climb up those stairs to reach the queue, its a tough choice between waiting half an hour and going back down the long stairs.  By around 6 as the sun sets, the park starts to shut down. But you can't leave before seeing the Once Show which starts at 6:45 pm. Its at the Fire Phoenix Square with the castle as its background. The seating area for this tends to fill up very very fast. So even though we were there at 6:15 - half an hour before the start of the show, there was no place at all. There was one reserved seating area on one side but the charges for it were exorbitant. The show on its own was worth the wait even after a long tiring day. It was a musical tale steeped in local history with a lot of fireworks and great light / sound effects. Only let down once being that the show was completely in Vietnamese - which now seemed really strange because the entire audience was made up of international tourists with hardly any locals being there.  Overall it was a theme work worth a day's visit especially with kids. Some tour packages make you do both the VinWonders as well as VinSafari in one day but there is no way you can cover both together. It was disappointing to see some of the main rides being shut. With the huge area they have covered I am sure there can be more rides planned in the future so that there is less of a walk between rides and the crowd spreads out. Also some more character meet & greets, parades and shows would be good.

We stayed at VinWonders Wonder World so we got a free shuttle to VinWonders (just a short ride of a few minutes). We went early as we were expecting it to rain in the afternoon but none of the rides opened til 10am. But there were some cute scenes to explore and photo opps without any crowds.  The aquarium was really impressive! It was so big and there were so many different animals to see. We came back for the mermaid show and it was ridiculously busy! We got there about 20-30 minutes early but the whole area in front of the acquarium was full. We ended up going upstairs to watch from the balcony which was fine. Our 5yo daughter loved it despite it being crazy busy.  Unfortunately it was raining most of the day, so a lot of rides were closed. And they are very strict on height requirements so we didn't make the cut on many. We did manage to ride the river rapids ride (unsure the actual name), which was fun. And because there weren't any lines we were able to go on back-to-back rides.  A highlight was that the ferris wheel was air-conditioned! A highlight for the kids was the free arcade.  I felt like we did more walking around than actual amusement park experiences, but the kids (5 & 7) enjoyed it and have asked to go back.
Big park with lot of facility suitable for couples and family trip. Worth to go try. Price is reasonable. definitely will go again.

Viet-Disney! We visited as a family of 4 (2 adults and kids 12 and 9). We had a ball. Rides are great with lots of different options and the aquarium was a very pleasant surprise. The water park is good but due to weight restrictions there were many rides our kids could not go on (min 60kg which our kids don’t yet weigh combined) Definitely recommend paying extra for the buggy pass as this cuts down walking in the heat.

We arrived soon after opening and it literally felt like we had the entire park to ourselves.  The park has a feel of a wannabe Disneyland, but definitely not at that level. On the plus side no queues like Disneyland - we walked straight on to nearly every ride. Rides choices for us were somewhat limited with my 3 year old not tall enough for many and my 10 year old thinking many of the others were too scary but we managed to all agree on a few. The ferris wheel has amazing views over Northern Phu Quoc and being air conditioned was a nice break from the heat.  My tip would be hit the rides first before it gets busy then head for the air conditioned aquarium, which far exceeded my expectations. Then head to the water park for a few hours. After that my kids were exhausted so unfortunately we didn’t stay for the light show.  We were staying 5 minutes drive away so it was very convenient to visit - if staying in southern Phu Quoc I’m not sure it’s worth the hour or more travel time each way. If you’re really into theme parks maybe worth it, but don’t go expecting Disneyland and then you won’t be disappointed.

We spent almost the whole day at the park. Lots of rides and shows available. We only spent one day here and couldn't cover most of the rides and shows. Still felt we missed a lot. Best to visit here for 2 days.  I really liked the aquarium and managed to watch the mermaid show. Recommended to stay until evening for the night show which was good. The lights and special effects used were spectacular. However, it would have been better if English was spoken during that show.  As for eateries, there are no halal options available yet in the park. However, there is an Indian vegetarian restaurant (Maharaja Lounge) which we tried. The food was good.  Recommended, suitable for all age groups.

Nice theme park, lovely aquarium, worth a visit just for the aquarium. Some of the rides have limits based on the age of a person, not the health of the person. Maybe that policy should be reviewed? Lots of fun things to do, helpful staff.
It's good but it's not Disneyland or Universal Studios. Nonetheless, we had a good time. As others have said, the aquarium is excellent.
Recommended over Nha Trang and HCMC. Need more covered areas but decent time at this park vs Nha Trang and HCMC. Xxxxxxxxxxxx

Парк развлечений довольно приятный, большая территория, прекрасный антураж. Но есть нюансы: 1. всегда имейте запас наличных, не все точки продаж на территории парка имеют pos терминалы. 2. Практически все точки питания это фастфуд, исключения составили лишь 2 кафе океанариума. 3. Аквапарк имеет весьма размытые правила на каждой горке, которые собственно даже не указаны на билбордах. Например с экшен камерой нельзя, но с телефонами пожалуйста. 4. На каждой горке вас ожидает взвешивание, и персонал очень строго следит за показателями, килограмм перевес, и вы никуда не поедете.

Десять лет назад я здесь был, думал я перед поездкой. Жил в отеле рядом с VinWonders Phu Quoc, имея свободный вход в парк. Оказалось, что того прелестного, не очень большого парка уже давно нет. Вернее он есть, но бездействует и разрушен. Его можно увидеть с колеса обозрения. А новый, уже не совсем новый сейчас ( , намного больше. Приезжать в VinWonders Phu Quoc можно надолго. Только аквапарк займет у вас времени не менее 3 часов. Минимум час на огромный (поверьте, мне есть с чем сравнивать) аквариум - океанариум. И часа три, опять же минимум, на аттракционы. Правда на все не попадете, какие-то в ремонте, какие-то не работают по непонятным причинам. Все-таки маленький бардак это постоянный признак отдыха на острове. Билеты можно брать на входе, можно в туркомпаниях у отелей. Мы брали через известный турсайт. Так дешевле всего получается. Питание можно брать вместе с билетом, а можно просто купить здесь. Примерно одни и те же не очень большие деньги.

A magical experience for everyone! From the moment you arrive and see the water feature & hear the music, it transports you back to being a child again (if you’re not one already!) Vinwonders Phu Quoc 是越南版的迪士尼。 門口一看就是歐式風格的建築，一進去是商店街，有餐廳和伴手禮店。設計和迪士尼真的很像，還有一座城堡聳立在中央。不過這裡包含了水陸兩種設施，大家可以帶泳衣去水上樂園區域玩。  旅行團有包午餐，會是在樂園的餐廳享用，都是吃越南菜。 吃完之後會去看一個表演，之後就是自由時間。  要看的表演會是在海龜水族館，水族館的外型是超巨大的海龜，非常特別。內裡不止很寬敞，還有一個很震撼的水族箱。也是我們要看美人魚表演的場地，能看到她們優雅的泳姿。每日會有兩場表演，我們看的是最尾那場 (下午兩點)，表演時間10分鐘。  看完表演後有參觀一下水族館，也玩了幾個機動遊戲。 因為人真的不算很多，所以每項設施都不要排很耐隊就可以玩，省下不少時間。如果想從高空看樂園的全貌，那便要去搭摩天輪囉。  若果對太刺激的機動遊戲泳興趣，這裡還有「機舖」。有各式各樣的遊戲機，像是射擊、賽車相關的也有，而且是免費任玩。  伴手禮的話一般般，去了 Weeny Beeny 夾糖。 中午都待在樂園，大約17:30多才離開，都有3小時多的自由時間。 不去水上樂園區域玩的話，時間都夠充裕。

There are enough activities to last a whole day with the aquarium, Alice in Wonderland, the Aladdin show and the night performance being the standouts. No crowds and probably a fraction of what you would pay at Disneyland. We are however lowering the review by one star because of lunch. We were informed that our tickets would only cover our kids' lunch and we two adults would have to pay. Fair enough but the food was expensive and really mediocre in a crowded and noisy atmosphere. Skip the buffet lunch near the entrance if you can - it was our worst meal in Phu Quoc.

We were staying at the nearby Sheraton Phu Quoc and we decided to embark on a family adventure to VinWonders Phu Quoc with my two boys. The park offered an exceptional mix of attractions, catering to a wide range of interests and thrill levels.  Among the park's many attractions, the tortoise aquarium stood out as a unique and educational experience. We were fascinated by the variety of tortoises and the detailed habitats created for them. The Ferris wheel offered breathtaking views of Phu Quoc, providing a serene moment amidst our exhilarating day. Additionally, the wet train ride added a splash of fun, cooling us down and adding laughter to our adventure.  The roller coaster, with its steep drops and fast turns, was a heart-pounding experience. It proved to be very scary for us, pushing our thrill limits to the edge. While it was an adrenaline rush, it reminded us of the wide range of emotions that VinWonders can evoke.  A minor disappointment was the height restriction for some rides, including our 8-year-old who was too short for attractions requiring a height taller than 1.3 meters. This limitation meant missing out on certain experiences, though the park offered plenty of other enjoyable options suitable for all heights.  The evening light show was a mesmerizing highlight, captivating us with its vibrant display and artistic execution. It was a magical end to our day, leaving us with a lasting impression of the park's charm and creativity.  Our adventure was slightly hampered by the early closing times of the restaurants, which required us to plan our meals more strictly than we would have preferred. Despite this, the variety and quality of food on offer were commendable.  VinWonders Phu Quoc is a treasure trove of fun, learning, and excitement, making it a perfect destination for families. From the serene beauty of the tortoise aquarium and the scenic views from the Ferris wheel to the thrilling roller coaster and playful wet train ride, there's something for everyone. While we navigated some minor challenges, such as dining schedules and ride height restrictions, our overall experience was overwhelmingly positive. We left with hearts full of joy and a collection of memories that beckon us to return. For any family journeying to Phu Quoc, VinWonders promises a day filled with wonder, excitement, and laughter.

Great day out for all ages. Show after theme park closes is worth staying for. Aquarium is amazing and the underground roller-coaster. Giant Ferris wheel shows the only downfall...the original park has just been left to rot and the new park just built on the land around it. Bit of an eyesore.
놀이기구 및 워터파크..수족관과 분수쇼까지 아침에 들어가거 저녁까지 신나게 놀고 왔습니다. 유치원생과 초등 저학년 같이 갔는데 놀이기구 잘 탔고 워터파크에서 잘 놀음. 다시 방문 의사 있음.

We like our Amusement parks and visited VinWonders on Christmas day 2023 as we wanted to do something different. The park/ rides itself is very good despite a few of the rides not being open. When we went, the park was not busy at all, so we managed to go on rides multiple times with no queue.  The Water world / aquarium is absolutely amazing and we spent at least 1 hour in there as it just fascinating. It must have cost an absolute fortune to create this aquarium and it is possibly one of the best ones we have been in anywhere in the world.  The water park is pretty good and again because the park was not busy, we went on rides multiple times, but again, a shame that not everything was open.  We bought the Vinpearl Safari & VinWonders 2 day admission tickets which come with a 250k VND food voucher each. This doesn't sound like a lot, but when main meals are under 180k then it really is good value. Splitting this over 2 days in our opinion is the better thing to do, and the cost increase vs 1 day is very marginal. You then have the benefit to spend as much time as you want at the Amusement park and get to enjoy the Safari (if that is what you want to do). Overall, a very good day out.

Гиды позиционируют это место как "авкапарк и сафари", но это далеко не так. Мы не представляли размеры парка VinWonders, пока не оказались там. Берите билет комбо на 2 дня - день сафари и день на все остальное.  VinWonders - это далеко не только аквапарк. Это: - парк развлечений с настоящими американскими горками, каруселями, которые поднимают к небу, и аттракционами типа "свободное падение" и так далее. Например, есть вагончики, которые завозят вас на скалу, а оттуда съезжают в реку и далее плывут как лодка! Круг на колесе оброзрения длится 20 минут, внутри есть кондиционер. - ВЕЛИКОЛЕПНЫЙ ОКЕНАНАРИУМ, пишут, что входит в топ-5 самых больших океанариумов мира. Лучше застать кормление рыб примерно с 13 или с 14 часов, это впечатляющее зрелище. Там же есть пингвины. - множество зон - деревня викингов c зип0лайнами и вообще полосой препятствий, Fantasy World с героями Диснея, зоны с южноамериканскими джунглями и индейцами майя, с Древней Грецией, - до всего этого мы просто не дошли. - аквапарк, кстати, среднего качества. Множество горок имеют ограничение по весу 60 кг (то есть девушки и дети меньше 100 кг автоматически не допускаются), на многих других горках можно ездить вдвоем, но если вместе вы весите от 100 кг (то есть мама + ребенок ростом до 140 см тоже, скорее всего, не проходят). На каждой горке стоят весы, и каждый раз вас взвешивают. В детской зоне комплекс горок, но с них так лупит сверху холодная вода, что получить удовлоьствие невозможно. На детские горки родителей не допускают, на каждой горке стоит по охраннику и останавливают. Полотенца, лежаки, локер, чтобы оставить вещи, парадоксальным образом в билет не входят, каждая услуга по 50000 донгов. В локер позволяют сложить вещи один раз, что очень неудобно.  Зона сафари расположена совершенно отдельно, между ними ходят автобусы. Это отвал головы! Оживший National Georhraphic. По части территории гуляешь сам, часть - на автобусе, чтобы не оказаться добычей льва. Жирафы едят с рук, обезьяны прыгают с ветки на ветку, бегемот купается в озере, слоны - в реке, на автобусе вы едете мимо носорогов, львов и медведей, стад антилоп, которые подходят вплотную, ЖИРАФОВ МНОГО!

Over decades of my travels I've visited the majority of big name theme parks all over the US, Europe and Asia. This was the first theme park I've visited in southeast Asia.  Vinwonders is located in the northwest of Phu Quoc island occupying a significant land size. Contrary to it, its biggest competitor Sunworld built another theme park, but in the island's south. Apart from these, both companies have other theme parks all over Vietnam, so they're something like Disney and Universal.  Vinwonders Phu Quoc is allegedly the best of all Vinwonders parks. It's a huge mixture of everything: there's a waterpark with waves pool, waterslides, boat thrilling rides, there's a huge aquarium all along with penguin area, enormous water tanks with all kinds of fish, with fish feeding shows, water tunnel and finally there're thrill rides like roller coasters,...etc All the above you get for a single entry ticket of around 40$ pp, which makes it around double cheaper than US and Europe. Besides that, thing I was shocked with - park is almost empty. There are NO waiting lines for any rides, including the most popular ones. Instead, the ride itself waits people to gather in enough number to start it !!?? You can go up and down, again and again as many times you want without any wait. Staff is very kind, directing you if you cannot find the way and at the same time very conscientious. They will measure and weigh you every time before letting you ride, no matter you were there 5 min before, which was little bit annoying.  In terms of the above I started to question myself how they cover and manage enormous costs of such a huge park ? And, we were there for New Year's holidays which I think is the high season.  At the end of the day, at 6.30PM there's a high tech spectacular show in front of the main Castle. In every aspect it matches Disney Magic show.  I cannot comment the food, as we don't like theme park junk food, but we consumed ice creams and drinks which were reasonably priced.  Generally speaking, I'd advise you to visit - it's a good value for money without any crowds and time wasting. The only drawback is that feeling of emptiness which makes it little bit weird what we're used to.

Our family enjoyed the time in VinWonders immensely. For the price we paid, it was totally worthwhile. I think it helped to not have high expectations and we also speak Vietnamese; so that must have helped with any possible misunderstanding.  We spent the whole morning in the water park, changed into regular clothes afterward and then spent 2 or 3 other them parks. Nowhere were there long lines, which is wonderful. We got to go on some rides or slides multiple times without a wait. The interesting thing is they weigh you every time before you get on water slides, even if they recognize you, but that must be policy, and I don't mind that.  The inconvenient thing about VinWonders is that there is only on entrance for each theme park, so you have to walk all around before getting to another park, which was fine for us, but some people may not enjoy the walk.  If you purchased the combo ticket with food & beverage credit, make sure you check with your agent the restaurants that accept the F&B credit because there are other eateries apart from ones that take the F&B credit.  Also, don't expect everything to be open all the time. We had the right expectations and it helped us have a lot of fun.

Not to be missed, great value. It is better to go to the water park in the morning because it is sunny and warm. If you go to the water park in the afternoon or near sunset, may be chilly. The park opens at 9 o'clock, but the rides(games) only start at 10 am, which is a bit strange. Remember to reserve some time to board the wheel before sunset to enjoy the full view of the Sea Turtle Aquarium! The 630pm light show is well worth watching, the atmosphere is fabtastic and the production is filled with passion!

We were uncertain but actually really enjoyed going to Vinwonders (family with teens). Recommend arriving early in the day. We were there for opening at 9am. We enjoyed going on the rides first, before the day was too hot (seats can get very hot!) and before eating!! There are plenty of water activities and wo many waterslides to enjoy. The Sea Shell aquarium was amazing and another way to relax out of the sun. We were there for 5 hours and didn't see everything. You could easily spend the whole day there. You can hire towels and a locker, plenty of stores to buy things and many options for food also. Recommend!!

Firstly, go early. There is a lot to do here. We made the mistake of arriving at 2pm, we should have arrived by 10am. There is an awful lot to do here so be prepared for a long day or to split over 2 days. We will come back later in the year to complete the park. The rides and attractions all look really good. We managed to fit a couple in. However, at least 2 (log flume and something similar) were both broken. For the log flume I managed to get half way up the first mountain before it broke down mid ride! We all had to walk back down and they closed the ride for the day. The other ride was just “under maintenance“ and would open soon for the whole day. In my opinion there is a lot of potential here. They need to get their maintenance up to scratch - not sure that’s a good thing to say about a theme park but it really is worth the money. Not too expensive either. Food was mediocre as you would expect at a tourist trap so eat a hearty breakfast. Also note there are a lot of hotels nearby offering free tickets with the rooms. Best part was the closing light and water show. Was on a par with any Disney park show and we really enjoyed it. Ah, also the aquarium is fantastic. Stop for a cold drink at the restaurant with the gigantic tank!  As I say a bit disappointed about the broken up idea but still highly enjoyed. Will be back to complete the park later this year.

Huge park with areas dedicated to children, adventure-seeker adults and water parks. The park is well done, props are very nice but the first area is dedicated mostly to small kids - not much interactivity. On the other hand, there are some attractions for adults in seek of adrenaline but not much in between. Water park was nice but the slides are subject to stairs of 5-6 stories which can be hard to climb at +30 Celsius degrees. Takes about 5 hrs to enjoy so make sure you get there in the morning as they start closing at about 6 pm. Huge aquarium, but some tanks look kinda small and penguins look a bit hungry ( can be their way to trick you hut that was the feeling we got).  Don’t get scared of the ghost city around it, you’ll get used to this in most areas of Phu Quoc. I recommend wearing flip flops and take your bathing suit with you.  Food in the featured restaurants is not the greatest but they offer you a 150.000 VN voucher that can be used in most locations.  Have fun!

We went right before the Tet crowd to a practically empty theme park. It was amazing to be there and be able to have no waiting lines for any attractions. The facility was clean and well-maintained. The children enjoyed every minute, including the food, rides and attractions. Map out your day Prices are reasonable. Don't miss the aquarium in the giant turtle! It was one of the highlights. The ferris wheel was a good 20 minute respite for everyone with spectacular views. The show at the end of the evening in front of the castle is interesting as the music is all in English while the narration is Vietnamese. Regardless, its was entertaining.

Wir waren einen Tag im VinWonders und waren wirklich positiv überrascht.  Pro : - der Park ist sehr wirklich groß mit verschiedenen "Bereichen" haben wir so nicht erwartet - gibt genug Fahrgeschäfte und auch viele Essensmöglichkeiten - man fühlt sich tatsächlich an Disneyland erinnert. - es war angenehm leer - das Aquarium ist genial  Kontra: - wenig Karrussels für kleine Kinder - Essen nicht die beste Qualität aber ok  Für einen Familienausflug sehr zu empfehlen

A surprisingly modern and good theme and water park.  We traveled on a weekday and there were no queues for any rides, which were all operating.  The location is out of the way but free shuttle buses are provided from the south of the island.

Đây là lần đầu tôi sử dụng app này , tôi đc biết qua bạn tôi . Tôi có dịp đi phú quốc lần 2 và đây là lần đầu tôi đi công viên vinwonders tôi phải ngỡ ngàng với trước khung cảnh nơi đây . Thật tuyệt vời và đẹp , vì tôi không chơi được trò cảm giác mạnh nên tôi trải nghiệm trò nhẹ nhàng như vòng xoay , trò tàu viễn tây và nhân viên ở trò tàu viễn tây tên là Quân rất vui vẻ , nhiệt tình hướng dẫn và giúp tôi biết từng khu vực ở đây . Tôi rất vui vì điều đó và tôi sẽ trở lại đây cùng gia đình nếu có dịp . .
何個かアトラクションは運休中だったが空いていました。平日なら待つことなく遊べます。ウオーターランドのアトラクションは子供連れで混んでいました。

Đi vào dịp này nên các trò chơi bị đóng băng kha khá, nếu không đi khu công viên nước thì chắc một buổi chiều đến tối là chơi đù rồi. Ở đây có rải rác nhà hàng nên không sợ đói, lại có nhiều chỗ ngồi được nên không sợ mỏi chân đâu. Nếu so với Disneyland, hoặc Universal Studios hoặc Everland của Hàn thì chưa bằng nhưng mình thấy Việt Nam có được khu vui chơi như thế đã đỉnh lắm rồi. Show Once cũng hay lắm nghen, nên coi, không nên bỏ qua. Chỉ hơi tiếc là khu thuỷ cung mới chưa khai trương nên có lẽ sau này sẽ quay lại lần nữa, hy vọng lúc đó tất cả trò chơi đều hoạt động.
Phục vụ khá là tốt, các nhân viên khá nhạy bén Nên trải nghiệm qua nha các bạn, mình nghĩ mới mở nên. Very good
Nhiều trò chơi, đẹp, rộng, sạch sẽ và thú vị. Nhân viên thân thiện, hướng dẫn tận tình Vài trò chưa đưa vào hoạt động
Cơm sườn ngon. Phạm Văn Được . Cách phục vụ ổn, nhiệt tình, vui vẻ. Chổ ngồi mát mẻ thoải mái. Đồ ăn ngon
Nhà hàng Outpost trang trí rất đẹp, đậm phong cách Nam Phi. Anh Lê Văn Ngọc rất nhiệt tình, vui vẻ. Không khí thoải mái vì không quá đông đúc. Món ăn ở đây khá phù hợp với khẩu vị của mình.
Nhân viên thân thiện, giúp đỡ nhiệt tình và gần gũi (anh Lê Minh Căng) - Cấu trúc ổn, thú vị - Nhiều trò chơi hay, đặc sắc
Đã mở rộng và có nhiều trò chơi, khu vực tham quan hơn trước nhiều tuy nhiên vẫn chưa xong 100%, còn nhiều hạng mục trò chơi vẫn còn đang làm dang dở

Đến vào mùa lễ Giáng sinh lm cho nơi này càng thêm lộng lẫy hơn rất nhìu, tôi vs ng yêu thật hạnh phúc khi đến nơi này. À tôi có lời khen đến anh nhân viên tên Đỗ Văn Lịch anh ấy hướng dẫn hai tôi rất tận tình. Dù xa nhà nhưng được đón giáng sinh ở đây làm cho tôi thấy ấm áp vô cùng.

Nhân viên nhiệt tình, vui tính. Hầu như trò chơi nào cũng nhận được sự giúp đỡ của các bạn nhân viên. Mình lên vòng xoay thời gian lúc gần cuối giờ sắp đóng cửa mà bạn Khang vẫn rất tận tình. Có điều thời gian này mình đi không có nhiều trò lắm vì đang bảo trì

Nhân viên rất nhiệt tình và rất quan tâm đến tụi mình khi tham quan tại Vin Wonders. Ấn tượng với sự chu đáo của bạn Khang. Tiếc là các trò chơi hơi loãng, ko thú vị lắm, rất nhiều trò chơi đang bảo trì nên mình chẳng chơi được. Thiếu rất nhiều trải nghiệm ở đây.

Our family very much enjoyed our visit to VinWonders Phu Quoc, but it's still good to state the pros and cons so you'll know what to expect when you visit during Covid in 2020:  Pros/ Tips:  1. It wasn't crowded at all though it was summer. Understandably, because: Covid. Though I do wanna point out that Vietnam has it under control quite well with one of the least deaths and infections per million residents. That said, you could take all the rides without queuing at all! We took many rounds of our favourite ones.  2. Though it wasn't crowded, appreciated that the stations were all still open and that service was great. Staff still greeted us with smiles and enthusiasm. The guides at the fairy tale stations still gave us a full story rundown, mostly in Vietnamese.  3. You can take many great photos with not many/ nobody in your background. This works well especially when you're taking photos in "Alice in Wonderland"... since Alice didn't see many other humans down that rabbit hole! Also, you may want to visit when the sun sets ~6pm since that's when the castle will be beautifully lit up.  4. This VinWonders amusement park is NEW! Everything we visited was in great condition. The new and improved Ferris Wheel is much larger than the old one and... has great air-conditioning too. And a great view of the park + hotels + beach beyond.  5. Food-wise, there are many more options than the old amusement park. They managed to get some familiar names such as Lotteria, Burger King and a Korean BBQ place to open in the park. There were also many small booths selling snacks.  Cons/ Good to know before visiting:  1. As mentioned earlier, there ARE many food options in the park, including international brands. HOWEVER, most seem to be closed by 7pm... or earlier. So you may wanna plan dinner elsewhere outside the park.  2. You may literally be the only person taking the roller coaster ride into the night (only ~7pm)... which we did. We didn't mind the added "thrill", but you might.  3. They're kinda in a transition phase. Their new aquarium was gonna be "Southeast Asia's largest aquarium" when it opens, and we kinda looked forward to that as it was supposed to open mid 2020, but because of the pandemic, we understand that it simply makes more financial sense to open it in 2021 when they presumably have more visitors. So right now, you gotta visit the old aquarium which is... much smaller. And the old aquarium is in the middle of the old, half-dismantled amusement park. Not sure how that works out as we didn't visit it. Also, the musical fountain show did NOT take place in front of the new and beautifully lit up castle. We're not sure if it was cancelled due to low turnout, or because it was happening at the old amusement park. I hope they would let us know at the point of entry. Either that or, you gotta remember to ask the park attendants before waiting there unfruitfully, like we did.
khu vui chơi rất đa dạng , khung cảnh đẹp lộng lẫy, nhiều hạn mục vui chơi đang trong giai đoạn hoàn thành,

В автобус лучше садиться на конечной...тк по ходу на некоторых остановках он даже не останавливается Парк прикольный, но с апреля 2020 видимо будет вообще нечто!!! Если сранивать с Нячангом, то тут ВООБЩЕ пусто...очередей нет! Мы когда были для посетителей был бесплатный ужин, но это надо узнавать при покупке билетов

Pour 80 euros / 5 personnes ...... cherchez quelque chose d'équivalent en France ! Parc aquatique et d'attraction avec la même entrée ... que demander de plus , même si le parc d'attraction est en travaux et que tout n'est pas ouvert . Aucune attente nulle part , personnel agréable . Bref très bonne journée .

The Vinpearl land theme park is already well worth a visit, and will be even better when the second phase of construction is complete. it was only around £10 pp to enter, and that gets you on all of the rides, 5d cinema, water park with great slides, aquarium and all the side shows . Food and drink is very reasonably priced, the place is well organised and suitable for all ages.
The hotel tuck-Turks take you free of charge to all areas on VINPEARL land which includes shops, restaurants, fun park, big wheel etc.

Vinpearlland var en oplevelse for sig da vi ankom om formiddagen var der måske ikke mere end 50 gæster i parken - vildt underligt at gå rundt i en tom park. Senere på dagen kom der flere til, vil tro der måske var 500 da der var flest. Når det så er sagt er parken ren og pæn, badelandet har nogle sjove vandrutsjebaner hvor sikkerheden er i højsæde, er du under 1,4m er der mange aktiviteter der ikke kan prøves. Ligeledes er der et stort vandområde kun for børn under 1,4m. Vores to børn på 11 og 13 år havde en skøn dag og når parken er færdig udbygget vil det helt sikkert have sat endnu større indtryk. De næste aktiviteter lader til at skulle åbne i april 2020.
Отличный парк, отличный аквариум, плохой аквапарк для детей, очень скользкое покрытие в детском бассейне.
There is not a huge amount of rides here and the water park is quite small. The aquarium is quite impressive though. A must see is the water show in the evening which is excellent.
Theme park is relatively sparse. You hardly have to wait in line for any ride, although there are not too many rides, it was fun and relaxing.

This place is like a small and empty Disneyland, which is a good thing for me. Its best assets are its staff, the water park, the buffet, and the water fountain show because:  1) Their staff were very friendly and professional, always keeping their facilities clean and assisting visitors with a very polite demeanor. I returned a day later because I had lost my wallet. Their cleaning staff had returned it with all my documents and all the cash money I had in it. I wanted to give their honest employee some of that money in gratitude because their management knew who it was, but they did not allow me to do so. Therefore, I deducted half a point for that in my review since I believe in compensating honesty once in a while. I hope that employee gets either a raise or an advancement offer for a position that increases in pay and responsibility.  2) Although there are mechanical rides at the park, most people prefer the water park simply because Vietnam is warm or hot weather throughout the year.  3) The buffet food and refreshments were delicious, although I couldn’t tell for sure since I was very hungry after swimming all afternoon.  4) The musical water fountain show is a great way to conclude the day with family after having had a delicious meal and feeling refreshed under a beautiful warm evening spectacle.  The other half point I deducted is because the place has little access for people in wheel chairs or with difficulty walking. For example, the entrance to the restrooms next to the aquarium exit and the ramp at the stairs of the changing rooms building at the water park.

We bought the combo ticket visiting together with the safari park, and spent 5 hours here in the amusement park. Glad that a dinner buffet was included with wide variety of delicacies. Different types of facilities can be found inside the park, including water park, ferris wheel, merry go around, bumper car, roller coaster, aquarium, game centre and more. A light and water show will start around 7:30 as well before the closure of the park.  The best part of this amusement park was that all facilities were just short-queued. With less than 5 minutes of waiting time, we were able to visit every single facilities here. A bit surprise by the size of the aquarium, we walked so fast through it but still took us 10 minutes to went out to the exit. The water park was average, consisting of several slides with not much visitors as well (the changing room could be improved, like the shower units and changing units, bad smell and verrrrrry weak water of shower). Towels and lockers need to be paid for rental.  The park is now under renovation and upgrade. Few more facilities will be added after its completion estimated in 2021. I suggest if you don’t have enough time during your stay, you can make a choice to skip it or just plan to have a stop at the safari instead.
빈펄랜드 입장권이 포함되어 있는 호텔 패키지여서 빈펄랜드에 두 번이나 갔어요. 저녁때와 오전에 가서 한두시간씩 놀았네요. 사람이 많지 않아서 기다리지 않고 여러 놀이기구를 실컷 탈 수 있었어요.

Первый раз были здесь пять лет назад. Изменилось что-то мало. Чуть чуть все постарело. Аквапарк совсем в этот раз небольшим показался. Самым лучшим впечатлением остаётся шоу фонтанов. Но по соседству возводится такой грандиозный парк, что все Диснейленды мира обзавидуются. Через пару лет можно смело сюда приезжать только ради этого парка. Я бы все таки определил Vinpearl Land Phu Quoc как весь комплекс отелей и услуг системы Винперл, системы , которой здесь ещё не было семь лет назад. А сейчас это огромный комплекс, целая страна, который постоянно развивается. Мне такой отдых, в окружении толп людей , не очень нравиться, Но это мне , а многим другим такой отдых в кайф!

Có khá nhiều loại cá khác nhau. Nếu có thêm các loại sứa nữa thì có lẽ sẽ màu sắc hơn. Cá mập khá ít. Nên có thêm màn hình trình chiếu 1 clip ngắn để tuyên truyền bảo tồn sinh vật biển cho các em thiếu nhi ✌🏻

On the 1st of January 2020 my fiancé and I visited Vinpearl Land and Safari Phu Quoc with my parents, it was an enjoyable visit with many positive comments but a few downfalls...the Safari; one of the most amazing safaris I’ve ever been to anywhere in the world so far, the animals are all well looked after and fed properly which is amazing to see!
