Been here twice and both times quiet so practically park to yourself so no queues - mega bonus. Relive your childhood as we did. Only downside half of amusement park rides down for maintenance which would be good to know from ticket office or even lower price slightly as you dont get the same experience.

After reading all of the mixed reviews on here I had talked myself out of going but I'm so glad I was dragged there. The theme park isn't too big but it does have a few rides, an aquarium and a water park. The rides weren't too scary but still a bit of fun and would be great for kids! The aquarium wasn't too bad- they had penguins, turtles, some small sharks and things like reptiles and tropical fish. My favourite part was the water park though. They had a few decent waterslides, a lazy river and a large complex for kids. The 'kids' area wasn't even that bad for grown ups, too! Overall, the park was great fun and even if you are from a country spoilt with huge amusement parks, there will still be something for you at vin pearl land- even if it is just the free arcade games area.
Don't forget to bring your walking shoes and flip flop. as there will be hot so may burn your foot. however, i was enjoy there half day.
Plus, the water was not that clean on the day we visited, given that there were very few guests at the time. The waterpark's staff were very nice and supportive.
I spent half day afternoon there after visiting the zoo near there. It is included in the ticket to vinpeal land. Don't forget to bring your waking shoes as you will need to walk a lot.

ヴィンパールランドに宿泊し、利用しました。日本に同じ規模の施設があったらきっと夏は激コミすることでしょう。が、こちらは身長制限のある大人向けのスライダーから子ども用のウオータープレイグラウンドまで、待ち時間なしで遊ぶことができます。 思いのほかパラソルなどの日影が少ないのと、遊んでいても日差しが強いので水着の上にＴシャツを着ていかれることをお勧めします。ロッカー、というかクロークみたいなのも有料でしたがあります。わが家が訪れた時は18：00までだったと思います。夜もやっててもいいのになぁ、昼は暑いから。

Парк расположен на удалении от города. Ехать на бесплатном автобусе примерно 30 мин. Сначала автобус останавливается у аквапарка потом едит в сафари парк. Первый автобус едит в 8.30-8.40 в парк приезжает к 9.10. Стоимость билета 500000 для взрослого в один парк или 800000 за два парка. В городе продастся ваучеры ( не билеты ) их над обменять в кассе парка на платковую карту для входа в аквапарк. Сам аквапарк построен по подобию Диснейленда: от входа идёт центральная улица ( справа магазины слева большой ресторан, давольно неплохое меню, только очень дорого по сравнению с едой на острове ) и упирается в типа "Замок" ( внутри кафе с напитками и закусками - общепит ) справа от "замка" аквапарк, слева парк аттракционов, шоу фонтанов ( в 19.00 и 20.00 ) далее дельфинарии ( при нас не работал ) и аквариум ( давольно красивый, много рыб, рептилий и змей ). Сам аквапарк состоит из 4-х больших горок для плотов на 4-х человек ( пускают только если вас четверо или ищите себе компаньонов для четверых ), бассейна с волной ( работает по часом, но волна дохлая - для детей ), в центре река с кругами на 1-2 человека и зана для малышей ( но это единственное место с прохладной водой и очень сильными струями - даже мне было больно, непонятно как там устоят дети! ), ещё есть горки для самостоятельного ( без круга ) спуска, их 6-8 шт. В аквапарке очень мало лежаков, правда когда мы пришли в 13.00 после сафари парка там было всего человек 20. Днём очень жарко и очень тепловая вода. Полотенец в парке не дают, надо брать из отеля и непродуманная положена плитка - ходить босиком невозможно, получается пошёл на горку оставил тапки у лестницына горку, спустился и безмолвно по раскалённой плитки за своими тапками!!! А так это лучшее развлечение по острове, но по мне одного посещения достаточно за глаза. И ещё при долгом плескании в бассейне чуствуется хлорка.
There are most popular games in this park, from water games to other dangerous games, underwater paradise, water-light music, etc

Easily the best part of this place was that there are no queues for any of the rides, just walk up and hop on. It was fantastic.  The water park is nice, with quite a bit to do. The aquarium is only ok, not that much to see there.  If you can take your own food inside, as the food inside only tastes average, and is quite expensive.

Gia đình có con nhỏ nên đưa đến đây chơi. Các trò chơi cảm giác mạnh phù hợp với người lớn thì trẻ em có nguyên một khu vui chơi trong nhà. Thuỷ cung rộng và phong phú các loại sống ở biển. Công viên nước hơi nhỏ so với các nơi khác nhưng rất tốt. Đặc biệt giá cả ở Vinpearl Land rẻ hơn so với bên khách sạn Vinpearl.
Было забавно, аквариум интересный, карусели неплохие, горки в аквапарке, кроме там где вчетвером на ватрушках показались не очень захватывающими.

ヴィンパールリゾートに宿泊したので、無料で利用できました。園内には遊園地、ウォーターパーク、水族館、ゲームセンターがあり、大人でも楽しめました。とにかくガラガラに空いていて待ち時間無しで遊び放題です。 遊園地の回転系の乗物は日本とは違いかなりのスピードで回ります！ ウォーターパークはスライダーが充実していて楽しかったです。が、ロッカーと貸タオルは別料金です。 水族館や広場などではショーも行われます。夜の噴水ショーはすごく良かったです。 園内の売店では３３３ビールが25,000ドン（約125円）、缶ジュースが20,000ドン（約100円）。軽食やアイスもあります。 ホテルからは無料のトゥクトゥクが随時送迎してくれます。また、ユーンドン市街との無料シャトルバスも運行しています。 宿泊客以外の方の入場料は大人一人500,000ドン（約2,500円）です。ヴィンパールサファリとのセット割引もあるようです。

Voor 500.000vnd inclusief gratis Pendelbus, die elk half uur rijdt. Leuk waterpark! Je kunt het beste met zijn vieren naar dit waterpark, want de meeste glijbanen moet je met zijn vieren nemen. Kindergedeelte was gesloten, werd opnieuw geverfd. Aquarium was prachtig! Met zeemeerminnenshow. In een tunnel door het aquarium. Ook nog een gedeelte met reptielen en slangen. Watershow met muziek ook erg mooi! Diverse kermisattracties en achtbanen voor de durfals! Nergens een wachtrij , ook voor 3 personen lieten ze de attractie draaien! Zeker de moeite waard om te bezoeken! Erg leuke dag en veel plezier gehad!

Definitely worth a visit. The water park is really fun and well designed. There was one ride we couldn't do as a couple but otherwise it was great! Very well managed with attentive staff members, lockers to put your bag etc in for a 10,000 dong. Enjoyed the games room and light show. Only negative was the aquarium - the fish / animals seemed quite stuffed into their cages at times, suggest this needs to looking into.
在Resort之前入住了这家酒店，住的别墅，100万美金一桩，首府30%，每年自己可以使用100天，其余时间交给酒店打理，每个别墅带小泳池，酒店主楼前也有个超大泳池，泳池前面就是大海，不过这里的海水很一般，要玩海的据说还是得去岘港不错，期待下次吧。关于其它的点评，跟resort差不多了。

We are both 20 years old and had a good time in vinpearl land. Litterally no people so we didn't have to wait in line ever. Some parts of the park is not built yet, like the cinema. The food there is also decent. The water park area is fun, but can be a bit challenging if you are a smaller group of people as some slides require 4 people. You just have to wait for another group of people and ask them to go with you. Check if there is a shuttle bus from your hotel.
项目丰富，各种年龄段的人都可以玩得尽兴。门口有保险箱出租，场内有餐厅。和国内相比人非常少，很少有项目需要排队，因此可以玩很多·

In general, Vinpearl Land is a fun and interesting place. Rides are not too scary, but can still give a thrill even to an adult. The aquarium is exciting to see, the games arcade is really fun and the water park is enjoyable both for adults and children.  I did not give it an Excellent rating, because: - If you're not a fan of water slides, there is literally nothing to do in the water park area. - Some of the best slides require 4 people to go on to them. What if I am only with 2 people? - Food could be better.  Still, the small issues above don't mean you should not go. Even as an adult, there is some fun stuff to do and the ticket is not that expensive.

Det bästa är egentligen den magnifika åkturen med kabinbanan till och från Vinpearl. Tar man sig sen bara förbi "turistfällorna" med alla småbutiker öppnar sig ett fint och trevligt badland med flera olika rutschbanor m.m. Dessutom en fin liten strand där man gärna ligger och vilar en stund.

We went mainly for the water park. Getting there at 2pm or so and leaving after the 7pm water fountain show (free hourly shuttle Bus ride from the afternoon) is probably the perfect amount of time if you are a couple i.e. so not having to cater to young and old alike. You'll probably need all day if you want to go to the aquarium and the arcades too  Having been to a fair few theme parks and water parks in "the west", though this isn't the biggest it does have 6 or 7 big water rides and seeming as there are zero queues it means being able to go multiple times, which counts for a lot in my book!!  As others have mentioned, so few people in the park (at least aside from families with young kids). Only downside of this is that the one ride for which 4 people is a must can difficult to get on to as you'll need to spend some time finding people to fill the gap (as we had to do)  Overall lots of thrills to be had, mainly in the water park (theme park part more of a fairground, no proper roller coasters) and decent value for money (500k entrance per person and free transport)
Thực sự nhiều trò chơi thú vị, tính giải trí cao cho nhóm mình, giá cả hơi cao so với budget của tôi. Lần sau tới tiếp
It was quite new when we visited, so everything was really nice, including the food (which normally is crap inside amusement parks).

tôi cãm thấy hài lòng vì những gì nơi đây mang lại, khung cãnh, trò chơi và cách phục vụ của nhân viên...nhưng ước gì ở đây có thể tích hợp thêm nhiều nhà hàng phục vụ ăn uống và giá thành có thể tốt hơn, tôi cãm thấy nó quá đắt cho việc ăn uống nơi đây !!!

Vinpearl is a locally known brand name for building one-stop destination entertainment for families. They're not up to international standards, but still good fun for a half-day trip.  The indoor aquarium is new, so it was rather enjoyable to roam in it as everything was in good condition and many creatures to see. There's the interactive pool with turtles, huge eel tanks, underwater tunnel, and mermaid show to watch. Do be early for the show to get a good seat. Maybe an hour and a half here.  The waterpark is large enough to have a wave pool, some 3-storey high slides and splashes for young and old. Good fun for young kids.  The theme park is simply not completed. You still can see hoardings around many uncompleted rides in the park. For those rides which were already working, you can still see wires dangling around and you can tell from the plasticky look that it ain't good quality machines. The indoor theme park/softplay activity area for todds/arcade area was under repair too with wires protruding out in some places. Not my idea of a safe day for kids.  The food was the worst. We went to the most decent looking cafe we could find near the entrance. Other than the fried rice which wasn't much to boast about either, the other items were inedible. The fish and chips was covered in a massive chunk of flour that was so hard it's impossible to bite. The fish is of questionable origin/taste.  TIPS: I say if you want to go there, have an early satisfying breakfast elsewhere, spend max half a day at VinPearl Land, and quickly scoot off somewhere else for lunch. Also, they've an hourly free shuttle bus that will pick you up from outside Long Beach Village hotel. It's a 45min ride so probably take the earliest at 8am? The park opens at 9am. Or else a cab ride each way is around usd20.
Khu VPland rất bổ ích, nhiều trò chơi hấp dẫn. Khu Aquarium và nhạc nước tuyệt vời. Đặc biệt các trò chơi đều miễn phí đối với khách nghỉ tại khách sạn VP

The Vin Pearl land is the big amusement park included Water Park and Musical fountain at 7:00pm and 8:00pm daily. With all included in one ticket, kids can play whatever they want and not limited of time.  This park is very good for family who stay at Vin Pearl resort because it is just next door. They have tuk tuk to transfer clients from resort to the amusement park. It is nice to spent time here.

If you ever been to Vinpearl Land at Nha Trang, Vietnam, you will not like this place much - significantly smaller and less impressive. Well, if you are tired doing nothing on one of the islands beaches, this is nice place to kill your time. Nothing super impressive, but better than nothing. Fountains show - really cool! Something like show in Singapore on Sentosa, but different. Very recommend to visit, even though it is at 19.30 (there is one more show time at 20.00). Water park - some attractions are really thrilling, but water sometimes was too cold for me.

Midt ude i et endnu ubebygget område, ligger det her kæmpe vandland/tivoli. Da der endnu ikke er så mange besøgende, var der ingen kø, hvilket var fantastisk. I vandlandet var der begrænsninger ved rutsjebanerne, hvor man skulle veje under 90 kg. Det vil begrænse mange europæere/amerikanere i at prøve disse, da mange mænd heroppe fra jo bare vejer mere og er meget større end asiatiske mennesker. Fliserne på jorden bliver ret varme, så behold sandalerne på. En sjov dag. Vi var ikke indenom se nogle af dyrene, kun Tivoli og vandland. Pris 500000 vnd.

We got discount tickets thru our Hotel in Phu Quoc so total cost for myself and my tall (over 140cm..this will make a difference to price ) 10yr old boy was only 900,000 ..once inside EVERYTHING is free except food and drink which is reasonably priced , varied and easy to purchase ... We spent 6 and half hours here and still didnt go on all the rides and slides... The Aquarium was AMAZING ...no queues is a total bonus ....we were the only ones on the Ferris Wheel which was HUGE...Games arcade awesome ...some of the childrens slides didnt really have enough water pressure for me to fully slide down without having to push myself along which was the only downside but the bigger slides no problem. ...the music was awesome ...everyone was getting ready for Lunar New Year so a few local tourists ...I would definitely go again ...best advice is to make sure you are there for the 7:30pm Waterfall Light show which was totally mindbowing ...we left on a high ...happy and satisfied ...if you have children this is a MUST DO while on Phu Quoc....

If you have more days on the island and your kids are fed up with the salty water it is a perfect option to spend a day away from the beaches. At the time we were there everything worked fine and there are some really cool staff for adults! Unfortunately, as I also read from others, they stick to their rules so won't let anyone use some slides or games under 140cm. It was quite annoying with a 4.5 girl and a 6 passed boy (much bigger and stronger than average) who both can swim but could only use the baby part. We just couldn't explain them why they can not use the same things as they can in our home country. The same everywhere in the the whole park, so we were wondering which consumer group they are trying to target :) maybe only Asian adults go and have fun without their children?? But overall it's a tidy, well operated place. By the way, never try to ask a pizza in the restaurant by the lazy river!! Not like on the pictures, they serve you a 15cm 'pizza cake' which we immediately sent back!!
설사 빈펄랜드에 투숙을 하지 않아도 셔틀버스 또는 택시로 와서 하루정도 보내기 좋은 장소입니다. 워터파크와 놀이시설 이용하고 내부 식당을 이용하면 충분히 하루 시간을 보내기에 충분합니다. 그러나 일정이 짧은분들은 굳이 오실 필요까지는 아니라고 생각됩니다.

Kävimme huvipuistossa ja safari parkissa tammikuussa 2016. Molemmat tuntuivat olevan juuri valmistuneet, mutta väkeä ei ollut kummassakaan lähes ollenkaan. Palvelut kuitenkin pelasivat ja ihmiset olivat ystävällisiä, niinkuin Vietnamissa aina. Kuumana päivänä myös huvipuiston vesipuisto toimii!

Vi havde en god dag i forlystelsesparken. Vi rejer med et barn på 10 mdr, så vi brugte mest akvariet, samt legelandet, og det fungerede fint. Maden var ok. Lidt syret oplevelse at være så alene i en forlystelsespark. Vil skyde på der var 150 mennesker i alt. Fedt sted for lidt ældre børn, da der selvfølgelig ikke var kø ved nogle forlystelser.
すいているので、乗り物は乗り放題。水族館やウォーターパークも併設。ゲームセンターも無料で、ゲームやり放題。暑いですが、子供たちは1日中遊べると思います。

Мы были здесь в первый месяц открытия. Внушительная по площади территория.Очень понравился аквариум,игры, рестораны, аттракционы.На фонтаны не попали, т.к. были утром.Жаль! Очень хвалят! Очень удобно добираться с их автобусом,который заберёт Вас в любом месте этого чудного острова. В будущем, я надеюсь будет не хуже, в Нячанге!

Our twins are one, but walking. We actually found a lot of things for them to enjoy. They have a dedicated swimming area for little ones, they enjoyed roaming around the aquarium, and most of all they had a play area with several areas they enjoyed immensely. If you want to take a break from the beach I definitely recommend a visit.

The park is beautiful and very spacious. I highly recommend taking the buggy, and if possible, the VIP buggy—it really makes a big difference.
There are many restaurants with a wide variety of food options. Restrooms are available everywhere, which is very convenient. Prices for water, ice cream, and even meals are very reasonable and actually quite cheap compared to similar places.
My only issue with this place is the timing. It’s a hot country with intense sun during midday, yet the park closes just when the weather finally becomes pleasant. The heat usually starts to ease around 5 PM, but the rides close at 6 PM, which honestly makes no sense.
In Gulf countries, even during summer, theme parks usually open from around 3 PM until midnight to avoid the extreme heat. Closing the rides when the weather is finally good feels frustrating—as if visitors are being forced to suffer in the heat.

The park management really needs to rethink the closing time. The park shouldn’t close before 9 or 10 PM, and there’s no need to open as early as 9 AM. Unfortunately, the early closing time ruins the beauty of the place, its cleanliness, and the otherwise helpful staff.

Rather average compared to European theme parks. The only place that really stands out and makes this place worth visiting is the aquarium and the only fun thrill rides are the Zeus and the Aztec one. Maybe it's more fun for children, or the water park is better but we didn't go there.

VinWonders Phu Quoc was honestly underwhelming for me, especially since I’ve already visited Disneyland. It felt like a poor imitation rather than a unique theme park experience. Most attractions can be covered within 3–4 hours, so it doesn’t really justify a full-day visit.
The main highlight is the Mermaid Show at 2 PM—if you plan to watch that, you’ll need to wait until then; otherwise, you can arrive, finish the park, and be done by around 1PM. Overall, it may work for first-time theme park visitors or families with kids, but if you’ve experienced world-class parks, expectations should be kept low.

A pretty mediocre park unfortunately. Not alot of rides, and all the big ones were closed for maintenance or other reasons - without any notice anywhere. I visited *VinWonders Phu Quoc* recently and had a good overall experience — the park is well-maintained with lots of fun attractions. However, one important thing to note for future visitors: the *internal buggy (cart) service* is a *paid add-on*, and unfortunately, there was *no option to include it while booking tickets online*, nor any way to upgrade once inside the park.

A Disappointing Experience

While the park has an excellent concept, the execution is severely lacking. Take the water slide attraction, for example: guests are forced to wait in line for over 30 minutes under the scorching 35+ degree Celsius heat without even basic fans to provide relief. To make matters worse, more than half of the boats are sent down empty, significantly increasing wait times.

The locker system is another frustrating aspect. It’s completely manual—you have to change, then collect paper receipts. Why? It’s 2024, and most places have modern electronic wristbands that eliminate the need to carry cash or paper tickets. Instead, you end up feeling like a walking keychain, constantly worrying about a physical locker key.

Honestly, this park feels like a one-time experience at best. The only redeeming feature was the aquarium, which was genuinely interesting. But beyond that, I wouldn’t recommend it unless you have extremely low expectations.

Nice unique amusement park. Crowd control was non-existent. Not enough English speaking security resulting in congestion. People were pushing n jumping queue at the rides. Be careful if you have kids, keep a close eye as the park is vast. Equipment are working well. One ride was under repair but the rest were ok. Enjoyed the visit. Lack of English speaking workers n crowd control was the reason for my 3 stars. And this was a weekday!

The entrance ticket is quite cheap, which is a big plus. We also visited when it wasn’t crowded at all, so moving around the park was easy and comfortable. Food and drinks inside are surprisingly affordable compared to other theme parks, which I appreciated.

However, the main downside is that many rides were not working during our visit. This was disappointing, especially considering that attractions are the main reason to come here. On top of that, the show schedules are not respected — shows did not start at the announced times, and some were delayed or unclear, which makes planning your visit frustrating.

Overall, I would rate VinWonders Phu Quoc 3 out of 5 stars. It has good potential and is budget-friendly, but operational issues really affect the experience. I also noticed that many other visitors mentioned that something different seems to be out of order each time they visit, so this appears to be a recurring problem rather than a one-off situation.

If you have been to Disneylan or Universal's then the experience here could be negative. Many things are dated, not well maintained and some rides could be on repair.

Don’t waste time because everything shuts down when it rains and most rides close by 5–6 p.m.There is nothing for younger kids below 100 cm. Very boring for them. Don’t miss the Mermaid Show and the Closing show both are amazing!

Vinwonders is one of the main attractions on Phu Quoc Island. It is an amusement park with multiple zones. I would recommend to bring swimming gear for the water park as the rest of the park is underwhelming. It has an empty, abandoned vibe. Lots of the rides had no attendants operating them (aka closed). The rides that were open had no line, though, which is a plus. There was very little to do with toddlers. Most of the rides had height restrictions of 1M.

The aquarium was large and had lots of displays. We enjoyed the aquarium and regret not bringing swimsuits for the Waterpark as we think it was the main attraction there.

Vinwonders are Vietnamese version of Disneyland (explanation done) Lots of games and attractions free of charge once you are in the park 👍. If you stay at Vinwonders resort and have a park package, you can come straight in without lining up buying tickets.
Important events:
19:00-19:30 everyday - The One show at the castle > this is an excellent show. If you have the park package, it worths watching it every night.
11:00 and 14:00 everyday - Mermaid show
15:00 everyday - fish feeding show
The Phú Quốc location is still being built up. They will have more things by the day. The schedule above could change.

Note: if you stay at one of the resort nearby. They have free electric bus / tuk tuk to take you straight to Vinwonders. If you stay in town, there are free bus taking you there as well, ask your hotel. After The One show, there are only a few free buses taking you to Wonder World and then back into the city. Don’t miss them.

Annoyance: The design of the park is for a colder climate. It doesn’t work here. Coming into the park and you will be heat up with a gigantic glass ceiling. There are not enough shades and big trees in the park. It’s really hot in Phú Quốc sun, and maybe it’s the way the park was built blocking nature breeze which makes it worse. Vin Group: Thanks for Replying but hope you would really check in detail before replying..it was a hot and dry day without rain so there is no reason acceptable on bad weather. Staff at the waterpark can be quite annoying, they try to force you to go on the slides they want instead of where you want to go.
They micromanage the access to everything and I understand they have to observe safety, however this is too much. I have been going to waterparks from before most of the staff was born, I know hot to get on and an inflatable.

Also, what's with the nonsense of weighing people before going down a slide? I went to waterparks all over the world, Europe, USA, UAE for over 40 years, the inflatable rings are the same size in any waterpark you go in the world and nowhere are people asked to weigh before they can go down a slide. If you want to attract international tourists, I suggest you turn off the scales or build stronger structures.

** Edit in response to the Representative:**
The point I am making since it wasn't clear to you, is that your waterpark staff has to stop MICROMANAGING visitors, especially non-Vietnamese visitors. For example, if walking path A and path B are BOTH open and functional and I want to use path A to go where I want to go, they should not insist I use path B. Furthermore, they did not do that with Vietnamese people, they were  bothering only me and my husband the only 2 non-VN people around that afternoon.

Secondly, during our visit they were overwhelmingly annoying interrupting every thing we were doing or not doing like we were sitting on a bench minding our business and they would tell us to go here or there go on the rides etc. Being so invasive  may be suitable for Vietnamese culture, but for western culture is incredibly irritating to feel constantly watched by staff.

Thirdly, I did not say I had problem accessing the water slides myself, I was able to go on those that were open because I am within the height and weight parameters, however, it's again highly annoying and invasive that you do ask people to weigh. I saw several people including Vietnamese feeling uncomfortable, and others may not have the courage to give you feedback but I do.

So to reiterate I would recommend that your staff - specifically at the waterpark - is properly trained not just in health and safety but also being informed that micromanaging visitors is equal to making them feel uncomfortable and unlikely to return.

Secondly if you want to be a place attracting international tourists then be aware of this non-sense about asking people to weigh. Waterparks in other countries where the average weight is double that of Vietnam do not have weighing requirements and they are perfectly safe, so I fail to understand why would you need one yourselves besides the fact that after living in Vietnam I know your country is obsessed with weight and knowing people's business.

Cons- We bought a 2 day ticket with food included, we didn't realize even though it was advertised as a food credit, it was only allowed in 1 buffet restaurant. The buffet was limited times and genuinely the worst buffet I've had, ever! We queued to get in, on entry there weren't any tables available and after asking for help finding a seat we were put on the end of a table with another family, no problem but 3 seats for 5 people isn't cool. When the other family finished they cleaned up and another family was placed in the now spare seats. I basically had to argue to be seated shoulder to shoulder with another family. The food was atrocious, I love Vietnamese food, I love all food, I love buffets but genuinely this was the worst I've ever had.. Genuinely terrible but luckily this was a small part of our great 2 day visit to the park.

Another downside was the height measurement for rides, I fully understand safety rules and agree with them but we were certain my daughter was about 121cm before our trip but according to the rides here she was 115cm at best. This put an annoying dampener on the days, she wasn't allowed on most things especially the water park. I have just remeasured her a few weeks after she's 121-122cms without shoes...

I could talk about the positives of the park all day, it was a great place, we enjoyed it thoroughly, I would have rated it 5 stars if we didn't have the negatives I mentioned above.

Edit: I appreciate the reply 🤙 In regards to the food voucher, we tried to use it in two other food outlets but the vendors told us Buffet only. Probably a language barrier problem.

- the rest of the park total mediocre with nothing really exciting to use

- the ice-cream voucher could only be used at 4 places in the park in different areas, I tried to use is at Viking trader and was sent and different directions by staff (in the Viking area) who clearly had no idea where it was and the fact that we didn’t use it by 5.30pm meant we didn’t use the voucher- total waste of money

- the total lack of English made is very difficult to navigate and at times was frustrating, but staff were nice

- the night show was amazing and worth staying for

But their processes for ticketing are terrible. It took 30 min to process three groups of 4 people each (the day we visited the park was almost empty). Also once you have tickets you have to go through a face recognition system that doesn’t really work and that is unlikely to process your data properly. Staff was overall friendly. Food is not great

Nothing for small kids! They have totally nonsense restrictions for body size, even when kids are accompanied by parents (for example mechanical riding horses where you hold your kid). Many things just are done without much love (a train ride through a deserted area without anything happening there).
It is okey, but not more

Mixed feelings about the park. When we first walked in I was blown away by the park's layout and architecture. It was pretty empty with almost no queues. Later we discovered though quite a few rides were closed due to the maintenance (at least 5 on that day). The biggest disappointment, however, was the fact that the park does not properly cater to the wide audience of 5-7 year old kids. If your kid happens to be below 120cm (which is a majority of kids under 7 years of age) they'll be allowed to go only on primitive kiddie rides. My 6 year old being slightly below 120cm was allowed only on rocking horse and kids gliders plus 2 family rides. The same story in the warer park where every slide was for 120cm+, even the kids zone except the tiny baby slides. Quite frankly this is the first theme park I visited that offers nothing for 100-120cm kids. The only highlight was the turtle shaped aquarium, its exhibits and the mermaid show.
If you're after a water park go to sun world. This has some good rides but a lot of rules I'd save your money the zoo nearby is great

VinWonders has strict height restrictions for children, so be prepared for that.  Also, outside food and drinks are not permitted; your bags will be checked at the entrance.  Inside VinWonders, you'll find a wide variety of restaurants and drink options.

If you plan to enjoy the water activities at VinWonders, be sure to bring your swimsuit and other necessary items. I didn't participate in the water activities myself, so I'm unsure of what specific amenities they offer.

Very good park make sure your kids are over 120cm or they will miss alot.
Reason for 3 star is the kids are not quite safe on the water slides . They don't let parents go down with their kids . They get smashed with water on their way up so they better be confident. U can't tell where they will come out and the life guards are not watching all the time. Be careful. My guess is they have had many accidents here.
Also if Ur kids can swim they will be told they have to wear a life jacket under 140cm. Which is dumb in 40cm of water
Affordable replica of Disney land but lack of magic.

It is an interesting experience.
The moment you get off the free shuttle bus there is no information on how to get to the actual park or any internet connection, so a lot of scammers are around trying to sell you rides. They lie to the customers telling them that there is no bus to the parks, or that it will take a long time to come and that they are missing out on activities. On the way back from the parks, at the very entrance these people are also there, so it is safe to know that the owners know and don't care.
The park itself is mostly a water park, many of the other rides keep on failing or are closed. The prices inside the park vary, a can of coke being 15.000 in some places and 30.000 on others.
I got the free meal ticket and chose one of the only two options across the parks and it was frankly underwhelming, mostly sea food.
It is an experience, a bizarre one: the off brand version of a very well known park.

The park itself is wonderfully built and the end show is great, but that's where it ends..

The lifeguards are so rude, so many rules that you cannot enjoy yourself. I couldn't take my 6 year old in pool as I kept getting shouted at for having fun with him. He cried so much as I had to leave the pool and he was scared. How am I meant to know rules if they can't speak English or any instructions in english. There is not much to do as a family if there are small kid and someone over 140cm, my 11 year old was considered an adult even though she was exactly 140cm. There were 3 power outages during the day, I'm so glad I was not on the big Ferris wheel or another ride. Once we were in the restaurant and it was pitch black but everyone kept eating like it was a normal occurrence. No instructions in English so my daughter who had one button on her bathing suit could not go a rides, how was I meant to know that. They all follow you and mother you, which is so off putting. So many closed attractions for maintenance. The pumps for the water slides went off and we all had stand around waiting for a while which was strange.
The aquarium was good, but everything else was disappointing. Inexpensive compared to international amusement parks, but there is really only one roller coaster. Water park was the worst.

The park itself is okay, feels a little lacking but it might just be over hyped. Nice for a day out.
The problem is largely getting there. There's a free shuttle bus to Grandworld, and from there there's another free bus that goes to both the safari and VinWonders.
When getting of the first bus, there's absolutely no information about the second bus. No sign about it's existence or anything. Instead, people unrelated to it will tell you that the bus doesn't exist, that you're missing shows, that the only way to get there is to get on their taxi. Those scammers are also wearing Grandworld tshirts. A lot of people were scammed this way, and could be easily solved.
Second day I went there, there was a security guard that was actively seeing the scammers scam people and wouldn't do anything.
The buses that go from the attractions to Grandworld were impossible to get once leaving the attractions, as they would tell you they weren't going where their signs actually said they did. Had to wait ages, with more scammers (who were, once again, friends with the bus drivers) trying to get us to take the paid taxis.
For future tourists: patience and a firm no will be needed.
Very empty and speakers played obnoxiously loud random sounds/ music but the go karts were reasonably fun though

Not a huge fan - everything is very spread out and takes a lot of time to get to.If you have trouble walking or have an ailment of some sort with legs, feet, knees  this place is not for you - lots of stairs and bridge crossings and down stairs etc.

Rides are ok but super strict on weight - so if you’re over 75Kgs, or a big chested lady,  then you may miss out on some rides as my wife did. Big chest unable to close the harness tightly so you can’t go on those rides - which are the good ones.

The park was very empty when we were there 20th April 2023 - my wife has knee issues and managed to get on a ride. Was refused to stay on to go again even though no one was waiting - she was asked to get off, walk down stairs - walk all the way around and walk up  flight of stairs to go on again. No discretion given even when they could see her walking stick - I found that a bit careless and rude.

Plenty of food to eat at normal fun park prices which was expected, and three of the main rides were closed.

The light show was really good at the end of the night. If you can get in just for that it’s worth it.

All in all it was ok

Interesting aquarium. Good and extremely roller coaster (only one), few good waterslides. Paid for sun loungers and no natural beach.
I liked this park more than vinpearl phu quoc but current price 900k per person is maximum for that.
Interesting theme park with water park, rides & a massive aquarium.

It’s nice water park and electric games with wonderful shows just if would not need to get scammed don’t take the chattel service with support of driver pay on customer service and get the combo future.
They refuse to give me my future and they say times up then some communicate with me and ask me to go to deferent place to change the future
Aqua park doesn’t have hot water, bring your own towel.

The close time said that 19h30 pm close but at 18 pm nearly all rides are started to close.

Not many people, its calm 🙂
Not so good, but not so bed

Aquarium is good - theme park pretty crap. The waterpark has some good slides but the lifeguards feel like they would have done well in the Stasi

Generalnie to mam mieszane uczucia po wizycie w tym parku z przewagą tych raczej mniej pozytywnych. Po pierwsze na plus to na pewno ceny które może w stosunku do tych w Wietnamie są zawyżone, ale w porównaniu do cen zachodnich jest to wszystko jeszcze wciąż bardzo atrakcyjne. Na początku mamy Europejską aleje gdzie znajduje się sporo różnego rodzaju sklepów w tym także wiele marketów typowo spożywczych z cenami piwa zaczynającymi się od 18 000 VND czy soju za 95 000 VND więc to są ceny praktycznie takie same lub niewiele wyższe od tych które możemy znaleźć gdzieś indziej na wyspie. Niektóre są podwójnie zawyżone, ale to zależy od produktu więc generalnie źle nie jest. Polecam zaopatrzyć się w tych sklepach ponieważ w dalszej części parku ich już nie spotkamy a jakieś picie będzie można kupić tylko w restauracjach. W tychże restauracjach lody na patyku czy też Pepsi mamy po 30 000 VND a mała woda, 0,5 L,  jest po 20 000 VND podczas gdy w marketach na początku duża, 1.5 L, jest w tej cenie. Co do samych atrakcji to niestety dla dzieci poniżej 120 cm jakieś kolejki czy inne karuzele można policzyć na palcach jednej dłoni. Niestety pod tym względem, jak i samej ceny biletu, ten park wypada absolutnie katastrofalnie na tle np. takiego Vinwonders Wonder Park w Ho Chi Minh. Jeśli chodzi o starsze dzieci i dorosłych powyżej 140 cm to niestety tutaj też nie wygląda to najlepiej, przejażdżek jest też niedużo. Park Wodny widać że lata świetności ma już za sobą, ale niektóre przejażdżki są naprawdę przyjemne tak że tutaj też jest mix zadowolenia i konsternacji. W strefie dziecięcej na przykład posadzka pod wodą jest w niektórych miejscach w tak kiepskim stanie że dziecko może się po prostu skaleczyć podczas zabawy. Na koniec zostawiam The Sea Schell bo to jest ewidentnie perła w koronie tego parku. Gigantyczne akwarium oraz cały kompleks robi ogromne wrażenie i tylko za to, plus może jeszcze za kilka atrakcji w Parku Wodnym, zostawiam te 3 gwiazdki.

Купили 2 дневный билет комбо с сафари. Внутри парка все платно - аренда ящика в душевой, полотенца, автобусов по парку.
В душевных нет мыла, ггеля для душа, фенов. Мы не нашли расписания включения волнового бассейна. Полчаса прождали, ушли.
Аттракционы в среднем состоянии, есть хорошие, один аттракцион не запустиллся, людей ссадили, проверяли его потом. Скрип от него был громче визгов людей с соседней катапульта. Очереди от 15 до 45 минут. Фасттрека по факту нет, кто купил - на аттракционах сотрудники им отказали и вернули деньги.
Океанариум очень даже понравился, обязательно посмотрите шоу кормления рыб в 11.00. Пингвины чудесные. А вот шоу русалок совсем не понравилось (в 14.00), там толпа людей с детьми и хаос.
С едой и водой, туалетами на территории проблем нет - продаётся на каждом шагу.
Не совсем ясно, как уехать из Vinwonders до автобусной остановки (17,19 авт и музея Тедди) - зелёные автобусы v1-2-3-4  отказывались сажать людей до станции - мы пытали уехать в 17.00. За полчаса не было транспорта до автобусной остановки. Вьетнамцы-помогалы втридорога предлагают свои услуги (от 100.000 за поездку 4 минуты). Мы уехали на Граб до автобусной остановки и далее в Венецию за 29.000 донгов.
В общем впечатления от парка смешанные, второй раз бы не поехали точно.

Wo soll man da anfangen..

An sich war es trotzdem ein toller Tag, aber das war es auch schon.

Is je kind kleiner dan 1 meter, is het kind gratis maar dan kan je kind ook echt NERGENS in!!! Het pretpark zelf is in een vervallen staat, het is oud en gedateerd. Het enige wat echt top was is het zwembad gedeelte, dit was echt mega groot …

Wir hatten leider nicht alles gesehen weil es an dem Tag stark geregnet hatte aber von dem was wir gesehen hatten war es ganz nett, es gab viele Attraktionen von Schwimmen bis Achterbahn und viele Shops aber es war auch an dem Tag recht wenige Besucher dort. Парк внешне очень красивый и ухоженный, но организация посещения шоу — это полнейшее неуважение к времени и комфорту туристов. Весь ваш день превращается не в отдых, а в бесконечное ожидание в толпе.
Что возмутило больше всего:
Шоу русалок: Идет всего 2 раза в день по 10 минут. Мы пришли за час, и мест уже не было даже стоять. Перед началом в холле набилось несколько тысяч человек. Это было невыносимо: жуткая давка, духота, люди ходят по ногам и буквально сидят друг у друга на головах. В такой атмосфере находиться просто опасно для здоровья, я в итоге ничего не видела и была вынуждена выйти.
Вечернее шоу у замка (в 19:00): Это вообще абсурд. Чтобы занять хоть какое-то место, люди приходят за 2 часа и просто сидят на бетоне с 17:00! Мы пришли в 17:30, и все центральные места уже были забиты. Провести 1.5–2 часа в режиме ожидания на полу ради 20-минутного шоу — это полное упущение организаторов.
Общий вывод: Вы платите огромные деньги за билет, но вместо прогулок по парку вынуждены половину времени проводить в очередях или сидя в ожидании шоу, чтобы увидеть хоть что-то.
Парку нужно либо увеличивать количество показов, либо вводить лимитированный вход на локации. Сейчас это не отдых, а борьба за выживание в толпе. Красивая картинка не стоит потраченных нервов и времени в очередях

Expérience un peu décevante / mitigée

Cependant l’aquarium était incroyable, grandiose ! Il y avait vraiment très peu de monde , on avait beaucoup de temps et d’espace pour admirer.

El parque tiene una infraestructura muy buena y agradable, muchas opciones para comer, muchos baños, y en general está muy limpio. Las atracciones no están al nivel de parques como Disney o Six Flags, son de tecnología más antigua y recorridos más cortos. Pero así y todo creo que es un parque divertido.
Tienen mucho que mejorar en términos logísticos... Las atracciones duran 45 segundos y el personal se demora casi 10 minutos en evacuar los carros y montar a los nuevos pasajeros ( falta personal) lo cual genera filas más largas de lo necesario.
Por otro lado, quería mencionar algunas cosas del parque de agua (hemos recorrido muchos parques de agua incluso en la misma isla de Phu Quoc, tenemos experiencia). El parque de agua de Vinwonders tiene un gran problema que lo hace muy poco disfrutable en familia y muy desagradable por momentos. Para empezar, el locker cuesta el doble que cualquier otro locker en Vietnam. Luego, cada atracción del parque de agua tiene requisitos diferentes de altura, peso, e incluso pesos combinados entre pasajeros. Yo sé que el bot que responde a las reviews va a argumentar razones de seguridad. Pero si un parque tiene tantas restricciones de peso y altura tan específicas y tan diferentes entre las distintas atracciones del mismo parque, significa que fueron mal diseñadas por alguien sin experiencia. El objetivo de un parque familiar es disfrutar en familia y este parque de agua precisamente logra lo opuesto, no permite a las familias disfrutar y recorrer en conjunto, y hay que andar con un manual de anatomía para entender a qué atracciones uno puede subir y a cuáles no... Para poner un ejemplo: hay uno de los toboganes de agua de pendiente suave que requiere un mínimo de 60kg para funcionar...esto deja afuera al 90% de las mujeres asiáticas! Obviamente esa atracción estuvo vacía todo el día en un día de plena temporada alta...
Para completar la locura, todas las atracciones tienen una balanza y pesan a las personas frente al resto de la fila expectante, al igual que se pesan los animales en una feria! (esto probablemente sea ilegal en muchos países). Un gran porcentaje de pre-adolescentes y adultos eran rechazados y debían bajar las escaleras frustrados frente a toda una fila de personas. Realmente esto es lo que busca generar Vinwonders? Reconsideren remodelar el parque de agua y homogeneizar el criterio de admisión de una manera simple y clara tal como lo hace el resto de los parques de agua del planeta.
Este problema también se puede ver el el Viking Village donde hay una atracción de puentes colgantes y tirolesas fáciles pero que requiere una altura de 1,50m. El juego es muy básico y no le atrae a los adultos. Precisamente el público al que le atraen esos juegos miden menos de 1,50m. El resultado? Esa actividad está vacía en plena temporada alta ( semana TET) como se muestra en la foto.
Estos ejemplos que pongo son prueba irrefutable de un mal diseño y mala gestión. Revisen sus estadísticas de uso de los juegos y podrán comprobar esto.
El acuario es espectacular, el show nocturno también es de muy buen nivel. El servicio de carros eléctricos hope on-off del parque creo que es bueno pero por momentos faltan carros y hay que esperar mucho tiempo, deben agregar más carros para la salida de eventos como el del acuario por ejemplo <fila de 25 minutos para subirse al carro eléctrico). Me gustaría realmente ver a este parque bien manejado porque tiene mucho potencial. Ojalá está review pueda llegarle al equipo de operaciones del parque.

我在klook 買了入場加高爾夫車遊走樂園。
最主要是去水族館。幸好包車，因為天氣很熱，走路太辛苦。一入去水族館剛好美人魚表演超多人，魚類多是東南亞魚，那裏也有企鵝餵食套餐，還送了個企鵝公仔不錯。因為老公不游水也不玩機動遊戲，我們只去了摩天輪，內有冷氣還不錯。入口右轉是水上樂園，比機動遊戲更吸引，我見有很直很高的水滑梯，一定很刺激。機動遊戲好似比較舊，我們只去了水族館和摩天輪就走了。由於在樂園在北面，我在Pullman beach resort 坐的士大約45分鐘。樂園和大世界很近，約5分鐘車便到。
建議包車。車不多。

•水族館目的で利用。
•エントランス前の雰囲気すごく可愛い。
•水族館の建物も巨大亀になっておりパフォーマンス◎
•水族館ではペンギン🐧の餌やり体験あり。
しかし、いらないぬいぐるみセット販売になっていて500.000VNDほど取られるのが不快。
拒否したら別の安いぬいぐるみセット販売で提案された。
餌やりを楽しみにしていたこともあり、シンプルに餌やりだけで販売して体験しやすくしてほしい。

其他陸上機動遊戲也偏舊，過山車舊款，勝在不用點排隊，埃及迷城就麻麻，因為太短，無乜離心力，摩天輪就可望晒全園。
水族館適合一家大細，避開太陽的地點，魚種類也一般。無太大驚喜

На экстремальных горках запрет понятен, но он распространяется и на такие активности, как веревочный парк - даже на несложных маршрутах. Мои очки сидят очень плотно и точно не слетают (я много разных активностей провожу в очках), однако правила неумолимы, и я не смогла попасть на эти активности.
В итоге многое осталось недоступным, было скучно и обидно. Заранее на сайте и при входе об этом не предупреждают.
Совет очкарикам:
• Если можете - надевайте контактные линзы.
• Если нет - будьте готовы, что большая часть развлечений будет вам недоступна.
Жаль, что парк не учитывает такие нюансы - хотелось бы большего дружелюбия и доступности развлечений для посетителей с проблемами зрения.

Khu tổ hợp với rất rất nhiều trò chơi, thuỷ cung,.. vô chơi cả ngày cũng không thể chơi hết
Giá vé vào cổng đã bao gồm chơi tất cả các trò chơi và kèm ăn trưa khoảng 120k tuỳ vào giá vé.
Vé người lớn: 950k
Vé người trên 60tuổi (cần CCCD), trẻ em: 710k
Dưới 100cm: free
Phần ăn kèm trong vé được áp dụng tại các quầy, cửa hàng khi vừa vào cổng. Nên khi đi đến khu vui chơi mà đói muốn quay trở ra ăn thì đợi xe điện cũng khá lâu, đi bộ thì rộng quá đi rất mệt nhất là gia đình có người giá, trẻ nhỏ.
Ưu điểm: rộng, nhiều khu trò chơi khác nhau phù hợp mọi đối tượng. Rất nhiều hoạt động cho bạn trải nghiệm nếu bạn đủ sức.
Nhược điểm: quá rộng, quá nhiều khu trò chơi nên không thể chơi hết, dù bạn chỉ muốn chơi/tham quan đúng 1 điểm bạn cũng phải mua vé toàn bộ khu phức hợp này, cảm giác rất phí tiền. Và vì quá rộng nên nhà có người già trẻ nhỏ sẽ phụ thuộc xe điện, nhưng xe điện là phải mua thêm vé riêng, và xe không đủ cho khách, phải chờ khá lâu.
Mục đích duy nhất nhà mình vào đây là muốn cho em bé coi nàng tiên cá 🧜‍♀️
Nhiều khi bước vào mình cảm giác đang đi vào khu xi măng đồ sộ.

Le parc est de taille raisonnable, les decors et attraction sont intéressantes.

Mais, j’ai fait des dizaines parc d’attraction dans plusieurs pays du monde dans ma vie, mais celui là a plusieurs gros problemes pour en faire un parc de classe mondiale.

les files d’attentes dans les attractions sont extrêmements mal gérés, des queues avec peu de monde qui n’avancent pas.

Par exemple le roller coaster un seul wagon circule au lieux d’en avoir 2, avec wagon en gare et un au stop de fin du circuit (ce qui est habituel dans les roller coaster) ce qui diminuerais par 2 la file d’attente. A cela vous ajoutez un personnel assez lent lors de la procédure du chargement et déchargement des personnes (quasiment 4 minutes a chaque fois). Et j’ajoute en plus que plusieurs fois les wagon ne partent pas rempli a 100% j’ai vue plusieurs fois des wagon partir avec 15-20 personnes au lieux de 24 (capacité max du train).

concernant la parti parc aquatique c’est la meme chose pour la gestion des files d’attente avec en plus un bonus désagréable de devoir vous peser a chaque toboggan car il y a un limite minimum et maximum en poids et taille a ne pas dépasser, et qui est different pour chaque toboggans, ce qui en fonction de votre gabari ne vous donnera pas accès a tous le parc.
Egalement du au manque de personnels certains toboggan sont simplement hors services ou sont ouvert de manières alternatives lors des rotations du personnel.

tous ces problèmes chaotique de gestion des files d’attentes, multiplié pour chaque attraction rend le temps d’attente extrement désagréable et me donne au final une sensation de ne pas avoir passé un moment genial.

Enfin je finirais par les différents problèmes techniques de plusieurs attractions rencontré lors de la visite qui a mit plusieurs attractions hors services pendant plusieurs heures.

Choć muszę przyznać że po przejściu obu tych krain stwierdzam że to są najsłabsze atrakcje tego parku rozrywki i można je zostawić na sam koniec jak będzie się miało za dużo czasu.
Pod koniec dnia jest jeszcze pokaz audiowizualny i uważam że jest to najmocniejszy punkt programu. Warto zostać do końca ponieważ robi wrażenie i potrafi osłodzić gorzkie chwile w Vinwonders.

Ponieważ część pasażerów wsiada w autobus który jedzie w przeciwnym kierunku tylko po to żeby tym autobusem wrócić z powrotem więc autobus restauracja jest zapchany pasażerami i próbuję się do niego dop i próbują się do niego dostać inni. Oczywiście biznes na tym robią kierowcy taksówek oraz tuk tuków którzy wożą zniesmaczony klientów Vinwonder do Grand World za 100 000VND
Niestety organizacja transportu po zamknięciu parku jest bardzo kiepsko zorganizowana, a wystarczyłoby po zamknięciu parku puścić 1 autobus  trasie Winwonder - Grand World , który zrobiłby 2 kursy.

В 2014 году я впервые побывала в винвондерс Нячанг. Это был восторг!
В апреле 2024 я вернулась туда с мужем.
И мы были в неописуемом восхищении.

Наш отпуск в декабре был на Фукуоке и мы, конечно, решили посетить парк этой сети.
И это было полное разочарование.
Мы поселились в Grand world, забронировали билеты на официальном сайте.
Билет был в парк+ваучер на еду.
Накануне вечером мы отравились в местном кафе.

С утра нам было не лучше, зашли в приложение чтобы вернуть билеты.
Такой опции нет.
Написали на официальную почту.
Нам ответили что билеты невозвратные.
И максимум нам могут перенести дату нашего посещения.
Но как же так? Даже авиакомпании при наличии справки от врача (а у нас она была) возвращают часть средств.
Через день мы поехали спрашивать на кассе про перенос, так как на почту ничего внятного нам не ответили.
На кассе нам сказали, чтобы мы продолжали писать на почту.
Если не ответят приезжать через пару дней.
Наш отпуск подходил к концу.
Чувствовали мы себя все ещё плохо, но решили сходить в парк. Раз уж деньги нам не возвращают.
В последний день мы приехали на кассу.
Там нам сказали что билет перенесли, но так как наступил 2025 год, то ваучер на еду они из нашего билета убирают.

Вся эта ситуация испортила нам впечатления от острова Фукуок и сети парков.

Касаемо самого парка.
По сравнению с парком Нячанга он нам абсолютно не понравился.

Да тут есть страшные американские горки.
Но на этом собственно все.
Нет игровых автоматов, VR, нет красивых растений, декораций.
Красивый океанариум - единственный плюс. Особенно шоу кормления рыб.
Само здание также восторг - в виде гигантской черепахи.

Но самое главное разочарование - это аквапарк.
Очередь на локер. Платишь деньги, твои вещи убирают и говорят, что ты можешь забрать их всего один раз.
То есть ты не сможешь ответить на звонки, взять воды, купить что-то.
Получается для посещения аквапарка тебе надо брать с собой некатающегося человека, который носил бы твою воду, телефон.
Сами горки скучные. Наверное, для взрослых будет интересна только капсула с убирающимся полом.

Перед каждым заездом взвешивают.
Первый раз вижу такое.
Ну понятно если двое на тюбинге и суммарный вес не должен превышать определённое количество кг.
Но можно же на вид понять что человек меньше 120 кг в большинстве случаев.
Нет, взвешивают всех: детей, стройных девушек.
Это создаёт огромные очереди.

Последняя надежда была на колесо обозрения.
Но там не работала вентиляция и мы просто задыхались.

В общем, три звезды только за океанариум и американские горки.

Не написано с каким весом и каким ростом можно на аттракционы, простаиваешь очередь , а потом не подходишь по росту или по весу. Платные кары на территории, такое сложилось впечатление, что платно всё, даже лежаки в аквапарке и те платные.
Khu vui chơi rộng đẹp, nhiều trò chơi hay , nhưng chỉ đẫn và hỗ trợ du khách kém, nên đi bộ nhiều mệt và nắng, sẽ không phù phù hợp với người già và những người sợ nắng 😝 …

Аквапарк ок, остальное сомнительно. На шоу русалок давка, не хватает разграничения потоков людей. Внутри душно и узко, стоять особо негде, помещение не рассчитано на большое количество людей, жирный минус за отсутствие организации. Очередь на лодку в деревне викингов не двигалась вообще. Повсюду стоишь в очередях, которые еле двигаются.

In terms of hardware, there are fewer amusement rides suitable for children. The aquarium restaurant has no lifts or elevators, so people have to walk up a lot of stairs or ramps, which is very inconvenient for the elderly. The water park facilities are obviously inferior to Sunworld's Aquatopia Water Park. There are only showers and toilets, and no changing rooms. The lockers are not self-service. There are about ten lines, but only five staff members. I didn't know which line I should line up in. When it was finally my turn after waiting for a long time, the female staff member actually chose to serve the customers who had just arrived in the next line. I immediately got furious, which may be related to racial discrimination. Other software issues: It was extremely inconvenient to purchase park tickets online, and all the staff didn't know where to pick up the complimentary ice cream. The redemption location was not clearly marked, so we had to queue up and ask before we got an answer. There were not many people waiting in line for the rides themselves, but they had to rest for at least five minutes after each start. The staff and the people waiting in line were just wasting time.

硬件上，適合孩童的機動遊戲較少；水族館的餐廳沒有升降機及電梯，須步行大量樓梯或斜道，對長者帶來極大不便；水上樂園設施則明顯被Sunworld的Aquatopia Water Park比下去，只有沖身間及洗手間，連更衣室也沒有，儲物櫃不是自助性質，有大概十列隊伍，但只有五名工作人員，不知應該排哪一列，而當我等候多時終於輪到我的時候，女工作人員居然選擇接待旁邊隊列才剛剛來到的顧客，我當即大發雷霆，當中可能與種族歧視有關。其他軟件問題：現場要網上購買遊園車票，購買極為不便，而所有工作人員又不知道附送的雪糕應到哪裡領取，換領地點本身又沒有清楚標示，我們要排隊輪候詢問才得到答案；機動遊戲本身輪候人數不多，但居然每次發動後要休息最少五分鐘，工作人員及輪候人士都在乾等浪費時間。

Nous y sommes allés un jour de pluie avec 2 enfants de 5 ans et 8 ans.
Les enfants ont passé une bonne journée.
Pour nous adultes plus de la moitié des attractions étaient fermées à cause de la pluie (ou manutention)
Ce n’est pas grave car on était avec les enfants. Mais c’est plus embêtant si vous y allez entre adultes.
Il y a pas énormément d’attractions pour les tous petits c’est aussi dommage.

On a pas testé la partie aquatique car il pleuvait mais elle paraît vraiment bien. Nous y retournerons si le temps le permet.

Mais le gros point fort de ce parc c’est son aquarium (la grosse tortue) : nous y avons passé beaucoup de temps et les enfants ont vraiment aimé le spectacle des Mermaids.
Ça nous a bien occupé pendant cette journée de pluie.

Autour du parc il n’y a pas grand chose : que de faux bâtiments non occupés. C’est triste pour la biodiversité de l’île.

Pour résumer : une bonne surprise. Un parc beau, propre, avec du personnel sympathique (mais qui ne parle pas anglais)

Lần đầu tiên đến Vinwoner 10h00. Cảm giác đầu tiên đẹp, rộng, thoáng, nhưng nóng khủng khiếp, toàn bê tông hóa nhiều.
Điểm chê :
- Không gian bên ngoài bê tông nhiều, cây xanh ít, nên rất nóng
- Không có khu vực gửi đồ, đem theo vali không có chỗ gửi.
- Bên trong không có xe đưa đi như Suối Tiên, đi bộ mỏi chân lắm luôn.
- Lối đi đến các điểm tham quan rất như ma trận, ngoằn nghéo, dùng app thì bị đơ, hỏi thăm mới ra được. Mỗi góc đường nên có bản đồ cho mọi người dễ đi.
- Không cho đem thực phẩm vào : đem vài chai nước suối thì được. Bên trong bán giá rất đắc đỏ , cụ thể :
+ Bún quậy : 100k/tô, tô đặc biệt 150k/tô.
+ Cửa hàng cơm bên trái (lối vào) : tô cá kho 200k
- Cho nên các bạn nhớ đem theo đồ ăn nhanh cho gọn, hạn chế tối đa mua đồ ăn trong này, giá rất cao. Nhiều người sẽ nói, đi chơi mà sợ tốn kém, xin thưa, nếu dịch vụ ăn uống trong này, giá cả hợp lý, thì chúng tôi không chỉ mua nhiều và còn nhiều hơn nữa, người đi chơi thoải mái hơn. Vin nên xem lại vấn đề này. Giá vé đã cao ngất trời 950k/người lớn.
- Bên trong khu vui chơi nước : gửi đồ 50k/túi sách cở học sinh, còn to hơn thì 100k dành cho túi quá khổ : như vali size 18 trở lên. Hôm bữa đem 4 cái, mất 400k ngọt sớt,kakkk quá đắt. Chưa nói, lỡ để quên đồ trong túi gửi, vào lấy và gửi lại sẽ tính giá từ đầu. Chặc quá đẹp luôn.
- Ghế bố thuê : 50k/2 tiếng.
Tôi mong Vinwonder nên xem lại vấn đề này :
1/ Giá hàng dịch vụ ăn uống
2/ Nên có chỗ gửi đồ bên ngoài : free
3/ Ghế bố : free.
4/ Các chương trình vui chơi đều free hết
5/ Nên tăng cường cây xanh các lối đi, nắng khủng khiếp
------------
Kinh nghiệm : Nên đi từ sáng sớm cho đến tối 22h00 về thì mới mong vui chơi hết trong này. Chú ý : người lớn tuổi không nên vào, lãng phí, vì đi xa, không có xe đưa đi bên trong, mỏi chân lắm.
Cái hay là mọi người nên dùng xe VinBus Free.
+ Từ trung tâm --> Bãi xe T2 và ngược lại.
+ Muốn qua Grand Wonder ngắm cảnh, có xe điện Free : nên ghé 16h00 đến 22h00 thì đẹp. Ban ngày không nên đi, nắng khủng khiếp.
+ Muốn qua Vinwonder : xe vinbus sẽ chạy từ Bải xe T2 --> Khu Safari --> Vinwoner.
+ Về trung tâm : Bải xe Vinwonder --> Bãi xe T1 -->Bãi xe T2. Từ bãi xe T2 ---> về lại Trung tâm PQ.

Kính gửi quý Công ty,  Tôi gửi Mail này đến quý Công ty để cho quý Công ty biết là đã có những trường hợp gây cho khách hàng không vừa lòng. Trường hợp của tôi là:
Rõ ràng phiếu tặng ghi có giá trị tới 18h mỗi ngày vậy mà khi chiều nay ngày 06/7/2024 hai bạn nhân viên không cho tôi đổi trong khi thời gian tôi lại quầy là 17g40. Tôi đã có nói là rõ ràng khi tôi nhận phiếu người phát phiếu có nói là thời hạn đến 18h, nhưng hai bạn nói 17g30 là ngừng không cho đổi nữa và kiên quyết mặc dù tôi đã có nói rõ

nice, large amusement park for adults.  We had hoped for a few more rides for children, since that's what amusement parks are for, after all.  To use the rides, you have to be at least 140 cm tall. Otherwise, it's best to go straight to enjoy the water park with the little ones but be aware that most of the slides allow kids from 120 cm and taller.  It's 2026 and there are no baby changing facilities.  Some of the rides are old and need maintenance or renovation, but the "Wrath of Zeus" roller coaster was the absolute highlight.  We didn't use the water park because it closed at 5:30 pm.  The aquarium is all right , but the building itself is truly impressive. I recommend buying tickets online, including buggy rides because it can get quite exhausting walking around.

There weren’t many people, so our kids could enjoy the rides with zero queues. In December, the weather still gets hot after 9:30 a.m., so please check the forecast before booking your vacation.  Final verdict: if you’re coming with a group of around 7 or more, you’ll have a great time because you can ride as much as you want. That said, for me, a bit of a crowd adds to the fun atmosphere—I’d personally prefer a place with more visitors.

Vinwonders looks great, but there are not that many cool rides! As my 13-year-old said, the cafes and shops far outnumber really good rides. One of the roller coasters was really good, and there was a shooting game/ride, but the volcano one was shut for repairs, and there was not much else. We ended up going to the water park which had great slides, and so it was easy to see why it was crowded in there but not at the actual theme park itself. Bring swimsuits!

Therefore, I do not recommend anyone to buy the tickets via Klook instead of the official web site, as it really does not value.   The worst experience in the VinWonders was in the The Gateway Restaurant, no matter the hygiene of the food and environment, they were super terrible. There were many flies around all the food, the hot food is cold and the furnitures were very very dirty. I advise the management of VinPearl to treat this problem seriously and I recommend them to try a meal there to experience it. It does affect the customer experience very much.

Also - the biggest disappointment is that there are no water coolers around. That is basic requirement from any theme part.  Overall - the park is average

I travelled with my small kids ( 6 years old and 1 year old ) my wife Was in Line with kids he first spoke rudely with her that kids are not allowed and we politely told him yes we know we are just in a queue and we will take a ride one after another . Then after my ride was done I very politely told him that I Am joining my kids as my wife will now take the ride as she has been waiting with kinds and please consider me going from within there instead i take a long exit route . At this time he started howling and yelling at me , probably he didn’t understand English or what, but this is not how anyone can be treated especially when my wife or I were not breaking any queue . We did the same thing in so many other rides and they were quite respectful with the idea of us waiting as it should have been but this particular guy was one heck of an ill mannered person not meant for an amusement park surrounded by kids and families. Travelled on 28th may .
