As a former south Vietnamese citizen, the museum does depict aspects of history, but it is very one sided and every piece in this museum was deliberately included to portray a very specific message: anti-American.

We came here as part of a tour. The rest of our trip was fantastic but I didn't see the point of this place. What's the point of gawking at the horrors of the past when we can't change the horrors in the present? It just demonstrates how little we have learned, which is depressing. The exhibition is also extremely biased, as you would expect. There's even a room called 'Historical Truths' - as if there is such a thing. I didn't like it at all - thought it was ugly and pointless.

Lots of photos of the Vietnamese being tortured, killed and suffering from the effects of agent Orange inflicted by the US and its allies. There was no mention of the torture, mutilation and inhuman treatment committed by the Vietcong. I got sick to my stomach because of what I did not see. After our visit, there were groups of “street folks” near the exit of the museum openly enjoying the distress of people who were obviously moved by what they had just experienced. If you want to know more about the war, get a book on the subject and read it in the quiet comfort of your air conditioned hotel room.

The war is about Capitalism vs Communism just like in the Korean peninsula. This time Communism won. War is brutal and dirty on both sides. This museum only shows one side of it. Most of the people in Vietnam do not care much about this war, only the old and hardcore communists are still hanging on to this victory and bitter against the USA and their own compatriots in the south.
Slanted history. But the winners get to write history so why should I not understand. If love old propaganda great place.

We recently visiting this museum. We were warned to expect an "in your face" experience. And it certainly was. My husband, who spent 20 years in the US Army, and I had to leave after about 20 minutes. Some of the displays are very hard to stomach, especially the one about John McCain---saying he was an "honored guest of Vietnamese government". That's when we walked out.

So the tanks and the chinook etc are nice to look at and draw you in. But my goodness this place is full of crap! It’s audacious the lines they have spun about the war in this ‘museum’. It’s embarrassing to admit I paid to read this stuff! There is no denying the horrors and atrocities committed by the US in the Vietnam war - they don’t need exaggerating (they’re bad enough as it is) and they’d be more accurately and soberingly understood if there was literally any inkling of the role of Vietnam in this war (the instigation, their own atrocities etc). The entire exhibit is titled ‘aggression by the US against Vietnam’ with many injuries and stories depicted with their own ‘twist’ on what might be happening ... They described the US POW as living in ‘serene comforts’ when in the ‘Hanoi Hilton’ ... tells you it all really doesn’t it.

We left this museum upset...our worse experience in Vietnam. All the exhibits are one sided communist anti-American propaganda....spreading hate towards Americans.  They do not tell the truth about what happened. The exhibits make the VC seem like angels and saviors of the Southern Vietnamese people. In reality the VC were the aggressors / attackers and the American were only trying to protect the good people of Southern Vietnam from aggressive communist control.

This attraction is a very graphic Vietnamese biased account of the Vietnam war. As an ex serviceman I found this very upsetting and felt it was just an account of any atrocity inflicted on the Vietnam people whilst trying to portray fair treatment to the American POWs I know there were attributes that the Americans did inflict but not at every turn as this attraction tries to portray. The use of dioxins (agent orange) was also horrendous but I felt to keep using this as a reason for every malformed birth in Vietnam now is unjust. I would not recommend this visit to anyone as it is very biased and graphic

We know very well man's inhumanity to man. We have seen and heard of many cases to prove this. Why would anyone wish to view in grapghic detail such attrocities. Shameful. Needless to say I did not enter and was actually shocked that people could rate this as 'interesting'. I rest my case!

please avoid , it is all about the horror of the war, in many pictures, and mostly what the agent orange did, generations later it is still ongoing, it is telling the world what the americans did to them, you walk into one room and you want to get out immediatly!!
Không có nội dung

This is a propaganda mess in the disguise of a museum by the Vietnamese government. Sole purpose is to shock and influence American and European tourists (I didn't see many Vietnamese here) with disturbing photos. The quotes are out of context, the photos of questionable sources, (photos could document any of the combatants actions but only single out the Americans ), the "photo journalist" quoted have an anti American bias, and there is a collection of military junk at the entrance. Fake museum .

We went to the museum hoping to learn more about the war and what happened. However we went in and their were mainly pictures and captions as to what is happening on the picture. Their were really graphic pictures of the effects of orange acid and feel this was a bit too much. We came away not really enjoying the experience and felt we maybe wasted some of our time as we could probably get more understanding of the war through the internet.
We went through the first room and read about the slaughter of an entire village and we were so upset we left. Although we’re Canadians and didn’t fight in the war, we lived through it.
War is horrible. Extremely graphic photographs of one side of the story. No mention of the US lives lost to help prevent the communists. Hard to give much credit when many facts are distorted
If you are an American be warned this is full of exhibits that are very mean towards Americans. If you know a vet I would not bring them in there
Es una visita obligada, por historia. Pero es poco interactivo y muy fome. Muestran muchísimas fotos de lo que paso y de lo que paso posterior a la guerra (muy fuertes imagines).
No need for a museum like this with hoorible foto's etc ; do not visit it it is a real tourist trap !

Cannot recommend this Museum, full of lies and false information about the United States and what happen during the Vietnam War. If you hate the United States you will like this place.  Interesting fact all the military hardware they have, planes, tanks, trucks, are from the United States..... so they hate us lie about Americans and use our hardware to make money. Places leaves a bad taste in my mouth. We as part of a tour group two times the second time I stayed in the van and did not even enter.

This museum just tells one side of story! I saw people inside the museum looked so sad and don't let it ruin your holiday mood! Some pictures tells how US torture Vietnamese people (not Viet soldiers!)..I don't think they are all true. They never mentioned 58220 US soldiers died there!
Small and uninteresting: Nothing to see but some small war objects, parts of shot down plains and helicopters, in an old communist building. Don't bother. waist of time

War is a terrible thing for all involved. I couldn't stop thinking about the 58,000 young boys we lost in that war and couldn't stomach the propaganda and hate toward Americans depicted in this museum. Would not recommend

If you love planes tanks and guns excellent. They had a photo of an American solder tor hiring a Vietnam man in a jail location they then later used the same phone to show how they tortured people when American solders distorted a village. Not that I don't believe the place instances occurred but as a museum it lost its credibility for me.

Looks up the names of what this museum used to be called before deciding to visit. Very anti-American so be warned. Grim pictures on the walls, not sure I'd have let me.kids see them when they were young. Wouldn't go back personally.

Too bad that they can't make a museum that tells the complete story, the story they tell paints the north Vietnamese army as a mixture of Gandi and Mandela, poor victims, they did not do anything. Museum is full of propaganda.
I was not to impressed with the museum. Mostly a propaganda museum against the US. I would pass on this as there are many more interesting places to visit in Saigon.

Para mi gusto fue una desilusión. Desde que entras hasta que sales solo te enseñan desgracias (que es evidente que las hubo), pero nos hubiera gustado que estuviera mezclado con un poco de contexto histórico.
Es un simple museo de fotografías del horrorizado de la guerra, todas ellas vistas ya en periódicos, revistas y telediarios.., no merece la pena

The museum has some good artefacts of machinery from the war, but overall it is mainly made up of photos showing casualties/atrocities and aftermath due to the American bombing/use of chemical war fare.  It completely ignored the atrocities caused by the North Vietnamese and Vietcong.  This museum is not an unbiased reflection of the war. Nor does it provide many details of the timeline/events of the war.

Totally bad museum a typical communistic museum and they tell only the badside from.america as the agressor. So dont need to go too. A lot of shocking photos without warning signs for those who dont want to see or for children. Its a waste of money
Fotos fotos y más fotos. La historia contada a su modo. Un calor asfixiante. Intento de adoctrinar al turista. No recomendable

HCMC's War Remnants Museum does not live up the expectation of a fair unbiased presentation. It is largely regurgitated (boring) propaganda of the Viet Cong, HCM's leadership and a few details of some events and places that glorify their achievements. In terms of war remnants, the visitor is basically challenged to find much of interest or significance. My prediction is that at some future point (not soon) this place will be renamed and reworked, possibly documenting the south's evolution and embrace of the system. For the time being, tourists and visitors can find other points of interest, including the wise preservation of many parks and gardens.
This is a very poor museum in that it portrays a distorted view of the war in a disturbingly graphic nature.
The Communists utilize propaganda stating that they captured all the American aircraft. However they lie to the public. The American aircraft were abandoned due to lack of space on carriers.

I was trying to do a rough count of the museums I have been to over the course of my life, I am sure over 500 at this point, and this place is top five worst "museum" experiences. Its not a museum. Its a building with some photographs, some information, and mostly misinformation. It is amazing to see a propaganda center with nothing but photographs and a few army artifacts in the courtyard brand itself as a museum now 'informing' the masses about a major war. Nothing but rooms and rooms of blow up replica photos aiming primarily to shock and upset the viewer, added bonuses like a whole floor on agent orange, with scientifically impossible inheritance traits and attributions. Wrong stats, missing stats, conveniently deleted historical facts...... Yes, the topic is a war that was horrifying and no one argues perpetrators have endless shame to bear for it -at many levels- for the rest of history - but impact of victim status is harmed with institutions such as this. This place reminds of communist-era or current dictator-era propaganda in any given despot-ruled land, at its very worst. Maybe old habits die hard.  If you care about history, do not go here. If you care about information accuracy, do not go here. If you want to learn, read a few books - and select careful sources if you are worried you won't get the 'real' stories-- but just give this place a skip.  I find that this sort of place best suits those with a simple-minded approach to history, easily moved by a few photos, having a thirst for shock value over exact historical fact, If you prefer feeding a preformed ideology -then by all means, this may be the place for you. But the Vietnam War is a far more complicated story, and deserves a far more careful and in depth 'museum' in the country that was ravaged by it.  Awful waste of time.

Sinänsä kalusto oli mielenkiintoinen, mutta melko poliittisesti ladattu ”museo”. Toivottavasti voitaisiin myös tuoda esille koko sodan kauheudet. Ei vain toisen puolen raakuudet ja pahuudet. Näyttäisi haavat olevan vielä levällään. Toivottavasti paranee. Vahva ei tälle museolle, mutta toosaalta ei täällä nyt nähtävää ole niin mahdottomasti. Eli vahva ehkä.

Exhibits of aircraft are inaccurate. A37 should be marked in Vietnamese, not USAF. Skyraider is miss marked. Indoor exhibits are all on student anti war and other horse droppings.  The Vietnamese should own up to their war crimes and atrocities. There is little truth in this place.

If you do not like confrontation a brutal war photo's this is not for you, If I had my choice again I would not attend. Propaganda alive and well and no consideration to other Countries who fought in the war. Give it a big miss.

I was prepared for a different perspective on the Viet Nam War when I went to the War Remnants Museum in Ho Chi Minh City. However, there isn't much perspective in this museum. It consists mainly of photographs on the walls with brief explanations, but no continuity or context. Many of the photos were difficult to see due to the glare of the lights off of the glass. I was very disappointed.

Museo che racconta la storia della guerra del Vietnam... Lo consiglierei, anche se mal organizzato, ma non accetto che i “malformati” nati così (purtroppo) dalle conseguenze delle bombe chimiche vengano messi a vendere souvenir all’interno del museo, oppure ad esibirsi come scimmiette...ma con quale scopo?! Ve lo dico io: quello di raccogliere soldi! Avrei preferito che il tour si concludesse con la sola visione dei loro lavori fatti all’interno delle associazioni e dove è anche possibile lasciare un contributo. Ho visto la speculazione sulla pelle di queste persone! Questo è il mio pensiero...

A very mediocre presentation of outdated weaponry and a very inaccurate presentation of events. Given that during the Vietnam war American & South Vietnamese forces have committed terrible war crimes, the presentation is heavily tinted by one-sided propaganda which completely ignores the inhuman cruelty of the North Vietnamese and the atrocities they have committed. Avoid visiting at all costs!

poor primitive displays which do not tell the complete story of the war or in a logic order. It is a small collection of very interesting photos. The building has many rats and the roads outside are very dangerous. There is a lack of rule of law, Or enforcement of basic traffic rules in HCMC. People do not stop at red lights, drive in the wrong direction and on the pavement. Very dangerous area with many pick pockets and thieves on bikes.

Our guide was not allowed in incase he aid the wrong thing. This gives a very slanted perspective of 'the American War'. personally felt that actually capitalism has certainly triumphed over communism in this sad city.

Sicuramente toccante e doveroso da visitare per capire le atrocità e la brutalità della guerra e dell’animo umano accecato, non grande, sono raccolte fotografie e bacheche di armi... manca un po’ di coinvolgimento che ti fa rallentare il veloce scorrimento delle foto
Tellement peu sur un sujet si large. Aucune cohérence entre les expositions. 90% du temps ce ne sont que des photos et du texte, cela revient au même si on prenait un livre à lire sur le sujet.

Make love not war. Very hard to see what war does to people. Nobody is the winner. The displays were very graphic, but well done. There are happier places visit so if you are already informed and opposed this war, go,to the happy places instead.
The best amongst the museum in Ho CHiMin, you need sometime to read the history and caption. Quite busy!

This is just a propaganda outlet for anti American and communist enthusiasts. They just printed random numbers and hung on the walls, No real facts or actual sources are provided. Don’t waste your time on this. It’s not serious.

Way too graphic for us, so we'll rate it 1 star. Fortunately, my college deferment kept me out of Vietnam in the early 70s and it's terrible to see what we did to the Vietnamese people and their country. It also has a definite communist slant, so be prepared if you're an American. We would NOT recommend seeing this museum.

If you are American, you're in for a scalding. Prepare yourself: this is a shame-blame exercise. Very poor information about the actual historic facts. But you can still take your picture in front of a Apocalypse Now type of chopper... I would skip it, no need to go see what we all already know. Go to the fine arts museum instead!
Communist propaganda with some harrowing photos

Museum is meaningful, BUT this is so one-sided against Americans and I'm a Canadian, that it was a turn off. Show two sides to the story and we'll be back to learn so much. In the meantime, don't go. It's communistic propaganda!

As I expected (had to go as it was part of the tour) this is the most one-sided war museum I have ever seen. It still goes on and on and on about US 'war crimes' without providing much evidence. Yes, I know My Lai did occur and other terrible things were done but there is absolutely NO mention at all of the atrocities committed by the Vietcong and N Vietnamese against S Vietnamese people - the village massacres, the city cafe/restaurant bombings, etc. It's a waste of time and an insult to anyone who is even vaguely aware that in war bad things are carried out by both sides.

I hate that Vietnam defines itself as a war victim. They have culture, history, architecture, and art that they seem to ignore. They have a good coffee shop. The mock prison reminds me of concentration camps in Europe.
Todos los países han sufrido guerras. No soy partidario de vender los malos recuerdos como un espectáculo.
Not for the faint of heart. The Vietnam War revisited through Vietnamese eyes. Graphic photos and some US tanks, helicopters, etc.

Only information of the USA losses is printed and informed. No information about the north Vietnam is mentioned at all. So sorry that all this information about what happened on the other side is hide.
Las salas de fotografias son demasiado crueles y de mal gusto. Hay maneras más adecuadas de mostrar la barbarie que fue la guerra.

A pesar de un calor horrible ya que funcionaba el aire acondicinado, las salas solo tenían ventiladores y en la calle se estaba a 34º, En el museo solo habían fotografías de calamidades de la guerra, a mi entender propaganda de partido, cualquier libro o consulta en Internet te facilita mas información y mas objetiva. No merecía la pena esta visita, hay lugares mejores que visitar. solo el patio vale la pena tienen material abandonado por el ejercito Americano, como botin.

Propaganda Museum would be a more apt title. It's a building full of photos of war atrocities with very little in the way of context or history. It's disorganized and frankly -amateurish. I went in with hopes of learning something about the Vietnamese people, their civil war, and our regrettable role in it. I left having learned none of the above. Go to the Reunification Palace instead.

Having a two year old we headed off to the war museum for opening time. We didn't bother with any tours & took taxi from the hotel. If you take Vinasun taxis they are metered clean & very reasonable. Entry is much cheaper than a tour and you can wander at your own pace. I enjoyed this history experience though felt queezy towards the end & a little tearful, so be prepared. Also be mindful that the history is from The Vietnamese viewpoint & they do not show the "other" side, in terms of what they did to the Americans. Their are always two sides of a story. We needed to sit & have a cool drink at the coffee shop after a couple of hours of wandering through the museum which was clean and had great service. The toilets were clean.
Obwohl die Geschichte nich vergessen werden darf, ist die Ausstellung leider nicht mehr aktuell. Ein "Facelifting" mit modernen Ausstellungstechnologien ist dringend nötig.

maalesef kötü bir savaşın kötü anlatıldığı bir müze. Çok bakımsız. Bu kadar yakın dönemde bu kadar film çekilmiş bir tarihi ancak bu kadar kötü anlatabilir ve müze haline getirebilirsiniz. Gitmenize hiç gerek yok. Hapishane bölümünde tavanda bulunan yarasalar görülmeye değer .
ベトナム人友人に連れていかれた。彼も初めてだったみたい。ショックを受けてた。外に置いてある戦車や飛行機はいいけど、建物の中の展示はあまり良いものではない。というか見る気がしない。悲惨なものを見たいマニア以外は行かなくて良いと思う。

The sole reason I came here was because my sister dragged me here. I've never really been a huge fan of war museums. Much as the purpose of such museums serves to educate civilians of wartime atrocities and to propagate peace, there exists a fine line between an educational museum which enlightens visitors, and one which sends visitors scuttering away in disgust and terror.  This museum felt like a slipshod compilation and exhibition of explicitly gory images aimed at inciting nationalism in its citizens and furthering government propaganda. There was no warning about how graphic these images would be, and I think it is only right that visitors be warned of it so that people who have a low tolerance for gore can give this place a wide berth. Besides, it felt like little thought was given to how the idea of the importance of avoiding war was to be imparted. If the idea was to 'gorify' visitors into supporting the idea of avoiding war, then I'm not sure this is the most effective way.  The random shops selling unrelated trinkets and souvenirs also seemed to have downplayed the solemnity and importance of such a museum. Overall, more should be done to improve the organisation of the exhibition, and also to preserve the integrity of this museum. Potentially a constructive concept, but was destroyed by poor organisation.
Aucun intérêt à part les appareils à l'extérieur du musée. Je ne comprends pas l'intérêt des salles suivantes

Very one sided view of history. The Vietnamese need to move on as they have in other aspects of their culture. Atrocities they site by the US during the war are no different than what they committed. Did not enjoy the propaganda thrown out by the guide.

Very disappointing one sided attempt to make America the cause of all the countries troubles. The "Vietnam" war was a civil war between the north and the south and unfortunately was , as most wars appear to be, littered with inconceivable atrocities way before the Americans were involved, yet no mention of any of that here, it's almost portrayed that they were having a "good clean war" before the Americans turned up. You're better off saving your money and going on a guided tour of the city and get some more accurate unbiased information on Vietnam's history !!

We went here thinking it was a war history museum but so wrong! A tank and helicopter in the garden and a couple of exhibits upstairs. The building was poorly maintained and they charged you to take photos, not that there was anything to photograph. Definitely not worth your time!

I am a Viet Nam combat veteran May 23 1968 to April 18 1970. This museum is very graphic and as I expected very one sided. There are many better places to go that are as one sided but not nearly as graphic.

In een land als Vietnam wil je naast de schoonheid ook de pijnlijke kant van de geschiedenis van dit land meekrijgen. Daarom gingen we naar het War Remnants museum. Het was helaas zeer teleurstellend. De achtergrondinformatie was zeer beperkt en eenzijdig. Alleen via heel veel foto's kon je zelf proberen de essentie en achtergronden te destilleren. Ik zou iedereen eerder aanraden eeen goed boek erover te lezen om een goed beeld te krijgen van deze vreselijke oorlog.

This wasn't a place for me, seeing the death and gore of the war. The place doesn't have air conditioning in the main areas, expect it to be hot. Very quickly got bored of the propaganda. Surprised America hasn't asked for its war hardware back. See for yourself and decide. Have to pay to get in. Good way to make money out of death.

This was a "must-visit" on my trip. However it turned out to be a photo collage of horror with graphic images of deformed children. As an American I was disgusted and saddened by the perspective of the museum. Not recommended for children.

Keeping in mind that Vietnam is a communist country that heavily filters the information it's people can access, the museum is still stunningly one-sided. Being accustomed to museums in the U.S. where the goal is to actually educate people and get them to think of the complex and multi-faceted perspectives, this museum gives only the communist perspective and not even all of that.

I went here as part of a tour, hated it and left early. That said, if you want to understand more about the war, then it was well laid out and, I suspect, informative - albeit somewhat biased from what I saw. I prefer to look to the future in a positive light and not dwell on a depressing past.
I didn't like the war remnants museum Not a place to go for Vietnam veterans Did not feel comfortable with the way they present the museum
I totally expected more from this. We arrived and saw a lot of photographs that had assumptions about what was in the photo. Okay to see but not recommending to anyone who is short on time.
The visit was not educational it was all about horrible pictures and big numbers without a lot of explanations.
The truth was not told, a weak country needs to blame a fault of another alliance for their own failures.
The victors write history as they see it. No mention of the atrocities committed by the other side. Certainly depressing.
There's nothing much to see in here. There's a lot of people here, but nothing much to do. Don't waste time.

The museum is a 3 story office building surrounded by war era tanks, planes & helicopters there are rooms of poor quality exhibits (mainly pictures) with very authotitarian text ok for teaching schoolchildren about the victors version of the war but nothing more i would call in if passing but would not make a special journey
This is a waste of time not more than half hour to go inside but if someone like to see one side of the story the way Vietnam show it go
The war remnants museum is VERY one sided and NOT EVEN CLOSE to being accurate, don't waste your time

I thought this was rather boring. Certainly wouldn't be going back here. What made it worse were he dodgy rickshaw drivers that give you a price than try to scam you for more after the ride. Awful experience

This place is not only a money scam, it is a waste of time. Go look at walls covered in the same photos you can see on the internet for free. Look a helicopter and a tank you can see from the street for free, and you're not aloud to take photos or this junk just in case there are time travelers for the mid 1960s trying to sneak information back in time about complete junk.

Just couldn't stay. Regardless of outcome I cannot chose to accept the level of atrocities that humans can inflict on each other. I felt I was in a mass funeral with so much sadness on everyone's faces - my heart was too heavy to stay 💜

I spent 15 minutes here and had to leave. While it is VERY well documented and offers a WEALTH of information, it made me feel sick, sad and heartbroken for what happened in Vietnam...and what continues to happen in Syria today...but different people. I learned a lot in the short time I spent here (I had a bit of an emotional break-down) and it wasn't made any better by watching people pretend to shoot and play with guns and weaponry from the war with big smiles on. This is NOT a happy place and if you want to go, go with the intention that you'll make an effort to ensure this doesn't happen again. And prepare yourself for the emotional visit.

Inutile de vous dire que cette visite vous plombera le moral et bien que cela fasse parti de la culture du pays, ce musée n'est pas une visite incontournable! Il y règle une atmosphère pesante et si oppressante, l'histoire est lourde et chargée d'horreur de guerre: juste le savoir suffit pour des vacances, le voir est peu supportable!
Very sad place to be,after watching the framed pics on the wall.The aftermath of The Vietnam war.Nit for the faint heart.
War is war and it all stinks. Over priced and the people are beyond rude! Shameful to try to charge 7 USD for a water.

We visited as part of a rather poorly done, mid priced city tour. Could not have been more scheptical about a really one sided view of what those awful US poeple did to us, mostly in grainy still pictures. Have seen lots of US military equipment before and could not wait to leave. The high point for us was the cafeteria had really teriffic chocolate milk shakes that we took 1/2 hour to savour.

The Museum is interesting.. It depicts a very biased opinion of the war with great emphasis placed on the bravery and intelligence of the Vietnamese people and the blundering and ineptness of the Americans. The Museum is in Vietnam so I expected that. It contained a very poor collection of war remnants and was really crowded on the day I attended.

sehr trocken und nicht so informativ bzw. in stand gehalten wie wir gehofft haben. Nach den ganzen positiven Bewertungen die wir gelesen haben, dachten wir dass uns ein interessantes und informatives Museum erwartet. Dies war leider nicht der Fall. Da es quasi nichts kostet ist es trotzdem ein Besuch wert.
Musée de la guerre ou l'on voit vraiment les souffrances d'une guerre, images et photos TRES DURES , avis pero à éviter avec des enfants , n'ai pas fini la visite compléte

An old dirty building with Photos of the war in no particular order. It is more an anti American propaganda museum than anything actually educational or informative about the war. I expected the museum to have a little bias but some of the captions are beyond belief. I have the upmost respect for anyone who has suffered as part of this or indeed any war but the "museum" goes a step too far and doesn't actually educate anyone as to the facts and dates of the war just seeks to show American atrocities.

We visited the War Remnants Museum and were there for about min. We didn't even finish reading half of what was posted on the walls on the first floor. Totally fabricated and propaganda. Yes there was some awful things done through that period of time but the Vietnam Government are so one sided and from the little I was prepared to see was so anti American. I know that bad things were done by the allies but some very bad things were also done by the Viet Cong, however none of this is represented in this museum.
Pas super ... Vraiment déçu Beaucoup trop de photos et de lourdes explications. Le musée est bizarrement constitué et les machines a l'extérieur repeinte ou laisser en piteuse état .
It was the war remnants of US solders. Not anything I ever want to see. I left very early on and I'll never return.

We went here as a part of a bigger tour. This is not so much of a museum as a collection of bits and pieces that suits an agenda. Not terribly informative or objective. Wouldn't bother returning here.
When you take the time to read the first two floors it will make you very very sad to see what other humans can do to each other in war time. Extremely interesting but heavy on the heart

We were excited to explore the museum and learn about the Vietnam war. I went there knowing that many people lost their lives during the war and left knowing that, many people lost their lives during the war. The exhibits inside looked like my 8th poster board presentations. The first 2 floors was basically how terrible America was for coming into the war and called it "Americas war". (I had to google to see why they intervened and found out it was to stop the communists from taking over, so I guess it was good they came to support the south). Fortunately it's only 15k dong and there's nothing else to see in HCMC except for the food scene so it's a must see. I was at the Korean War Museum last month and was amazed at how incredible their story telling and documenting the chain of events. Perhaps I expected too much from this museum.

I was looking forward to going to this museum, The military hard wear was interesting to look at. The photos were interesting to look at. The lay out is poor. The explanation to the pictures, was long and tedious reading. not recommended for young families. This very nearly put me off going to the tunnels. I would recommend the tunnel tour over this.
Very one-sided portrayal of events. Graphic photos. They have some military aircrafts and weapons on display that could be of interest to some. Other than that, not the best display of war remnants

Read so many good reviews of this museum and friends had recommend it to me. I think i missed something as i thought it was a waste of time. It was nothing but propaganda going on about how terrible the Americans were. It was a horrible war but a war is two sided. Not a very objective museum. Also lacked structure - not really sure what you are reading about and fails to give you any context or an overview of the war. Was really looking forward to this and was very disappointed.

This is not a museum, it is photos on the wall with pure socialist propaganda. A very one-sided view of the Vietnam War. Plus: we were not allowed to ask questions from our Vietnamese tour guide on this topic...

В музее представлены черно-белые фотографии времен Гражданской войны во Вьетнаме. Подчеркиваю, Гражданской! Хотя благодаря коммунистической пропаганде у посетителей музея складывается устойчивое ощущение того, что это была не война Северного Вьетнама с Южным, но агрессия США против всего Вьетнама, что на деле совершенно не соответствует действительности. Общеизвестно, что северян -коммунистов поддерживал СССР, снабжал оружием, военными советниками и деньгами, а США поддерживали южан. В итоге с обеих сторон полегло не менее 2 млн человек. Причем войну развязали северяне при поддержке СССР. Разумеется об этом в музее вы не найдете ни одного факта. А в целом музей представляет собой унылое и печальное пропагандистское зрелище в духе закостенелой совковой пропаганды: "во всем виновата Америка" без малейшей попытки взглянуть на войну объективно.
you would have to be an enthusiast to like this place or a nutter about weapons. or american... really boring

Whilst I'm sure both sides committed atrocities in the Vietnam war , this museum is quite one sided. There is a whole room dedicated to the effects of Agent orange on adults and children and is presented in poor taste. Yet outside they appear to be proudly displaying well preserved US fighting machines. Definitely not a highlight of our visit to Ho Chi Min and could have given it a miss.

The artifacts are nice to see but the story being told here is completely biased and not accurate. I would hope that young people are educated enough to see through the propaganda and know that this museum is a feeble attempt to brainwash

This is a rather hateful place & while there is undoubtedly no good side of war, the propaganda displayed in this museum is rather offensive and one sided.  There are various sections & if you do visit I suggest you read up on both sides of the war before making any informed decisions as you'll only see one half of the story shown here & you'll see it in rather graphic detail.  I don't think the Americans had any business in Vietnam in the first place but they are portrayed as hideous monsters in this museum.  Personally, I'm old & ugly enough to read about the wars & make my own decisions but I found it rather sickening to find a classroom for young children inside, much the same as the effects of agent Orange that still affect the indigenous population.  If there was a winner of this war I couldn't tell you who it was, only that the local people I personally spoke to in various locations, North and south, harboured no ongoing resentment toward the Americans & have moved on in a way the museum doesn't appear to have done.  That isn't to say you should avoid it but I personally feel you shouldn't go in blindly & see this as a true depiction of the war.  These are my own personal views from a Londoner that had several conversations with a German submarine commander at our hotel.  I think if there's a moral to this story it's that war does not seem to be the answer to anything except for hurt and hate.

This is one of the place must visit.. There's so much history behind Vietnam war that I did not know. The first part of the history on first floor was full of anti-american propaganda from different countries that denounce American present in Saigon. It went on to show the devastating of the war, the affect of agents orange, and types of weapons use for different defense. It is very interesting. Must see....

Having read the amazing reviews I had to go, being very interested in the history of the American / Vietnam war. The photos (spread over 3 floors ) are disturbing as they're meant to be and are a tremendous insight to the disgrace of war, and the tanks and aircraft outside are good to see, but the crowds.....groups of clueless European teenagers doing stupid selfie poses in front of important historical hardware, pushing and getting in the way when trying to absorb the thoughs was incredibly annoying. It's not Disney land! I visited the war museums in Hue and Da Nang ( where I was lucky to have the place completely to myself ) and if you've a serious interest in would suggest those places ....space to breathe and take it all in. Da Nang has a couple of Mig fighters as well as the US hardware.
A bit too graphic, would definitely not take children, not expensive, spread over 3 floors and all much the same

A very biased, one sided exhibition. Yes, terrible things happened in the war, but the atrocities were on both sides. You get only the agent orange, my lai, etc version of the war. I understand you win the war, you get to determine the version of history. But, this is the only instance of anti Americanism I saw in 3 weeks. If you are American and were not an anti war protester, this will offend you. It's on every tour itinerary, but I would skip it if possible

No wonder the museum used to be called the "American War Crimes Museum". The place is just propaganda and I was fine with that. But visually it is just disappointing as there isn't much on display at all. I would skip it and spend that time in taking in the awesome energy of the city.
你們在這家博物館看到了什麼？你們的判定如何？你們所看到的只不過是片面的資訊而已！幹你娘我們在越南的華人犯了什麼錯？越共怎樣對待我們？這些你們知道多少？
일단 2층에서 파는 물을 5만동 달라함 (편의점에서 4000동) 또 하나 보자면 오만 전쟁사진을 보는데 맘이 아파서 별로 보고싶지 않음..  남편이.보는 동안 난 그냥 의자에서 기다림..  다시는 가고싶지 않은 곳임.

Admission fee is 60000 dong. Many old photographs and some artifacts from many years ago. The only thing is interest is a plane and helicopter from the Vietnam-American war... Some arms from yesterday year. Not sure I would recommend going especially if u running short of time.

I was not impressed with this "museum". It is one thing to show how the war was perceived from the Vietnamese side, but another to act as if your military did nothing. The "facts" displayed are misrepresented and skewed to fit an agenda. It is has a lot of anti-American sentiment which we did not find to be the norm among the people of HCMC. I would not recommend.

If you want to have a clear picture of what the history in Vietnam is about, this is not the place for you to visit, because as I said, it is where the winner side showcasing what are for their favor. Half-truth is never a truth. But if you are curious, you can come and have a look. I have come here once in my life and never come back. I don't recommend my foreigned friends to come either.
Der er foto og krigsudstyr, fortalt på en kedelig og meget forvirrende måde. Vi fik ikke et program, og der var ikke mulighed for nogen audioguide.
Communist propanda...depressing photos...we felt it was completely against the United States...who was their ally in the Vietnam war.
One sided story about America only. Nothing about Vietnam. Looks like a propaganda theatre from the government. Not what I want to see.

As part of a tour group I have no idea why the operator would schedule a visit to this place for Americans. Sure it all happened, and most wish it didn't, but for a county that is now a good partner with the US the slant in this museum is so anti-American I don't know why a "yank" would want to visit it. Spend your time absorbing the culture of the city and the people and avoid this very unpleasant place!

The Good: you learn about the war history, if that is your interest. The Bad : it gives you a strong hit of what chemical weapons are capable of doing (agent orange) It shows how bad war can be. I personally have no interest in anything war related but it was recommended should go. I walked out half way into the agent orange room. People should not hurt each other and I felt visiting the museum supported that. I have no interest in visiting the tunnels either. The address way better things to invest your time in.

A waste of a few hours. A large amount of the information is biased towards the Vietnamese (which you may expect in Vietnam) but to educate someone in a museum it should surely have both sides of the story. A few stories that were on the walls felt fictional or dramatised for effect and to make the Americans appear all the more evil. Large waste of time. Do not find it interesting to read propaganda all day.

This place is an insult to the term "museum". It's a collection of snuff pictures and Vietnamese Government propaganda. No attempt is made to portray the Vietnamese War in a balanced way or the hundreds of thousands of people killed by the North Vietnamese, including a number of massacres, e.g. the 6,000 - 8,000 massacred in Hue by the Viet Cong. One dimensional representations of history do not lead to insight or peace and the Vietnamese people deserve better.
In my opinion, the most interesting part of the museum is the helicopters, tanks etc located near the ticket booth. Enjoyed our Highland Coffee there as well.
its a horrible museum not at all for kids, filled with so cruel pictures and everything its from the vietnameses side of the story. I had to wait for my husband outside with the kids.

Us westerners live in a bubble fed with media rubbish and political spin, and although whilst walking around the museum you have to bare in mind it is very much a one sided view...im sure there were war crimes committed from all sides involved. I left feeling angry, disgusted by the U.S. Military, government and global corporates such as MONSANTO = AGENT ORANGE. How the Vietnamese people forgive you I will never know...

A parte gli aerei e carri armati presenti nel cortile e la ghigliottina con le celle, non ho trovato nulla di interessante: solo foto. Tantissime foto. Sicuramente interessanti e toccanti, ma le foto posso tranquillamente guardarle su Internet dal PC di casa mia. Niente di tangibile. Niente di speciale. Niente che mi abbia fatto dire "abbiamo fatto bene a venire qui". Io non lo chiamerei museo, ma galleria fotografica, almeno uno sa cosa aspettarsi.
It certainly makes one think. This is probably a good thing. See this and then go to Cu Chi Tunnels.
Very biased museum dedicated to bashing the south Vietnam and the americans, with absolutely no mention of even a single wrongdoing conducted by the north. Pure propaganda.

There's no doubting that America was heavily involved in the Vietnam conflict and that napalm bombing left terrible consequences, but this 'museum' suggests that American forces just decided to invade the country for no reason. This place offers a completely biased account of the "American" war in Vietnam - and gives no history on it being a battle between the communist North Vietnamese (backed by Russia, China etc) and the South Vietnamese (backed by the US) other than a brief mention of the "South Vietnam Puppet Army" (on a statistic on the number of troops involved in the war). It's the equivalent of visiting Berlin and finding a museum blaming WW2 on the Jews. The photography collection is actually excellent, and it has the potential to be a top-notch museum, but only when the country admits to things in their past. If you won't admit your mistakes, how will you ever learn from them? Go see it - but with your eyes open. FYI - I'm British, not American, and I'm not biased in any way - just want to see accurate accounts of history. Also, photographs in the museum are very graphic and unsuitable for children (but they do have a creche on the top floor).

the most horrendous place I ever seen, when you see the pictures of bits of bodies held up by soldiers, We were reduced to tears, we were told that would take 45 min to go through, we were standing outside in less than 2 minutes

Siamo stati pochi giorni fa in questo museo! Nonostante gli argomenti trattati siano molto interessanti gli orari di apertura e di chiusura sono indecenti ... Non adatti alla visita di turisti stranieri! Noi siamo entrati alle 11 e non ci hanno fatto finire di visitare le sale che Alle 11,30 ci hanno sbattuto fuori ... Non in modo gentile e garbato come meriterebbero tutti i turisti che pagano il biglietto ma chiudendoci le luci delle sale ,lasciandoci letteralmente al buio !! Peccato ...

This is at its core a propaganda museum for the Communist Party. It has some nice military equipment in the parking lot but the exhibits inside portray a very one-sided view of the conflict. It's almost completely focused on America's negative impact on Vietnam during the war and frequently with claims that can't be substantiated. All of that said, this shouldn't really surprise anyone. It's a war museum in a one party state.
Someone should have warned us ahead of time. Didn't like the portrayal of the American participation in the War.

This was very interesting to see, although I was very disappointed in the remnants or photography and information given. The display of US aircrafts and tanks outside as you walked in was up close and personal experience.

This museum has some old bombs and military equipment but almost everything else in the museum is photographic, it took less than 15 minutes to see 3 floors of exhibits. Nearly everything in the building is available on my computer.

I found it really disturbing. It seemed very slanted, as they did win the war and it is their country. The American War of Aggression was a particularly disturbing exhibit. From an American view it was not a great or truthful exhibit.

this is the only part of Ho Chi Minh City we did not like. the photos in the museum were very confronting and upset us. I don't think this should be on every tour. only if the traveller asks to see it...........(we travelled in February 2014 not December)
Interesting if you are wanting to learn the Vietnamese perspective on the war and see aircraft and tanks left behind by the Americans but I didn't enjoy my visit here.

The photography is a brutal reminder of what war is really like. If you enjoy graphic photographs of war victims and want to have mental images burned into your head then this is right for you. I grew up in the US while the war was going on and I have plenty of images in my head to remind me of what happened there. No need to see it all over again to refresh those images. The communist propaganda copy that accompanies the photography is, well, just silly. Go for the photography and don't bother reading the captions if you feel the need to see how awful it was during this time in Vietnam. There is a section, I believe on the second floor, that I did find interesting that profiles the photographers of the war. I spent all my time here reading about their lives and careers while others in my group toured the museum. Don't bring your children in to see the photography, but they may enjoy seeing some of the weapons of the war in the courtyard in front of the building.

I think that such museums are important to society so i do jot rate the exhibition but my experience. I came to vietnam for a nice holiday and i wish i dis not make myself exposed to all those pictures of peoples pain. You can call me ignorant and you will be right but this is the honest review of my visit.

Typical communist propaganda trash. They put on display a one sided story of how the side that they invaded committed war crimes whilst they conveniently leave out their war crimes (Hue Massacre 1968 and million dead caused by their purges and land reform in the north). Don't waste your time!!!

I only had two separate people tell me about the War Remnants Museum before I went there and both of them described it as "emotionally draining" and this is probably the most accurate description I could think of also.  I'm not big on history and only went there because my partner wanted to. The pictures and stories are very confronting and by the time we had finished the exhibit I felt nauseous and we both left feeling regretful that we had visited this place.  Even while I am writing this now, a whole day later, I am feeling nauseous again just thinking about it. Do not recommend unless you like to see scores of photos of human suffering, torture and deformed babies.
One sided ,papoganda. Of course all war are evil, civilian dies ,only the people heading them gains.

Full of propaganda, opinions and 'quotations'. Quotations are not facts and facts is what I would expect to see being represented in a museum. Therefore this is not a museum but a building of misinformation and bits of old paper. I expected to see a bit of one sidedness however the amount of western animosity expressed here made me nervous.
I know that in war, every side wants to remember the things in a different way... But this museum trying to rewrite the history in a very unfair and one sided way!  Not an accurate information!

If you happen to be a history buff or close, this may be a good stop for you. I found it rather dreary and depressing, much of it related to the French occupation. I am a VN American War vet, USMC, so I recognized somewhat the scenario here. But perhaps that is unfair to say. Anyway, unless you need, must have some past tense today gore & commie propaganda, check it out. I did about 20 years ago. Thinking it has not changed much. The displays are old and not well put together.
Not a fan of this one, there's a few choppers, tanks and planes.. but most of it is just posters, flags and propaganda from the war, it's not that thrilling and you can cover it in about 20-30 mins.

This was not what I was hoping for. We went hoping to learn a little bit about the war and appreciate the history of Vietnam a little bit more..this is not the place for that.  It's a collection of photos and relics of the more horrific side of the war. I don't deny it was terrible war and many inhumane things occurred and civilians suffered greatly, I don't even mind having to confront these uncomfortable things, what upset me was the lack of information or context provided in either English or Vietnamese. It turned it from what I hoped to be an educational and enlightening experience into morbid gawping. Made worse by tourists taking pictures of the pictures, for what purpose I don't know. Personally I felt the 'museum' did the opposite of it's intentions and did no justice to the pain and horror the Vietnamese people incurred, it just paraded around their suffering for all to stare at, without providing any actual context, information or explanation.  We left after 15minutes feeling quite upset (especially, as previously mentioned, at the people morbidly photographing the exhibits).  If you want to stare at human suffering, this is for you. If you want to learn about the war, the consequences and lessons learnt from it..buy a book and avoid here at all costs!
Um museu da Guerra do Vietnã que conta só um lado da historia. Pura propaganda comunista. Exibe fotos sensacionalistas de péssimo gosto. Nao voltaria.

Un luogo imperdibile dicevano: un posto deludente nella realtà. Si tratta solo un edificio di due piani con delle sale dove sono appese al muro delle foto della guerra con gli americani. Non c'è un percorso o un senso ma solo immagini appese con delle didascalie sotto. Una ricerca su internet può fornire le stesse informazioni senza dover pagare un biglietto di ingresso. Il senso ultimo del museo è: quanto sono stati cattivi gli americani! Che poi è vero, la colpa degli USA assieme alla loro follia è incancellabile ma un museo parte da altri presupposti e questo non insegna nulla. Fuori nel cortile aerei e mezzi americani e un esempio delle prigioni e delle torture durante la guerra franco-vietnamita, meno cosnosciuta ma altrettanto squallida. Alla fine è un posto che potete evitare senza perdere alcun chè.

Went to HCMC with a group of friends. This was the self-inflicted "low-point” of our fun vacation together. While all of us wanted to go because of the Trip Advisor rating, we could have done without visiting this very old-fashioned museum. I would not advise anyone go and pay entry fee if you have a weak stomach. But, if you enjoy paying to look at a wall of pictures that make you sad, this might be for you. While it is not unexpected, this museum shows a very one-sided "propaganda-like" depiction of a very sad and terrible time in history. I say that it is a very old-fashioned museum because it is just a bunch of rooms of pictures with captions depicting gory war scenes (that were very anti-American and anti-French). This museum is not very creative in their story-telling, it felt more like an art gallery since it consisted of just pictures on walls (which you could easily “google” if you desired to - and you might get better quality images on your computer screen anyways). In my opinion, there are so many other beautiful and less hate-filled venues and attractions to see around Ho Chi Minh City, that this museum could easily be skipped. I was with a group of friends (our group consisted of Europeans and Americans) and all of us felt really down and sad after visiting. The majority of us did not even finish the tour of the museum, due to the very repetitive hateful/sad nature of the museum.

Wow is all i can say...visited here on the last day of our trip and its fair to say...wish i hadn't of.... Confronting, sad, morbid and visually shocking. War is a terribly shocking event and this country has endured more than its fair share but to so graphically display it in such a confronting way is, in my opinion, not necessary... We went to Vietnam to enjoy the beautiful country that it is and this really put a dampener on a wonderful holiday.... My advise, just dont do it....absolutely nothing to be gained by this place...its a testiment to the people that they have moved on from this shocking event....this place serves as a constant reminder and if they're fair dinkum about showing what really happened...they should show both sides....
If you can't watch a horror painting, don't come here. After leaving here, you would get upset seeing all the lies the Vietnamese Communist government say about the war.

Really awful for kids and Americans--two floors devoted to Agent Orange victims. I actually felt as if other tourists were giving us dirty looks there. Only things of any interest were the photographers exhibit on top floor and the planes, tanks, etc outside--all US. Overall, a waste of money. Much preferred the Reunification Palace nearby.
Nothing to really look at and certainly not very informative. A very nice building, and not much else.

This is in no way a museum, rather a picture galley of gore and destruction. Yes the atrocities were horrendous, but right or wrong both sides including the allies suffered and this should also be recorded in this gallery so a more balanced view of the TOTAL loss can be appreciated.

Todo el mundo sabe que USA es, fué y será un país interesado en las guerras por cuestiones monetarias y políticas sin tener nada que ver con el territorio al que invade. Está muy bien que un museo de guerra tenga tanques, aviones, misiles, armas y material bélico en general, lo que no hace falta es poner fotos de gente masacrada (incluyendo niños), mutilada, deformada por las bombas químicas y todo ese tipo de atrocidades. Para colmo no hay 3 o 4 fotos, hay como 200 y dejan entrar a niños sin antes avisar lo que pueden llegar a presenciar. Entiendo perfectamente que Vietnam quiera mostrar el daño que EEUU les hizo en la guerra, pero no hace falta el morbo. Si estás de vacaciones en Vietnam tratando de pasarla bien, no vayas a este museo porque vas a salir deprimido e impresionado por lo que el ser humano es capaz de hacer.

I know this is the number 1 attraction in Ho Chi Minh but I found it to be a very biased crash course on sadistic torture techniques and the suffering caused from an Agent Orange attack. I did not like it one little bit.
easy to faint inside because of No ventilation and air conditioner. Full of sweat taste inside museum
The history part is very interesting but very small. Then it's pictures and pictures of the atrocities in a very propaganda way...
It was very hut the day we went to the museum . There is no AC in the building . There was a lot of pictures from war and that was not pleasing .

Le Musée veut présenter les horreurs de la guerre du Vietnam, vue côté vietnamien ou côté du vainqueur. Ce n’est que de la propagande anti-américaines et anti-françaises par ailleurs. Il conviendrait de rétablir un juste milieu, la cruauté a toujours existé dans tous les camps et dans toutes les guerres. Ce Musée ne présente aucun intérêt, si ce n’est le matériel de guerre exposé dans le jardin et abandonné par les américains lors de leur départ de Saigion.

This museum certainly details that "war is hell" playing up the anti-American angle in a biased one-sided propaganda piece. Unfortunately, for those Westerners with minimal history education on the Vietnam-American war, the propaganda will be completely successful. Of course, the government can choose to gloss over communist massacres and re-education camps; however, there is no attempt at some introspection. Having been to other museums for horrible events (e.g. Holocaust, Dachau, Auschwitz), there can be some attempt at a historical museum. This is a very biased one-sided propoganda museum. If one is blissfully unaware of the savages of war, then this is a must see; however, look at the pictures and ignore many of the captions.

Definitely not recomended for children. This place will give em nightmare.  Me personaly not recomending this place for anyone on holiday. Because when we are travel we want to have some fun not ruining our mood..  For you guys that love this kinda place. U guys definitely should visit DOG FARM in all around vietnam. Not for children or fainted heart though.  Cheers
Tears and anger enveloped me.......why don't the Americans stay home? I knew the American War in Vietnam was shocking. The Vietnamese are so forgiving.
This is just a horrible horrible did I mention horrible museum. There is nothing that is super accurate in this museum.

A few photos in this museum were very powerful although the commentary under the photos was not appropriate as they were biased and offensive. Majority of the photos were of dead children, people being tortured and dismembered bodies. These pictures were not necessary and not something that the public needs to see, especially children. It was possible for this museum to have displayed photos in a tasteful manner however they have chosen not to.

I went because it was one of the recommended things to do in Ho Chi Minh. Initially a lot of reading required in the first exhibits which was interesting. The photos in the later sections were very confronting. If you don't want to see tragic photos of victims of war, injured soldiers and death, i woukd advise not to go. The planes, tanks and helicopters outside were great for photos. Overall interesting but depressing!
Not sure why this is ranked so high.....sad. Prepare yourself before you go....lots of disturbing images. That is war, but be prepared.

The Remnants Museum was very informative and shows the ugly side of the Vietnam War... especially the American genocide, brutality and destruction of Vietnam. However, looking closer you realise that both "sides" are guilty of atrocious war crimes.

Dieses Museum ist der absolute Beweis, dass in Vietnam der Kommunismus herrscht. Also wird nur aus Sicht der VC dargestellt. Die US-Armee waren die einzigen grausamen Menschen in diesem Krieg. Vietnam stellt sich hier als reines Opfer dar. Kinder und Jugendliche sollten Teile des Museums unbedingt meiden. Die Bilder sind zum Teil nur noch geschmacklos und grausam. Das können Kinder noch nicht verarbeiten. Es wird rein garnichts über die Geschichte erzählt. Reines Propaganda Gebäude! Spart euch die Zeit und besochtigt lieber noch einen Tempel.

So ein schreckliches Museum! Leider nur einseitig dargestellt. Die Bilder sind unglaublich schrecklich. Ich frag mich, wenn man die Angst der Soldaten sieht, wie man da noch mit der Kamera drauf halten kann! Auch behinderte Menschen zur Schau stellen ist geschmacklos. Hier wird vor nichts zurück geschreckt. Auf keinen Fall mit Kindern besuchen, die bekommen Alpträume von den Bildern.

Mình định quay 1 đoạn videpo 360 độ để chia sẽ với bạn bè thế giới về bảo tàng nhưng gặp khó khăn vì chính sách kỳ lạ. Nhân viên bảo tàng không cho phép mình quay phim, bảo phải có giấy giới thiệu... Mình quay phim cá nhân thôi lấy đâu ra giấy giới thiệu mà đưa.  Trong khi đó có nhiều du khách nước ngoài trò chuyện hứa thú và chụp ảnh với thiết bị quay 360 độ của mình. Mình quay yên lặng khung viên bên ngoài cũng không làm phiền ai. Vậy mà?

If you are interested in how a Communist country uses propaganda, then this "museum" is worth a visit because this is what the museum is all about. It is dedicated to decrying the American involvement in the Vietnam war. Some of the pictures are interesting but it is definitely a one-sided view.
I expected a balanced view but only saw extremely biased propaganda. Extremely disappointed but, I guess that I was naive to expect balance.  American planes and military equipment was worth seeing.

This museum gives a very one-sided horrible display of the American participation in the Vietnam conflict. It attempts to make you believe that the US broke most Geneva convention rules of war. It blames all birth defects in Vietnam on agent orange. It talks of extensive use of chemical weapons but close review shows some "Tear Gas" canisters. Its hard to believe that Vietnam wants good relations with the US after seeing this horrendous display. Skip this attraction. It totally ignores Communist atrocities and is pure propaganda. I'm sorry I visited this place with my son and grandson.

Total one sided view based on the American "invasion" of Vietnam. Although the US did a lot of wrong things it tells nothing of the Communist side. On a high note, they did remove the jars of deformed baby fetuses allegedly caused by Agent Orange...

It took us ages to find the museum and when we got there it had closed early for lunch which was really disappointing considering the time spent to find it. We walked around outside looking at the planes and tanks but we along with other people were told to leave the grounds as they wanted to close that also. It didn't reopen for another couple of hours, we couldn't wait that long. We would have loved to have gone inside the museum.

Felt it was very biased. The U.S. armoury outside was interesting. But we skipped half of the lower ground floor after having gone round half way and realised that it was saying the same thing. The floors above are definitely not for children, graphics are shocking and depressing. I don't think we need such graphic imagery to make one realise how sad war is and the impact it has. Very one sided too, it did not mention the atrocities North Vietnam caused as it "liberated" the South.

The War Remnants Museum was formerly called the Exhibition House for US and Puppet Crimes and then the Exhibition House for Crimes of War and Aggression. The names pretty much sum up the exhibits. Over all, it was probably once a valuable part of the war effort, but that was decades ago. Today it is just a lousy museum.  Over and over again, the war is described as a war of aggression and the typical conduct of a war -- e.g. using guns, cannons, and bombs -- is referred to as an atrocity. There is little or no mention of Vietnamese actions -- neither those of the North nor the South Vietnamese armies nor is there much discussion of Vietnamese leaders. For the most part, the exhibits do not reveal that the Vietnamese did anything but serve as targets for American atrocities. There is little mention even of Vietnamese soldiers. The French are barely mentioned.  Someday, perhaps, the Vietnamese with come to terms with how the war came to be; that Vietnamese were actually combatants and not universally passive, patriotic victims of Americans, Americans who for reasons that are never explained simply wanted to bomb, stab, and shoot Vietnamese people. The ruthlessness of the Viet Minh toward their own people would be useful to add. But, maybe they never will -- one of the prerogatives of the winners is not to look back.  There's space to explain more and to give a more complete picture. This would probably strengthen the impact of the museum in telling Vietnam's story. I'd be grateful for exhibits that discussed the horrors of any war and the benefits of peace, but that may be too much to ask of a museum like this one.  But the problems of this museum go deeper than simply a fragmentary and one-sided view. Most of the exhibits in the building are simply printed panels that don't attempt to explain anything. It's not in a real sense a museum at all.  It's encouraging that at least in the South, the people and the society seem to have moved well beyond the time when this museum was relevant to anyone but tourists.

Let me say a couple of introductory words about myself: I have a majors in History and worked six years in a military museum - one of the most visited museums of my country, Portugal. I know a thing or two about museums and I visit all military museums I can around the world, which happens quite often.  Now, this is the statement; the worst military museum ever, not considering those in little nations and/or nations with no considerable military past.  The outdoors exhibit left me a bad taste... I wasn't even through the gates. What's that... one M-48, one M-60, one M-113 and not much more... banal items. Nothing interesting. Being European I still can imagine this kind of hardware can be found everywhere in USA... in small local museums, I mean.  Then, the indoors exhibit - if one can call "exhibit" to a few rooms with pictures and propaganda and little more. All this topped by an immense horde of tourists, so numerous it becomes suffocating.  An awful experience. I don't understand why is this place so rated. Only good thing: the ticket is so cheap it didn't hurt to leave after 10 min and call it a fail.

It's a shame that people think they are getting an accurate picture of the Vietnam War from this museum. The War Remnants Museum mentions NOTHING of the hundreds of thousands of Southern Vietnamese who were killed at the hands of the Northern Vietnamese. Nor does it mention the thousands of Southerners who were forced from their homes and businesses (by the North) to "relocate" after the war. It somehow forgets to mention the executions of its own people after the war or why the U.S. came to Vietnam in the first place. When you visit this museum please bear in mind that this is still a communist country, and information comes from the government. You are only getting one very filtered side of the awful and horrific war story.
